### JanSz — 2015-09-12T00:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-paleos-bitter-truth.13285/post-177015>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=165041)
>
> ^^^those numbers seem very tied to a karyotype issue. I think klinefelter syndrome is a plausible explanation with an excessive blue light lifestyle. Even Jaminet made the connection with his weight and the flight to Oz........that tells you JM has a massive ubiquitin problem.

Klinefelter syndrome or Klinefelter's syndrome (KS) (/ˈklaɪnfɛltər/) also known as 47,XXY or XXY, is the set of symptoms that result from two or more X chromosome in males.\[1\]
The primary feature is sterility.\[1\]
Often symptoms may be subtle and many people do not realize they are affected.
Sometimes symptoms are more prominent and may include weaker muscles, greater height, poor coordination, less body hair, smaller genitals, breast growth, and less interest in sex.\[2\]
Often it is only at puberty that these symptoms are noticed.\[3\]
Intelligence is usually normal; however, reading difficulties and problems with speech are more common.
Symptoms are typically more severe if three or more X chromosomes are present.\[2\]

Klinefelter syndrome usually occurs randomly.
An older mother might increase the risk slightly. T
he condition is not inherited from one's parents.\[4\]

\===================================

A karyotype (from Greek κάρυον karyon, "kernel", "seed", or "nucleus", and τύπος typos, "general form") is the number and appearance of chromosomes in the nucleus of a eukaryotic cell.
The term is also used for the complete set of chromosomes in a species, or an individual organism.\[1\]\[2\]\[3\]

Karyotypes describe the chromosome count of an organism, and what these chromosomes look like under a light microscope.

..

### JanSz — 2015-09-13T02:38:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-paleos-bitter-truth.13285/post-177050>

> [yewwei.tan said:](/goto/post?id=165025)
>
> \+ I'm not sure I'm getting the PG/E2 ratio correct. I'm getting 0.60 ng/mL progesterone, and 21.40 pg/mL E2 => 0.0214 ng/mL E2, and PG/E2 (0.60 / 0.0214) = 28.03. This is very far off from the 500 that Dr Kruse has recommended in the past....

I am not sure either.
I am not sure what dr Kruse meant when he said 500 (for women).
500 is big number and am sure he means it. Lots depends on laboratory used, type of test and type of sample (serum/saliva).
When trying to make all this plausible or close I came with this:

\--------------------
[http://www.johnleemd.com/bioidentical-hormones.html](http://www.johnleemd.com/bioidentical-hormones.html)
John R. Lee, M.D

For example, in healthy women without breast cancer, we find that the saliva progesterone level routinely is 200 to 300 times greater than the saliva estradiol level. In women with breast cancer, the saliva progesterone/estradiol ratio is considerably less than 200 to 1.
\--------------------
But saliva tests are often all over the place. Lot depends on laboratory that does that testing.

This saliva test from Genova Diagnostics:
[https://www.gdx.net/core/sample-reports/Rhythm-Plus-Sample-Report.pdf](https://www.gdx.net/core/sample-reports/Rhythm-Plus-Sample-Report.pdf)
uses same units for progesterone and estradiol, pmol/L
looking at the sample it may be real hard to get anything over 100.
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

I think when talking P/E ratio, some mean to be molar ratio.
So when using blood report one may want to convert to molar units first.

Progesterone Molecular mass 314.46 g/mol
Estradiol Molecular mass 272.38 g/mol

......
For healthy man, testing using serum, at LabCorp, using Estradiol, sensitive test
good numbers are:

progesterone=1.4ng/mL
E2=20 pg/mL
P/E=60.6 mol/mol

I calculated other combinations and posted them on table here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/)

.

### Nittygrittydanny — 2015-03-13T16:47:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenagers-acne.13295/post-158441>

my 16 y/o daughter is having breakouts on her face. Her diet is mostly carb based, breads, corn etc. doesn't like seafood of any type, love bacon and beef?

Is her diet mostly to blame for her breakout?

Danny

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-13T17:17:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenagers-acne.13295/post-158446>

all about a light mismatch. Ubiquitin issue. BLUE LIGHT!!!!!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-13T17:18:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenagers-acne.13295/post-158447>

Without seafood she can decipher sunlight to DC current........and the biologic toll is in her skin. First place a mismatch happens is skin and gut in kids.

### Nittygrittydanny — 2015-03-13T18:11:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenagers-acne.13295/post-158455>

Thanks Jack. I'll give her this site to read.
She is on a medical/nursing path in school, going into her 3rd year of medical academy.

I figured gut was part of it and like most teens her phone is almost always in hand. She was concieved while my wife was still breast feeding our daughter prior to her. I kinda wonder if her growth as a fetus was tampered with due to that? She is our only child with cavities and gets sick easier than the others and was colicy as a baby. She's smart, but doesn't exhibit the memory skills of her older and younger sisters.
My wife took fish oil her whole pregnancy with our last child and she walked faster and learns/retains info very well.

### JMO — 2015-03-17T03:57:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenagers-acne.13295/post-158731>

> [Nittygrittydanny said:](/goto/post?id=158441)
>
> my 16 y/o daughter is having breakouts on her face. Her diet is mostly carb based, breads, corn etc. doesn't like seafood of any type, love bacon and beef?
>
> Is her diet mostly to blame for her breakout?
>
> Danny

Hi Danny,
My daughter is 15 and this is how we got her to eat oysters. She likes shopping and new clothes so I told her that she could work for them. I set up a system where she could get so many dollars for eating 3 raw oysters, drink xx bottles of water, or ct for 15 min. She took the oysters almost every time -with horseradish and Tabasco to hide the taste. I tracked it on a piece of paper on the frig. We went thru a few rounds of this. Pretty soon she realized that her face was really clear Everytime she ate oysters. So she started asking for oysters almost everyday (dont give her points for them anymore--we need to work on water and CT). We buy them by the case and keep them in the frig in the garage. We also have salmon (I posted the recipe under epi-paleo recipes) and other seafood (coconut shrimp is kid friendly and crab legs with butter, seafood Alfredo--which is butter, cream, and Parmesan cheese). I turn off her phone at bedtime....she doesn't like that.

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-03-17T06:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenagers-acne.13295/post-158738>

i was raised eating seafood at least 3x a week and alaskan salmon and onion sandwiches at school, never saw blue light too much because i was up and down with the sun and we only had amber bulbs at home in lamps that were shaded, was hardly allowed television
had awful acne in early teen years all the same...

### Nittygrittydanny — 2015-03-18T22:02:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenagers-acne.13295/post-158890>

Thanks JMO. She won't eat ANY seafood? I hate it for her, but she has to learn on her own i guess???  She likes red meat and chicken though), and I she like salad. I guess she'll have to spend her hard earned $$$ on suplements?

### JMO — 2015-03-19T00:43:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teenagers-acne.13295/post-158902>

> [Nittygrittydanny said:](/goto/post?id=158890)

> Thanks JMO. She won't eat ANY seafood? I hate it for her, but she has to learn on her own i guess???  She likes red meat and chicken though), and I she like salad. I guess she'll have to spend her hard earned $$$ on suplements?

Just throwing an idea out there. My DH wouldn't have just eaten oysters either especially if I told her it was good for her. I had to bribe her. It didn't take long for her to make the connection on her own that it clears her acne and helps her feel better. IMHO money well spent. As Dr Kruse says 1 oyster is worth a lb for grass fed beef liver. Chicken and red meat (only if grass fed) has very little DHA.

# Thread: CTswwimmers in San Francisco?

### ON MY WAY — 2015-03-16T04:37:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ctswwimmers-in-san-francisco.13317/post-158617>

Hello Everyone,

I'm loving this forum.

I live just north of San Francisco, in Mill Valley, Marin County.

I'd like to be going into the Pacific ocean for some CT a few times a week but don't want to go alone (seaweed monsters and all that).

Are there any SF'ers in this group who would like to meet up for a dip at say, Muir Woods Beach, or Stinson? Or even at Fort Chronkite?

Let me know and thanks-

Lorna

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-16T05:04:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ctswwimmers-in-san-francisco.13317/post-158624>

Elaine used to live there but she left a while ago........

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ctswwimmers-in-san-francisco.13317/post-158653>

will be there visiting in june for 4 days before the cruise...

### ON MY WAY — 2015-03-18T17:24:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ctswwimmers-in-san-francisco.13317/post-158876>

Thanks, Caroline!
Josh, if you want to get in the very very cold water, let me know!

### sjoshua — 2015-03-18T18:16:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ctswwimmers-in-san-francisco.13317/post-158878>

>

Hello
I'm in SJ, but usually end up going to Santa Cruz for the beaches since it is easier to get to than SF... Also, much quieter down there  I do end up near the City on occasion for bike parties and such though, and still have to visit Muir... If I work out a trip up that way camping or similar I will give you a heads up!

If you ever plan to make it down to SC on the weekends, I'd probably be game to join you for a dip too.

@[kyrakitty](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9895/) I believe is in SF, but only pokes in here once and a while these days, trying to dodge the noise I imagine.

The weather is beautiful in the Bay, but we humans have created quite the microwave here. My time here is ticking down as my SO awaits some new lungs, then we get to escape!

### RobH — 2015-03-20T06:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ctswwimmers-in-san-francisco.13317/post-159005>

just got back from business trip to east bay. Had 2 dozen oysters while there and a few glasses of Malbec. Tried hard to stay out of the traffic, and unfortunately the bay near our offices was pretty nasty -- I did consider jumping in but the toxin load was a bit high...

RobH

### ON MY WAY — 2015-03-21T06:26:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ctswwimmers-in-san-francisco.13317/post-159092>

Hey SJoshua- You know, I have only ever been to SJ once in my life and it was about 30 years ago! I was a kid! Funny, it's only a few hours. Alas. I like it up here in the trees. Or in France/Hawaii/you get the idea. Santa Cruz is so nice, though. Swimming there would be great, I'd think. Once you got used to freeeeezeeeing temps! Your SO awaits new lungs? There's a phrase I've never seen written. Godspeed with the new lungs! And then to where? I always wonder... where is it okay? I used to live on Kauai and I was happiest there, though the GMO spraying is terrible, and the navy does a lot of sonar experimentation there. Strange to think of paradise as a toxic soup, isn't it? Though CA is that way now too despite an utterly gorgeous spring! Gorgeous. Best best to you, your SO and the lungs!

RobH,
YES- very great oysters here! I was driving an hour up to Hog Island and buying a bag of 50 every week for a few weeks there! You buy them right where they pull them out of the water. They'll even give you gloves and a shucker so you can sit on the benches there at water's edge to chow down! So good. I was shucking for breaky daily!!

The East Bay is so intense! It's locked in totally by freeways and the oil refinery is right there! Very happy to hear you didn't swim! I live on the ocean side, so at least there's more movement, though I'm sure it's still not the greatest. We're doing our best, aren't we?

Cheers-

Yes, it is a weird phrase - she has cystic fibrosis. We are both originally from Upstate, NY but moved out here a few years back for a second opinion with Stanford's CF program. The move was prior to my current level of understanding health; likely never would have picked the valley, but am glad I did in some ways since it has been such a beautiful experience otherwise. Our next stop will be gulf of mexico area for all of the reasons Jack has said (or I have gathered as implied)- sea level, northern hemisphere water, ample seafood supplies, deep crater making Earth's resonance stronger, close(r) to the equator, less human-driven nnEMF. It will also be somewhat closer to family in NY.

# Thread: Beginning the Journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beginning-the-journey.13330/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AJC — 2015-03-16T15:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beginning-the-journey.13330/post-158665>

Hello everyone,

Must say I'm extremely happy to have found this place. It's very exciting to see someone finally merging the sister sciences of physics and biology into a whole that can be implemented by all for their benefit.

I've dipped into a little of this and that so far on the blog part of the website and am coming into a realization as to the vastness of this material as well as the fact that new research is constantly being done and new protocols constantly created. Because of this I'm having a little difficulty in determining where to begin on the road to optimal. For instance, in the most recent Ubiquination article Jack mentions that diet is really the third thing to yoked, but I'm having a hard time figuring out where to go to learn how to optimize the first two.

I don't have any acute concerns I'm looking to take care of, but am simply seeking to start the path "from the beginning" so to speak. Could someone point me in the right direction (series of articles to read) so I can begin without having to spend the next few days digging around to determine where the "beginning point" is? Judging from the most recent series I'm assuming it will have to do with EMF or CT, but as I said at this point I am finding it hard to determine.

Any advice is very much appreciated.

### JMO — 2015-03-17T03:32:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-power.13335/post-158725>

Just got back from FL vacation. Spend almost the entire week walking in the ocean or on the beach. Several years ago I started being extremely sensitive to the sun. My face would turn really red after only a few minutes in the sun. Last year was better but after working the CT and eating cases of oysters all winter, I had no problem in the sun. I just tanned. I drank a lot of water and had absolutely no hunger (neither did DH)..no cravings (I brought back my chocolate unopened). Skipped lunch eventhough we packed food. Living on solar power! Will be working the am sun more diligently. Thank you Dr Kruse!

Sleep wasn't as good as being home on the Magnetico and our condo was not in a good location. One thing that has me wondering ......is why I sleep on my back while on vac but I seem to end up always on my stomach on the magnetico? We were in a condo and a hotel and I slept on my back. As soon as we returned home..sleeping on stomach again.

# Thread: Data Breach BC/BS Anthem
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/data-breach-bc-bs-anthem.13350/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fitness@home — 2015-03-17T14:56:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/data-breach-bc-bs-anthem.13350/post-158772>

I didn't really know where to put this, but wanted to make others aware of the data breach if you hadn't already heard. My daughter and stepson were on our insurance at one time and both received letters yesterday. Just scanned the letters so I can email to them.

Our particular policy isn't managed by Anthem, but both of them used the insurance in Ohio which is an Anthem state. Other Anthem states are: California, Colorado, Conneticut, Georgia, Kentucky, Maine, Missouri, Nevada, New Hampshire, New York, Ohio, Virginia, or Wisconsin.

My husband and I had already signed up for the 24 month identity protection that is provided for free. Chelsea and Charlie need to sign up too. Better safe than sorry...

[https://www.anthemfacts.com/](https://www.anthemfacts.com/)
For additional protection, and at no cost, you may also enroll in the AllClear PRO service at any time during the 24 month coverage period. This service includes credit monitoring and an identity theft insurance policy. Pleas3 enroll at:
[https://anthem.allclearid.com/](https://anthem.allclearid.com/). Those without Internet access can call 877-263-7995.
To access identity repair services, please call 877-263-7995
For additional information regarding your protections, please visit:
[https://anthem.allclearid.com/faqs](https://anthem.allclearid.com/faqs).

_My public service announcement for today_

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-17T15:06:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/data-breach-bc-bs-anthem.13350/post-158776>

For those who don't know.......medical data breeches are worse for identity protection than anything else. Maybe people will see why going off the grid makes sense.

### nicld — 2015-03-17T15:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/data-breach-bc-bs-anthem.13350/post-158777>

We got the letters too. First it was a Target breach and now this!! And still they are trying to get people to put EVERYTHING on their cell phone like banking and debit cards. No thank you!!!

### Shijin13 — 2015-03-17T15:42:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/data-breach-bc-bs-anthem.13350/post-158781>

This is the largest risk area for Personal Identification. Such that I'm in the process of identifying this as a large weakness for DOD personal especially those who receive treatment outside the Military/VA medical systems (which in and of themselves aren't as secure as they should be).

What most don't understand is the new technology such as the tools Physcians use (such as new MRI machines and such that can transmit patient scans automatically) have no IT Security built in at all. Its huge!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-17T16:32:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-life-gets-order-from-chaos.13351/post-158783>

Most of you don't understand quantum physics well, but here is some new data showing you how biology uses electrons and protons from your mitochondria to build your body to work in optimal fashion. Biology can only explain order from order, but it is clear that life requires an explanation that can explain how something made up of atoms becomes living. We call this chaos to order. This is how it is done. By increasing the tunneling rate of particles (electrons and protons), a phase transition is induced. In the new state, particles are free to move in a coherent fashion, producing a single wave function, a Bose-Einstein condensate. This allows long-range, system-wide communication. When you biohack yourself and your environment, you're literally changing the structure of your matter to a more optimized state. This is info you wont get in the doctors office or from any paleo/alternative practitioner. I also have a blog on its implications that I will link below in comments. [http://phys.org/news/2015-03-emerge.html](http://phys.org/news/2015-03-emerge.html)

### PaulG — 2015-03-17T18:32:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-life-gets-order-from-chaos.13351/post-158789>

We forget more that we remember...the tragedy that is human existence

"This will probably be the most important blog in the [entire Quilt.](http://jackkruse.com/the-quilthow-to-beat-agin/)"

I personally apologies for not giving your work due diligence....Deep connective knowledge is hard... you deserve better....

I really wonder how far ahead you are? I personally would rate you as a modern day [Bodhisattvay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bodhisattva) though I could be wrong

> 1.  **Great Joy:** It is said that being close to enlightenment and seeing the benefit for all [sentient beings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sentient_beings_\(Buddhism\)), one achieves great joy, hence the name. In this _bhūmi_ the bodhisattvas practice all [perfections](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paramita) (_pāramitās_), but especially emphasizing [generosity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dana_\(Buddhism\)) (_dāna_).
> 2.  **Stainless:** In accomplishing the second _bhūmi_, the bodhisattva is free from the stains of immorality, therefore, this _bhūmi_ is named "stainless". The emphasized perfection is[moral discipline](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C5%9Aila) (_śīla_).
> 3.  **Luminous:** The third _bhūmi_ is named "luminous", because, for a bodhisattva who accomplishes this _bhūmi_, the light of Dharma is said to radiate for others from the bodhisattva. The emphasized perfection is [patience](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/K%E1%B9%A3anti) (_kṣānti_).
> 4.  **Radiant:** This _bhūmi_ is called "radiant", because it is said to be like a radiating light that fully burns that which opposes enlightenment. The emphasized perfection is [vigor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/V%C4%ABrya)(_vīrya_).
> 5.  **Very difficult to train:** Bodhisattvas who attain this _bhūmi_ strive to help sentient beings attain maturity, and do not become emotionally involved when such beings respond negatively, both of which are difficult to do. The emphasized perfection is [meditative concentration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dhy%C4%81na_in_Buddhism) (_dhyāna_).
> 6.  **Obviously Transcendent:** By depending on the perfection of wisdom, \[the bodhisattva\] does not abide in either _[saṃsāra](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sa%E1%B9%83s%C4%81ra)_ or _[nirvāṇa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nirv%C4%81%E1%B9%87a)_, so this state is "obviously transcendent". The emphasized perfection is [wisdom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wisdom_in_Buddhism) (_prajñā_).
> 7.  **Gone afar:** Particular emphasis is on the perfection of [skillful means](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Upaya) (_upāya_), to help others.
> 8.  **Immovable:** The emphasized virtue is aspiration. This, the "immovable" _bhūmi_, is the _bhūmi_ at which one becomes able to choose his place of rebirth.
> 9.  **Good Discriminating Wisdom:** The emphasized virtue is power.
> 10.  **Cloud of Dharma:** The emphasized virtue is the practice of primordial wisdom

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2015-03-18T13:26:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-life-gets-order-from-chaos.13351/post-158861>

"Stop allowing your beliefs block you from reality" Dr K. applying a little 'philosophy' or as Neil would say 'epistemology' to this that implies 'reality' can ever be perceived without some 'beliefs.............. to me that does not seem correct. "Reality" is always made up of a 'composite' of 'external reality' and some subjective component 'beliefs' if you like. You said yourself in a recent blog one of the UB ones 3 I think that water was a 'de-coherent lens' which sort of blocked us from seeing the quantum reality but at the benefit of there BEING a reality.

This I am sure sounds like quibbling but some of these kind of 'philosophical/epistemological' beginnings can have un-intended consequences later on. If something in it's very beginnings is even slightly 'off' it can and usually does become 'way off' later. I see a consistency in the whole statement above and if I can summarize it in the way me and really only me sees it.....................it's like you RULE OUT the 'mind' or 'beliefs' as in 'reality is that which exists even when you no longer believe in it' but the problem then is once 'ruled out' is has to be RE-INTRODUCED in some fashion later. As in things like 'one thought can change your DNA'. I see that a lot in the kind of 'hard sciences' they are hard core about it being 'all physics' to begin with but since that does not satisfy in the long run then the 'mind' is introduced later in almost too 'powerful' a role. Then it becomes a lot of talk about 'belief systems' etc. With a better 'philosophical/epistemological' beginning or foundation most of this would not be necessary later on.

It reminds me of R D Laing's book "The Divided Self" it's like first the self or reality is 'split' and then later it has to be stitched back together again. In a more real and less messed up world this 'split' would never have been done in the first place so no stitching together needed later on.......................but it's true we do not live in an non messed up world so I suppose people are just doing the best they can. But I think these things are tricky and a bit more complicated than at first glance.

# Thread: What is Life?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-life.13352/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 35
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2015-03-17T17:54:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-life.13352/post-158787>

In yet another attempt to make me seem smarter than I actually am I have invented this game which has six rules:

1) Buy two bottles of wine (preferably Malbec) but drink one before beginning
2) Think of all the words that come to your mind when you think of the question **WHAT IS LIFE?**
3) No googling "What is Life" until you really run out of your own thinking, for me that was about 20 mins but I am a particular sad case of the modern era.
4) Drink more wine if you feel the need to Google
4) Use positive terminology where possible
5) Repetition is ok, there is [Joy in repetition](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=K_DH-3K09hc)
6) Jack Kruse must join in and sticky this post....

**So, what is Life?**
If there were a dictionary with only words or phrases that captured the essence of life as we know it from the sciences, what words would be in that dictionary??

or alternatively if you were God what principles or mechanisms would you put into play at the beginning of the universe?

Evolution, Increasing complexity, interdependence, efficiency, opportunistic, redundancy, emergent design, structure, symmetry, fractal geometry, laws of nature, light, water, magnetism, tensegrity. quantum, as above- so below, scale, Incredibly large, incredibly small, cycles, feedback mechanisms, coherence, entropy, proteins, enzymes, photosynthesis, chemical reactions, elements, molecules, atoms, variation, genes, epigenetics, environment sensing, circadian rhythms, failure, disease, decay, destruction, renewal, rebirth, regeneration, balance, equilibrium, thermodynamics, mimicry, replication, union, chaos, uncertainty, mystery, darkness, antogonism, opposition, attraction, absorption, induction, transcendence, transformation, transduction, communication, signaling, memory, intelligence, metamorphosis, cooperative, exploitation, survival, instincts, chance, intelligent design, irreducebility, uniformity, consistency, discovery, invention, engineering, puzzles, death, conservation, adaptation, variety, reproduction, metabolism, physiology. protophysiology, sacred, energy gradient, abiogenesis, microscopic, diversification, bacteria, microbes, pathogenesis, Two red circles and a thick black spine , convergence, parthenogenesis, specialization, charge, frequency, modulation, vibration, space, time, DNA, RNA, order, disorder, regulation, replication, cellular, membrane, amino acids, language, _panspermia, electrons, Life is nothing but an electron looking for a place to rest, clocks, parasitic,_ hereditary, meaning, discovery, brinkmanship, **teleology, mind, matter, relationships, conservation, expression, divinity, ecstasy, senses, pleasure, pain, indulgence, comformity, consciousness, [SQUID](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SQUID)**

**It is very rarely I have original thoughts but you can bank this one...**
"Life on the time scale can be characterized fundamentally in its basic reducible form as being the movement from simplicity to complexity. I think of it as Russian Doll where layers of complexity are put on top of each other
Human discovery/history works inversely to this, that is peeling of the outer layers to the inner layers to get to the truth.
A smart protophysiologist/protobiologist would look to see what quantum phenomena manifest in proto cells and primordial conditions as this does not have the obfuscation of the layers of evolution and history"

****[http://www.lyricsmania.com/gunning\_for\_the\_buddha\_lyrics\_shriekback.html](http://www.lyricsmania.com/gunning_for_the_buddha_lyrics_shriekback.html)**

**

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-17T19:32:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-life.13352/post-158793>

Life is made up of the following:

gravity = light
the electromagnetic force = magnetism
archimedes principle = water chemistry.

Hence the 3 legged stool of life manifests from the fundamental physical forces.

That is EE 4 in a nut shell..........this is how the matter on Earth were organized. They were the thermodynamic givens in the equation of life.

What gave life its spark?

The sun.

How?

It created the first battery using sun in sea water to make viri in bacteria, archea, and plants who remain fixed to the magnetic field and sun. Those things floated in the water until land mammals could harvest this prescription of life from the sea at some point in the water and it to cause a denovo gene change to change the trajectory of life.

It created a battery using water (EZ) and DHA in eukaryotes so they could move across the tectonic plates while being disconnected at times. The more disconnection the animal would be the more problems would manifest.

Disconnection in nature is allowed by things that can break natural symmetry of nature's laws. What is the best example of this? Water.......it can be tap water, a drink, a shower, a coolant, and solvent, or a battery for life. It is the "jack" of all trades and master of none.

Sorry, no Malbec for me... Grange is my weapon of joy tonight.

Thinking and crunching is not my thing, i do it simple.

Waves, the word i can think of is waves.

I soak in them, in good and in bad, favor those who are natural. I sleep on naked ground and let the flavors of spring wake me up, i sit by the sea and see the ice crack up. At night i listen to music, the old fashioned way, vinyls. Let the needle get into the grooves, sending waves into my body, waking my dreams.

When a dancer dance on stage i feel her inside of me, i team up with her even if i am at safe distance, we ride the same wave. I look into that special girl and sense a wave of feeling, my heart skip a beat, can hardly breath... Drawn away on waves into another realm, i resonate like time stood still, think it does.

I lower my body into the sea, take that last breath for a couple of minutes, priming myself to be of another kind. Down here it is safe, nothing else matters, i enter the world of origin and almost feel sad knowing i can not stay forever.

Life for me is waves, always has and always will.

Photo: Annelie Pompe

Ishihara Yumi, PhD (Japan), established that our immune system much stronger when the body temperature is increased by 1.8°; inversely, the immune system is weakened by 30% if the body temperature is lowered by 1.8°.

Yeah, Broda Barnes, MD, PhD, has a bunch of books and papers on treating patients (for decades, I might add, with lots of success) using saturated fat based diets and thyroid replacement therapy, attempting to get their under the arm temperature upon waking in the morning between 97.8 an 98.2 degrees. He was one of the first people to experiment with thyroid blood tests, and found that he could test the same patient three times and get three different results; he has a large set of papers titled "Heart Attack Rareness in Thyroid Treated Patients" or something along those lines that are definitely worth checking out. His books, and that of McGavack and Crille are definitely must reads on the thyroid; after reading them, most arguments against high levels of T3 are laughable to say the least.

In terms of body temp, I do in fact believe 97.8-98.2 is optimal (or even higher), but the timing of this temp I believe may be key. For instance, Dr. Barnes wanted this temp to occur upon waking. Generally speaking, we're in a more oxidized state during wakefulness. So I think we can become cold adapted and lower our temp, as Dr. Kruse has alluded to, but have our body temp and circulating T3 change in an ultradian fashion - high body temp-metabolism-thyroid hormones upon waking, and at night, a surge of melatonin and darkness, reduced body temp, etc. to become chemically reduced at night to prepare us for the next mornings adventures.

On a side note, I also don't think CT necessarily chronically lowers body temp. Acutely, sure. You're plunging in cold water. But I've noticed that after some time, my body temp actually increases after CT. During and shortly thereafter, it is lower, but after 15-60 minutes (depending upon how cold I go) I become warmer than before I did CT. I also appear more vascular and my muscles are tighter. There are some times I have found myself becoming hot, too, when normally I'd feel "thermo-neutral". I know Dr. Kruse and Dan Ordoins have indicated that they essentially radiate heat, yet they both do a lot of CT and frequent ketosis.

Life is a dream I believe the supreme consciousness made up to see what it might be like to feel separation from itself. We are fragments of the whole containing the whole in every spec of our physical and non physical existence. This dream of separation feels so real that we truly have forgotten who we really are collectively and believe ourselves to be solely our life story created by a physical experience in a body. The complexity is overwhelming and we get mesmerized by the fine details in this experience. As we slowly begin to wake up to the truth of who we are supreme consciousness begins to know itself better, I believe. Much like when we loose something valuable and then we find it again. In its abscence we remember many details we forgot to value when we had it. Then we find it and know and value it better. That is life, I believe.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-09-28T19:41:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-life.13352/post-251896>

What is Life - I fully laid it out in April 2016 webinar for the interested.........

That was the hardcore physics behind life as I see it.

**_What is the hardcore biology behind it?_**

Once you have added the Leptin Rx to your new template of living and you have successfully experienced all the “small wins” that I mentioned in the Leptin FAQ’s blog, what should you do next? If you recall reading the blog on how the leptin Rx works, it basically is a plan to use circadian cycles to make your eye, skin, subcutaneous fat, and gastrointestinal tract perform with an optimized physiology by supporting ECT and the ATPase spin rate by improving mitochondrial functioning. People who have defected leptin have a process where this molecule is somehow damaged by light where retinol structure is changed after it is liberated from melanopsin. This free retinol atomic structure is changed and that change is now well known to destroy all photoreceptors. Since leptin atomic structure is loaded with aromatic rings that collect photons these small atomic changes affect leptins' ability to send proper messages during the night to the hypothalamus to affect the metabolic rate in the animal. When someone suffers from a circadian arrhythmia, this mechanism is usurped and broken, and mitochondria cannot function as they are accustomed to performing. Mitochondrial functioning in the hypothalamus must be circadian certified in order to cause the PROPER neuroplastic changes in your hypothalamus’ arcuate nucleus for sensitivity. This process uses excited electrons falling back to the ground state to be freed to proteins and the spin of protons to control the spin rate of the ATPase in the neurons of the hypothalamus and brainstem. These two regions of the brain share information in the arterioles of the vascular watershed region called the circle of Willis. This area contains melanopsin/retinol in its vessels creating a dynamic fidelity of the system. The PVN and the vagus nerve acts as the dials in the autonomic nervous system to control its function. The vagus nerve acts as the “stimulator” to send these new messages to the brain via blood vessels and CSF water networks. After a period of time, when deuterium is cleared from Kreb's bicycle region of the matrix in mitochondria, the inflammatory effect of a slowed ATPase will slowly dissipate at the median eminence in the hypothalamus, and these afferent signals will force expression of certain genes that have been repressed since we were in utero. This changes our metabolic rate and programming in the PVN. This alters the stress response the animal experiences through its exposome.

These genes and pathways are hardwired into our mtDNA at conception epigenetically and used until the child is 12-24 months old. After this time, they are not expressed any longer, because transgenerational epigenetics of the exposome favors instead the use of the leptin receptor from an evolutionary perspective. This occurs because the leptin receptor in the arcuate nucleus is far more sensitive and accurate in accounting for electrons and proton spin from food than was using older circadian and ultradian cycles that we used in uteri during morphogenesis. The human brain learns “what neural circuits” to use by repetitive firing. We have a saying in brain surgery, nerves that fire together wire together. This is the basis of the theory of Hebbian learning and this is the wisdom of nature built into your brain to control metabolism using LIGHT via melanopsin/leptin signaling in your skin, arteries, and subcutaneous fat.

**Now add the physics and biology together and what do you get?**

How can life do what it does? Why is life so counterintuitive to YOUR common sense? Nature takes full advantage of quantum mechanics of electrons and protons. This is why evolution selected mitochondria to sort electrons and protons in ways humans still do not understand well. This is why I train Black Swan mitochondriacs on this site.

Did you know that subatomic particles can rearrange themselves within an atomic nucleus in a zeptosecond (a billionth of a trillionth of a second) and you can fit more zeptoseconds into the blink of an eye than you can blinks of an eye within the age of the universe?

That is what nature allows for and life has been taking full advantage of this for 3.8 billion years and in that time she has optimized the process to build reality as we experience it. Get up to speed folks..........cause I am not slowing down for anyone.

Every day before I die I want to do 5 key things each day to improve. Lead, grow, create, excel, and serve. I lead myself and my business to fulfill my purpose of leading to changes I want in medicine, so I begin with me. I find a theme of the day and I live it that day. I try to become the change I want to see in my world. I become radioactive to the old paradigms. I try to grow myself and my business in some small way to achieve my potential. Potential is worthless without the action of kinetic energy. Daily creation of world-class leadership ideas in some way: resources, experiences, ideas. Chase excellence in all I do. I may not always be successful but I won't allow stasis to hold my mission back. I want to serve my members, patients, clients, colleagues, and community. For too long I used to serve only myself. The obstacle to daily accomplishment is implementation. What every starts well ends well. Sharing knowledge is easy. Implementing said knowledge is power. Behavior precedes belief. Action precedes buy-in; it does not follow it. Are you ready?

Check out my latest article: CELLS ARE AN AMO PHYSICS EXPERIMENT OF NATURE [https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/cells-amo-physics-experiment-nature-jack-kruse](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/cells-amo-physics-experiment-nature-jack-kruse)

Time is a critical issue for dissipative systems. THIS DEFINES WHAT A MITOCHONDRIA WAS DESIGNED TO DO AND TO BE IN A VARYING EMF FIELD CREATED ON THIS PLANET by the sun.

I have loved Dr. Gilbert Ling’s work. I am very glad that even though QFT & QM were in their hay-day during many of his years as a true scientist. Dr. Ling did not go down that rabbit-hole, which was based on failed photon-splitter experiences and subsequent postulations, mathematical theorems to support fantastical probabilities of photon-electron-particle believes. Dr. Ling lived a long life – 100 years from 1919 to 2019. He could have learned that the "_Quantum Mechanics Measurement Problem_" had been resolved in 2003 by an experiential physicist, Eric Reiter. Please see my write-up on the subject:

starting at [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/318875](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/318875)​

Dr. Ling’s work was more concerned with _hydrated_ _electron donating strength_ of the amino-acid through the protoplasm (endoplasm) rather than trying to “split-hairs over light.”

He debunked the sodium pump hypothesis:

Debunking the Alleged Resurrection of the Sodium Pump Hypothesis
[http://www.physiologicalchemistryandphysics.com/pdf/PCP29-123\_ling.pdf](http://www.physiologicalchemistryandphysics.com/pdf/PCP29-123_ling.pdf)​

He developed the Induction Hypothesis

[http://gilbertling.org/pdf/The-Association-Induction-Hypothesis-42-page-summary.pdf](http://gilbertling.org/pdf/The-Association-Induction-Hypothesis-42-page-summary.pdf)​

One of his many books:

What is Life Answered by Gilbert Ling
[http://gilbertling.org/pdf/What-is-Life-Answered.pdf](http://gilbertling.org/pdf/What-is-Life-Answered.pdf)

He developed:

-   The _Polarized Multilayer_ (PM) theory of cell water and model systems (Ling 1965), only to be replaced later by the name, the _Polarized-Oriented Multilayer_ (POM) theory of cell water and model systems (Ling 2003.)
-   The _association-induction_ hypothesis is built are (1) the science (physics) of Statistical Mechanics and (2) the (correct part of) protein and polypeptide chemistry and physics.

Ling pastoralized: First, _properties of matter_ can be roughly sorted into two categories.

-   Long-range attributes include sight and sound that can be perceived at different distances away from their origin.
-   Short-range attributes include textures and taste of an object, which can be perceived only by direct contact.

An important point made in his 1965 presentation was that all or virtually all the water in the living cell assumes the dynamic structure of polarized-oriented multilayers.

Figure above. The original illustration of the _polarized (oriented) multilayer theory of cell water_, in which each water molecule is represented as a circle containing a curved arrow. The length of the arrow indicates the assumed degree of motional freedom. Later work shows that the degree of motional restriction is much more uniform rather than showing a steep gradient as suggested in this original illustration. (From Ling 1965).

Ling introduced a quantitative parameter called the (true) _equilibrium distribution coefficient_ or q-value to represent the equilibrium distribution ratio of a solute _between two phases_ like the total cell water and the external bathing solution.

Discovered by K. Lohmann in 1929, **_ATP_** was for about 15 years widely believed to carry two high-energy phosphate bonds, each represented by the symbol-Pin a theory proposed by Lipmann (Lipmann 1941.) This concept turned out to be mistaken as shown by Podolsky and Morales (1956.) They found _no usable energy to do work in any one of the three phosphate bonds_ (Ling 1992 p. 179; Ling 2001 p. 234, 306.) This iconoclastic development left ATP without a function-for six years only or altogether, depending on one's familiarity with or lack of it with the association-induction hypothesis.

The mistaken notion was created in part by the failure to recognize the association-induction hypothesis, which gives _ATP_ a new function as an important cardinal adsorbent. As such, it is distinguished by its strong binding energy with a standard free energy of binding, .l'.W0 equal to-14.3 Kcal/mole (Ling 1992 p. 180.) Thus the binding energy on myosin of ATP is ten times higher than the binding energy on myosin of its hydrolytic product ADP, (Ling 1992 p. 187.)

Dr. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) wrote of “ATP is designed to unfold proteins fully to open their carbonyl and imino side chain groups on all amino acids to intracellular water. This action allows binding and polarization to separate water into subatomic particles that are positively and negatively charged. This action is called building or expanding the exclusion zone (EZ) of water. This is the core work of Dr. Gerald Pollack.” [https://jackkruse.com/ee-6-quantum-cell-theory-life-collective-phenomena/](https://jackkruse.com/ee-6-quantum-cell-theory-life-collective-phenomena/)

The physiological role of ATP as an _electron-withdrawing cardinal adsorbent_ (EWC) has received further confirmation because H+, its substitute in the present study, is nothing more than a positive charge and hence by definition an EWC.

As a whole, cardinal adsorbents can be divided into three categories:

-   electron-withdrawing cardinal adsorbents (EWC),
-   electron-donating cardinal adsorbents (EDC) and
-   electron-indifferent cardinal adsorbents (EiC).

Ling introduced us to the following, as he states:

1\. In 1981, I introduced the theory of associative-inductive coupling mechanism for electron transport and oxidative phosphorylation. Through this suggested mechanism, "each cycle of oxidation and reduction of the respiratory chain center leads to a cyclic change in the c-value of appropriate sites on the ATPase, triggering the generation of one ATP molecule" (Ling 1981; Ling 1984 p.517.)

2\. In 1986, I demonstrated that the a-helical potential of each of the 19 amino acids is strongly correlated to the electron-donating strength of the 19 amino acids's respective side chains. This then led to the conclusion that high c-value analogue of the backbone carbonyl groups of a protein favors the assumption of the folded a-helical structure and that low c-value analogue favors its assumption of fullyextended conformation (with the consequent adsorption of deep layers of water molecules.) (Ling 1986, also Sect. 4.4.5.2 below in this volume.)

3\. In 1993, using statistical mechanical methods, I derived an equation for the q-value of solutes dissolved in multilayers of polarized-oriented water, implicating the "size rule." (Ling 1993; also Sect.4.3.1 below in this volume.)

4\. In 2003, I discovered a short cut that allowed me to produce a new theoretical foundation for the theory of polarized-oriented multilayer theory of cell (and model) water. I did this by proving what is called an Idealized NP Surface-in which each N or P site is separated from its nearest neighbor by a distance of 3.lA-can polarize and orient multilayers of water ad infinitum. Moreover, water so polarized-oriented cannot be frozen at any attainable low temperature. (Ling 2003; also Sect.4.3.2 below in this volume.)

5\. In 2007, I discovered the possible reason why Troshin's simple two term equation can explain almost all the solute distribution data in systems as complex as a collection of living cells. The reason could be what the AI Hypothesis suggests: qualitatively and quantitatively similar nano-protoplasm maintained in the resting living state makes up the bulk of most living cells (Ling 2007 a.)

6\. In 2008, Ling and Ochsenfeld published an article, bearing the title "A historically significant study that at once disproves the membrane pump theory and confirms that nano-protoplasm is the ultimate physical basis of life-and yet so simple ... " To understand why I think that this is the last of the string of clinching events for arriving at the essentially definitive answer of what is life, I have reproduced the Abstract of this article as well as a list of what the article has brought to light in the last Section 5.2. of this volume (Ling and Ochsenfeld 2008a.)​

_Don't you just wish he was still with us?_

We need to be constantly reminded of the basics of life so we remain directed at our life's purpose. When we live by assumptions, too often and we forget its fundamental concepts. Stop living your life by assumptions. When you assume things about the basics of life and forget what they are, errors in judgment follow. Become mindful to find time every day to reconnect with Nature, and you will realize what the fundamentals in your life really are.

Most of the scientists of the 19-21st century had a myopic take on a facet of "What is Life". It turns out many of them saw a facet of it and expanded that facet. I believe they made a myopia mistake by focusing on humans and not looking at the group in total.

Life is understanding where your family fits into the history of Earth and what created their deterministic unpredictability in the unfolding story of evolution.
Of all classes of animals, mitochondriacs should be most concerned with and fascinated by class Mammalia, of which we are members. Class Mammalia contains warm-blooded animals that have hair or fur, produce milk, and typically give birth to live young. As of 2023, there are approximately 5450 living mammalian species that inhabit every biome on earth, ranging in size from the 2 g bumblebee bat to the 170-ton blue whale, and exhibit unprecedented phenomic diversity and ecological adaptations.

Class Mammalia is divided into two subclasses: Prototheria, which contains the egg-laying monotremes (platypus and echidna), and Theria, which contains the placental and marsupial clades. The mammalian fossil record extends deep into the Triassic (approx. 220 Ma) and records the evolution of mammalian lineages through extreme changes in flora, light environments, and landmass movements made during the Cretaceous Terrestrial Revolution (KTR) and the Cretaceous–Palaeogene (KPg) mass extinction events

The human clade that matters most in understanding life is the class mammal. The Theria subclass is special because they seem to have used a particular strategy to break time symmetry in the mammalian class by adopting Noether's theorem to break time symmetry by conserving one issue. These hidden “forces” in the game of life in mammals are the key to understanding human life in the 21st century. To understand fully the human condition you must fully understand how Noether's theorem interacts with the conservation c=hoices of Theria to act as the key “hidden variable” that is deterministic for that subgroup.

Theria breaks time symmetry by conserving UV light to build a non-linear optical framework. The QE series is about understanding how and why that hidden variable was used and how they did it. It was the innovation of POMc to facilitate an underground existence to survive. That unique ability was sculpted by the environment and then expanded once the subclass could go from subterranean to diurnal creatures. Without a full understanding of the nuance of
non-linear determination of the mitochondriac will remain subject to half-truths of the myopia of the paradigm of science.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-08-01T16:28:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-life.13352/post-337922>

LESSONS FROM A DECENTRALIZED DONKEY: Embrace the suck in life. It is not about survival of fittest, it is about survival of the wisest.

One day, a farmer’s donkey fell into a well. The poor animal cried loudly for hours while the farmer tried to figure out a way to get him out. Finally, the farmer decided that the donkey was old and the well was dry and needed to be covered anyway; it wasn’t worth pulling the donkey out of the well.

He invited all his neighbors to help him. They each grabbed a shovel and began to throw dirt into the well. The donkey realized what was happening and cried out loudly. Then, to everyone’s surprise, he quieted down after a few shovelfuls of dirt.

The farmer finally looked down into the well and was amazed at what he saw. With each shovelful of dirt, the donkey was doing something incredible: he was shaking off the dirt and stepping on top of it. Very soon, everyone was astonished as the donkey reached the mouth of the well, climbed over the edge, and trotted out.

Life is going to throw dirt at you, all kinds of dirt. The trick to getting out of the hole is to shake it off and use it to step up. Each of our problems is a step up. We can get out of the deepest holes if we don’t give up.

Use the dirt thrown at you to get ahead!

Remember the 5 rules to be happy:

1\. Free your heart from hate.

2\. Free your mind from distractions.

3\. Simplify your life.

4\. Give more and expect less.

5\. Love more and shake off the dirt, because in this life, you have to be a decentralized solution, not the problem!

I have had insomnia for over 10 years started with crohn's bleeding and inflammation, never ever naturally sleepy (tired but wired all the time), became dependent on prescription sleeping sedatives.
For the past 2 years I have been withdrawing from the pills, and had to suffer the most extreme sleep deprivation and withdrawal symptoms from these drugs, rebound insomnia.
I did not know this at the time, withdrawing from all these pills effects the HPA axis, and my already weak adrenals cannot cope, I had Adrenal Crash about 1 1/2 ago, about 7pm at night, and my Endo took my 9am cortisol test of 350 (not fasted test- no range given), and said I was fine.

My DHEA level 1.7 (1-7) - so barely in range - I have been taking 50mg DHEA pill version, and this has hardly done anything.

I am not fine - I am struggling badly, also going through menopause also. All my hormones are low, along with thyroid. plus, they are often local to you.

\----------------------
Growth Hormone

When testing do the tests using RIA methodology (hand made by technician, not by robot)

You want:
IGF-1=(220-300)ug/L
IGFBP3=>4000ug/L

The system may throw you thru hoops and make testing other things.
Oblige, but practically only these two counts.

Preferably use Norditropin as your GH but that is less important. Chinese from gym trainer also works.
\------------------------
Steroid Hormones
almost menopausal, aim for about constant progesterone & estradiol levels
look for charts and table here [http://tinyurl.com/nxqrxhd](http://tinyurl.com/nxqrxhd)
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

check in blood (no blood drops) and use enough supplements to get to this goals:

pregnenolone= as close to the top lab range as possible but not over
progesterone= 10-20 ng/mL
cortisol= 8AM top range but not over
DHEAs=275-400 μg/dL
FreeTestosterone=mid to top range, if need to raise do it after DHEAs is at good level

Estrone(E1)
Estradiol(E2)=20-40 pg/mL
Estriol(E3)

If need to lower estrogens eat cruciferous vegetables, supplement with calcium-D-glutarate
use AI, Aromatase Inhibitors, Arimidex

Often need to raise progesterone, then
line up test results of
pregnenolone, progesterone, cortisol --->they metabolize in that order
at first loop try to get the job done using:
pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology, doses up to 1200mg/day my have to be considered
increase dose until top of one of the three is reached

Until stabilized at good dose monthly blood test are suggested.
Do not exceed pregnenolone or cortisol desired levels.
It is ok to raise progesterone over level recommended above.

\------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Thyroid Hormone

Until stabilized, test monthly for

TSH
T4
FT4
T3
FT3
RT3
Thyroid Peroxidase (TPO) Ab
Antithyroglobulin Ab

If you need thyroid hormone supplementation make sure that you have decent test results for cortisol,serum-AM.

Generally you want to have FreeT3 within (3.5-4.2) pg/mL (correlated to life expectancy, important)

[https://www.worldhealth.net/pdf/Rothenberg\_Thera11.pdf](https://www.worldhealth.net/pdf/Rothenberg_Thera11.pdf)
Rothenberg optimal FreeT3= (3.5-4.2) pg/mL)

There is number of other concerns about thyroid but within this Cliff Notes make sure that you had
Spectracell Micronutrient test done and adjusted your supplement list so the next test report looks better.(This is more than important, more like do or ....)

Add to that plenty of Lugol's solution (after you are sure that there is plenty of selenium).

1/eod-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)

1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day
Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
[http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW](http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW)

\================================================

'''''''''''''''

### JanSz — 2015-03-19T18:16:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-advice-please.13360/post-158962>

> [Debbs said:](/goto/post?id=158857)
>
> Low DHEA also, barely in range. Would my Endo not know the importance of Adrenals for inflammation??!
>
> \-----------
>
> I have had insomnia for over 10 years started with crohn's bleeding and inflammation, never ever naturally sleepy (tired but wired all the time), became dependent on prescription sleeping sedatives.
> For the past 2 years I have been withdrawing from the pills, and had to suffer the most extreme sleep deprivation and withdrawal symptoms from these drugs, rebound insomnia.

When reading DHEAs,serum levels disregard lab ranges as written on test report.
Use this to evaluate:
Women 275-400 μg/dL

[http://jackkruse.com/cellular-depletions-why-should-you-care/](http://jackkruse.com/cellular-depletions-why-should-you-care/)
Read the whole blog, but this stands out:
\-------------------------
For example, in ICU patients who are sick and cant sleep well they suffer from very low levels of DHEA. Low DHEA levels correlate with very high IL-6 levels and poor sleep. If this is allowed to go on long enough patients may get an acute ICU psychosis called delirium. In brain trauma patients we see very low levels of progesterone and this correlates with poor neurologic outcome because progesterone is a co factor in brain derived growth factor to make new neurons.

### Linz — 2015-03-20T11:07:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-advice-please.13360/post-159021>

And have a look at The Taymount Clinic for faecal transplant - but not a cheap option.

I think you should start to reset hormones by taking care with all the timing issues - meal times, light exposure etc. It is easy to do even when you don't have much energy. Eat a really big breakfast with lots of protein and building up the fats when you can (fish and bacon is best) outside in the morning sunshine whenever possible. Keeping a big pot of broth on hand (bones/seafood/onions/seaweed) will help with cravings and the seaweed is the safest way to supplement iodine (and other minerals too). If you are going to do it with supplements read the whole of the iodine thread carefully first - and it is long! Perhaps read from the end backwards. I have ibd and ate a lot of 24 hour fermented cream yoghurt when I needed to gain weight but dairy is not good with autoimmunity and lots of fish and seaweed when I started to read here was a big turning point.

Have you read the CT blogs yet? Cold stops inflammation, dumps excess oestrogen (some unpleasant side effects!), speeds up replacement of fats in cell membranes, uncouples metabolism from light and the thyroid is hardly needed in the cold - CT 6 I think - but start the right way with face dunks. Take very great care with the hormone supplements if you don't have the right lab tests and interpretation. I think it is easier and not too expensive (and safer when good medical back up is not available) to try mitochondrial supplementation to reinforce the energy regulating system. D-ribose, ubiquinol, magnesium, acetyl- l- carnitine. Curcumin and resveratrol are good too and lots of information here.

And CT will help restart your ability to regulate temperature properly.

Just keep reading. Beating your head against the NHS doesn't help cortisol, melatonin or sleep and there is so much you can do here at little cost or even savings when the electricity and technology bill falls! I would just use the NHS to stabilise thyroid/crohns crises and get working on the long term improvement here. Don't predict overnight changes where you expect them. The first improvements you see will probably be things you had no idea were wrong. When you start to see the changes you can make for yourself you won't look back. Warmest wishes

### JanSz — 2015-04-01T02:03:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-advice-please.13360/post-160002>

> [Debbs said:](/goto/post?id=159654)
>
> **I was taking 100mcg T4, and 20mcg T3 at the time**
> FT4 18.2 (normal 11-22) - before taking NDT, I always had high T4 but didn't feel like it was going anywhere - cannot do Reverse T4 test.
> **FT3 3.99 (3.1 -6.8)** <<--missing units
> TSH - suppressed
> the **TSH remains < 0.01 mU/l** so the pituitary is obviously not sensing a lack of thyroid
> hormone. (I AM TAKING T3 SO NOT SURE WHY ENDO PUT THAT?! - altho Endo has said my thyroid levels are fine I don't need thyroid hormones at the moment - he has said this so they do not have to prescribe expensive liquid T4..mmm.

FT3 3.99 (3.1 -6.8) <<--missing units

One of the bottom lines on thyroid hormones is FT3 in good range.
You want to have FT3 in upper half of the range.
Mid range =(3.1+(6.8-3.1)/2)=4.95
so **you want to be somewhere within (4.95-6.8)**, better somewhere in the middle
that would be =(3.1+(6.8-3.1)\*(3/4)=5.875

Numbers vary depending on which units are used. I worked from what you posted, (3.1 -6.8)
\----------------------------------------
Copy this document, print it out, give it to your doctor.

[https://www.worldhealth.net/pdf/Rothenberg\_Thera11.pdf](https://www.worldhealth.net/pdf/Rothenberg_Thera11.pdf)
Rothenberg optimal FreeT3= (3.5-4.2) pg/mL)

\---------------------------------------
Why are you low on FreeT3 when you have overall too much thyroid hormones, indicated by TSH,
just
not the right kind.

To convert T4 into T3 you need
good cortisol level (that optimal level changes throughout the day, Circadian Rhythm)
good enzymes that do the conversion
the enzymes need selenium
the process need iodine/iodide
\---------------------------------
You are missing:
good Circadian Rhythm
cortisol
selenium
iodine/iodide
you are missing some of the 35 micronutrients and numerous fatty acids.
The tests I mentioned previously should identify many of them--->
Spectracell,
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
\---------------------------------
Your doctor's job is to make sure that you have what you need.

...............................

.

I see that my favorite pregnenolone is in short supply in USA. Both below are same product.

There is/was exactly same looking product without those words, do not buy that.

No need for licorice when using this.

>
> After reading the forum/blogs.. with results like this, I did not have enough cortisol/dhea to cope with the inflammation (Dr K will probably be shaking his head at this!).
>
> I have had insomnia for over 10 years started with crohn's bleeding and inflammation, never ever naturally sleepy (tired but wired all the time), became dependent on prescription sleeping sedatives.
> For the past 2 years I have been withdrawing from the pills, and had to suffer the most extreme sleep deprivation and withdrawal symptoms from these drugs, rebound insomnia.
> I did not know this at the time, withdrawing from all these pills effects the HPA axis, and my already weak adrenals cannot cope, I had Adrenal Crash about 1 1/2 ago, about 7pm at night, and my Endo took my 9am cortisol test of 350 (not fasted test- no range given), and said I was fine.
>
> I am eating so much liquorice and salt also,
> My DHEA level 1.7 (1-7) - so barely in range - I have been taking 50mg DHEA pill version, and this has hardly done anything.
>
> I am not fine - I am struggling badly, also going through menopause also. All my hormones are low, along with thyroid. I have so many symptoms, along with thyroid and the consequences of the sleep deprivation, I get 1 1/2 hours to 3 hrs if lucky still and it continues. my face is skin and bone now, and need some help and advice! I feel like I have destroyed my mental and physical health by going without sleep for so long.
>
> I have order some Pregnenolone to see if this will help. What dose should I start at, without taking over my adrenals altogether? I unable to get Twist Dhea Cream, will other creams be ok to use? Lifeflo etc.?, and what dose should I use to try and get my hormones up quickly. Is it better to take the pills/cream first thing in the morning, does DHEA follow the cortisol curve also (even though I have feel I have no curve at all, as I feel flat all day- cannot do own saliva test as still on prescript meds)
>
> I am doing everything I can at the moment the advise given when joining -with diet/CT etc. wear blue blockers, which I think help now, but this has a taken a while.
>
> So any advice on my hormones and test levels would be appreciated.
> Thanks

IMHO - you should try taking melatonin - along with a P5P and 5http before bed... timed release if you can get it -

You should also find out what pathogenic bacteria is giving you Crohns - the cheapest test is from [http://www.britishgut.org](http://www.britishgut.org) - they are happy to serve anyone in the U.K. - the most detailed taxa on the planet IMHO -

Pay particular attention to the evening light - wear blue blockers - no carbs at night (carbs=blue light)

Go to bed with an ice pack and read/look at a red incandescent light or a candle before bed -

Bone broths + probiotics + coconut oil would be fantastic for you - make sure the probiotics contain: bacillus subtillus, l. reuteri, l. plantarum, saccharomyces boulardi plus the usual bifidum and acidophilus -
I would also be popping some sublingual b vitamins - especially B5 (pantethene - if possible) -
Look into adaptogens like rhodiola/maca/cordyceps
Astragulus root might be a good idea - assuming you can digest it -
Read:
[http://www.jackkruse.com/the-leaky-gut-prescription/](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-leaky-gut-prescription/)
[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/)
[http://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-2-cortisol/](http://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-2-cortisol/)

Ubiquinol/d-ribose/epsom salts/mag malate would be amazing for you - also, try sucking on some zinc lozengers - I would bet my right arm zinc is in the toilet...

You environment is probably awful - this is why embracing the cold would be a great idea -

Progesterone cream would be a good idea - it will support aldesterone -

Are you taking any digestive enzymes/betaine hcl/ox bile? It's not what you eat, it's what you absorb...

I really feel for you - hang in there -

### billy — 2015-05-06T17:52:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-advice-please.13360/post-163454>

My 1st labs; Hormone Panel for Females, $70
[http://www.privatemdlabs.com/lab\_tests.php?view=category\_result&show=1032&category=6&search=#1032](http://www.privatemdlabs.com/lab_tests.php?view=category_result&show=1032&category=6&search=#1032)
Order online and choose the lab nearest you to get the blood draw.
I am male and wanted Estrodiol for bodybuilding pursuits. You may need a different panel.

\-Estradiol, serum (E2);
\-Follicle-Stimulating Hormone (FSH);
\-Luteinizing Hormone (LH);
\-Testosterone, Serum (Total Only);
\-Complete Blood Count (CBC) w/ Differential: Hematocrit; hemoglobin; mean corpuscular volume (MCV); mean corpuscular hemoglobin (MCH); mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (MCHC); red cell distribution width (RDW); percentage and absolute differential counts; platelet count; red cell count; white blood cell count; immature granulocytes.
\-Comprehensive Metabolic Profile (CMP) (includes eGFR): A:G ratio; albumin, serum; alkaline phosphatase, serum; ALT (SGPT); AST (SGOT); bilirubin, total; BUN; BUN:creatinine ratio; calcium, serum; carbon dioxide, total; chloride, serum; creatinine, serum; globulin, total; glucose, serum; potassium, serum; protein, total, serum; sodium, serum.

I noticed your high SHBG while searching my shiny new hypothyroid issue. I'm male and SHBG affects me slightly differently, but that _is_ high. Mine was 44.2 (19.3-76.4) , down from 65.7 previous labs. Females (non-pregnant): 18-144 nmol/L, but I don't see your age group in your profile.

Vitamin D; I use Carlson Super daily D3 liquid. Glad your level is in range, given your UVB issue.

Dreams at night (unrelated?); I started taking magnesium, Calm powder, and started having vivid dreams. But I slept a bit more soundly.

EMF; I want to build a faraday cage around my bed with steel screen. I can't find anything but expensive stainless steel so far and need a ton of it. I also need to design an easy roll up mechanism on the end and one side.

I'm a carb addict. I used nutritional ketosis to get off carbs. Sadly, I don't handle lipids well and had to go back to standard macros. BUT! I did get off carbs at night and still off. I enter ALL food intake on Sparkpeople.com. I have weighed all food and tracked macros for years on a digital scale. Do you track your macronutrient intake? How many grams of carbs do you eat?

Not all carbs are equal, obviously. I eat Fiber One bars for example, where I should be eating nutrient dense veggies for fiber etc. Try searching Youtube, search terms; keto + Dr. Phinney, Dr. Attia, Dr. D'Agostino, Dr. Jeff Volek - and of course Dr. Kruse!

JanSz mentioned progesterone, a progestagen hormone, converting to cortisol, a glucocorticoid hormone which metabolize carbohydrates. Could it be that excess carbs are triggering conversion?

Last; "I don't seem to have a thermostat," ...hypothyroidism? Low AM temps?

# Thread: Hyperthryoidism >>> Adrenal Fatigue / Derealisation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hyperthryoidism-adrenal-fatigue-derealisation.13386/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shads — 2015-03-20T03:00:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hyperthryoidism-adrenal-fatigue-derealisation.13386/post-158996>

Hi guys, im new to the forum but been aware of JK for a couple of years through the usual paleo websites/podcasts. Seems like a wealth of info on here and lots of knowledgeable people around!!

I was just after some advice with working with my current condition. My health fell off a cliff about 6 months ago when I had a sudden onset of hyperthyroidism (i'd been sub-clinical hyperthyroid for a couple of years which was being monitored by my doctor). Prior to that id been pretty healthy, mainly following a paleo type diet, getting outside lots, playing lots of sport, etc.

The hyperthyroidism was caused by a hyperfunctioning toxic nodule which was surgically removed October 2014 (~90% of my thyroid remains). When I went fully hyperthyroid I essentially had three weeks of hell with constant feelings of severe anxiety, several panic attacks a day and inability to sleep or eat much (went from 72kg to 65kg in two weeks). Saw a psychologist who diagnosed panic disorder and has been helping with coping strategies.

After the operation I returned to being euthyroid pretty quickly but, 6 months on, I am still with many issues and feel a long way from being anywhere near to 'normal'. I feel I am making progress, but its a lot, lot slower than I hoped it would be.

Symptoms include:

\- anxiety - although much better that it was at the start
\- derealisation/depersonalisation - hard to describe but my vision feels like im watching a film on tv, life doesn't quite seem 'real', I also sometimes feel like I don't visually recognize loved ones/myself in the mirror. I also don't feel like im not making memories properly as I have a hard time remembering when things happened and the order in which they occurred. I can tend to get lost in existential thoughts about how complicated the human body is, thoughts about space and time, why am I the person I am and not someone else?, etc, which compounds the feelings of anxiety. I don't remember being like this before being hyperthyroid.
\- constant tinnitus in one ear and occasional distortion of sounds (odd robotic sounding speech)
\- constant fatigue and easily tired, can't exercise - adrenal fatigue has recently been confirmed through salivary ASI, 8am test was low/normal, the other three were all low. Low DHEA and low testosterone (for me) too.
\- inability to cope with any stress - pyrrol test has come back positive (is this even a real condition though?) MTHFR just tested, results back next week.
\- poor sleep - this has been an issue for most of my adult life
\- brain fog and lowered IQ

Current treatment:

\- Gut/liver focused detox and super clean diet - functional doc recommendation
\- Moving to epi-paleo focused diet - 3 or 4 fresh oysters a day and some form of fish every day (manly canned at the moment though)
\- Eat every 3 to 4 hours - i feel terrible if I don't eat regularly, lots of protein seems to work best
\- I tried 20mg DHEA but made me feel spaced out so stopped it
\- Personalized chinese herbs blend (contains licorice, ginger, cinammon, astragalus, ginseng, oyster shells (!), mammal bones (!!))
\- In bed by 10pm
\- I have a pyrrol supplement from functional doc but not started that yet
\- Daily meditation (when i remember!)
\- Getting in the sunshine at lunch time and grounding with barefeet on grass
\- Trying to reduce EMF in house
\- Low lights and blue-blockers after sunset
\- Vit C and Mg
\- Trying to reduce carbs (I definitely don't eat high or even moderate amounts) but feel like I need a reasonable amount to feel ok. Ive always been thin and have a very difficult time putting weight on. I could (but don't) eat carbs all day and not put any weight on. I tried ketogenic a few years back and feel like I don't do too well with very high fat.
\- R/O water

So, sorry for the super long first post, but any help would be massively appreciated...!

### Penny — 2015-03-20T17:32:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hyperthryoidism-adrenal-fatigue-derealisation.13386/post-159050>

Hi Shads! Oh, do take the pyrol sup in the morning - it probably contains zinc and P5P (b6) which will help you make serotonin+dopamine - plus, if you have pyroluria, you most likely have some kind of gut infection -

My go to supplement for any kind of anxiety is GABA Calm by Source Naturals - and magnesium at night - take the mag at night and zinc in the morning - I like to take zince/d/k2 at the same time in the morning -

I see you have yet to "embrace the cold" - since your environment is obviously a massive issue - this would be very helpful - try some ice packs on your body when you go to bed plus get a red incandescent light at a big box store and expose your eyes to it - I like to read with a red light with a few ice packs and 7 minutes later, I am gone - and leave a salad bowl filled with ice water on a counter - dunk whenever you remember -

Unplug unplug unplug - cordless phones, routers, everything that can be in sleep mode, put it there - no carbs after noon as that is just like a boatload of blue light - also, remove any electronics from your head - > 3 feet away -
Don't have your head against the wall either -

Sunshine is better from 9:00-noon -infrared light - and you need DHA to convert the sunshine to electrons - sulfated d - also, Epsom Salt baths/foot soaks/creams prior to sun exposure is massively helpful - you must get vit d up -

To help you digest fat, buy some ox bile - cheap and a life saver -

My, what a high dose of methylfolate... I never go over 200mcg myself... When dealing with an ill person, I use Thorne b-complex #12 - it has \*all\* the b vitamins in it which work together - supporting the whole cycle is more helpful than one part of it -
plus, since it is water soluble, more frequent low dosing seems to make more sense to me...

If you get a headache from the methyl-guard plus, it means you are not using all the methyl groups and they have turned into glutamates, thus giving you a headache - magnesium will help the headache... you must at the very least add b12 to this regiment - I don't see it in your supplement list - take the b12 with or before the folate -
