Tonight's deepest thought for you to ponder:

Maxwell's equations only cover two dimensions of space, not three; Most people do not know this. and it turns out to be a big point............and the third part is covered by de Broglie's standing waves around the 3 fundamental particles in matter. These are scalar waves (standing waves) and the pictures from 2008 (Youtube) of the electron now point to de Broglie being correct that the waves are in all three dimensions. The problem is physics uses math to describe everything. Physicists math don't handle these waves at all. Most of you know I hate anything modeled on math. It is why I dont like Taleb. I like experimental data to tease our environmental cues. The space around these particles is the key....space = distance (ubi 5 alert)...that space in cosmology is space/time and in biology we call that the cell. Biology's action is within the cell. Within the cell, things are subdivided it to the atomic level and light scales from nucleus to the CM surface. Around mitochondria these scalar waves change how condensed matter interacts with light. Why are waves big? Ans: Because they can be polarized. Light makes the amplitude of the wave have a DIRECTION. Direction of a wave gives it arrow for time. Light usually polarizes condensed matter perpendicularly to the direction of travels. Why? That is how the electromagnetic force is organized. This means that the condensed matter protein polymers in use in our tissues have to have a CERTAIN specific molecular arrangement for this process to work with light that hits it. What happens when this does not happen? The momentum and energy and light are not deposited properly in tissues and this alters our redox potential and we lose energy. This is what neuro-degeneration is at its quantum core; its a loss of that particular arrangement from protein folding that alters light's path.

### Scompy — 2015-11-13T19:32:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-181911>

> [Hansen Kenimer said:](/goto/post?id=181907)
>
> Well, fortunately it's NOT Me. But unfortunately, it's my daughter. When she was little she could spend all day outdoors and never really tan or burn. Now that she's older, she burns fairly easily. I KNOW she's terribly UV deficient and blue light toxic, but until I can get her to care about her health enough to get outside and do something about it, it's just me talking to a brick wall.

@Hansen, I wanted to share a bit of trickery I've learned. I've got a 6 and a half year old wearing blue-blockers every night right at dusk an on, and he even asks for them when he's misplaced them. My trick is that I kept my mouth shut and make my behavior look like normal behavior. By not saying a thing over time, he got curious and asked me about the glasses. He sees the parents put theirs on, and thinks it's normal. Monkey see, monkey do, but it requires patience. I had 2 pairs of kid-sized blue-blockers ready when he was ready. Kids know that they are dependent on us, so I think the more we talk or try to force it too fast, the more resistant they become because they are not owning it.

Recently, I've changed his behavior so that he's drinking water throughout the day and he knows that grounding = health too. For the water, I simply started putting a straw to his mouth without saying a word at first and he has a natural behavior to reach out with his mouth and take a sip. Once he got hydrated (without me saying a word), the next phase was putting the same glass and straw near him whenever I could and maybe saying just a "here you go." By instinct, monkey see, monkey do...now that he craves water more frequently, he sees the water in his space and wants to drink from it, because he owns it. I didn't tell him, "now drink your water." Instead, if he didn't drink anything, I'll only remind him by saying, "are you finished with this?" 9 times out of 10, he'll own it and realize it's there again and start drinking again. The same tactics work with food. He's now eating carrots all of the time because I simply leave a little bowl in his space, and ONLY that food. Nothing else to tempt him. Now if I say, "I'm peeling some carrots for myself, do you want any?" he'll usually say "sure." If he doesn't want any, I don't force it. I want him to own it, but he has no clue how I stealth-changed his behaviors.

He's also getting a lot of morning light because his school does 3 recesses a day, 20mins at a time. I'm getting him out on the weekends more too. We have a sword-game called 'mailbox monsters.' We take foam swords outside and point the swords at mailboxes to 'destroy them' with imaginary plasma-beams, fireballs or lightning bolts out of the sword tips. It's a bit of a competition because he always wants to destroy the mailbox monsters first. He has no idea that walking around the block took 20-30 minutes and he just got a good dose of sun. Distraction is key here, because if I would have said, "We need to go get sunlight, let's go outside" that would be a big fail for him because he's very independent and wants to be in control over things. Instead, I say, "I think it's time I take out some mailbox monsters. Want to join me?" If he says no, I'll continue to get ready myself and talk about how I'm gonna get at least another 20+ to catch up with his score. Again, he usually shifts and then joins me out of competition instinct... use that drive to win against them when you can

Also, when he sees me outside on my own lifting weights in the sunlight outside in the morning without a shirt, he'll equate that for being healthy without me having to say a word. Silence often is a weapon that fixes everything.

Granted, he is getting close to 7 years old and has a good sense about himself and understand his own health too. I'd have to change my tactics if he were only 3 years old. How old is she?

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-11-22T23:06:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-182508>

True or false

1) Cooling IR light shortens its wavelength

2) Compressing IR light (or heat) also shortens its wavelength

3) Pressure cools and slows time (this is why we apply pressure instinctively whenever we bash our shin on the coffee table. The yelling is the release/detoxing of entropy)

4)The power of IR light should equal the power of UV light so that the two will resonate (more IR to less UV). With resonance, 1+1 can equal 4

6)a cell's center (nucleus) is cooler than its surface (just like a star)

7) a cell's center has more electrons than its surface (" ")

8) a cell's center has more compressed light (UV) than its surface (IR)

9) If the earth was like a cell, seafood would be the most energy dense since it would be closer to the 'nucleus' than any other food source.

I spent 3 months in Finland in 1994 and it was the most profound summer of my life when I went from about 280 pounds down to 220. What was I doing daily? I adopted their lifestyle of taking a long sauna, then jumping into a 50F swimming pool, the back to the sauna, then back to the swimming pool, etc. An hour later, my body was so numb that I actually \*craved\* the cold rather than wanting the warmth. Weirdly, I also was sleeping on a \*heater-turned-off-waterbed\* each night because it was broken.. I had to bundle up on it because the bed was damn cold. Was also eating all of their foods, which was mostly ketotic except for the rye bread and some sulfur-licorice. And Finland summer-sun translates into 18 hours daily--I was getting outside A LOT because I was in a remote location in Lapland, and needed to bike to places to get basic supplies.

But think about this--60 pounds in 3 months. I didn't have access to a scale there, and got back to California and saw a 221 on the scale and just about fainted. But each week later in Cali, eating the same volumes/calories of food and going to the gym, guess what happened? Blam, 3 months later I was back to 280 pounds. I never correlated light use and cold use to my 1994 Finland-Success until 20 years later after reading Dr. K.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-11-27T07:40:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-182734>

If we evolved in and around the ocean, can a little bit of sea water really be that bad for us? I've seen seagulls gulping the shit down like there's no tomorrow. I imagine that our ape ancestors probably slept in groups right in the ocean with our heads above the water and that explains why we have hair on our head- to keep our head warm. I really can't see them running up to find fresh water every time they were thirsty. Then there are sea lions and other mammals that live in the sea.
Does sea water really make us sick or is it just a very serious detox reaction? After all we did come from the sea.

I don't know if anyone else has experienced what I have the last few weeks but ever since I started using a purple light at home I swear that I am able to 'see' the big picture of things much much clearer. Its like my intuition is really tuned in to my surroundings. I 'see' things that no one else sees. and Jack's blogs are making more and more sense.I even find that everywhere I go now there just 'happens to be' lots of young attractive girls around and they are all quite friendly to me. I remember that they are tuned in to their environment more than the general pop. because of the lack of myelin. I'm not high on myself or anything but I swear that its happening.
It kind of freaks me out a bit..but it kind of rocks too!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-04T21:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-183317>

Proteins like the ones dopamine and GH are made from in solution absorb ultraviolet light with absorbance maxima at 280 and 200 nm. Amino acids with aromatic rings are the primary reason for the absorbance peak at 280 nm. Peptide bonds are primarily responsible for the peak at 200 nm. This is why protein's peptide bonds are so stable on earth.......UV light never goes to 200 nm in the visible spectrum. Secondary, tertiary, and quaternary structure all affect absorbance, therefore factors such as pH, ionic strength, etc. can alter the absorbance spectrum.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-04T21:56:19+0200

Curves.......gave you lots to think about here above: you've been loyal and a long term learner. I applaud you:

Now a gift:
**Dopamine and its analogues are like Growth Hormone from a molecular standpoint.** The have similar 3 D atomic dimensions. This means their geometry is similar. So lets scale this to physics. This means dopamine has a molecular symmetry to GH. The principle of symmetry as it is used in physics and mathematics can be described as "change without change." While this may sound psychotic to you now (it should not after Dec 2015 web and Time 4), it makes quantum sense and is something simple to grasp. What makes a circle (dopamine) such a symmetrical object? It's that you can rotate it around its center and it will remain a circle. In the case of an equilateral triangle (GH), small rotations will change it, but if you rotate it by 120 degrees it comes back to itself. It thus has some geometric symmetry, though less than a circle. What did I say in Pasadena at the bio hacking conference?

In QED things are rarely exactly the same, but they rhyme. Biochem exp require reproducibility but photochemistry rarely does. It depends upon the proper time light is present or absent. This is why guys like Peat miss the keys........light is the lever for the protein gears in the eye clck. Their pathways explain nothing with the light levers. Light changes the proteins by changing the charge of the protein and in this way change appears to occur without change. I can explain it, they make fun. You ultimately win. That is all I care about. This is where the concept comes from. I think I wrote that in Tensegrity 5. This concept of symmetry as "change without change" can easily be generalized to laws of physics, or to the equations that express them. So I want to know if Dopamine and GH work within the same symmetrical paradigm with light................and soon you'll know what I know about that in another time blog.

Spending time with my family over the Thanksgiving holiday I realized that most of them have completely lost their curiosity about the world. Rather than remaining inquisitive and open-minded, they think they have it all figured out. My daughter pointed out to me that when I make statements about what I've discovered, they feel threatened. They don't hear anything I have to say. It's too "outlandish" for them to consider; too outside their comfort zone.

The only person that was intrigued was my step-mom because she though I looked younger. I suppose when you compare me to my sister who is 7 years my junior, who only went outside in the LA sun huddled under thick clothing and dark sunglasses you can definitely see the difference between us. She looks 5 years older than I do and is completely close-minded.

Frankly, I can't imagining how boring your life would be without wonder and awe.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-12-04T23:21:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-183328>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=183318)
>
> **Clothes, contacts, glasses all block sunlight to the skin and eye send the signal of winter year around. While carbs out of season, indoor heating, blue light, nnEMF send the signal of perpetual summer & daytime. Our ability to manufacture a comfortable environment is driving neolithic biological chaos. The mind can't distinguish comfort/convenience from safety and has created a hell for the body.**

So....I've learned this:

Body temperature regulation has everything to do with blood sugar levels, and vice versa. If you are insulin-sensitive, under powerful enough non-native EMF assault, you will become unable to maintain your core body temperature. A metabolically healthy person with a "wintertime" or keto-adapted metabolism (who maintains their blood sugar levels via physiological insulin-resistance) exposed to a sudden, strong EMF assault (think "super-summer signal") will have FA oxidation suddenly and drastically down regulated at the same time glucose metabolism is suddenly and drastically upregulated, causing a sudden and drastic drop of the stable blood sugar levels they had priorly been maintaining with ease as the muscles soak up the blood glucose like dry sponges. This causes a sudden, extreme hypoglycemia and corresponding hypothermia. (Did I say sudden and extreme often enough here????) How do I know this? Our wifi here at work got upgraded the other day to the fastest business internet one can get. I actually went home feeling awful and not able to get warm on Wednesday at lunch. Took Thurs off to recover with UV and grounding. Came in today to conduct a final biohack before I resign this weekend.

This morning I came armed with my thermometer. As per usual, I did my fasted resistance training this morning and had a cup of coffee with cream on my drive to work. Felt great, energy was in excess. I took my temperature out in the car just before entering the building. It was normal. Within 5 minutes of entering the building, I had a headache. Within 15 minutes of first exposure to the new, super-speed wifi, my core temp had dropped two degrees. TWO FREAKING DEGREES! I have to say I was surprised at the reading, because I was actually expecting an INCREASE in normal temperature. Now I know that was an illogical expectation. Care to know the ONLY way I was able to re-establish (almost 3 hours later) a normal temperature under the super high nnEMF exposure? I put on my coat. And then my scarf. And then I ate Skittles. (I know. I did it. I had to, just to see what happened.) And even more unfortunately, because I am so insulin-sensitive, the glucose was shuttled into my cells as fast or faster than I could consume them. So, quite literally, the ONLY way to maintain normal core temp in a high nnEMF environment is to become PATHOLOGICALLY insulin-resistant. Duh.

Anyway, I don't recall reading anything here in regard to blood sugar and core temperature regulation, but it is certainly logical under the seasonal eating paradigm. However, winter/keto metabolism is totally impossible to maintain under high nnEMF conditions. I know that now. You can mitigate low enough nnEMF conditions, but the healthier you are, the less you are able to handle high nnEMF environments because of the up regulation of glucose metabolism and the corresponding hypothermic response in an insulin-sensitive person.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2015-12-04T23:25:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-183329>

This would have occurred to me sooner, but I guess I was never before in an EMF environment I couldn't mitigate with hydration and ketosis.

Oh wait. Yes I was. I lived very near high-power lines for 11 years before I was diagnosed at 34 with breast cancer. Come to think of it, my only symptom in the year leading to my diagnosis was very low energy and....wait for it......hypothermia.

I see now that mitigating the effects is a half-assed approach. One must ELIMINATE.

Here by the end of the work day, however, I am starting to become slightly hyperthermic, which is more along the lines of what I expected. I was probably confusing the effects of acute exposure (hypothermia) with more sustained exposure (hyperthermia.)

Whatever. After this day is over, I'm out of here, never to return. Working on my resignation now. Good thing I don't really have an important job...I'll be easily replaced.

So, quite literally, the ONLY way to maintain normal core temp in a high nnEMF environment is to become PATHOLOGICALLY insulin-resistant. Duh.
>
> Anyway, I don't recall reading anything here in regard to blood sugar and core temperature regulation, but it is certainly logical under the seasonal eating paradigm. However, winter/keto metabolism is totally impossible to maintain under high nnEMF conditions. I know that now. You can mitigate low enough nnEMF conditions, but the healthier you are, the less you are able to handle high nnEMF environments because of the up regulation of glucose metabolism and the corresponding hypothermic response in an insulin-sensitive person.

Wow. So that would mean that a person who has really bad labs but feels okay is really just adapting to the nnEMF environment that they are in. This sets them up for real problems down the road but makes them feel like nothing is wrong. However they clearly would be more disabled now compared to how they would do in an environment with much less nnEMF. But because of the lack of ability to connect thoughts it is completely beyond their perception.
Am I in the the ballpark?

> Am I in the the ballpark?

That is my theory. Being insulin - sensitive actually sensitizes you to nnEMF. My n=1 is finding wifi to be the most problematic. I was looking around on Wednesday wondering why I seemed to be the only one being affected, when I started to pay attention to a young woman I'd expect to have trouble as she is a Crossfitter and therefore probably more insulin sensitive simply due to the nature of the workouts. I thought she looked pale and depressed on Wednesday. When I talked to her yesterday I asked how she was feeling and she complained about being unusually tired that week. She attributed it to just a post-holiday slump. So I took the opportunity to share with her what was really going on. The only man that has complained this week of any health issue is a runner...weird tingling in his hands/arms and heart feeling funny. So....it seems the 3 people in the office having symptoms are daily exercisers. No one else is. However, I'm the only one practicing fasting and ketosis in winter...and I've been hit by far the hardest.

I've been having a lot of trouble thermoregulating today...After rising this morning I had a major hot flash and two hours later I sat in the deer stand with my husband in a brisk 25 degrees....and got very cold. Took my temp when we got back and it was shockingly low. Below 95. I didn't start shivering until I got back in the house. My ability to access thermogenesis has clearly been drastically impacted. My thermostat at is jacked up! I also woke up at 1 am this morning and was unable to go back to sleep. All this, in one week. Well, 3 and a half days of exposure.

It inactivates the stress hormones created by the presence of light and stores the massive amount of DC electric current from the later appearing UV AM light in proteins like melanin. The RPE in the retina is a massive warehouse of melanin. Melanin releases its payload to tissues at night in the absence of short wavelength blue (melanopsin 400-465nm) to replete the DC electric current in RPE and retinal cells at night.

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-12-08T02:38:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-183659>

Okay, so, hope @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) can chime in.

I was in contact with some researchers that work with the new Vielight product, the [Vielight Neuro](http://vielight.com/product/vielight-neuro/) (transcranial + intranasal LLLT, with the transcranial LEDs targeting the [Default Mode Network](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Default_mode_network) and [Salience Network](http://www.jneurosci.org/content/33/16/7091.abstract) of the brain, and with the light on the intranasal applicator offering MORE power and irradiance than the Vielight 810's nasal applicator!). They're offering me and a couple others I'm friends with some discounted, refurbished products for a hack. I was wondering:

1\. Would the use of the Vielight 810 prior to or during vestibular and/or proprioception training enhance the effects or cause the learning curve to decrease?
2\. I was going to engage in some various forms of weekly psychotherapy, particularly Somatic Experiencing and some other somatic therapies coupled with the typical talk therapy (at the same time) - there is research via the Vielight people that intranasal light causes fear extinction and can enhance the acquisition of 'replacement memories', in other words, eliminating fearful reactions that are stored viscerally, and re-opening certain neural pathways that can allow for one to re-learn/grow healthy behaviors, thoughts, patterns, etc. Would it make sense to use the device directly before, during, or directly after these therapies?
3\. Would using the Cryoscarf, which cools the underlying neck/throat tissues enough to mildly lower body temperature (temporarily) and potently erase headaches allow for an enhancement of the Vielight if used concurrently?
4\. Have you, Dr. Kruse, ever used the Vielight _**while**_, doing CT, say, an ice bath?
5\. Are there any ways to enhance brain tissue absorption of this light for one who does not **_currently_** have access to medical technology, drugs, syringes, etc (I remember you mentioning something regarding IM injections prior to using the vielight)? By not currently having access, I mean that in addition to my university studies, I'll be undergoing training as an EMT in a few weeks, and already have family members in other medical sciences and medical positions, and thus will have access to various (legal) things.
6\. Lastly, and perhaps most importantly -> I wake up for work and school extremely early. I sometimes arrive at 4 : 30 AM. **Would it make sense to use this before work, _prior to sunrise_**, and NOT use something such as a full-spectrum wake up light to simulate sunrise, as to not risk sending my body mixed signals (wake up light says "It's sunrise!!!", then I travel in a dark environment to work for 15+ minutes, exposed to little/no light, sending signals of a low light environment/time, then arrive to work/school with tons of fluorescent light, and when I get back outside, it's still dark!)

### bio-fractal-soul-self — 2015-12-08T02:54:26+0200

Thank you so much for the rapid reply! Very quick follow up questions, then:

1\. Context: Does this mean particular vestibular/proprioception exercises, or specific vestibular/proprioception problem?
2\. Even somatic therapies? If memories and/or energies can be stored viscerally as people such as Dr. Porges or Biophysicist Peter Levine, PhD, believe, causing the visceral release seems as though it would be a potent way to restore energy flow to specific tissues
3\. Is there a max dose for the Vielight?
3.1 Would "pulsing" the dose work better -> 20 mins during the drive from work to university; 20 minutes during drive home; 20 minutes in CT tub; as opposed to 60 minutes at once?
3.2 Are there psychological, cognitive, or physical signs to watch out for to see if one surpasses the "biphasic dose response"? Perhaps decreased joint mobility, brain fog, restlessness, or something else?

Thanks again.

[http://tagsync.com/complexity.htm](http://tagsync.com/complexity.htm)

"there is a simple law of physics that governs the development of complexity in all inanimate, animate, and technical designs - **Bejan’s Constructal Law of Physics** (also see section on CLaw). “**For a finite-size flow system to _persist in time_ (to live), its configuration must evolve in such a way that provides easier access to the currents that flow through it.**” (Bejan A 2012 - Design in Nature \[1\]). The snowflake is a living morphing design to disperse heat to the atmosphere during freezing. The complexity is a result.The mind, scientific organizations, communications grids, etc., are all living morphing designs to increase the flow of information through them. Similarly the evolving diversity and complexity in geological formations, plants and animals provides more efficient flow of matter, energy and information as well.There is a growing variety of mathematical methods for calculating complexity such as dimensional complexity, Lyapunov exponent, approximate entropy, Lempel-Ziv complexity, correlation dimension, Hurst exponent, **multiscale entropy, synchrony coalition entropy** and others. These are applied to analysis of behavior as well as to biological signals such as ECG and EEG. This mathematical complexity is not needed to understand the basic concept that **wellness behavior involves evolution of complexity, and sickness behavior involves loss of complexity.** Standard measures of entropy only measure randomness, but biological complexity grows in a special region between rigidity and randomness such as in small world networks."

"Aging: [▲](http://tagsync.com/complexity.htm#CMPLX_TOC)“Considering the general "loss of complexity" theory of aging, our finding of **increased EEG complexity in elderly people with heightened creativity** supports the idea that creativity is associated with activated neural networks.”Ueno K, et al (2015) - Neurophysiological basis of creativity in healthy elderly people: a multiscale entropy approach. Clin Neurophysiol. 2015 Mar; 126(3):524-31. \[[Abstract](http://pubmed.gov/25066939)\]“We observed that physically active elderly adults had better accuracy on both visuo-spatial attention and working memory conditions relative to their sedentary counterparts. Additionally, these physically active elderly adults displayed **greater MSE values at larger time scales at the Fz electrode in both attention and memory conditions.” MSE = Multiscale Entropy.** “Wang CH, et al (2014) - The association of physical activity to neural adaptability during visuo-spatial processing in healthy elderly adults: A multiscale entropy analysis. Brain Cogn. 2014 Oct 29; 92C:73-83. \[[Abstract](http://pubmed.gov/25463141)\]“Thus, the **"wisdom of old age' may find its neurophysiological basis in greater complexity of brain dynamics compared to young ages.”** “The results confirm the hypothesis: after a jump in the brain dynamics complexity during puberty a linear increase with age is observed. During maturation (7-25 years), the maximum gain in complexity occurs over the frontal associative cortex.” Anokhin AP, et al (1996) - Age increases brain complexity. Electroencephalogr Clin Neurophysiol. 1996 Jul; 99(1):63-8. \[[Abstract](http://pubmed.gov/8758971)"

### Psyche — 2015-12-17T23:57:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-184388>

Holy shit, this is huge. And it's happening to me as well. Quite suddenly, I have a total aversion towards doing CT and also have intense sugar cravings. Completely, completely unlike me. I LOVE CT and I've been so looking forward to winter! Then, a couple of weeks ago, _apparently out of the blue_, I just stopped wanting to do CT. Period. It was awful. I would go as far as run the bath, but just stand in the tub, and then give up. I suddenly just can't seem to make myself get in the cold. And I have MASSIVE sugar cravings.

What changed? I temporarily moved out of my office, which was in a relatively remote place with no one else's computer nearby. I'm temporarily sitting in an open cubicle area...I have my computer base at my feet, someone else's is directly next to it on the other side of the wall, and another person's is in close proximity as well. Also- the kitchen microwave not too far from me.
I have felt like total shit since sitting here.

Wow. It's so interesting to note that when this first started happening, my reaction was quite "normal"- I tried to figure out what could be happening (outside of me) to make me react this way. As the days went on, however, and now two weeks later, my thinking has degraded into "what's wrong with ME?" In fact, I was going down a thyroid rabbit hole, which shows how fucked up my thinking has gotten, because I KNOW thyroid won't BE an issue if you're cold adapted. But I sure am glad I went there, because it's what made me understand Hansen's post. HUGE A-ha! moment. Grateful, Hansen.

For three days, I've been taking my temperature to establish a baseline for my thyroid. I take it upon waking, then 3 other times at three-hour intervals. The average of the three is what I plot.
What I've found, is that my waking temp IS a little low. 97.9, 98.2, 97.7. What I've been EXPECTING to find is to have a slight increase with each successive temperature. This has NOT happened. Each day, my morning temp has been my HIGHEST temperature of the day...it PLUMMETS after that. It seems spiky within the plummet, too. Like it will go from 98 to 95 and then back to 98.
Ho-ly SHIT. It's not me , it's my fucking environment. Wow. Just....wow.

These are the kind of experiences that make me go "holy shit!" just hearing about them! We are so fortunate to be in the know about the huge problems this shit can cause. So many people right now have no idea what is causing their miserable health conditions. Then when you tell them, they think YOU are the one with the problem.
A guy at my work has been having problems with allergies for quite some time now. He works in the electronics lab with the wifi router above his desk. I said that maybe he should consider his 'field'.
Bad move.
" Well maybe we should all just go live in the bush then  and live off the land like normal animals, Mike!! "
I'm not quite there yet but I am learning to just keep my mouth shut and let people succumb to their fate. as heartbreaking as that is.
Never in a million years would I have thought that I would see so many people in fierce denial.

>
> Body temperature regulation has everything to do with blood sugar levels, and vice versa. If you are insulin-sensitive, under powerful enough non-native EMF assault, you will become unable to maintain your core body temperature. A metabolically healthy person with a "wintertime" or keto-adapted metabolism (who maintains their blood sugar levels via physiological insulin-resistance) exposed to a sudden, strong EMF assault (think "super-summer signal") will have FA oxidation suddenly and drastically down regulated at the same time glucose metabolism is suddenly and drastically upregulated, causing a sudden and drastic drop of the stable blood sugar levels they had priorly been maintaining with ease as the muscles soak up the blood glucose like dry sponges. This causes a sudden, extreme hypoglycemia and corresponding hypothermia. (Did I say sudden and extreme often enough here????) How do I know this? Our wifi here at work got upgraded the other day to the fastest business internet one can get. I actually went home feeling awful and not able to get warm on Wednesday at lunch. ### seanb4 — 2016-02-27T13:54:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-189865>

Help me understand some physics here. I'm still not sure on how proteins use an electron being raised to make work/energy.

If a photon of wavelength 259nm hits NAD then an electron will be raised from it's ground position, a split second later it fall back and releases a photon of lesser energy than the original.

So the electron must become useful when it's raised.

All I can think is that the electron could be then knocked out of orbit (by more photons?) and thus can flow like electricity around NAD creating more magnetism making reactions easier. OR the raising of the electron for a split second assists a reaction that NAD is doing at that time by the electron being closer to the target and thus more accessible?

I know I'm probably way off so can anyone help me out here?

### seanb4 — 2016-05-04T21:46:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-194171>

Carbs give mitochondria a break by using fermentation. Does this mean
1\. The mitochondria are recieving fewer, higher powered electrons and hence less work.
2\. Gut flora mainly breaks down the carbs releasing electrons and light which go straight to the mitochondria???
3\. Mitochondria use fermentation pathways instead of normal electron chain transport to generate energy (what pathways would these be?)

Thanks.

### lohd2015 — 2016-05-04T22:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-194175>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=194171)
>
> Carbs give mitochondria a break by using fermentation. Does this mean
> 1\. The mitochondria are recieving fewer, higher powered electrons and hence less work.
> 2\. Gut flora mainly breaks down the carbs releasing electrons and light which go straight to the mitochondria???
> 3\. Mitochondria use fermentation pathways instead of normal electron chain transport to generate energy (what pathways would these be?)
>
> Thanks.

Great questions, seanb4! May I please add some more?....

I got this far with fermentation: From Google: " **Cell Respiration**: **Fermentation**. All cells are able to synthesize ATP via the process of glycolysis. In many cells, **if oxygen is not present**, pyruvate is metabolized in a process called **fermentation**.**Fermentation** complements glycolysis and makes it possible for ATP to be continually produced in the absence of oxygen."

Don't think this is quite accurate because I found another article that says anaerobic respiration is mainly done by microorganisms that use other elements, such as sulfate, instead of oxygen as their electron receptor. Further:

([http://www.shmoop.com/cell-respiration/fermentation-anaerobic-respiration.html](http://www.shmoop.com/cell-respiration/fermentation-anaerobic-respiration.html) )

"If a cell has a short supply of oxygen because happens to be in a body running at full speed away from a lion, however, it will not use anaerobic respiration but will start fermentation. Fermentation extends glycolysis with extra reactions that replenish NAD+, so that glycolysis can keep running and keep producing small amounts of ATP. Fermentation comes in two types:

• Lactic acid fermentation
• Alcohol fermentation

Lactic acid fermentation happens in bacteria, fungi, and animal cells. It is a pretty simple follow-up to glycolysis: the pyruvate molecules are reduced to lactate, while NADH is oxidized to NAD+. In this way, NAD+ is replenished for glycolysis. Lactic acid fermentation happens in some fungi and bacteria, but also in animal muscle cells....."

I have this question:
Do cells default to fermentation when complex I is defective, or when one is psuedohypoxic?

Is this a good reference for Complex I?
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4706149/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4706149/)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-05T00:42:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-194181>

Carbs give mitochondria a break by using fermentation. Does this mean
1\. The mitochondria are receiving fewer, higher powered electrons and hence less work: NO. Carbs have higher powered electrons because of the photonic power.
2\. Gut flora mainly breaks down the carbs releasing electrons and light which go straight to the mitochondria??? No true......causes microbiome to release more light from bacterial cells and this lowers their diversity and numbers eventually which gives you more issues.
3\. Mitochondria use fermentation pathways instead of normal electron chain transport to generate energy (what pathways would these be?)

Mitochondria use all pathways........the key is what is the distance in Angstroms between respiratory proteins. Sean the blogs have been very clear on this point. Bio-physics is not about substrate biochemistry it is about the bio-physical organization of the cell.

So many here have made this error I am just glad they are all becoming sparse. It is fundamental to the work being done in quantum biology.

### lilly antic — 2016-05-05T20:51:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-194250>

If you have a high heteroplasmy % in your mitochondria , you want to get rid of them. As carbs go through cytochrome 1, which produces ROS, these can be helpful in eliminating the high heteroplasmy .
electrons from fats enter at cytochrome 2, so bypass the ROS station at cytochrome 1 = less ROS to effect and eliminate mitochondria.
So someone with high heteroplasmy misses the chance in summer ( without causing a light signal mismatch) to replace the damaged mitochondria, and in winter, instead of nurturing mitochondrial maintenance, this person has to get along with weak ones, increasing the heteroplasmy % .
Someone with a good heteroplasmy % to begin with might not have so many problems with ketosis in summer as someone with high %.

My contribution to the stupid questions revolve around morning sun light:

1\. Is being grounded necessary for morning sun exposure, especially in regards to improving hormonal health?
2\. Once again, with regards to improving hormones, does there need to be UV light present in the morning (ie, morning UV light starts showing up around 930am here, but is not present when I wake at 6am)?
3\. For morning light in general - is more per day better? Or does one simply need to reach in minimum dose?
4\. When going for optimal, how does one manage morning sun during our rough Canadian winters? Being outside naked is quite difficult when its -15 C.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-11T02:22:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-194532>

1\. Yes. Grounding is required for maximal assimilation of photoelectric power.
2\. No.
3\. Morning light from sunrise to the first UVA ray is the key
4\. At sea level you cannot. You need to be at 5000 ft above sea level and in a population sparse area simultaneously.
5\. In most cases yes.........but altitude and population density are also big variables.

### seanb4 — 2016-05-11T12:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-194547>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=194229)
>
> Is this why ketosis is a bad idea in the summer because of the lower O2?
>
> If that is why, then if one can somehow maintain adequate O2, would ketosis+sun in high temperatures still be preferable to carbs+sun?

I think with regards to O2; when it is hotter O2 is less dense particularly in things like water, which means not only will you breathe in less but your body could hold less than when cold. So it is essential for your mitochondria to be working well in heat as the better they work the more magnetism they produce and the more O2 they bring in as it is paramagnetic. So carbs allow you to produce ROS at cytochrome 1 to fix the bad ones.

However if you don't repair what caused them to get bad in the first place, blue light, viruses, and other stresses then they will keep getting recycled, broke, and recycled; this will deplete you of energy and stem cells. Ketosis means you can use the mitochondria without recycling but if they are bad to begin with then this isn't much good to you.

Problem is I tried high carb recently to fix my mito yet there must be some stressers still about because all that happened is I got fatter and not much healthier at all. I am now going to try messing around with ketosis, nicotine, and niacin for a while, hoping it will be enough to trigger recycling yet maintain the the benefits of ketosis whilst correcting NAD+/NADH ratio.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-11T18:17:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-194558>

As wellness fades to black, and we get ill, we need to recall life's story has no beginning or end: we arbitrarily choose that moment of experience from which to look back or from which to look ahead. Slowly, with many lost days, we can feel life returning. Rescue is in the air now. The end is in the beginning, and the future lies far ahead. Buckle in, the ride is not over during sickness, it will soon resume. Rage, recover, repeat. Life is a play, we can't afford a replay. Experience life and appreciate the present moment, for this moment, not the replay.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-02T22:41:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-207804>

As a RBC travels through the vessel it sheds sulfate groups which carry a negative charge. This also means within the circulatory system we develop both and electric and magnetic field. The voltage change is caused by the motions of the RBC itself and DHA and sulfates within the cell membrane and endothelium. The hemoglobin inside of the RBC is a ferry boat of light to be given to cytochrome 1 of mitochondria. It cannot have any mitochondria or nuclear DNA for this job. It is not involved in the shedding phenomena. Remember RBC's have no mitochondria by design......they have to bring light to cells with mitochondria. The voltage change with blood flow acts like an induction magnet and it can induce both electric and magnetic fields to alter the DC electric current and water networks in blood plasma to become a different fluid between night and day time for tissues. As the RBC's moves through the circulation the magnetic field induced allows the vessel wall to release nitric oxide and to sulfate cholesterol, DHEA, and the newly created Vitamin D3. This occurs in the blood plasma which is filled with 93% water. The EZ mechanism augments blood flow this way when the skin is hit by sunlight because there is a lot of positive charge in the artery wall and a large negative charge in the RBC's and EZ of blood plasma. This allows the "like likes like" action to occur between the EZ and RBC's to improve laminar flow in the blood as the moving RBC's induce their fields as they move.

Cholesterol plays a huge role here......Cholesterol stabilizes the artery wall by stablizing the cells in this tissue by stabilizing the inner mitochondrial membrane to lower vibrations in the Q cycle. This makes sure the " light deli slicer" (recent webinar) is accurate in making the key red light frequencies from the 340 nm excited electrons (excitons) that enter at cytochrome that will be shuttled to cytochrome 3 for the distal cytochromes. Most people know that sunlight has 42% IR-A light present in it. Most people do not know that red light at 613.5 and 623.5 nm (in one spectrum, at 606 nm), in the red maximum have some interesting effects on human tissue. RBC's assimilate red light up until 600 nm only. Young blood assimilates all frequencies between 250-600nm. As we age our RBC's lose the ability to absorb all frequencies between 250nm-600nm and this is why as we age heteroplasmy rises, and older people need more and not less solar exposure. 600nm is where hemoglobin's peak ends sharply for absoption. Are there other key frequencies buried in the sun for healing other parts of our tissues? Yes. The far-red maximum has exact peak positions between 667.5 and 683.7 nm in different spectra. Two near infrared maxima have peak positions in the range 750.7-772.3 nm and 812.5-846.0 nm. Might these peaks be critical in our mitochondria too? Might this be why low cholesterol kills us with many types of mitochondrial diseases (cancers)? I think so and this mechanism above shows you why.

Cholesterol is a polar molecule that needs the sun to sulfate it so that molecular vibrations are lowered. The isomerization step in the skin requires an slightly elevated surface temperature, that UVB light gives the skin to photonically, and this enables the change of cholesterol to its sulfated form and then S- cholesterol to its sulfated Vitamin D3 form. This is fundamentally why Ancel Keys 7 countries Study was completely worthless on quantum biology merits. He found that high un-sulfated cholesterol linked to heart disease. That link should have been expected because in all seven countries he sampled the zip codes were missing UVB light. Lack of UVB light is what drives heart disease not the presence of LDL cholesterol. When you ascribe causation to biochemical changes or food and not to light you make catastrophic errors in science.

### smith5 — 2017-04-21T12:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-212687>

Just a few misunderstandings I've found:
1) as JK mentioned in several podcasts and FB, positive charge=inflammation. That was meant as protonic positive charge most probably, but would be interesting to see opinions on the more or less established framework of Na+/K+ currents as main information (neuron action potential) carriers in the nervous system.
Because by the token of "positive charge=inflammation" it turns out that any neuronal action potential carried out by Na+/K+ currents equals inflammation.
2) it seemed that in several podcasts JK unintentionally misinterpreted electron transport for electron quantum tunneling in which an electron does not require a carrier by definition (just an unsystematic observation).

### Jake Tassell — 2017-11-20T14:48:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-228482>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=212689)
>
> [http://www.life.illinois.edu/govindjee/biochem494/foerster.htm](http://www.life.illinois.edu/govindjee/biochem494/foerster.htm)
>
> **Excitation Energy Transfer and Energy Migration : Some Basics and Background**

Oh yeah, Heinz Von Foerster....

It's a shame a lot of what you can find out about that guy is internet gossip/apocrypha...

"Actually I'm interested in a detailed wartime history of the Nazi
microwave scientist, Heinz Von Foerster. He seems to have no history
prior to his arrival in the United States in 1949 presumably as a part
of Operation Paperclip. This might be an over statement for we find
in reference \[4\] below that he wrote a paper in 1943 about the
klystron while in Germany. We also find in reference \[6\] the
following quote, "Friedrich Kittler had asked me to inquire into the
research Heinz had done in Berlin, which seems to have been of some
considerable importance for the war. But Heinz only told me how he had
managed to get out of Berlin in the very last days by offering to take
the valuable instruments and important documents of the laboratory to
the safety of Vienna." In any case an American engineer for Raytheon
claims to have discovered the microwave oven in 1946 when a candy bar
melted in his pocket. See reference \[1\]. This despite some websites
\[2\] which claim the microwave oven was being developed by the Nazi's
for food preparation on the Eastern Front.

Returning to the subject of Heinz Von Foerster, the above listed
references are all I have found concerning his war time activities.
Was this record expunged as a part of Paperclip? Was he ever involved
in human experimentation? His later biography is suggestive. Upon
coming to America he heads microwave research at the University of
Illinois, Urbana \[5\] and participates in the Josiah Macy Conferences
on Cybernetics \[11\]. We should note that these conferences were a
conduit for CIA MKULTRA funding \[10\]. In 1958 he founds and heads the
Biological Computer Laboratory at the University of Illinois, Urbana.
A university which has been designated an NSA Center for Academic
Excellence \[13\]. A lot of the work done at the BCL was for the
military \[9\], "on the technological realization of computational
mechanisms observed in living organisms, the theory and application of
computational principles, the mechanization of cognitive processes,
direct access intelligence systems and information processing." It
looks like Heinz did some work modeling the basilar membrane of the
inner ear. Was this to study the effects of microwaves on the basilar
membrane. He became the darling of the American Society for
Cybernetics (ASC) which was and offshoot of the Macy Conferences \[11\].
An interesting organization on nobody's radar - check out the list of
ASC cyberneticians \[11\] and explore their curriculum vitae.

References:
Interesting Links

\[1\] The apocryphal story of the Microwave Oven, and note the year of
invention.
[http://www.gallawa.com/microtech/history.html](http://www.gallawa.com/microtech/history.html)

\[2\] Was it really a Nazi Invention to cook food on the Eastern Front?
[http://www.fourwinds10.com/news/06-...-01-28-03-effect-of-microwave-appliances.html](http://www.fourwinds10.com/news/06-health/C-harmful-products/2003/06C-01-28-03-effect-of-microwave-appliances.html)
[http://www.divinehealth.com/effects\_of\_microwave\_appliances\_.htm](http://www.divinehealth.com/effects_of_microwave_appliances_.htm)

\[3\] Also of interest here are the Nazi Hypothermia experiments. Note
the use of "sun lamps."
[http://www.remember.org/educate/medexp.html](http://www.remember.org/educate/medexp.html)

Heinz Von Foerster, Nazi microwave scientist, darling of the American
Society for Cybernetics (an outgrowth of the Josiah Macy Conferences
which he attended) and founder of the Biological Computer Laboratory
at the University of Illinois Urbana in 1958 prior to which he had
been in charge of microwave research. Note he has no detailed
biography prior to 1949 when he came to the United States presumably
as part of Project Paperclip. Co-authored papers with Margaret Mead,
William Ross Ashby and others
\[4\] [http://www.univie.ac.at/constructivism/HvF/bib.htm](http://www.univie.ac.at/constructivism/HvF/bib.htm)
\[5\] [http://www.oikos.org/foerster.htm](http://www.oikos.org/foerster.htm)
\[6\] [http://www.univie.ac.at/constructivism/HvF/festschrift/baecker.html](http://www.univie.ac.at/constructivism/HvF/festschrift/baecker.html)
\[7\] [http://www.isss.org/lumhvf.htm](http://www.isss.org/lumhvf.htm)
\[8\] [http://www.asc-cybernetics.org/2003/index.htm](http://www.asc-cybernetics.org/2003/index.htm)

The Biological Computer Laboratory at Urbana, papers.
\[9\] [http://web.library.uiuc.edu/ahx/asc/bcl.html](http://web.library.uiuc.edu/ahx/asc/bcl.html)

\[10\] Josiah Macy Foundation, conduit for MKULTRA funding.
[http://www.frankolsonproject.org/Student papers/Ransom/Ransom8.html](http://www.frankolsonproject.org/Student%20papers/Ransom/Ransom8.html)

\[11\] About the American Society for Cybernetics (ASC) which evolved
from the Macy conferences.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-11-20T16:24:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stupid-questions-thread.13517/post-228488>

Who is really behind your door? When you watch this film you should keep this question in mind: Is there any evidence that technology has reached such a level of perfection, the bureaucracy such a level of integrity that societies should defer without questioning them? The purpose of bureaucracy is to compensate for incompetence and lack of discipline–a problem that largely goes away if you have the right people in the first place in your dopamine network. The 5 people who spend the most time with are the keys to wellness creation. It is an inconvenient reality for most. So who you choose to spend time with this Thursday is a big tell for most of us. In your family or in the socio-economic structure of today society, it is a man made bureaucracy you helped build; people become invested in and identified with their past ideas, associations, beliefs, and current possessions. We're addicted to consumption because of how we've become programmed. That programming is to keep us from nature at all costs. To adapt to the moving target of natural truth as it is being discovered requires extraordinary mental, emotional, and existential flexibility today, that quite frankly, most people don't have. To change the world, people with optimized dopamine levels created by nature are the people you should seek on your team or in your network. Walk as if you are kissing the Earth with your feet, and kissing the sun with your eyes as you holds hands with those who you are grateful for. In my life, the people I am grateful for I interact with offline in real life. The internet is a place I come to find people who are searching for natural truths and not the manufactured ones of man. That is the constant challenge of innovators and renegades and why they are so hard to get along with at times. If a person is to remain true to their mission, I predict that many members of your network will come and go, as they have in your life, and has occurred in my website. Not everyone is capable to get to optimal, but even the worse among us want to peek of that yellow brick road. It is what it is and it ain't what it ain't. As I have said before, this life is a circus, and we will see who is left standing at the end.…....….This week in the USA we'll be celebrating gratitude. What are you grateful for right now before you sit down with your key 5? For me, I am grateful that I woke up 15 years ago to see the wizard behind the curtain in my own life. When I realized how technology was being used to control populations and lead to many of the things we all are trying to mito-hack away with N=1 knowledge it was my day of enlightenment. Our lives will continue to explode in many ways we don't see that lurk in the shadows of our senses if I continue with the escapism that technology grants us, that was built by governments/military/corporations we all supported...............

“There will come a time when it isn't 'They're spying on me through my phone' anymore. Eventually, it will be 'My phone is spying on me'.”
― Philip K. Dick

and despite several interesting conjectures presented in the article I'm still approaching the 46% level of confidence that quantum tunnelling has been exploited to "just fill in the gaps", specifically regarding electron tunneling microscopy a microscoped object (e.g. an insect) should still have or be coated by a dielectric (in a conventional sense) surface or "voltage bias" to get electrons tunneled from a scanning tip, thus making quantum tunneling a form of electricity. 2) ~"Light slows down in matter thus forming matter which emerges as light slows down in it". So light slows down in matter (~permittivity/permeability) which already

Im currently buying frozen NZ mussels instead.

Thanks all!
Tony.

Water cycle is an open system and totally critical to life's quantized ecosystem. The best thing to do is allow nature to restructure it for you....oysters are one way nature does it. ...........Now consider how it is commonly done..............Water needs to boil......so as it goes to the water table deep into the earth it gets heated to a boil and then as the magma chamber (where magnetic effects come from) the gas rises and condenses in ground water. You then can see this water rise to the surface on the top of mountains and it freezes.......it is perfectly restructured to carry info and energy.........for animals to use it. This is how the water cycle remains opened.........Humans can access it. I just spoke in LA to a group about QED effects of water. Luke Storey (find him on my twitter feed @LukeStorey School of Style, to back the story up) told the group I spoke to about his water gathering ritual in LA mountains. I was so impressed by the lengths he went to get high quality water. And his actions were 100% scientific and quantized. Luke has no clue about why he did but he is drawn to do it.......Why? He lives in an EMF cesspool called LA.

# Thread: Manganese
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/manganese.13527/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 34
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-28T15:46:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/manganese.13527/post-159621>

What's the story with manganese? I've come across references to it here and there in different postss and blogs, but generally speaking don't know anything about it.

I ask because we live rurally and have well water, and when we had it tested many years ago it had several kinds of bacteria including e. coli so we had a filtration system with UV light and sediment filter installed. The water company who came out to install it commented on the thick layers of orangish goo that lines our faucets and leaves rings around the drains and causes stickiness aroudn our filter and bulb at changing time, and they said they were pretty sure it is manganese and he gave us the impression we should not be ingesting it. Which is an issue because the sediment filter doesn't remove it and the UV bulb doesn't affect it either of course. This being the case we've been buying cases and cases of spring water for over a decade and use it for everything from making coffee to drinking. (I do brush my teeth in sink water and also use it for cooking though, but my husband doesn't even do that. He uses bottled spring water to boil spaghetti and eveything.)

So.. .manganese... is it bad or not? Should I start drinking the faucet water, or stop using it for even brushing teeth, or what?

Thanks!

### Hope2Learn — 2015-03-28T16:13:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/manganese.13527/post-159625>

Ew I think we have this - [http://www.garreltswater.com/water-quality/iron-bacteria/](http://www.garreltswater.com/water-quality/iron-bacteria/) - anyone dealt with this before? It suggests our issue probably comes from "The most common bacteria known to feed on iron \[,which\] are [_Thiobacillus ferrooxidans_](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thiobacillus_ferrooxidans) and [_Leptospirillum ferrooxidans_](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leptospirillum_ferrooxidans)."

"Both of these bacteria are used in a mining technique called [bioleaching](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bioleaching) whereby metals are extracted from their ores through [oxidation](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxidation). The bacteria are used as [catalysts](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catalyst)."

Hmmmmmmm so my sink water is putting these bacteria into my body, and these bacteria are oxidizers... I do not know what this means but I find it interesting so far...

edited to add: wikipedia says oxidation means loss of electons. Ohno!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-28T16:22:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/manganese.13527/post-159629>

its a transition metal in mitochondria........and it is important in overall design and not individual atomic structure. It is part of how free radicals are made. Those that live in magnetic field above non native range (50 microtesla) will lose superoxide and H2O2 and make a lot of hydroxyl free radical and your micro biome will shrink and you will lose body comp as you get obese............because your mitochondria swells and you no longer can tunnel or entangle electrons and as a result you age faster.

### kimeka — 2015-03-29T04:02:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi.13529/post-159697>

My husband noticed that my depression is at its worst during the winter months. I'm not open to trying things or listening to people. During the spring and summer, I'm quite optimistic and sometimes surprise him on wanting to try new things. I can't say I totally give up. I'd say a small percentage of me does. I've come a long way since December 2014. I've lost close to 80 lbs by avoiding grains most of the time. I'll admit I've occasionally eaten something made from wheat. I have a hard time getting away from soy. It really bothers me when I think I've found something free of it and its really in there by another name. I also don't have knee and feet pain anymore. My husband likes to remind me on the rough days that I've come a long way. I haven't gotten to the finish line yet but I'm getting there albeit slowly but slow is better than too fast.
My hard days consist of having heartburn so bad my chest hurts that I start to have an anxiety attack. When these started a year and half ago, I would go to the ER to have my heart checked. Only to find that my BP was up and my heart was fine. I'd be sent home and told to take prilosec. I used it only once and never again.

Micronutrient Test

Cortisol is a part of Steroid Hormone Panel.

pregnenolone
progesterone (low may be the reason for your headaches)
Cortisol (8AM, 12PM)
DHEAs
Estradiol

1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day

heartburn----> HPylori must be excluded
usually it is sign of low gastric acid

# Thread: Gilbert Ling's Association Induction Hypothesis PDF Version
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gilbert-lings-association-induction-hypothesis-pdf-version.13530/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2015-03-29T05:27:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gilbert-lings-association-induction-hypothesis-pdf-version.13530/post-159699>

Attached is a PDF Version of my research on the Association Induction Hypothesis in collaboration with Gilbert Ling.

I would further recommend people purchasing Gilbert's book [What is Life Answered?](http://www.amazon.com/properties-activities-microscopic-assemblies-nano-protoplasm/dp/061594793X/ref=la_B001K8UKCY_1_2?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1427595589&sr=1-2) from Amazon, it is small at 120 pages and worth the effort.

A documentary is being made on various people such as Gilbert Ling, Mae-Wan Ho, Gerald Pollack , Ray Peat.

See [here](http://www.perceivethinkact.com/blog/philadelphia-june-19th-2014) some pictures and video on Gilbert

### Christos — 2015-03-29T08:49:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gilbert-lings-association-induction-hypothesis-pdf-version.13530/post-159711>

Awesome read, thanks for posting... his 2001 monologue was awesome, will have to read his newest book, keep us updated on the doco

### PaulG — 2015-03-29T10:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gilbert-lings-association-induction-hypothesis-pdf-version.13530/post-159712>

Thanks for reading Christos - its not the easiest of topics.

Attached is one of my favorite Ling paper entitle **Can we see Living Structure in a Cell?**

I like it for two reasons, 1) it seems clearer and easier to understand than many of his papers and 2) At the end of the paper he addresses objections from other scientists such as Dubochet, Edelmann, Clegg, Roomans and is the only time I have found 'serious' discussion of his work and I find it pretty interesting reading.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-29T23:15:29+0300

Mankind has been poised and on the threshold of revealing the ultimate structure of matter, but they have fumbled the ball badly. Understanding the smallest scales of the universe, being able to partially see the beginning of time, and documenting every gene that encodes a human being. Yet, we know little about what has made all these achievements possible: LIFE. This lopsided understanding of Nature is not accidental; it has been done by willful neglect, in my opinion. The path to knowledge has always been smoother in exploring the unexplored, than in rethinking the familiar. It is time to sink to the quantum scale where atoms get together and do things with the power of light, water, and magnetism to create all living things. EE4........read it

I'm new the "paleo/epi-paleo" community and to this site so am not fully familiar with many of the terms or concepts yet so please bear with me. I'm in the process of reading this blog basically from the beginning using the posted study guides (am just starting the CT series now) but I wanted to get some clarification on a few basic concepts so I can begin putting them into action.

Basically, I have some confusion about aligning our diets with the seasons--our circadian biology. I have been seeing a lot of members talk about the PPP and fat oxidation as being preferred over our normal 36 ATP oxidation. I also just read here that cold adaptation is the key to entering the PPP. However, in the summer it is not cold. Does this mean that during long light cycles it is optimal to stop eating a ketogenic diet and exit the PPP in order to undergo more or less "normal" metabolism, with the added power of the photoelectric effect "making up the difference" so to speak (i.e. is one pathway absolutely better than the other or is it only so in the environmental context we find ourself in?), or is the diet still basically a ketogenic one, only with more vegetables added--remaining in the PPP/ketosis? I understand we're not looking at food as macronutrients, but for ease of putting this into practice are there some basic guidelines to be mindful of to remain optimal during the long light cycles?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-03-29T17:10:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mastering-the-basics-pathways-and-seasons.13531/post-159739>

The biologically mediated movement of carbon and nitrogen through our cells depends wholly, on the physiological properties of plants, animals, bacteria. These act based upon light, water, and soil determinants of the environment they are found in. All our coupled to one another. Notice you did not hear the word food in this story. You heard light...........first. Ubiquitin is a story of light. It is the first coupled cycle that all of biology organizes around. This is why it is ubiquitous in plants and animals.

You need to realize that light at the wrong time and wrong frequency uncouples your biochemistry........this is why you're confused now at the beginning of your journey. Modern life uncouples you to this reality. You are just beginning and many here have gotten that message. If you want to read one blog to get it quick......read ubiquination 4 then read my book........this will make your reading of all the blogs in order like hologram (the quilt document is the founding corner stone of this story of humans.........as you add pieces the 30,000 ft view becomes clear. The individual pieces are the blogs entries

Fake light exposure anytime, especially at night time, increases ubquitin rates in plants and animals. This is why ammonia shows up at higher levels in uncoupled states and diseases. This is why the Warburg effect exists. It is a beacon of an uncoupled cycle, not a marker for a fuel source. Nitrogen cycling is designed to be stable by evolution in plants and animals and when it is not you know immediately it is tied to an altered light cycle. High glucose and ammonia production in cancer cells metabolism is a greater marker for a bad light environment. Glucose is fully capable of braking ubiquitin when ubiquitin is coupled to the cell cycle, but not when it is uncoupled and isolated. When it is isolated glucose levels go through the roof to stop the PER 1 and PER 2 genes from turning over proteins in cells. The Warburg metabolism is not what we think it is today; it is a circadian mismatch signal looking for a brake pad that no longer can work when ubiquitin is uncoupled by blue light and chronic artificial night time light. Getting into the PPP is tied to this anomaly............to stop the process while you remediate your environment. In that sense it is like a natural chemotherapy for bad thinking and choices

I have to ask this question because it's been burning in my mind the last couple of days: So, this is a story of light, as the Ubiquination series is indicating. However I'm reading in the CT series that being cold-adapted uncouples the SCN from using light to entrain things. CT is advocated at all times of the year here, not just to be in the "Ancient Pathway" but to increase mitochondrial efficiency and mitigate EMF effects. This seems contradictory to me--based on my current understanding--for the reasons that a) if CT is done year-round and we are cold-adapted year round, then how are artificial lights screwing up our internal clocks? CT tells our body it is winter time--why, then are people eating any carbs--electrons carrying information telling our body it isn't winter (vegetables,fruits) at all, even in long light cycles? What is it I'm missing here?

Thanks guys

### NeilBB — 2015-04-12T23:27:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mastering-the-basics-pathways-and-seasons.13531/post-161021>

> [AJC said:](/goto/post?id=161016)
>
> I have to ask this question because it's been burning in my mind the last couple of days: So, this is a story of light, as the Ubiquination series is indicating. However I'm reading in the CT series that being cold-adapted uncouples the SCN from using light to entrain things. CT is advocated at all times of the year here, not just to be in the "Ancient Pathway" but to increase mitochondrial efficiency and mitigate EMF effects. This seems contradictory to me--based on my current understanding--for the reasons that a) if CT is done year-round and we are cold-adapted year round, then how are artificial lights screwing up our internal clocks? CT tells our body it is winter time--why, then are people eating any carbs--electrons carrying information telling our body it isn't winter (vegetables,fruits) at all, even in long light cycles? What is it I'm missing here?
>
> Thanks guys

My take on it is this: Life is complex. Doing CT in long-light cycles can be a mild chronobiological mismatch, but a major thermodynamic advantage in the modern overenergized world. Unless you are literally living in the ice bath, you will likely not be "fully cold-adapted" in the summer--your body will still appropriately sense the light-based circadian signals. Also remember that the body expects colder temperatures and ketotic-fasting every night anyhow, even in summertime. Nighttime is always cold, dark, and ketotic, a microcosm of winter--or should be at least. This also suggests that the "most ideal" time to do CT in summer is probably in the evenings, which Jack usually does himself if you listen to him closely. But even doing CT in the sunshine in summer can yield major thermodynamic advantages at the cost of a mild chronobiological mismatch. Also, if you happen to be obese or inflammed, you probably should be doing even more daytime CT and not eating so many veggies/fruits, even in summertime, in order to better compensate for your longstanding "winter deficiency" and to reverse your chronic disease. Everything is relative--there are no absolutes. This is why people need to deeply understand principles, monitor personal responses, and then actively titrate the interventions to their individual situations...

You're only a plant in summer when you can use light to drive higher carbon carbon cycling and nitrogen cycling your body to drive growth..........light controls nitrogen and carbon use in all living things. How they change is 100% tied to the environments signals.......light being the biggie. ### AJC — 2015-04-13T02:02:21+0300

Thanks for the insights everyone. Must say--this is by far the most supportive online community I've been a part of, very encouraging to be receiving feedback of this sort. I was starting to think the Leptin Rx was mostly obsolete if CT entrains the SCN to temp and not light. Neil--that insight is appreciated. I was looking at it like you're either cold adapted or not--no continuum. I suppose it's not simply one or the other though. I know what you mean about "winter deficiency" and the healing that is brough about by getting your fix. I too have been thinking as the Cold Pathway as the microcosm of nighttime, only thing is--we don't sleep all the time. Yet CT is beneficial year round (assuming microwaved environment) and carbs are being eaten seasonally. I still don't quite understand that. I'm trying not to get bogged down in the minutia of the details, but instead looking to understand principles as you said. The principle I am struggling with here is how it is harmonious with our circadian biology for our skin receptors to be telling us it's cold in the spring/summer when our taste receptors, ingested electrons, and SCN are telling us its warm? Am I thinking in too black-and-white of terms here and/or am I missing the part where CT is basically good year around in our microwaved world because of its beneficial effects on our mitochondria (e.g. magnetism)?

Jack--to go off your post. I'm assuming that one of the effects of living in our EMFd world is that since blue light is destroying DHA in the retina and thus our ability to yoke ourselves to the light cycles and adequately use the photoelectric effect that CT is helpful in pretty much all cases, even closer to the equator. To take this from the general to specific: you live in New Orleans and I will be moving to Phoenix. Both places have warmer temps/more sunlight most of the year. Yet, you do CT in the summer and eat vegetables? To offset the hazards of your day job like I will have to do being a student in school. And this is not a biological mismatch akin to doing VLC in a warm-adapted environment?

To take this from the general to specific: you live in New Orleans and I will be moving to Phoenix. Both places have warmer temps/more sunlight most of the year. Yet, you do CT in the summer and eat vegetables? To offset the hazards of your day job like I will have to do being a student in school. And this is not a biological mismatch akin to doing VLC in a warm-adapted environment?

The short answer regarding the Leptin Rx and the high protein load at breakfast: Light entrains the Central Nervous System (CNS) to circadian cycles, while food entrains the peripheral tissues (anything not the CNS).

Food stimulation on the Leptin Rx is done through the vagus nerve, hence why gastric bypass patients don't benefit from the Leptin Rx (they lack the connection from the vagus nerve to the brain).

Long answer is on the blog  -- [http://www.jackkruse.com/how-does-the-leptin-rx-work/](http://www.jackkruse.com/how-does-the-leptin-rx-work/) , including why CT (direct stimulation of TRH) is useful with the Leptin Rx -- [http://www.jackkruse.com/rewiring-the-leptin-rx-reset/](http://www.jackkruse.com/rewiring-the-leptin-rx-reset/)

\----

CT always condenses matter, improves electron density of cell water, and improves energetic efficiency of surface. It makes a better battery so to speak, and this is irrespective of a warm external environment.

ALL the body's cells are semiconductors (read Robert Becker's 'The Body Electric' for experimental proof), and all of them operate at physical distances close enough for Quantum Mechanical effects to take place (superconducting charge-separated water in spaces <10-100nm next to hydrophilic proteins -- see Mae-Wan Ho's 'Living Water H2O' for experimental proof).

All these semiconductors work on a Quantum Hall Current that is enhanced by larger magnetic fields and lower temperatures. The collagen and water network of the body allows for local efficiencies to become global efficiencies, so long as these parts are connected. ie: Cooling of the skin on your arm can increase energy delivery to the muscles of your leg, but maybe not as much to your brain (CNS vs peripheral tissues).

CT may not make your battery much larger at all, if you live in a high light environment, and take steps to ensure that your body can use that light (sun exposure, grounding, DHA, sleep at the right time, etc ...). It's still a benefit, but it is a marginal benefit if you're already living optimally in a tropical environment.

CT is still great for recovery. eg: if you go out for a night of partying in Hawaii, you bet that soaking in a cold tub is going to help you there. (and alternate between the cold tub and a hot spring for even better effect)

Cold entrainment of the SCN is just one signalling factor:

The amount of light entering the eye (and SCN), and the angle of that light, are very important factors in telling the body that "winter is here".

Another major factor is the increase in the magnetic field of the Earth during winter time, which the SCN can definitely sense by way of melanopsin, rhodopsin, and some FAD-based flavins as well. Note that all cell membranes are magnetoelastic, meaning that incoming magnetic fields from the earth can be transformed into electricity and/or light for use in signalling.​

.....

The Leptin Rx is based upon light. And you need to make this link: light and temp are link in photosynthesis too.........why? In cold photosynthetic efficiency drops. And vice versa. Light links directly to nitrogen.......and this is what controls the carbon cycle in plants and in us.........in us it is way more complex the mechanics are exactly the same in living things. When you go to PHx you have two things to worry about.......EMF and lack of water........a very serious lack of water coming in a deep way. Plants in NOLA are quite robust because we have light and water. This is reflected in leaf structure.........leafs tie to photosynthetic capacity........In the desert leaves are not a big part of the equation.
