### Sue-UK — 2015-08-02T11:12:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-173779>

Update on the overnight grounding. Sleep really good, I didn't think it would make the difference it has. But I get my skin cool with a shower, make sure its really dark and have the grounding band around my foot. The most obvious thing I can see is the change to my solar panels, just wish the weather was better to use them ... Did get one morning this week, the ground was sodden from the previous week's bad weather so after a short CT session and a nicotine gum, I used a fitball. Just sat on it to start with reading a blog, but finished up resting my back on it and holding myself steady with my legs, then when the ground had dried enough I flopped over it with my lower legs and hands grounding, with the printed blog in front of me.

I found myself moving around and using my muscles, but it was gentle and seemed natural, and I could easily get the sun at the same time. So I'm adding a Fitball Sunbathe to my toolkit.

### Sue-UK — 2015-08-04T16:07:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-173909>

Ubi 21 ....... Is ACV a good summer bio hack because it is associated with indole? Does it somehow become like an auxin? (3-indole acetic acid)?

### Sue-UK — 2015-09-05T10:57:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-176532>

So September has rolled round, my FIL died at the end of August, and my mother is now in the system for Alzheimer's. One family relationship has broken down completely (don't stand in the doorway ) Coldest wettest August I can remember, the early morning CT bath seemed to hover around 59 degrees, dropping to around 55 as September arrived. I feel like I was treading water in August, some days I thought I was sinking, but I let CT take the steam coming off me ....Just hopped in the CT bath when I could, even in the rain, and got what light I could. Sleep generally was pretty good. But without the summer sunshine, I think I've got to up my game now . Ubi 24 reflects why I intuitively prefer outside AM CT, so I'm back to my winter schedule, but stretching out on top of the water if there's a break in the clouds.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-05T18:16:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-176545>

keep the good fight up

### Sue-UK — 2015-09-06T19:29:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-176610>

Beautiful September day, blue skies, a very gentle breeze. Going on holiday early next month and was supposed to be going shopping today for a few new clothes. Said forget it, the sun is more important today ... so they went off on their own and I just sunbathed all morning instead, after an hour's CT at 7.30 a.m. (50F). Sat outside and ate tinned mackerel. Re read Ubi 23 and 24.

DH popped in to the car accessory shop and picked me up a couple of chamois leathers, great for cooling the carotids ...  Adding to my evening protocol.

I think my skin is changing.....its generally cooler, but when sunbathing the section that faces the sun feels warm but not excessively so, but as soon as I turn over or round, the same surface becomes very cool, very quickly.

### Sue-UK — 2015-09-30T15:50:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-178267>

I went to a seminar on Saturday about wifi, they banned mobile phones, no artificial lights etc. Lot of ill people there. I had a slight problem with my vision in my right eye when I got there, but it got gradually worse as the day went on. I had a three quarters eclipse ..Left eye was fine. It is the same eye I had a retinal tear in a few years ago. But one of the speakers at the seminar was talking about microwaves coking from the inside out, and my feeling was that my optic nerve was inflamed, got DH to search optic nerve neuritis, which gave me a breather from being dragged off by him straight away and having strong lights shone into my eyes. If I could see some improvement by the morning he would give me another 24 hours .....Step 1, CT. Early night. Sunday morning 4 hours mix of CT and AM sunshine, used a cold wet chamois to cool my eyelids, and then faced the sun with my eyes shut. every so often I would hop out of the tub and get the sun on my cold skin. Enough improvement to buy me another 24 hours. good night sleep Sunday, more improvement Monday morning. Bad night sleep Monday (family meeting about my mum's Alzheimer's did not go well) but still some improvement. I can now see the light coming through the windows, I can see the grass, I could see the dog walking towards me across the grass. the eclipse is shrinking and I'm getting more light coming thru what is left. Main treatment CT, AM light, grounding, seafood, water. But it's working, big time

WIfi router also coming out shortly, no longer just on a timer, going to cabled. DH realises I'm as strong as my weakest link, and this is my second scare ......Anyway, time up on computer ...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-09-30T16:18:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-178271>

The UK is among the lowest quantum yield places on the planet to live.............and I would evacuate it if I lived there no matter what.

### Sue-UK — 2015-10-11T20:40:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-179107>

Well I managed to evacuate for a week .....Good points, Island of Menorca, accommodation right by a small beach, stayed out in the light for most of the day, AM sunlight by the bucket, a bit of shade midday and then usually headed off to a more distant beach where nude sunbathing was allowed, and stayed til early evening. Lots of water, seafood. The only real downside was the artificial lights at the resort, flimsy curtains, and shutters that let light through. We were standing chairs in front of the floorstanding light outside, hanging towels up etc, but it wasn't ideal. No TV, phone switched on twice a day to check for messages from the kids but otherwise off, same for getting emails. In and out of the sea, amber glasses on as soon as it got dark, and then I usually went to bed really early. I bought a pair of reading glasses at the airport on the way so that I could read a menu while we were away, but that was the only time I used them, I took some blogs to read and Lyall Watson's book "Jacobsons Organ" and used a large magnifying glass so that my eyes were always in the light. If there wasn't enough natural light to read, I didn't read.

DH went off and did his own thing in the mornings, I was forever watching my feet climbing up the coastal paths, my vision was changing daily, and I wanted to concentrate on getting the right frequencies on my eyes. DH read ubi 24 and 25...and because my eye was improving while we were away, he is totally on board for another week in the sun in 6 weeks time, and then 6 weeks after that ..... Purely medicinal .....Back to low light levels and trees changing colour - trying to work out the best way of moving forward til I can get away again.

As for the family's views on my Mums situation..... lets just say that someone else doing the caring for a week and my next 2 holiday plans has led to a change of tune .......

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-11T21:26:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-179118>

smart to move closer to the sun........even on holiday

### Sue-UK — 2015-10-12T16:05:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-179210>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=178271)
>
> The UK is among the lowest quantum yield places on the planet to live.............and I would evacuate it if I lived there no matter what.

So I am thinking that temporarily I might either have to increase the quantum yield artificially, or amplify the natural quantum signal ...... In Jim Al-Khalili's book Life on the Edge, in the chapter on smell I think he says that we have lost the auxiliary olfactory sense found in other animals i.e. Jacobson's organ. Lyall Watson's book "Jacobson's organ and the remarkable nature of smell" disagrees ..... So I'm wondering, thinking of light as information, is the Jacobson's organ tissue more light sensitive than other nasal surface tissue, is it a more primitive put your nose out of the burrow and sniff the light and non odorous molecules using the accessory bulbs of the brain kind of thing that can be a useful back door amplifier.....? I bought some nasal spray nicotine a couple of months ago, and because of the Jacobson's organ issue I just kept looking at it wondering, was it a good idea to use it instead of the gum .....

### alyson thiessen — 2015-10-12T16:38:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-179219>

Highly interesting!!!

### Sue-UK — 2015-10-15T15:40:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-179550>

The sight in my right eye is gradually returning, but its more difficult to see than when I had no vision in it at all. If I close my left eye, through the right its like looking through a smeared amber glass , through a tight mesh cobweb, (which vibrates in the early morning light) and if for example I am looking at a sunlit window frame, the right eye is dropping the image of the window frame down on the left hand side. I am getting light through, and I can see colours. It started with seeing greens, then reds, and now on a bright clear day what was a turquoise sky now looks blue. The cobweb is clearing a bit from the middle. Its easier to see with just my left eye, but I'm working them as a pair, its a bit more tiring having the second slightly tilted amber image as well, but its the best way of getting the right frequencies, and I'm hoping it is encouraging repair of the camera .... That's what it feels like anyway.

Poor light this morning, so I hope that a bit of added nostril flaring and sniffing the air is doing something ....

Now that I'm back the same old crap about my Mum's care slowly resurfaced, so by Tuesday DH had booked flights for the end of November to Fuerteventura. He ordered a map of the island on Monday, and new flight bags for hand luggage only. There's lots of accommodation available for the week we are there, so we are taking our time in finding that - the remoter and simpler the better.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-15T16:05:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-179558>

I think we lost it because we went from trees to the water to become human...........but I think the more we stray from it the more we can increase it........if we collect purple light to do it. Light alone makes matter as you will soon find out.

### Sue-UK — 2015-10-26T15:46:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180481>

After a row with my brother, (who was in the UK for a couple of weeks) I finally got the support I needed to move my Mum into residential care. When Mum had mental capacity her decision was that residential care was always going to be the next step, but when she lost mental capacity she didn't accept that she had got that bad. I thought her behaviour said otherwise, and that she was verbalising an old pattern, (the pattern that she wasn't that bad), my brother said she still had enough mental capacity to say no to residential care. I told him I wasn't waiting to go under or lose the sight in my right eye permanently, he either supported me in getting her into residential care, or he moved back to the UK and took over her care completely.

Although she changed her mind when it came to taking her in, when the subject was first raised with her, Mum agreed to go. On the Friday she agreed, on the Monday my brother left the UK as planned, by the Tuesday she had her assessment, by Wednesday she had forgotten she had agreed (and didn't remember my brother being around for 2 weeks). She moved the next day. That night was the best sleep I've had in weeks, she was safe.  She's only been in a few days, but its interesting to see how much she was reliant on patterns .... she couldn't understand that she couldn't make tea in her own room, so was trying to make it anyway ..... with toothpaste ... So its been watching old patterns dissolve and trying to establish new ones ....

My eye was slowly getting better, the image of the window frame through my right eye is now not dropping down on the left hand side, its straight. But when I come out of the nursing home my eye always feels a bit swollen and sore. I used cold compresses to start, but I've got hold of an eye bath and have also been using that as an eye dunk. Last couple of nights I've used the bioptron with the red filter over closed eye lids, last thing before I go to sleep. Better dreaming.

The weather has changed, the clocks went back on Saturday night so early morning CT is better, its still cold but there's more light - I'm getting into winter battery mode.  What happened with my eye was my own stupid fault - but with my Mum now sorted I think I can turn this round.

### Sue-UK — 2015-10-27T17:46:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180563>

Went out to the CT tub as soon as I got up this morning, stopping to grab a jug of water on the way. AM light on eyes, CT and a bit of light harvesting, 40 minutes .... Body surfaces got nice and cold, so I went back in and used the IR and red lamp, and the UV facial tanner together. Was toying with an idea and decided to go for it ..... I split open a omega 3 capsule (EPA 410 mg, DHA 315mg,) and spread a thin layer of the oil on my wrists and hands, and used what was left on my belly. Stood in front of the facial tanner and the IR and red lamp, and let the light hit the cold, oiled surfaces, 10 minutes. I felt I was harvesting light more efficiently but that might have been my imagination, or placebo .....

I've also cut a small square of chamois leather to fit round the eye bath, I just slightly dampen it to start and put it round the eyebath which has an cube in it. Use that to cool down the surface of the eyelid, as the cube melts it cools the chamois, but the chamois is thick enough to protect the eyelid from ice burn, and as the cube melts the chamois stops the water dripping all down my face. I pat it around the eye socket as well. Much easier than trying to make a waterproof seal around the eye using the eyebath with liquid. Easy to take regular breaks in the day to pop and get a fresh ice cube.

### Lahelada — 2015-10-27T17:58:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180564>

Hiya Sue,
We are thinking along the same lines. I am using red palm oil. My musings come from an anecdote from the Kontiki expedition,where they used salami fat to protect them from the sun. Salami,sulfates,fat grease d3...are my musings.
I used butter before but added red palm oil well because its red.
Err was going to dabble with fishfat but was a little worried about odors. How was your experience with the capsules odour wise?

### nonchalant — 2015-10-27T20:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180577>

Red clay has iron oxide in it, right? It would have to be a thin coating, though, to let in the sunshine.

### Sue-UK — 2015-10-28T14:13:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180609>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=180564)
>
> Hiya Sue,
> We are thinking along the same lines. I am using red palm oil. My musings come from an anecdote from the Kontiki expedition,where they used salami fat to protect them from the sun. Salami,sulfates,fat grease d3...are my musings.
> I used butter before but added red palm oil well because its red.
> Err was going to dabble with fishfat but was a little worried about odors. How was your experience with the capsules odour wise?

Thinking some more about this .....Maybe I'm not drawn to red palm oil because it isn't native to the UK, and I don't want protection from the sun, I want to amplify what little we are getting .... Yesterday I cut the capsule into an egg cup to use it, and left the eggcup until this morning .... still no fishy or yukky smell. Is the DHA component of fish non odourous.....? Perhaps with UV its more pheremonal than olfactory ....

Repeated yesterday this morning, and then sat outside in the sun and used the nicotine nasal spray, after a bit of eyebath CT. Interesting ..... I put it up my left nostril first, sneezed and then my left eye watered a lot, but my right eye stayed dry. So I put it up my right nostril, sneezed and then my right eye started streaming as well. I just let my eyes stream in the sunshine, sniffing in the sunshine as well and then tested my vision. Sky definitely bluer in my right eye, and better colour vision with both eyes at the same time, but its possibly only a temporary improvement because my right eye is generally still drier than my left, so not maintaining the charge?? .... Not sure yet how often to use the nicotine spray ...

### Lahelada — 2015-10-28T16:38:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180620>

We are actually thinking the same things but saying it differently. Studies say it protects against UV. I venture that it makes the skin more receptive to UV, facilitates absorption and storage. If I am wrong I still get supple skin in the process. I still use an Epsom salt water Ritz though for the sulphur part of the equation.
Palms are growing here so no mismatch.

### Lahelada — 2015-10-28T16:55:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180621>

I explained badly. My view is that the studies that show red palm oil as UV protective are working from the premise that UV needs to be avoided . Turning this around /reading from left to right, seeing UV as good it may be preparing the skin surface for optimal UV absorption instead.

### Sue-UK — 2015-10-30T11:49:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180768>

Early morning CT in the rain yesterday, nicotine spray up right nostril and then facial tanner and IR and red light ..... I've been rereading the book Better Sight Without Glasses by Harry Benjamin, (a version of the Bates Method) first published in 1929. Although the interpretation of why it works was different, it was interesting that the treatment includes sunshine, splashing the eyes with cold water, blinking a lot... . But one suggestion was to check that the muscles at the back of the neck were relaxed and no spinal defects. So I had an appointment with my massage therapist friend yesterday who did my back shoulders and neck, took out loads of knots from the left hand side, definitely inflammation in the muscles, and then she did my face. Gave me some facial pressure point stuff to continue at home.

I was thinking yesterday that I'd lost some weight, it hasn't been important since I lost the sight in my right eye, but I had bought some shorts 18 months ago without trying them on and couldn't get them up over my thighs at all. Yesterday I tried them again, too tight to wear for long but got them on and buttoned easily.

### Sue-UK — 2015-11-01T13:02:07+0200

Update - I left a bit of the oil in the cup for 24 hours, still no fishy smell. Checked again at 48 hours and by that time there was a delicate fishy smell. Not yukky but definitely there.

### Sue-UK — 2015-11-02T14:45:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180976>

There was a write up on this device [http://noctura.com/](http://noctura.com/) in the UK press .... I'm not sure what the technology strapped round my head for up to 8 hours is going to be doing .... and I'm wondering whether it not affecting sleep just means the quantity of sleep, rather than also the quality of sleep .... then there's the long term of not allowing the rods to act naturally .. lots of unknowns .... but interesting about the green light .....

Bought a vertical tanning panel .... collecting tonight. May switch out a couple of the UVs for red .... Need to research it a bit more ..... but having a week away at the end of the month, and DH has also booked a week in Marrakesh at the end of December for the light ..... My eye is getting more and more expensive ... Need to work out what to do between the 2 trips to keep up the quantum yield momentum .....

### Lahelada — 2015-11-02T14:53:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180977>

Sue ,if you are not already,think about reflective surfaces.

### Lahelada — 2015-11-02T15:05:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-180978>

### Sue-UK — 2015-11-06T16:24:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-181272>

Have used the vertical tanner for 4 days now, not going pink, a gentle "dose". I know its not ideal, but I need to do something .... Using it with an IR and red light, and after either CT or a cold shower, and I open a window wide to let in the cool air. Drinking water, and moving around in front of it, exercising, have used some hand weights, done stretching etc, because I want to, inner doctor thoughts .... I've used the goggles when facing the tubes, but taken them off when my back is to them. At the end I move much further away and then face the tubes and let the uv onto my closed eyelids, or do some rapid blinking. I've also used the nicotine nasal spray a couple of times, gets my eyes watering ...

Feel like I need a siesta after lunch .... and without it I'm more tired earlier, when the blue blockers go on in the early evening. But its a nice tired .... Used red light on my eyes and wrists still before I go to sleep. Dreaming well ..... wake feeling refreshed. Dropped to 2 meals easily, usually a small lunch and moderate meal in the early evening. Want sardines rather than mackerel ..... more like what would be my summer choice.

Read Time 1 for the first time this morning ...its come just when I need it....  going to review what I'm doing in the light of that blog ....

### Sue-UK — 2015-11-23T18:40:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-182537>

Temperature down to 5c this morning, ct water was 1.8c. Followed it with uv tanner, with red and IR light pointing at the reflectors behind the uv tubes, and the bioptron pro. Nicotine nasal spray gets the eyes watering, didn't use goggles, kept my eyes shut except for some rapid blinking, and then shielded my eyes with my hands, palms towards the tanner. Did the same yesterday, CT water at 2c. Then I go outside and ground, wearing tan through clothing. Energy levels much better, and I think my eye is improving. One thing I noticed was that I wasn't shivering after the CT until I put the UV on ....then it's not a nasty shiver, it's more like the muscles are responding to the UV rather than the cold.

I'm going to try bouncing the NIR and red light off the reflector in the dark tonight with the tanner off, to bathe my skin, and put the bioptron portable red through my wrists.

### Cpt.Tired — 2015-12-06T08:13:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-183497>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=178267)
>
> I went to a seminar on Saturday about wifi, they banned mobile phones, no artificial lights etc. Lot of ill people there. I had a slight problem with my vision in my right eye when I got there, but it got gradually worse as the day went on. I had a three quarters eclipse ..Left eye was fine. It is the same eye I had a retinal tear in a few years ago. But one of the speakers at the seminar was talking about microwaves coking from the inside out, and my feeling was that my optic nerve was inflamed, got DH to search optic nerve neuritis, which gave me a breather from being dragged off by him straight away and having strong lights shone into my eyes. If I could see some improvement by the morning he would give me another 24 hours .....Step 1, CT. Early night. Sunday morning 4 hours mix of CT and AM sunshine, used a cold wet chamois to cool my eyelids, and then faced the sun with my eyes shut. every so often I would hop out of the tub and get the sun on my cold skin. Enough improvement to buy me another 24 hours. good night sleep Sunday, more improvement Monday morning. Bad night sleep Monday (family meeting about my mum's Alzheimer's did not go well) but still some improvement. I can now see the light coming through the windows, I can see the grass, I could see the dog walking towards me across the grass. the eclipse is shrinking and I'm getting more light coming thru what is left. Main treatment CT, AM light, grounding, seafood, water. But it's working, big time
>
> WIfi router also coming out shortly, no longer just on a timer, going to cabled. DH realises I'm as strong as my weakest link, and this is my second scare ......Anyway, time up on computer ...

Good work Sue!

### Sue-UK — 2015-12-07T12:35:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-183607>

Got back from Fuerteventura on Saturday, got some sun and relaxed .... Watched the sun rise from the roof terrace of our apartment ...... we should have been in a wifi hotspot but it didn't work , we didn't report it all week and just went up to the hotel to use their wifi to let the kids know we had got there OK. (It had been accidentally switched off). Decided to have a meal at the hotel .... and it was an all you can eat buffet ..... with lots and lots of seafood ...... Talked it over with DH and we decided to eat there every evening .... I was there to help repair my eye and this was an easy way to get as much DHA as possible. So I had a seafood starter, usually 3 types of fish with some high pigment veges, and then a seafood main, again usually 3 types of seafood and a bit of high pigment veg .... I just pigged out ... Add that to picnicking at lunchtime with tinned sardines and I got a lot of DHA,

Being at sea level, grounding on volcanic rock, the resort street lighting is still the old style and with the apartment shutters I could make the apartment pitch black at night, sleep pretty good, lots of dreams ....

Tan through clothing is my new best friend ..... Couldn't always do nude on the beach so 80 per cent UV via tan through was a bonus, and if we went into a town or village a tan through tunic top with a short white skirt meant 2/3 of my skin surface was still harvesting light, but I looked dressed. I only got out of the sun if my eyes started getting dry ... which leads me to my other new best friend - eyeball CT ...

I'd got a jade face roller, its one that has a roller at each end, a large and a smaller one .... I used the small one to cool my eye on the flight over, and then got hold of a small plastic cup and if my eye was starting to feel uncomfortable while we were out and about I would get out the sun and put water in the cup and dip the roller in and use it to cool the surfaces of my eye. On the flight home at night this time, the terminal building had left my eyes feeling dry, so I used the cup with a little bottled water after we took off, but then when the service trolley came round I got another bottle of water and asked for a cup of ice ..... As the ice melted I was dipping the roller in and cooling my eyelid surfaces down with the icy water. Gentle pressure, icy cold not just cool .... When the right eye felt better than the left I then treated the left ... About 20 minutes of eyeball CT, and then some closed eye blinking behind blue blockers ... Kept the blue blockers on until I got home, apart from about 30 seconds when I went through passport control, got home at midnight and went straight to bed .... slept really well. Last night I sat in the dark when I went to bed and CT'd the eye before going to sleep ... Again really good sleep, and I think my vision is improving again ....

So its had me thinking ... if 50 percent of the blood passes behind the eyes every 40 minutes and picks up light, and cold surfaces help assimilate light better ... and presumably the cold would cool the blood slightly as well, like a mini internal CT .... I can use the roller as a tool both day and night ..... I'm working on a protocol for some eye exercises at the moment, which seem to be natural to do now the eye is not inflamed ...

### Sue-UK — 2015-12-08T18:37:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-183701>

So today was a UV index of 1 ..... I'll take what I can get An hour outside in tan through this morning.... I took out a printed out blog. Last week I needed either reading glasses or a magnifying glass to be able to read a blog in daylight using my left eye but today I could read it pretty comfortably without. With my right eye I can see the page and that there is text on it, but I can't read it, probably a greater improvement but it just doesn't seem like it yet... But its pretty cool that my left eye is improving, and I now also have a benchmark for the right eye. I thought my night vision was better last night too ......

### Sue-UK — 2015-12-14T13:03:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-184069>

I had a couple of days where I thought things were going backwards a bit ..... When I thought about it I remembered that during my holiday I used a 24 hour nicotine patch for 5 of the 7 days... the lowest dose, 7 mg over 24 hours, the last step of a stop smoking programme. I switched to that because I don't think using nasal spray to force my eyes to water is good over the longer term, it almost felt like irritating them into action, and my eyes generally were producing tears better. But after taking a couple of days break from the patches, I hadn't gone back to them..... So I started them again on Saturday and can feel (and see) the difference. Last night I CT'd my eyes and chewed a gum as well before going to sleep and slept really well. But my "need" for nicotine to keep improving is saying something about my redox and my difficulty in harvesting light . Nicotine also helps keep the carb cravings at bay. I've been making a soup with some duck fat, stock or water, red onions, carrots, and beetroot for some seasonal carbs, added turmeric and garam masala, just whizzed so that its not completely smooth, I can just take a portion when I want it. I've found if I just open a tin of mackerel, chop it into a soup bowl and add a ladleful of hot soup, I can make a quick warm DHA and pigment rich meal, which keeps me going for hours.

I also listened to the Mitochondria RX webinar twice on holiday and am adding in CoQ10 ....

Got into the outside CT tub when I got up this morning, took out a cup of iced water and the jade roller and CT'd my eyes as well. Also pulled out the exercise bike, put it so that its by an outside door .... Thought a gentle warm up and add 30 seconds of going full out would help keep my electrons hopping in the ECT (read Nick Lane's the vital question on holiday ...) whilst I'm having to scavenge for light.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-14T17:27:10+0200

### Sue-UK — 2015-12-15T12:41:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-184160>

Observation 1 - I'm obviously CTing my right eye more than my left, my right eyelid is thinner than my left. (Thought I had a bulging left eye until I stopped panicking and checked where the difference actually was. ) But I'm thinking that maybe that is an adaptation in cold climates to absorb light more efficiently?

Observation 2 - I outside CT'd in the rain this morning, but it was pretty mild and although I had cold surfaces I didn't feel cold. I did a session with the tanner and when I turned my back to it, the muscles in my back started to quiver, not a shiver ..... its happened before and I have lost some back fat .... I'm wondering if the quivering is some form of photon sucking?

### Sue-UK — 2015-12-28T11:50:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-185036>

My right eye seems to have lost any pain or sensitivity to the touch. I've used mini ice sticks a lot on both eyes, and gradually the feeling of rolling a piece of grit down has gone, both eyeballs seem less protruding, there is no muscle strain, I'm not trying to peer out of my right eye, I'm just observing. I hadn't used nicotine for a few days before Christmas eve, but in the evening I started to get an abcess under an upper tooth, right hand side. It wasn't too bad but I was getting some face swelling and I wasn't going to get to see a dentist that night.... so I chewed a bit of nicotine gum and used it like a poultice over the offending tooth and gum. A bit of what happens at the surface changes the biochemistry underneath .....?  I also iced my cheek and gum. I unintentionally fell asleep with the gum pressed on, woke in the night, took it out and went straight back to sleep. DH thought I was nuts until I mentioned that nicotine used to be routinely used as a spray to kill garden pests ..... and that maybe my white blood cells could use a bit of a superoxide boost. (My redox still sucks.....). By morning there was no pain or swelling at the tooth, I could eat normally, my cheek has taken a bit longer, nicotine nasal spray works better there, but I didn't eat for 48 hours after Christmas lunch, and it made me a bit spaced out on an empty stomach, the dose is less controllable. Although there was a good improvement within about half an hour, because of the time I took it, I had to get DH to drive me to visit my Mum. Timing is everything!

### Sue-UK — 2016-01-07T10:44:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-185630>

So I'm back from Marrakech ...... The light was amazing ...... The call to prayer before dawn had me up on the roof terrace. The moon and starry sky, very cold .... And I would stand on the stone bench looking across the rooftops and wait ... And watch the sky between a couple of the roofs in the east ...... I would hum "sunnnnnnnn" instead of aum ...... My call to the sun, resonating through me. Eventually the sky would reveal the red ...... Time definitely seemed to be slower, later as the blue appeared it seemed to speed up, taking out the stars .... Except for two bright ones that held out .... I would go back in chilled ....

Later we would walk to the square to get breakfast, and I would sit facing the sun ..... dopamine time, I could really feel it lifting my spirits  It was interesting to sense it just through the eyes and on the face, had to be pretty well covered out and about. Then back to the roof terrace for some sunbathing. the roof terrace was about 12 ft by 12, with a high wall. Completely secluded, so I could do some photon harvesting before going out to explore Marrakesh. Worked my way up to doing some sun gazing as well. Started with moving my head across the skyline, stopping briefly to blink rapidly in front of the sun, then gradually increased the time, and then just went for it ...... I reasoned that the actinotherapy treatments on chronically infected skin done in the 40s caused acute responses that also took care of chronic conditions, and the eye is another surface . So I would ice the eye (I packed my own ice tray so I could use ice wands) and then sun gaze. It got easier to do after about 3 days, and I felt less anxious because although on day 1 my sight seemed worse, by day 3 there was definite improvements. My night vision is better, I can see much more in low light levels. Back to the UK, yesterday was blue skies so I sun gazed at the weaker sun, and am trying to work out how to keep triggering an ongoing improvement ..... Acute mega cold air comes to mind .....

### Sue-UK — 2016-01-09T12:54:44+0200

I dried off in front of IR and uv, and then regularly iced my eyes through the day. ### Sue-UK — 2016-01-12T13:39:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-186063>

Weather not great, but have got some CT in.... and I tramp across the field through the mud to get my water from the well, in my tan through ... I don't want to go back to Fuerteventura next month .... I'm not sure having the additional flight time so soon is a good idea, and it somehow just doesn't seem right. I need longer than a week's uvb and that just isn't practical. I've ordered some new tubes for the tanner - both UVB and red tubes... Going to try a combo.....

I've taken to having a cold shower in the late evening and icing my eyes, drinking cold water and using a nicotine gum. I either go outside to breathe in cold air, or stick my head in the chest freezer. All surfaces cold when I get into bed (DH not a fan ......) I've been drawn back to nicotine .... I wanted to know why I was being drawn, and was reading about nicotine's affinity to melanin. I'm wondering if its an attempt to hold on to the tan I got in Marrakesh .... or if its something to do with eye pigments or neuromelanin. Still thinking about it .....

In the daytime it certainly seems to give me a bit of a dopamine boost in the absence of much UV light.

### Sue-UK — 2016-01-27T16:42:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-187467>

I've been using the new tubes on the tanning unit, I switched it to 3 uvb and 2 collagen, and I have a red/infra red lamp that I point at the tanner to bounce light off the reflecting surface at the back. Although not ideal, it's definitely helping.

I was watching a TV programme called "the brain" with David Eagleman. (Still on bbc Iplayer). The first is called "what is reality?" There was some interesting stuff about vision, about how our eyes don't take in enough to form an accurate image, so the brain completes it, so that what we think is "reality" is more of a simulation. The time it takes for the brain to process the external information with what the brain does to complete the simulation means that visually we are not experiencing the present reality, but one very slightly in the past. The programme had me thinking that maybe I need to be thinking not just about the eye as a camera, but my brain as a visual simulator.

### Sue-UK — 2016-01-28T12:18:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-187538>

I'm icing my eyes while in front of the tanner, allowing the light to hit my eyes for part of the time, and closed lids the rest. I'm working on the theory that the slight damage will stimulate repair of any new "damage" and take care of the existing damage at the same time. I ice my eyes at least 3 times a day.

I searched on Amazon for a book on vision, and got Bruce Fife's book " Stop vision loss now! " I started massaging a bit of coconut oil onto my eyelids and the tear ducts before going to sleep, and that has helped prevent eye dryness. Got some interesting info from that and then bought his book "stop Alzheimer's now!" I'm incorporating coconut oil and epi paleo, I think it will help me get over this plateau ......

### Sue-UK — 2016-01-28T12:55:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-187539>

So I'm wondering about the relationship between MCT's ( in both breast milk and coconut oil) and UV light. An infant mammal born underground during winter, on mothers milk......

### Sue-UK — 2016-01-29T19:10:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-187637>

I think the coconut oil is helping .... I'm still holding too much weight round my belly ... So I'm working on the theory that if the BBB is resistant to insulin, blood sugar will pass, but the insulin my brain cells need to use it probably isn't. So the coconut oil as a source of ketones helps keep my energy levels up, so no carb cravings, and using nicotine to stimulate the superoxide burst ..... Morning light, grounding, water, uv tanner to offset UK weather .....

Cognitively I feel better .... Working up the dose of coconut oil slowly.

Watched the second episode of "The brain" last night. Some really interesting stuff again. One bit was about a study on nuns who are tested regularly over a number of years, physically, cognitively etc. On their death their brains are taken and tested for degeneration. IIRC one third of the brains tested showed the signs of full blown Alzheimer's, but pre-death testing said otherwise ..... Cognitive reserve. Wonder if it extends to eyes

Sue..I find coconut oil helps me a fair amount when it comes to cognition. I don't get the peaks and valleys that I experienced in the past and far less "deer in headlights" style anxiety (brain shutting down completely under stress). I usually take a TBSP in the AM and one more in the evening. I also put it in my coffee like Jack does.
Lately I am finding that deep breathing a la Wim Hof is also helping a lot with mental energy.
Just adding in my experiences..but it sounds like you have a real grasp of this stuff that Jack is sharing anyway. I hope that one day I will be able to get a stronger grasp than I currently have.
I am noticing that sun gazing is contributing to headaches but I'm thinking the same like you- that maybe I'm gaining more from sun gazing than I am losing with headaches.
Sound accurate to you?

Exercise has to be natural for me. If I HAVE to do it, I'm trying to work with energy I can't properly tap in to. Yesterday I picked up the hand weights and exercised whilst using the tanner, inner doctor at work ......Later helped DH load a very heavy conference table into into the back of his van no problem. I was helping him move equipment and furniture a lot over the weekend too. I'm spacing out small amounts of coconut oil throughout the day, with a small amount at around 8 pm to keep ketones available for my brain all the time. I didn't ice my eyes yesterday or use coconut oil on my eyelids when I went to bed, and my eyes weren't dry this morning. I wanted to see if it's helping my eyes yet ..... It's like it's making the water I'm drinking wetter IYKWIM ..... I seem to be holding onto it better. Reading is less tiring .....Will go back to icing my eyes now I've got that feedback.

### Sue-UK — 2016-02-15T16:15:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-189088>

My body seems to be working with water better ..... no waking up thirsty and dry eyed ... Sleep is good, vivid dreams I can usually remember....

On Thursday of last week the appointment I had at 11 a.m. was cancelled and the sky was blue.... and the air still.... So 2 hours outside sat on a bench, moved it so that my feet were on the cold wet grass .... Best spot for the sun but possible to be seen from the road ..... so tan through it was ..... At 1 pm it started to cloud over again ..... and weather back to usual.... but for a while a window to view the coming spring was open ....

More blue sky today, less time available and much colder ..... my goosebumps had goosebumps but did an hour, and then ate my lunch outside.

But finally there is a shift in the light .....

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-17T21:45:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-189264>

The cosmos begs to be observed by living things but today we ignore that. That quantum energy transfers and travels to and fro when people pay attention to nature. We lose that energy when we use technology as a transfer tool and we remain unaware of that loss of mass equivalence until we see our healthcare premiums...........Change only follows alterations of waveforms in your environment = seasons = light inclination = fruit = water + nitrogen = choices = reality you get

### Sue-UK — 2016-02-18T20:14:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-189329>

Yesterday it poured with rain all day .... But today the skies were blue again. Wandered round looking at the blossom, the primroses and the snowdrops. Cleaned the outside bath and refilled it ... Spent a couple of hours outside..

I've been thinking about trying MB for a while, keep reading and rereading about it. Interesting mention in energy and epi genetics 5, the quantum brain ....so have been rereading that, with my human brain colouring workbook to try and get a visual to go with the text. But the bit about normally MB is excluded from entering the brain by the BBB , but the BBB is made permeable by a NNemf field which lets it through had me thinking. A damaged BBB would do the same ....? So where would my MB end up without NNemf? So this morning I painted MB on the veins of my wrists and went outside to use it with natural light on the eyes and skin. It seemed as if my right eye (the problem one) was retaining the blue, but the light was resonating .... The camera is darker but I can "see" more light.

Love time 9....and I dreamed last night that I had regained full vision ...

### Sue-UK — 2016-02-21T15:46:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-189517>

Have plans to move the outside bath ...... There is an outside door that is never used, that leads to a now also unused small greenhouse. A couple of the glass panes in the greenhouse roof are broken, but I'm thinking if I take out some more I could use polytunnel plastic, which lets uv through, and open the sliding greenhouse door, or at other times be protected from the elements ....or use it at night by candlelight. I could also stand the tanner inside by the door, and use it while grounding just outside, perhaps after a bath. DH rolling his eyes again but agrees he's got off lightly with this change of spec ...

### Sue-UK — 2016-02-24T00:45:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-189681>

Been rereading EE5, the quantum brain again ..... This morning the weather was lovely, cold but nice in the sun. Did a CT session and then spent 3 hours in the sun. Decided I won't be looking back on my life wishing I'd done more housework .... After rereading the blog yet again, drank lots of water, I spent some time standing grounding with my back to the sun, to let the sun fall directly on the back of my head, to stimulate the oxygen burst on the neocortex... Then I covered my left eye and was trying to manually activate the visual cortex neurons. Then did the right. From the blog "Another unusual finding is that the brain is in complete control of its own blood supply by having the ability to auto regulate where the blood flow should go over the neocortex based upon neuron recruitment or activation."

### Sue-UK — 2016-02-24T19:12:37+0200

### TerrierMom — 2016-02-24T20:46:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-189718>

### Sue-UK — 2016-02-25T10:22:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-189744>

It felt pretty good. I think there's some mileage in it for me, but I need to find a visual  on light and images entering the right eye and it's pathway to the visual cortex. If I can see an animation I can do a reverse sphynx  meditative simulation grounded in the sun, with the concept of the brain as an anticipation machine ...... I've seen an animation of neurons firing when we are experiencing empathy, and I can flip feelings of that on without a stimulus. I think that _anticipation_ of something good coming up can raise dopamine, we "look forward" to it, whether or not the event eventually occurs. I think it could work with vision ..... I've been working front to back, so time for a bit of back to front .....  I want to "see" what happens when my visual cortex _anticipates_ better vision ...

### TerrierMom — 2016-02-25T19:10:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-189778>

I am with you on the "looking forward to". My friends and family know NOT to surprise me with a party or weeekend trip - because when they do, they ROB me completely of the anticipation (months even!) of looking forward to the event. For me it is AS IMPORTANT as the event. Also, I tend to be kind of rigid and like to know what's coming - if I had a plan for the day and it gets upended, I have trouble adapting (though I work on that!). If I know I am going to just plan a "go with the flow weekend" then I can go with the flow. I do think there is something to the anticipation of it all, for sure!

and there is a lot to visualization as well. If you find the animation you are after, do share

### Sue-UK — 2016-02-25T22:45:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-189788>

Today I was out early to get the first light .... And then at 8.30 the sun was on the bath. Thin layer of ice on top, 1.2c, air temp 3.5c but air still, sun glorious ..... 30 minutes half in half out . Then a couple of hours outside. I faced the sun part of the time, and something I tried was to gently hold my right eyelid up, and let my eye look down, exposing a part of the eyeball normally under the lid to the light and cold air. I then stood with my back to the sun and looked at light bouncing off the grey stone wall. With my left eye shut and the right open I could see the wall but not make out the lines of the bricks. If I raised my left eyelid a fraction I could just see the bricks with my right .... But open the left eyelid more and it was like the right eye stopped trying ..... Need to think a bit more on that .....

Read time 10 later. Lots of eye stuff ....... Also had me looking for thalamus animations....

### Sue-UK — 2016-02-26T12:47:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-189812>

Having read time 10 again, I can see how yesterday was an important step forward.  By the cold, drinking water in the sunshine whilst grounded on the damp grass, in that particular spot of the garden, my thalamus was acting as a tuning fork, probably leading to intuitively doing things to support the healing of my eye. I woke up at 3 am feeling my eye was dry, but I "knew" it was nothing to do with the eye surface, that it was inside the eye, and that despite drinking lots of water yesterday, til quite late in the evening, it had to do with the one glass of Malbec I had at around 9.30 which I finished at around 10.30. (Watching the last episode of the series about the brain, blue blockers and head covered). I drank a large glass of water and went straight back to sleep. So this morning I started looking at alcohol and the eye, and discovered why, at autopsy, they often take samples of vitreous fluid to determine alcohol levels . So alcohol is now off the table..... I think the residual effects of what happened to my eye reside in the vitreous ... (First thought was about light and a vitreous EZ.....?)

When I walked barefoot across the field this morning to get water I would scan the ground ahead first, and then walked with my left eye covered. It seemed better than yesterday .... I stood beside the well and drank water, thinking about my thalamus tuning ..... Faced the direction of the sun (clouded) and lifted the lid again and moved the eyeball down like yesterday ..... When I came back I went round to the bench again and looked at the wall. Despite it being a duller day, and with my hand blocking my left eye, I could just make out the lines of the bricks with my right.

Love love love time 10, thanks Jack

### Sue-UK — 2016-03-01T22:24:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-190037>

So back to treading water this week  I spoke to the manager of my mums nursing home last week about a problem with another resident, but Sunday's visit was the last straw..... So I was back in yesterday to see the manager again, and they've got til the end of the week to get it sorted..... And in the meantime I'm going in to look after my mother during the day ..... The environment and the situation is not eyeball friendly ......

I got caught on the hop yesterday and wasn't organised at all, but today I took in lots of well water, and the fourth phase of water book, (still thinking about the vitreous humor and EZ, and all things vitreous). I want to avoid a vitrectomy. I was thinking that if I don't want alcohol in the vitreous what do I want getting in there .... Yesterday I put a few drops of "highly charged" ionic mineral complex into my water when I got home, during the evening, and today in my well water, it seemed to make my eyes feel better, but I couldn't make any comparison because apart from getting out when I first got up, I haven't had a chance to be in natural daylight. Tomorrow morning my brother is taking a shift with mum, so I'll be out in light harvesting mode. Fingers crossed for some good weather.

### Sue-UK — 2016-03-18T14:51:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-191145>

So I was stuck in the nursing home all day for the best part of a week, my one morning off from it was torrential rain and high winds. So I used a light therapy unit for a few minutes each day on my face with closed eyelids. My sight in my right eye seemed to stay the same at the time, but now isn't as good... I don't know if it was the environment and stress alone, or (after reading time 11) whether using the light therapy unit (polarized light) has contributed. I asked a question in ask jack, but finished up going round and round in circles trying to work out what it might have done at a mitochondrial level. But the unit is in the range of 400 to 2000 (a little IR, but other wavelengths in the brochure 760.8, 656.3, 589.3, 527.0, 486.1, 430.8 and 393.4), polarized, incoherent light. 40mW/cm2 at a treatment distance of 10cm, halogen bouncing off a Brewster mirror, through a filter). I've had to leave trying to work it out .... just moved on from it for now. The spring is here, along with the daffodils, primroses and bluebells, so that unit and the tanner are gone for the summer ..... This week we've had 3 days of UV of between 3 and 4, the bath water is ranging from 3.1 to 5C when the sun first hits that part of the garden at 8.30. I've gone out when I first wake up and looked towards the sun, and grounded for an hour or so, then into the bath for 30-40 minutes at 8.30, and then sunbathed for a couple of hours during the morning. First belly grounding of the season

### Sue-UK — 2016-03-24T12:16:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-191431>

I went down with a chest infection ... So not assimilating enough UV. One thing I had noticed was that when I was outside in the sun, if a cloud passed in front of it, I would immediately get very cold, (and dopamine would plummet) but there was no way the air temperature could have dropped that much. So I got to thinking about the IR range. The weather is back to being cloudy (and seems cold), so I've used an FIR sauna since Monday. Decided it was worth a shot rather than resort to antibiotics. Ground before and after, drinking loads of water, contrast shower straight after, getting as much natural light as I can during the day, nicotine ..... Used the tanner afterwards this morning after the shower, when I felt warm (and skin pink) but skin surface cold. Got to get my head round the science as to whether in the longer term I should use different IR ....[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3699878/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3699878/) but gotta say I am much much better ... I also felt after the first couple of sessions that my eye had improved a bit.

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-03T16:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-191962>

The sauna is my book reading time, and apart from a distraction to an iridology book looking at pupil displacements from a light water magnetism perspective, I've been drawn to reading Feynman's book on light, William Tiller's book Science and Human Transformation, and today I was drawn to Bill Gray's book Homeopathy Science or Myth? That one I was drawn back to the drawings depicting electron clouds and dipoles, clusters and also the photos on the back cover of normal water in an electromagnetic field and clustered water in an electromagnetic field. I've also been drawn over the last week to watch science programmes, Jim Al Khalili and anything about the expanding universe ..... it was like lots of scientific concepts and images from the books and programmes had been suspended in my brain waiting for the April 2016 webinar to animate them ..... And then spin them in another direction. Fab webinar.

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-03T17:36:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-191963>

This is the iridology chart I've been drawn to, thinking about pupil displacement. Nasal superior position pupils are related to "liver and spleen insufficiency" but upwards and inwards pupils are away from the mother earth (grounding), water, and putting pressure on the intuition and reason segments of the chart (would include eyes and frontal lobes on other charts). A sign of chronic low dopamine? Emitting excessive light?

I wonder if the pupil placement of astronauts changes when they are in space ......

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-04T13:49:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-192009>

Recording my sauna observations ..... When I started I noticed that I took a long time to start sweating, and when I did it wasn't much. But now I am starting to sweat much faster, and there's more of it. So my skin is starting to respond to the environmental change more efficiently, so hopefully helping with the tanner UV harvesting afterwards. I've increased tanning time by 5 mins each side.

Using time 9 as the template for what I'm trying to achieve, I'm trying to time sessions to when if it wasn't cloudy or piddling with rain  there would be stronger uv and IR available naturally, with the grounding before and after my "treatment" it's taking up to 2 hours a day, but it's worth it. It's slow and subtle, but my eye is improving.

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-06T15:57:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-192173>

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2016-4-6_13-33-56-png.3379/)

Yesterday I was outside grounding, looking at the genesa crystal on the grass, so I went and got it and put it in front of me on the ground in front of the bench, and stuck my feet in the middle with my legs resting on the metal. Had been looking for ages at a print of the periodic table. Then was looking up biological transmutation later and found this with the geometric shapes diagram:

[https://www.21stcenturysciencetech.com/Articles\_2012/Spring-Summer\_2012/06\_Nuclear\_Biology.pdf](https://www.21stcenturysciencetech.com/Articles_2012/Spring-Summer_2012/06_Nuclear_Biology.pdf)

So this morning I did the same thing, and put my hands on it as well, and imagined it expanding, and then shrinking right down .... Easier to imagine an atom's electrons, protons etc moving much closer together or much farther apart, and the change in geometry. If nothing else my ability to visualise something and follow its path is improving ....

Interesting stuff on bacteria too. Thinking about the size of the gut bacteria and bacteria on the skin in relation to the mitochondria.

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-08T16:14:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-192350>

My sauna reading material is now Energy Medicine- the Scientific Basis by James L Oschman. I was drawn to the section on the thalamic rhythm generator, and the free run period. So my outside grounding meditation this morning involved keeping my eyes closed (apparently easier to demonstrate strong alpha waves when the eyes are closed), and I was trying to follow the waves to my eyes. A sort of intentioned thalamus eyeball entrainment whilst connected to the earth, the Schumann resonance, cold surfaces and in the natural light.

I turned the sauna up a bit this morning, and actually massaged the sweat on my skin ....and showered after my UV time. I was thinking maybe sweat is not just for cooling the surfaces but by bringing up stuff (good and bad) maybe UV (with elements from the air, and the sweat) may make beneficial things for the skin ....

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-25T10:37:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-193426>

Time 14 is epic .... I booked a session with a chiropractor on the basis of that, had an interesting booking conversation,  my back felt OK but I wanted to know if anything was going on that could be affecting my eyes . Just happened to have a copy of time 14 with me when I went for the appointment.

It turns out my pelvis was misaligned, and I could feel pressure on my toes but couldn't feel vibration at all on the right toes, and very little on the left. My mother is deaf but I've always said she hears through her feet. A fire alarm can go off and she doesn't hear it, but footsteps up the corridor she hears ... So it got me wondering how badly its been affecting my ability to ground, if I haven't been hearing the earth properly. Since I was realigned my back muscles have let me know there's been a change, but nothing too bad, and then my right leg and foot started to wake up. Weird when I didn't know they were half asleep ..... So I've been concentrating on grounding and getting what natural sun I can over the weekend, and letting my brain adjust to my new body position and message changes. I had a fall down a flight of marble steps at the Louvre in my twenties, and the adjustment brought memories of that difficult time flooding back, but its rear view mirror stuff, so just letting it come up and then letting it go ....

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-26T16:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-193541>

My feet are continuing to adjust .... But it made me think that maybe it's why I find belly grounding so good .... I went straight out first thing and got in the tub, with my feet out facing the sun, didn't take a clock, just decided to stay there until my body said enough ...... It turned out to be an hour in glorious sunshine, very little wind. Then it started to cloud over and the wind grew ....So I spent a couple of hours outside in tan thru, grounding facing the sun when there was a gap in the clouds and some warmth, and belly grounding if the clouds came over. Just came in and did some work indoors when we had 2 showers of rain, and then heavy hail .... Now we have torrential rain, thunder and lightning ....

I'm taking a break from the tanner, but one of the things I did was to experiment with a fan,in front of the wide open window. Facing the tanner with the wind on my back was pretty comfortable, but with my back to the tanner and facing the wind, it was a different story. I would instinctively cross my arms, and my tolerance was much lower. So I was interested to read in Lyall Watson's book "heaven's breath, a natural history of the wind" that females breasts and nipples are much more sensitive to cold than a man's "slabs of muscles on a masculine chest" and "chill can shock a nursing mother to a point where her flow of milk ceases altogether." So it kinda makes sense of my belly grounding in a cold wind, and why sometimes after first looking towards the sun, a reverse sphynx seems better. The cold wind signal on the brown fat on my back is hopefully an important stimulus, even if there is less UV about. "Both sexes respond equally to winds above a certain threshold by moving more quickly." So in stronger but safe winds I'm planning to do a barefoot circuit round the field ..... When I think of "solar wind" and separate it .... Solar plus wind .....I'm hoping there's some quantum yield for my mitochondria up for grabs from the wind ....

### Sue-UK — 2016-05-23T09:42:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-195196>

Love CPC 12's references to the wind.

I've bought Principles and Practice of Sleep Medicine (kindle 6th edition, Kryger, Roth, Dement). Amazon. UK has the 5th edition available as separate chapters for under £2, so I picked a couple of chapters to check it wasn't going to be over my head.... decided it was worth the investment, and went for it ..... I want to understand sleep more so that I can bio hack my own sleep better, and have more of the basics to bolt the quantum sleep understanding on to. It's about 170 chapters so I might be a while......

### Sue-UK — 2016-05-24T18:41:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-195273>

I knew when I went to the chiropractor on Friday that the adjustment from the previous week was holding, I feel so much better. I was right and can also now feel vibration on my right toes.

My nicotine bio hack is also bringing some interesting results, I've experimented with different doses depending on the time of day and the weather, and from trial and a fair bit of error now know what symptoms I am likely to get if I go too far, and listen to my intuition when it tells me to stop. But something has shifted ..... And that includes my weight. I finally got on the scales after a couple of months of not wanting to ..... And I've lost 10lb.

### Sue-UK — 2016-05-25T14:35:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-195313>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=195290)
>
> I "accidentally" lost some weight with nicotine also. Nice surprise.

My bio hack involves the circadian cycle of csf production, water and nicotine's breakdown to cotinine.

I was trying to understand polarization better and came across this:

_"Celestial polarization survives poorly in hazy or cloudy conditions, as the presence of suspended particles or water droplets rapidly depolarises light by multiple path scattering. Nevertheless if the sky is not entirely covered in cloud, the patches of open atmosphere that are visible from the earth's surface retain the polarization characteristics of their relationship with the sun. The animals that rely on celestial polarization can continue their activities as long as the sky is not completely occluded by haze or clouds. "_

So if it's wall to wall grey cloud, I change my eating and nicotine pattern.

### Sue-UK — 2016-06-20T14:14:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-196949>

The weather has been rainy ..... Most mornings I've still gone out and got in the tub. If it's bucketing down I might leave it til later, but mostly I just get up, and go straight outside, get in and wait for the gift. There is always a gift. One morning it was the realisation that despite the poor quantum yield, the garden is thriving. Flowers are blooming, the fruits are coming on as normal..... Nature is working with what it's got, so quantumly, so can I ..... Another morning there was some welcome sunlight, and I was watching a leaf that was hanging from the lowest branch in front of me. If I moved my head to the right it would eclipse the sun. I had the thought that a leaf could do what a lunar eclipse can do. I could feel the observation changing me. I wondered how my observation of the leaf in such wonder had changed the leaf. It was "observing" something in the garden that was changed..... Me!

That got me to thinking, does the garden "observing" a low quantum yield change it too, so that it adapts its own light, water, magnetism recipe? Water and magnetism taking centre stage while light is waiting in the wings, occasionally stepping in and out, to remind the audience of what treats are to come. Enough to signal? I wondered if that is why I still go out and ct in the rain ... The leaf taught me to be less stressed about the lack of natural uv. Just get out there ... Stay out there .... And wait for, and acknowledge, the signal, and the gift. Cell vibration .... Cell-ebrate!

### Sue-UK — 2016-06-20T18:15:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-196958>

I did my ct today after lunch. There was a brief patch of blue sky and I just stopped what I was doing and went for it. I have just got a solar powered floating water feature, which I used for the first time today. I put it in the bath with me. When a dark cloud passed over the sun, the fountain pulsed very slowly, or made like a small ball of water droplets, and when the cloud passed over it would suddenly spray a jet of water up into the air. The changes in quantum yield were reflected in the vibrations through the water so I imagined the solar panel as the solar panel on my friend the leaf, with the bath water as the cell water. Then when the rain started to fall I was looking up at the clouds and thinking about sunlight hitting the clouds, the rain water had effectively been sunbathing before falling .....so perhaps my leaf also obtained sunlight by proxy..... Then I was thinking about the jet of water capturing sunlight as it flew up into the air ...informing the bath water?

The solar water feature is now sitting in a bowl of water on the table next to the bath ... I can look out of my bedroom window and have a better idea of the quantum yield. If there is a jet of water I'll be heading out ....

### caroline — 2016-06-21T12:03:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-196994>

I am in total awe of you Sue.....

### Sue-UK — 2016-06-24T16:00:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-197175>

My water feature was a big hit with my granddaughters yesterday  weather not good but outside in the afternoon, barefoot like Nana, I put the bowl of water with the feature on the ground, and they watched the little bubbles form and then shrieked with delight when a cloud passed and the sun had the water shooting up in the air. They got soaking wet but were happy. Worth buying for that.

My hazelnut leaf has a few little holes appear along one vein, the outer leaves on the same branch are so eaten that they look like lace .... Will be interesting to see if my leaf gets worse, or if the tree has produced enough of the chemicals yet that make the leaves less tasty.

My DD is home for the summer after graduating, before going to do her masters abroad in September. Having to CT in tan through as her boyfriend is with her, but it's working out OK. The wifi going off at night is non negotiable, and she is doing the evening meal most evenings so I am taking the chance to get an extra short CT in later in the afternoon.

### Sue-UK — 2016-07-01T18:24:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-197691>

Had a set back on Saturday .... woke up and realised my right eye was much worse .... decided if it was no better by Monday I would have to see about CW treatment. When I thought about it I realised that I had been watching the referendum results on tv on Friday, during the day, without blockers. The nuance about the spike in unnatural blue during the day slipped past me that day.... Add maybe a couple of other slides .... and it was what it was.  So Saturday I was outside and in the cold water three times, and regularly used cold water in an eye bath to cool my eye surface. Doubled my water intake. Found a way to overcome my aversion to oral MB. Sunday I was back to Thursday's position. Phew! That got me thinking that with the current low quantum yield I need to do that as a matter of course, I could be moving forward faster. The setback became a gift .... ..... so come rain or shine I'm out there 3 times, only missed one so far when it was a proper storm. A bath was being taken out of the house next door, so I've nabbed it this morning... My current short bath with seat faces east for my morning session, this full length one has been positioned to face west, and unshaded in the late afternoon sun (if we get any). Depending on the weather I will be stretched out sunbathing or cold air/rain bathing in water, but gotta shrink those respiratory proteins, work on the EZ, the less UV about the more tub time I need. There was an aha! moment in the June Q&A recording that had me going back to Ubi 24. Time to up my game ...

### Sue-UK — 2016-08-12T15:11:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-199549>

Got back on Wednesday from a week on Fuerteventura, UV "extreme" Arrived at lunchtime and could feel my vision shift almost instantly. The hotel was right on the beach, so I could hear the sound of the waves as I drifted off to sleep at night. (Thalamus tuning?) Light from outside was a bit of a problem, but I bought some clothes pegs and used them to pin the curtains tight together, which improved it a lot, and then used a sleep mask as well. From night one I didn't wake up needing a drink of water .... Ate breakfast outside, then headed up the coast to a better beach. Much less people, could swim and sunbathe nude. First day I didn't have the confidence to walk to the water nude, so put on tan thru, by day 3 I just went for it.Lunch and a rest in the shade, early dinner outside ..... 13 hours a day outside either nude or in tan through. When I was in the ocean in the sunshine with the waves lapping over me I thought I could "feel" 100 hz. I was reading Lee Know ND's book "life- the epic story of our mitochondria" on the plane going over, (a much easier read than Nick Lane, I could get the images in my mind straight away, retain the info easier, and bolt on the light aspects....Dots connected better.) Just at the right time, ready for the August webinar ...... Which seemed to explain why my vision seemed to improve as soon as I got off the plane ..... I've listened to the webinar several times .....it's like I knew how important time 19 was going to be and I wanted to be ready.

Weather here a bit better, clear skies and uv 6.

### Sue-UK — 2016-08-27T15:17:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-200317>

I was having a discussion with DD earlier in the month, I reckoned autumn had arrived by the 7th, despite the hotter temps. I could sense the light shifting, and the air crisper during my dawn CT. By August 18th the first of the leaves were starting to drop .... And not from lack of rain .... But a few really good sunny days. Had 2 days camping with my 3 children, DIL, DDs BF and my 2 granddaughters. One of the selling points for the campsite was the lack of wifi, but the campsite was grim, it had a road bridge for a major road, and a huge electric pylon near the entrance and toilet amenities. I was surprised at the number of people willing to camp that close to the pylon and the constant traffic noise. Fortunately it was a big site- the furthest part was a 20 mins walk from the entrance, lots of trees then into a quiet almost deserted field. I was spared refusing to stay there, (just....) but note to self, if we do it again, me and DH do the booking ......

Loving the latest webinars and blogs ..... I've been trying a migraine cold hat for an hour before bed, with a cold wrap round my neck, as an added signal for the day/night change as well as its cooling properties. I know that with the changing light levels I have to do everything possible to optimise my sleep efficiency. I use one for 20 mins then turn it inside out for 10, then use a second cap. There's flaps to pull down over the eyes, so when I get into bed in the dark I pull them down and cool my eyes as well, before taking the hat off to sleep. Sleep is deeper, remembering my dreams better. I don't need water during the night (am I just better hydrated or am I getting water from fat burning, like a polar bear...? Or both?) . Time 21 has me thinking it is helping create the conditions necessary for better nighttime semiconductor laser light emission, but I'm still working through the science. Epic blog.

### Queen of Elves — 2016-09-02T16:39:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-200714>

Hello Sue, as you too live in an environment with a low quantum yield, may I ask you how your diet looks like?
I am a bit insecure about the amount of carbs one should eat in summer and autumn in Northern Europe.

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-02T18:31:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-200730>

> [Queen of Elves said:](/goto/post?id=200714)
>
> Hello Sue, as you too live in an environment with a low quantum yield, may I ask you how your diet looks like?
> I am a bit insecure about the amount of carbs one should eat in summer and autumn in Northern Europe.

I'm currently eating twice a day, most days about 11 am and then at 5. If I know I'm heading into a high NNemf or stressful environment I'll add in a BAB and shift the other 2 meals, but I am aiming to eat no later than 6pm. My aim over the autumn and winter is to scavenge what light I can and get in the cold lane .....stay outside as much as possible and match the sun's barcode to the environmental temperature. Then concentrate on fat and protein, and eat carbs that I could theoretically go and gather locally that day, which will get less and less as the winter progresses. If I eat the protein and fat first, the amount of carbs will hopefully take care of itself without counting .... But to avoid carb cravings or energy plummeting I've somehow got to keep my mitochondria humming at 100hz...... I'm going to ramp up the outside CT which should help reduce appetite, and partly offset the low quantum yield, but I've also bought some tan through harem pants and a poncho top to give a bit of wind protection to do some cold air sunbathing to scavenge as much light as possible, and ground, however gloomy or cold...... Well that's the evolving plan at the moment anyway .......

### Queen of Elves — 2016-09-03T22:22:49+0300
