Yes, I did. It was probably at least 24 months into the journey, maybe a bit longer ....(I joined Jan 2014) ..I'm an ex chronic dieter so even when I felt I had overcome leptin resistance I didn't want to do it to lose weight ....then I would be skipping meals whatever my environment or my stress levels, and going back to the number on the scales dictating my life. So I waited til it became a completely natural progression, which I think in the longer run has a better chance of helping improve my heteroplasmy. For the length of time I've been on this journey, in the conventional diet industry and medical profession I would probably be seen as a failure, but as a post menopausal woman heading for 60, I don't worry about that, I'm not interested in later life becoming the slimmest person in the dementia unit ....

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-04T13:39:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-200846>

So yesterday I started looking for marginal gains ...... (Like an athlete minus the sport ). When we cleared my FIL's attic I came across a couple of animal skins, and we found a photo of DH's aunt as a toddler sat outside on the grass on one. They have been kept really well considering they must be around 90 years old. Now the ground is colder and wetter , yesterday and today it became my meditation mat, scavenging photons in 17c air temp under cloudy skies and a cool wind after an hour in 14c water. I had an image of sitting naked, meditating on it in falling snow ...... But if not enlightenment  the marginal gain might be being closer to the earth and with more skin surface contact than being sat on a bench.....

I was also wandering around looking for marginal sleep gains. I'm getting occasional tinnitus, which I can usually clear with CT or the migraine hats before bed, but I know I need to change my environment more. I searched the word tinnitus on the forum and copied and pasted things Jack had said, one thing was: " AC currents really drive a brain nuts." I was peering at the fuse box and noticed that DD's bedroom, the office, utility and shower rooms and back porch sockets can be switched off separately to other parts of the house. DD is away til Christmas so I made up the bed and was ready to switch off the breaker before going to bed when I remembered the freezer in the back porch. Mission aborted ... But going to run a long extension lead from the freezer to the kitchen so I can try again tonight. If I'm lucky the gain will be far more than marginal. DH rolling his eyes a bit about changing room, but I've told him he's getting off lightly ......he's not (yet!) got the spec for a bespoke sleep pod in the garden.

### Lahelada — 2016-09-04T14:49:37+0300

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-11T18:17:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-201305>

The change of bedroom took 3 or 4 days to get used to. Found I woke after about 4 hours, then would take a while to go back to sleep, and then I'd sleep for another 4 hours. On night 3 when I woke up I used the walls to guide me to the freezer to get a migraine hat, then back to bed. Lay there with it on and the eye flaps pulled over my eyes, and just meditated until I felt drowsy, took it off and then went to sleep easily. I think my waking was partly because DH was sleeping restlessly, it's a double not a king size, so he felt too close to the edge. But as I want to continue the Biohack we go to bed together, but he will go back to our usual bed if he needs to. Since then I might wake up and notice he's not there, roll into the cool spot and go straight back to sleep.

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-12T17:16:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-201348>

Seven hours sleep straight, and DH still there when I woke up.  Out to the tub at 6.30. I was thinking about looking for another marginal gain. Later something Videha said in another thread prompted me to look up the spectral emission data on the amber gel filter I am using on my devices, and see if I could find something better. Found my filter swatch and checked out a few possibilities, and then looked up the data online and compared. Ordered a sheet that cuts out more blue ..... 550 instead of 475. .....

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-19T11:55:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-201593>

I've been using my migraine cap outside in the bath, pulling the cold flaps over my eyes for 3 or 4 minutes, then pulling the flaps back and looking in the direction of the sun. It seems to help and I was thinking cold and pressure .... then light.

I listened to the 2 Waterside Ape radio shows, and it reminded me of an episode of Human Planet ....the one where it featured people living on boats, relying on the sea. They filmed a man doing breathing exercises and getting into a meditative state before going into the water, where he was striding really quickly along the sea floor, with a spear in hand, speared a fish before going back to the surface with his lunch. I remember thinking at the time about how developing bipedalism made perfect sense. But something else was that some children spend so much time in the sea that they are better adapted to seeing underwater than in the atmosphere... But I was wondering if just regularly going into the water and free diving actually helps keep the eye gels clear? Time 22 references James Oschman's book Energy Medicine, so I read it again. In it Oschman describes gel-to-sol-to-gel transformation with pressure, and how that might release toxins, the example was not for eyes, but a gel is a gel ... So would free diving provide cold and pressure .... and coming back to the surface for light...... Is my migraine cap providing just enough cold, just enough pressure to my eyes... Is the "treatment" time related to how long humans can generally hold their breath.... Does coming to the surface "exercise" the adaptive capability of the pupil? I went back to see what Gerald Pollock said about gels ... and Ubi 24 (Are Myopia, Light and Aging linked) was the bookmark from the last time I was reading it ....

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-20T22:53:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-201673>

Transitioning from a solar to a cold switch..... Not enough sun, not yet cold enough .... Did CT at dawn in the rain, then wished I hadn't, started feeling like I'm on the verge of a cold, and low energy...... Craving IR light and warmth ...

Today I was rereading electrohealing, the medicine of the future by Roger coghill. The chapter on "the amazing ultraviolet healing machine" added a different dimension to the uv of Actinotherapy. It was about how red blood cells can become over magnetised, which affects their oxygen carrying capability, and that they can attract white blood cells, which then don't do their job. A fever heats up the blood, which demagnetises the blood, lymphocytes no longer hang around the attracting blood cells, and the body carries oxygen more efficiently. Degaussing and percussion as well as heating can demagnetise the blood ...

So I had a short sauna and a few minutes in front of the tanner, then grounding. Feel much better. I want a drum....

Ordered a new EMF/rf tester today. I've had mine for years and want something more versatile and more sensitive. Need to re hack my environment. I'm missing something ....

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-24T17:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-201852>

So my meter arrived yesterday. Measured at the pillow and at the end of the bed in my usual bedroom where the breaker is on at night, and also in DD's room with the breaker off. Doesn't seem to be any difference.  Not sure if I've got caught up in nocebo and placebo effects, or if the meter isn't sensitive enough, or if it's how the house is wired (wiring for each socket is dropped from the attic to 4 feet above the ground).

This morning, with the wifi on, I checked the readings by the outside bath, and in a few places I often sit. Going to move one bench ....I started tying myself up in knots trying to work out what the different readings "should" be, so finished up going out into the middle of the field behind the house and taking ambient readings there. Next step is to draw a plan of the house and garden, note the readings and compare them to the ambient readings in the field.

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-26T12:19:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-201901>

The DD bedroom hack is finished, couldn't argue the numbers.. But a gain in the sitting room lighting. Now it's getting darker earlier DH is flipping the overhead lighting on, so my blue blockers go on earlier too. But we compared the emf readings from the overhead light to a floor standing vertical 3 led bulb lamp and the figures were much better. DH could see the numbers .... Then made the led bulbs their own blue blockers with my new gel filter sheet. Bought some clear safety glasses yesterday and covered with the gel filter, for if the TV in on. I'd already covered the front of the face shield DH uses for hedge cutting (more eye rolling but he doesn't need 2) but apart from how it looks,  it makes drinking water or using my migraine caps awkward. Overall a step in the right direction

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-10T16:47:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-202591>

Doing well with my new home made blue blocking glasses. Sleep is better. One thing I've noticed is that my cold tolerance is lower, the temp of the outside bath is dropping, but I want to get out much earlier than I could previously tolerate. I want to walk round the garden for the remaining time, its almost like an inner doctor exercising command. I was wondering why this might be, and then realised I am suddenly becoming much less glycated. Perhaps I am sensing the cold more because I have less biological concrete blocking the signal ..... Or perhaps my "thrifty genes" are finally having the volume turned down. (The biometric measurement indicators for my fetal landscape have me down as "exercise challenged" which very accurately describes me ) Does having the "thrifty genes" mean that I have a hair trigger response to blue light toxicity, so that what I hoped would be a "marginal gain" is actually giving me more bang for my buck? Whatever it is, I'll take it.

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-28T12:38:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-203399>

I'd lost a few pounds before I went on holiday last week . Flight out wasn't good .... set off the detector going through and they sent me through the x ray.... Cross at myself for refusing and asking to strip off some clothing and my shoes and going through the detector again .... That and the flight (4 hours) and the damage to my eye was done .... Coming back I wore tan a through top and a short cotton skirt and walked through barefoot and didn't set it off. Phew! On the bright side I decided to see what light water and magnetism in abundance could do. So I lazed on the beach most of the day and got into the ocean 5 times a day, walked along the beach in the evening and got one last session in. There was a naturist resort next to ours so no problems with nudity on the beach. I'm a poor swimmer but found I was building up my stamina as the days went past. I swam longer and the steep climb back up from the beach got easier. Within 2 days my eye started improving. No wifi access in the apartment, and I pegged the blackout blinds together to stop the outside light at night. Vivid dreams. Lots of water and seafood every day. My eye is marginally better than before I went, which considering what happened I'm pleased about, and I physically and mentally feel stronger. Now got to keep up the momentum in a different climate .....

### Sue-UK — 2016-11-29T18:20:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-204493>

So the last month I have immersed myself in blogs, webinars, (going from the earliest and working forward to see what I missed/have forgotten), and you tube videos of Doug Wallace, James Oschmann, Jodi Nunnari ...

The weather is getting colder, CT in the mornings has been hovering around the 6C mark, so was doing about 30 minutes, this morning it was 1.6C under the ice .... Did 10 minutes, watching the steam come off me from my exposed calves and feet, came back in for some light therapy with my Bioptron Pro, and then went straight out and got in again .... My feet got very cold from walking across the frosty grass, and even keeping them out during the session, the walks back at both sessions were almost painful they were so cold. But the recovery time is within a few steps of getting off the icy grass. My body isn't shivering or preshivering ....The pilot light seems to be coming on as soon as I hit the cold water. I only start to feel cold when I finally get dressed, but its minimal and soon passes.

Doing an hour before bed with migraine cap, a shoulder wrap and a wrist wrap, take out of the freezer an hour before bed, do half an hour and then get the second set out ... Sleep is really good ... lots of vivid dreams, no waking wanting water ..... Woke up on Sunday morning feeling .... well joyful... for no obvious reason but I'll take it as a good sign ....

Dopamine rising ....

### Sue-UK — 2016-12-03T12:32:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-204701>

2 days of having to take a hammer out to break through the thick ice. My thermometer was reading the water temp as 0.0.C  6 minutes, 30 minute light therapy afterwards then got back in for another 6 minutes. This morning there was no frost on the ground, some of yesterdays ice still in the water, and a balmy 1.3C, 10 minutes, light therapy and then back in the water .... Feels amazing afterwards .. My skin was already improving, but the second dunk seems to be speeding up the process.

Loved the December webinar ....after I had done my CT session yesterday I listened to it once without taking notes, and then took notes on the second listen through, with a picture of the ETC . My immersing myself in all things mitochondria over the last month has paid off ..... Then I read Reality 2 ......  A delicious day .....

I got out the manual that came with my Bioptron and looked up the specific frequencies used in it, and can relate some of them to the info in the webinar and the blog, and can picture better what is happening with those frequencies, and how the CT is helping the process ..... Going to relisten and reread to try and grasp the bits that still haven't stuck ....

Does listening to the December webinar 6 times count as an obsession Then the November one twice and now working backwards .....

I'd got myself in a knot last winter about using the Bioptron because its polarised light, not unpolarised like the sun. But when I went back to the Bioptron website recently I found this [http://www.bioptron.com/Professionals/Doctors-Corner/18-Years-Of-Intensive-Study-Into-Bioptron.aspx](http://www.bioptron.com/Professionals/Doctors-Corner/18-Years-Of-Intensive-Study-Into-Bioptron.aspx) , published at the beginning of the year, which is why I decided to biohack it over this winter. (I've had my devices several years, and the frequency range is now different. The current model seems to be from 480 to 3400 whereas mine is 400 to 2000, with 393.4 nm listed as a frequency in my manual, along with 760.8, 656.3, 589.3, 527.0, 486.1 and 430.8). So the July webinar mentioning S cones, M cones. rhodopsin and red shifted opsins is resonating big time.

Its an 11cm diameter device, and I picked spots on my body to deliver the light to, face and neck, sternum, liver, belly, base of spine and between my shoulder blades as standard, and then under my arms and the girls if I have time left. The skin condition of the main irradiated areas has improved dramatically, and with a CT and grounding session before and after its like a turbo boost of energy. The July webinar may be a 6 timer .....

Love it.  I think it takes Pollack's work to the next level. I'm up to the last chapter which introduces Ling. The later chapters are a bit easier than the first, so once I've read the last I will reread the chapters in reverse order. My Mum has been very ill over Christmas so I've spent a lot of time at the nursing home, and it was my main read when there was nothing to do but wait. I swear it helped keep my dopamine up ... I would go out at sunrise and do a CT session, come back in for a light therapy treatment and then back out to the tub. Really helped get my batteries charged, even after one very bad night's sleep. Then if was off to the nursing home, and when I got the chance to come home to take a break I would take a barefoot walk round the field, I've used aromatherapy oils, had a foot soak in Epsom salts, gathered virtual electrons and photons through meditation, drank lemon balm tea, had a few minutes in front of the tanner .... Just went intuitively with what would mitigate what I thought I would be facing, or afterwards what would mitigate the actual damage. Mum is now out of danger, and if anything my energy levels are better than before she became ill, and there's a subtle improvement in my eye ....

### Sue-UK — 2017-01-07T15:06:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-206268>

So Mum became ill again on the evening of new year's day. All drama as they got her into the ambulance which then wouldn't start, so they had to order another one .... Once inside the hospital she did not respond to the antibiotic treatment, was getting symptoms and extreme pain for something undiagnosed but difficult to explore in her current condition, and on Tuesday it became an end of life care plan. I had stayed with her on Sunday night, and gone to my brother's house 5 minutes away (8 wifi signals and smart meter....)for a 4 hour break to shower, change and lie down for hour, while my brother stayed with her. I did the same on Tuesday and Wednesday. DH would pick me up to take me there, and I would wait for him outside watching the sunrise. He brought me fresh clothes, and my Bioptron compact, and I would shine the light on my wrists for 20 minutes. It was just enough to keep me going. I would doze for a few minutes if she slept, or meditate. The last couple of years of caring for Mum, and being the only daughter meant I was in the best position to be her voice when she became agitated. No to a male nurse wanting to do personal care..... her pain was getting worse ..... and so it went on.... She went to sleep after being moved to a side ward Wednesday lunchtime and didn't awaken again. My job was done, by nightfall she was breathing but no longer there, the staff put a guest bed in the room for me that night, I dozed whilst my brother was on duty sat in the chair and soon after midnight it was over...... I was like a zombie when I was taken home and got to bed at 4 am, but at sunrise I was awake and went out to the bath. On my first step outside on the frozen grass I started to cry, and I just bawled my eyes out through the whole session. But outside I could just let it go ....saw the sunrise through my tears, whilst the wind carried my sobbing away .... but it was me and nature, natural, cleansing ... and I felt so much better afterwards. I realised I had been grieving for her for a long time during her dementia, it was her time, and all is as it should be. She is at peace, and I felt - and still feel - peaceful.

The whole experience was a masterclass in the senses, mitochondria and heteroplasmy. I was able to use what I've learned to help my Mum and myself. As one sensory system went offline I tapped into another to give her reassurance. I talked, sang, hummed into the palms of her hand, I stroked her face and forehead, applied gentle pressure to the top of her head, gently massaged her ears. At one point she was holding onto my hands and I could feel her electron sucking. I went into a meditative state, opened my crown chakra and drew in light which I directed down my arms, mentally repeating the word "love". There seemed suddenly a change, the flow seemed to go down my right arm, and come back from her through my left .... the same positive energy, but I had the feeling she was downloading data, but I couldn't "see" what it was. When I finally got home and got into bed, as I started to drift off I had a series of images that weren't mine, as if I was watching a replay of memories she had been replaying. A glimpse of what was happening, showing she was not afraid any more, the dementia had let go its grip on her. A gift from her to me.

### caroline — 2017-01-08T00:42:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-206292>

What an amazing account Sue of love and caring and courage and trusting in what you have learned and applying it in real time to real life.

I applaud you for doing so many wonderful things for yourself and your Mom - all by instinct.

You filled your Mom's life with love and light .... and you did the same for yourself.

All of the above took so much love and courage and trust from both of you. I wish I had know all of this when I had to go thru it. I was constantly fighting against the establishment and their views when all I wanted to do was go by my instincts. I have so many regrets that I wasn't strong enuf to fight and do what I innately thought was right.

Dementia robs us of those we love - it is so cruel and heartbreaking.

I am so proud of you for so many reasons. You are in my thoughts and prayers.....

xo

### Sue-UK — 2017-01-10T16:09:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-206503>

I've been intuitively cautious about having red light unopposed on my eyes, (not directed it at my head) then found this [http://www.healingtheeye.com/Articles/AJO\_IOP\_ light.pdf](http://www.healingtheeye.com/Articles/AJO_IOP_%20light.pdf) about darkness, red and green's effects on IOP........... ### Sue-UK — 2017-01-22T12:35:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-207200>

The funeral was held, and my elder brother gave a eulogy which instead of being a chance to heal the family rift, just opened new wounds. I held it together until I got home and then blew an emotional fuse, which stunned DH and my children but I woke up next day with a new perspective, and the ripples are settling. Done my CT, light therapy, grounding, meditation, and immersed myself in learning .... light therapy, altered states of consciousness, quantum theory, just going with what intuitively draws me .....

I'm heading back to Fuerteventura next weekend. Going to the same place we stayed at last time. We know it and will be able to slip quickly and quietly into the routine of heading to the beach early and spending our days harvesting light water and magnetism. I'm already visualising it in meditation ....

### Sue-UK — 2017-01-24T12:39:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-207272>

I've reflected on photons and electrons during meditation, when I've visualised myself sat on the beach at Fuerteventura, drawing electrons from the earth and photons from the sun. I've been thinking about the physiological effects of these "virtual" photons and electrons. When I use my bioptron it has an effect, but its not the same as the photons directly from the sun - I've started calling the sun's photons "wild photons" and my bioptron is a source of "zoo photons." I'm trying to imagine the difference between a mitochondria's "observing" a wild photon (with its information about its creation and journey from the sun), compared to a "virtual" or "zoo photon."

My holiday reading will be Cosmo Sapiens, The TM technique by Peter Russell, and I've just got Phototherapy by Niels Finsen.

### lohd2015 — 2017-01-25T02:27:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-207305>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=206268)
>
> So Mum became ill again on the evening of new year's day. All drama as they got her into the ambulance which then wouldn't start, so they had to order another one .... Once inside the hospital she did not respond to the antibiotic treatment, was getting symptoms and extreme pain for something undiagnosed but difficult to explore in her current condition, and on Tuesday it became an end of life care plan. I had stayed with her on Sunday night, and gone to my brother's house 5 minutes away (8 wifi signals and smart meter....)for a 4 hour break to shower, change and lie down for hour, while my brother stayed with her. I did the same on Tuesday and Wednesday. DH would pick me up to take me there, and I would wait for him outside watching the sunrise. He brought me fresh clothes, and my Bioptron compact, and I would shine the light on my wrists for 20 minutes. It was just enough to keep me going. I would doze for a few minutes if she slept, or meditate. The last couple of years of caring for Mum, and being the only daughter meant I was in the best position to be her voice when she became agitated. No to a male nurse wanting to do personal care..... her pain was getting worse ..... and so it went on.... She went to sleep after being moved to a side ward Wednesday lunchtime and didn't awaken again. My job was done, by nightfall she was breathing but no longer there, the staff put a guest bed in the room for me that night, I dozed whilst my brother was on duty sat in the chair and soon after midnight it was over...... I was like a zombie when I was taken home and got to bed at 4 am, but at sunrise I was awake and went out to the bath. On my first step outside on the frozen grass I started to cry, and I just bawled my eyes out through the whole session. But outside I could just let it go ....saw the sunrise through my tears, whilst the wind carried my sobbing away .... but it was me and nature, natural, cleansing ... and I felt so much better afterwards. I realised I had been grieving for her for a long time during her dementia, it was her time, and all is as it should be. She is at peace, and I felt - and still feel - peaceful.
>
> The whole experience was a masterclass in the senses, mitochondria and heteroplasmy. I was able to use what I've learned to help my Mum and myself. As one sensory system went offline I tapped into another to give her reassurance. I talked, sang, hummed into the palms of her hand, I stroked her face and forehead, applied gentle pressure to the top of her head, gently massaged her ears. At one point she was holding onto my hands and I could feel her electron sucking. I went into a meditative state, opened my crown chakra and drew in light which I directed down my arms, mentally repeating the word "love". There seemed suddenly a change, the flow seemed to go down my right arm, and come back from her through my left .... the same positive energy, but I had the feeling she was downloading data, but I couldn't "see" what it was. When I finally got home and got into bed, as I started to drift off I had a series of images that weren't mine, as if I was watching a replay of memories she had been replaying. A glimpse of what was happening, showing she was not afraid any more, the dementia had let go its grip on her. A gift from her to me.

This brought up so much buried emotions in me Sue. Thank you for sharing. Your love and light shines so bright. It is truly beautiful.

### Sue-UK — 2017-01-27T12:41:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-207428>

So I'm thinking that after a night of darkness, natural morning sunlight would give a balanced dose of red and green on the eye, but maybe I need to rethink my use of artificial IR/red?

So I've added green to my bioptron treatment. Back in 2002 I bought a set of Spectro chrome filters, which I'd cut pieces off to use with the old fashioned stage lights, and the cut pieces fit over the bioptron pro nicely. So I use the full spectrum standard filter, and then just stick on the green for a few minutes. I also use it when I'm using the tanner. I was interested in green in photosynthesis, and found several articles like this one
[https://www.heliospectra.com/sites/default/files/general/What light do plants need\_5.pdf](https://www.heliospectra.com/sites/default/files/general/What%20light%20do%20plants%20need_5.pdf)

Then I was listening to one of the two new podcasts where Jack talked about photosynthesis ... then I picked up Mae Wan Ho's book Living Rainbow H2O and opened it on the page in chapter 21 where she gives the formula for photosynthesis and respiration ... and the audible and the written became a picture ....

A word in the article had drawn my attention - photomorphogenesis, so I looked that up ..... is what the human eye perceives non visually the human equivalent of photomorphogenesis?

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3357375/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3357375/) is interesting too....

### Sue-UK — 2017-02-17T13:05:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-208769>

Copying posts I've made on another thread because they are relevant to what I'm thinking about .....

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=208044)
>
> From Niels Finsen's book Phototherapy (1901 translation of an 1894 paper):
> "Engelmann says that the _Pelomyxa palustris_ (a kind of amoeba) contracts strongly when exposed suddenly to light, but that it quickly expands under the influence of a sudden darkness. The same author has pointed out that the rods and cones of the retina shorten in light and lengthen in darkness."
> (Engelmann, _Pfluger's Archiv_., xxxv., p498)

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=208047)
>
> I've been thinking about it in terms of photosynthesis (and human photosynthesis). I've seen pictures (light Medicine of the Future by Jacob Liberman) of how chloroplasts stream under natural daylight, another pic done under a light source that lacked UV (many chloroplasts dropped out of their normal streaming pattern and formed a clump at one end of the cell), and a picture of chloroplasts at rest. _"Illuminating the chloroplasts through a red filter, which allowed only the longer wavelengths of light through, resulted in some of the chloroplasts remaining in their normal streaming pattern, some totally dropping out of the pattern, and some beginning to shortcut the pattern. If a blue filter was used, allowing only the shorter wavelengths of light through, some of the chloroplasts again remained in their normal pattern, whilst those that were previously short-cutting moved to a different position prior to beginning their short cut. Dr Ott also found that when he added some long-wavelength ultraviolet light to the microscope light source, so that it more closely simulated sunlight, all the chloroplasts returned to their normal patterns. By the end of a normal day, chloroplasts, like people, gradually slow down, come to a stop and then remain motionless during the night time period. This required period of rest allows them to once again respond to light energy and resume their normal streaming pattern the following day. It appears, therefore, that altering plant cellular function by altering the light source affects the normal process of photosynthesis and the resulting cell chemistry."_
> The same book also says: _"In further studies utilizing pigment epithelial cells from the retina of a rabbit's eye, Dr Ott found that filtering out normal sunlight also caused abnormal cell function. When he used a blue filter, the cells went through all sorts of contortions, while a red filter caused apparent cell-wall weakening, followed by rupturing and the eventual death of the cell."_
> So my feeling is that any light after dark could be interfering with the "dual control" switch from "day mode" to "night mode" (perhaps 2 modes trying to cycle at the same time?) and (although I don't know about the intensity or duration of the rabbit experiments) I'm cautious about having my eyes (already have damage to one) too close to an unopposed red light source.

### Sue-UK — 2017-02-25T13:14:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-209265>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=208396)
>
> So is orange an anti glycation frequency? I'm much less glycated ..... After the Feb webinar and Reality 7, am I the human equivalent of a C4 grass...

@[lohd2015](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18338/)

Before Christmas I was using the standard Bioptron filter on my outer thighs, and had already noticed an improvement in skin condition and less glycation. The only way I can describe it is that I could see my "real" shape emerging, and my skin is smoother, less lines and more resilient with no use of lotions or oils). For my thighs it was enough that I was able to buy a pair of skinny jeans in my correct waist size that didn't make my legs look like sausages trying to burst out of their skins. Over Christmas after using the standard filter first, I was also using the Bioptron orange colour therapy gel on its own, but after that was using the Spectrochrome orange placed in front of the standard bioptron filter. I don't know the frequencies of the Bioptron orange filter, and I only know that the Spectrochrome filters are polychromatic, so I'm assuming they are both a range of orange frequencies, and the bioptron is a source of NIR). Used it most days, but now I don't crave it in the same way, its as if I don't need as much to top up, and my shape is still improving slowly. The Bioptron with the standard filter is used medically, but the colour therapy set is promoted for cosmetic purposes and people use it for chakra balancing etc. I was watching a training video for the cosmetic uses of the colour therapy set and there was a slide in the video which gave the following attributes to each filter (which would still have an NIR source). They are used after the full spectrum filter. At that time I hadn't found any studies ....
Red - energy depleted skin
Orange - stimulates fibroblasts to boost collagen and elastin production thereby eliminating fine lines and wrinkles, also used for cellulite.
Yellow - stimulates the repair of damaged cells and achieve a smoother skin
Green - regulates skin melanin synthesis and skin pigmentation _(I'm thinking age spots ....)_
Blue - reduces sebum and eliminate bacteria known to cause acne, making skin look smooth and trouble free _(I'm thinking excess (particularly without the balancing effect of NIR/red/orange) could cause dry skin and promote wrinkles)_
Indigo - for scars and stretch marks, has a calming effect on overall well being, tightening, toning and firming skin
Violet - relieves any skin inflammation and irritations, redness and itchiness.

There's an interesting study by Alexander Wunsch that I think ties in to some of the above .... my next rabbit hole is to go through the study in depth and connect the dots and I'm also thinking about the signal through the eye (for example does a non linear effect of excess exposure of unopposed blue to the eye cause the skin to reduce sebum production?).
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3926176/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3926176/)

### Sue-UK — 2017-02-26T12:27:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-209299>

So I’m putting my thoughts into my journal, in the hope that having it on record gives me some momentum to keep going, and keep researching … I’ve named the structure of my current “plan” Photodynamics, and tried to split it into sections to help me assess the value of what I’m doing, seeing where I'm lacking, and choices I’m thinking about in terms of light, water and magnetism.

**Light – natural, as lifestyle choices
**
Beneficial – sunlight in line with circadian rhythm and harvesting photons. Plus the reverse, darkness at the right time.
Neutral (the wrong word but I need a label….) – an example would be indoors with no artificial lights on, but using/losing energy at a slower rate than a more toxic light environment. Go out for regular “light breaks” to help mitigate.
Damaging – examples, staring into a strong sun, or letting myself get sunburned.

**Light – unnatural
**
Potentially beneficial – specific range of wavelengths, and set number of treatments and treatment duration, for specific purposes, taking into account circadian rhythm, and my context including being at a higher latitude. (I feel like a C4 plant trying to heal in a C3 environment …) Or mimicking sunrise frequencies when cloud cover intense in already low quantum yield. Where light directed (such as eye or skin) important because of my eye injury. Used as short term treatments, not lifestyle choices.

Neutral – may have mood lifting effects that acutely outweigh any negatives like NNemf, but chronically might not be a good idea. For example UV/collagen tanner. “Treatment” not a daily lifestyle choice.

Most damaging – most artificial lighting, computer screens, technology, tv etc. Less damaging if blue blocking etc, but it doesn’t move it to neutral. Improved artificial lighting (incandescent, DC current etc) moves it closer to neutral. (Lot of eye rolling from DH when he set that up. One small one in sitting room now for early evening, DS2 sent a pic to DD of it wearing my blocking glasses).

**Water**
Well water but I could improve intake, and stop being too lazy to use it in the kettle.

Magnetism

Grounding – need to do more. My sleep still hasn’t fully recovered since the sleep deprivation surrounding Mum’s death, but I have found that if I do wake up and can’t get back to sleep quickly, (fortunately not often now) if I go outside and ground in the dark/moonlight for 10 minutes, I can usually come back in and go back to sleep. I’m taking that as a sign I need to do better with that leg of the stool.

**Miscellaneous
**
I’ve started trying to go to bed at 9pm 5 days a week. Diet wise I’m paying attention to DHA from seafood sources and the value of seaweed, but otherwise I know it’s not about the food. Eat when hungry, and in line with the circadian rhythm. Listen to what my body is asking for and don’t feed it crap. I naturally and comfortably fast for at least 16 hours doing that. Think about the mitochondria. Don’t be distracted for too long by what my senses are saying, they aren’t always right. Something that smells/tastes/looks or "feels" damn good has its place, and occasionally is what it takes to get me out of a hole, but I'm not interested in relying on temporary “lifts” of my sensory sails, when it stops me noticing the mitochondrial ship is sinking. What does the “mitochondrial swarm” see and what does it need? Fiddling with my diet, fatty acid ratios and supplements is pissing in the wind, and a distraction from the important stuff. I’ve read an abstract of a study showing that a specific light frequency increases leptin, IL-8 and VEGF, (could be good or bad dependent on context, so I’m not jumping on that bandwagon without a lot more thought), another in relation to eyesight on a frequency of LLLT that “led to a significant improvement in visual acuity in adolescent and adult patients with amblyopia caused by ametropia or strabismus”. …..Nothing about food ….nothing about supplements (what has Jack been saying ……) Light alone (or the absence of it) can initiate a response, different frequencies initiate different responses, so I need to keep it in my head that it’s primarily about light and my mitochondria’s ability to harness the light (hello water and magnetism).

At the moment I’m in the first phase of my next eye biohack. It’s rechecking things like RF etc with my meter, but also properly assessing artificial lighting in terms of flicker, possible mercury resonance etc, deciding if I can move things from damaging to at least less damaging or neutral if I can’t avoid it completely, and getting more of the beneficial natural light and grounding. I want to see what benefits phase 1 brings before beginning phase 2, but even if I don’t see anything obvious … it’s got to give phase 2 a better chance …..? Having a phase 1 to go through also gives me a chance to give phase 2 a lot more thought , doing something instead of just standing still waiting for a eureka moment .....

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-01T18:22:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-209481>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=209279)
>
> I am really interested in your hack, especially your theory on glycation and light harvesting. Please update progress....
> No significant improvement on my pole pinging this morning. Sigh. Progress takes time.
> Love the study. Thanks Sue!

Yesterday and today I've switched my hack with the orange filter to my face and neck. (I've been checking the light frequencies both outside and with the filters with the spectroscope). Closed eyes, with some rapid blinking. From what I've seen the Orange filter doesn't block out everything else, so seems to have enough of the blue to signal in the morning, (but I'm grounding outside before and after), but going to test if the red-orange frequencies help my sight through the melanopsin chromophore regeneration pathway mentioned in the study. Intuitively if my eye damage was the result of blue light, perhaps I should be looking at the other end of the spectrum first. It will be interesting to see the effects on glycation systemically, as I won't be using it on my legs during this hack. I didn't use nicotine yesterday, but this morning used nicotine nasal spray afterwards.... Won't repeat that in a hurry ....spaced me right out for a while... I wouldn't have been safe to drive if I needed to ... (Went back to time 14....). Yesterday I spent too long on the computer, at one time I had 13 tabs open, blogs and anything eyeballs trying to follow pathways and looking up terms I couldn't remember or picture, and looking for why I'm intuitively avoiding yellow ...(pupil response? Yellow chromophores? Fovea? ).I woke up in the night after dreaming about an animation of blood flow to and from the retina .... But to Biohack it properly I've got to cut back the computer time, and stick with things I print out.

Can't do much damage control until I get to Mexico in a week or so.

I went outside and used the spectroscope to see what the frequencies were like, and I was surprised at the range of frequencies even under quite cloudy skies. I could see how I get a chronological effect, but under cloudy skies its like its not intense enough for the mood boosting (and possibly the regeneration pathway). The filter seems to give the extra oomph that is missing under cloudy skies, but from the spectroscope still seems to give similar frequencies to the natural light at that time of day, and the device gives a source of NIR. I'm wary of using limited bands of visible light, because in natural daylight its "antidote" would be available to a degree, and also I think NIR is essential for diffusion deeper in the eye. But that said I did find this article I've printed out interesting and thought you might too .... its the article in this ILA newsletter called Syntonic Phototherapy.

[http://www.international-light-association.org/pdf/members/ILA-2011-Journal\_of\_Light\_Web.pdf](http://www.international-light-association.org/pdf/members/ILA-2011-Journal_of_Light_Web.pdf)

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-02T19:26:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-209529>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=209522)
>
> Thank you Sue...fascinating article.
> There's a reference on page 11 about blue light delivered on the skin behind the knee resulting in significant changes in circadian rhythms. I have found the accupressure points behind the knee to affect pressure diuresis and hence RAS.

I was interested in the Nitric Oxide reference at the top of page 12 ..... Another door in a low UV environment?

I've gone back to this I posted earlier:

"I've been thinking about it in terms of photosynthesis (and human photosynthesis). I've seen pictures (light Medicine of the Future by Jacob Liberman) of how chloroplasts stream under natural daylight, another pic done under a light source that lacked UV (many chloroplasts dropped out of their normal streaming pattern and formed a clump at one end of the cell), and a picture of chloroplasts at rest. _"Illuminating the chloroplasts through a red filter, which allowed only the longer wavelengths of light through, resulted in some of the chloroplasts remaining in their normal streaming pattern, some totally dropping out of the pattern, and some beginning to shortcut the pattern. If a blue filter was used, allowing only the shorter wavelengths of light through, some of the chloroplasts again remained in their normal pattern, whilst those that were previously short-cutting moved to a different position prior to beginning their short cut. Dr Ott also found that when he added some long-wavelength ultraviolet light to the microscope light source, so that it more closely simulated sunlight, all the chloroplasts returned to their normal patterns. By the end of a normal day, chloroplasts, like people, gradually slow down, come to a stop and then remain motionless during the night time period. This required period of rest allows them to once again respond to light energy and resume their normal streaming pattern the following day. It appears, therefore, that altering plant cellular function by altering the light source affects the normal process of photosynthesis and the resulting cell chemistry."_
The same book also says: _"In further studies utilizing pigment epithelial cells from the retina of a rabbit's eye, Dr Ott found that filtering out normal sunlight also caused abnormal cell function. When he used a blue filter, the cells went through all sorts of contortions, while a red filter caused apparent cell-wall weakening, followed by rupturing and the eventual death of the cell.""_

This and something Alexander Wunsch said in a video had me thinking about mitochondria in the eye. It started with would an excess of blue or red light (or other colour? why am I cautious about yellow?) in the eye cause the mitochondria to move, or change position in some way, and effectively stop human photosynthesis in the eye? This moved on to could mitochondrial change of angle or position be the equivalent of a magnolia tree leaf changing its angle of inclination, because of what is being sensed by its chloroplast "eyes". Off to rest my human chloroplasts .....

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-03T11:27:21+0200

I think unopposed blue light behind the knees is pretty risky, because of the blood circulation taking it to my eye. Unopposed green is another I'm now very cautious of. When I was on holiday I took my Dinshah green filter, and one day spent 10 minutes looking through it at around noon. It was nice, and seemed to soothe my eyes (like taking a break in the shade) but something stopped me doing it again. I think its a pro growth frequency .... for example a plant at the bottom of the forest floor may be using the green canopy above as a signal to grow towards the light. I found this article on green light for wound healing [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22380691](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22380691) and the VEGF reference caught my eye.... A look at the VEGF in disease section on the wiki entry for VEGF rings bells with my intuition about being careful with using it, especially unopposed...

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-03T16:33:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-209556>

For the last few weeks DH has got serious about blue blocking at night.  Getting up at night to pee is much rarer. I was watching a programme about brown bears which said they don't urinate during hibernation (cold, dark, no food....) and instead recycle the nitrogen in order to maintain muscle mass. [https://www.nps.gov/yell/learn/nature/denning.htm](https://www.nps.gov/yell/learn/nature/denning.htm). Interesting about the fat liberation ...Linking it back to sleep in humans (cold, dark, no food after sunset, less food in winter) and ubiquitination ... First stop back to Ubi 12 nitrogen cycle ecodynamics.

I was also amazed at the amount of urine I could hold after a really cold night's sleep. I can only recall similar incidences when I was a teenager. Waiting for muscle mass to increase.....

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-04T11:33:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-209599>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=209279)
>
> I am really interested in your hack, especially your theory on glycation and light harvesting. Please update progress....

I've done the orange on the face and neck for a couple of days, but the neck more in passing IYKWIM, depending on where the filter is on my face or more as a reflected source. I haven't deliberately done my neck. I have a couple of skin tags on the side of my neck (one on the left and two on the right). They are not big, and one of the two on the right is smaller. But I noticed the smaller one was a bit painful, so I got a magnifying mirror and had a good look. (Depressing when magnified ...). But the smaller one was showing a bit of inflammation and swelling, like a spot just before it erupts ... I saw it as the chronic becoming acute during healing, and I think it is starting to shrink. So I'm going to deliberately target the one on the left of my neck, for 2 x 10 minute sessions a day and see what happens. (I did get shrinkage when I was using cold packs round the neck to cool the carotids to help sleep - but the downside of that is that it was causing the fat round my neck to disappear. Great if I was carrying excess fat there, but I learned the hard way that turkey neck isn't a good look. ).

The spectroscope has been a real gem ...... What didn't register with me  until I started playing with it and looking at the filters through it, is that I am not treating myself with orange light, (a limited range of frequencies in the orange range with a visual orange signal) I am using an orange filter, which is more about what it allows through, and doesn't let through, and just happens to visually look orange ..(like amber blue blockers). For example it doesn't look visually red, but it allows the red frequencies through....(mitochondria alert ...). So any results I have are not about orange alone ... So I'm now going to use the spectroscope on all the Bioptron filters and the Dinshah ones, and write down what they exclude, how the 2 systems of colour filters differ, and any potential chronological or mitochondrial effects of the excluded frequencies.

Will report back - hopefully with a nicer looking neck.

Skin tags are somewhat strange experiences for me.
I'd like to say they exhibited quantum zeno effects. Many years ago, I had one HUGE one, which I attempted to remove by tying a floss around it. I worked on it for close to a year. Then a change in my daily habits did not allow me the the time to continue the routine. After some time had elapsed (maybe a year or two?) I suddenly realized that the tag had completely vanished. Without a trace, no mark, no scar, no discoloration.
Same experiences with smaller ones that I would find. They usually disappear if I don't focus on them.
Lately I have one on my neck (also LOL), that I can't resist playing with. It almost feel like it's going to fall off....but if past experience is a good barometer, I should really just forget about it....

More of an issue with me ... I have several under my arms and this blog [https://www.jackkruse.com/what-is-biologic-concrete/](https://www.jackkruse.com/what-is-biologic-concrete/) was an eye opener. CT reduced them considerably in size and number, and hopefully lessened my risk of breast cancer, but its still a work in progress, and now also using light. I am looking at them (and glycation generally) more in terms of the heteroplasmy rates of the surrounding tissue.

Had a breakthrough yesterday with why I keep getting nudged about yellow .... I think it (or its absence) has more chronobiological effect than I realised. I spent ages yesterday looking at a diagram of the human spectral sensitivity to colour, and a diagram comparing the frequencies of light when the sun was under or over the horizon. Preparing my next biohack ....

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-05T15:45:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-209663>

A continuing train of thought about yellow …..

That the frequencies of first light, when the sun is below the horizon but slowly takes out the stars (love that time of day) as it rises isn’t the same as last light, after the sun has sunk below the horizon. About different implications to diurnal and nocturnal mammals. It couldn’t just be about blue? Noticed on a wavelength chart that the yellow part of the spectrum is quite small, wondered what was its chronological significance. Although I think full moon also affects the “tide” of water in a cell, I wondered if the yellow light aspect contributed to my sleep sucking at last full moon (1 lux, so it doesn’t take much ….) (Need to further blackout one window in bedroom which isn’t as blacked out as the others).

Amber glasses may mimic sunrise and sunset to a certain extent, but don’t account for any differences between first and last light. What are the chronobiological implications of wearing amber glasses for extended periods during the day, (I prefer to cover screens in amber filters for daytime use rather than use amber glasses which stop me getting indoor natural light). OK I’m not doing amber glasses during the day whilst I'm avoiding artificial light, but what I was doing going from amber glasses straight to complete darkness. Am I missing any transition signals? I also thought that if the brain could compute the amount of UV by the amount of blue, what other calculations was it making using other visible colours?

Looked at the human spectral sensitivity to colour and looked for peaks in red and green cone sensitivity to the yellow frequency range. Then at rod sensitivity – how much is unsubtracted yellow impairing my adaptation to night vision?

Remembered green at 518nm raising leptin. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22380691](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22380691). Does the green in the light from the sun below the horizon after sunset influence leptin receptors? Could other colours that should be subtracted affect the transition?

Finally found a diagram comparing the spectral frequencies of “average” daylight when the sun is 6 degrees below and above the horizon. _“In addition to the pronounced difference in the amount of light, there is also a substantial change in spectral composition, with reduced middle wavelength (green-yellow) light at negative solar angles. “_
Then found

From this paper: [http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article/file?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1002160&type=printable](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article/file?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.1002160&type=printable))
Would unsubtracted yellow at night affect the circadian cycle of the liver (solar organ) of a diurnal mammal, by delaying or missing a transition phase? Could it affect a circadian rhythm of long loop of DHA to the RPE? How would it affect regeneration pathways? Ability to dream? Etc etc etc As usual more questions than answers .... but I'm going to hack it and see what happens .....

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-08T18:55:54+0200

I'm not sure about recycled, but was wondering if a mammal was fattened up and well hydrated when it enters hibernation, the cold would shrink the ETC, and fat burning would produce water, which could provide enough water to meet the lower demands of energy, slower heart beat, breathing etc, plus for a female, provide water for milk production for any cubs born. In life in shaping life, there's a bit on photon emission in mammalian milk, so I'm thinking of body fat as a photon storage unit, photon emissions and water for either sex, and for a female, the milk as a supply of photons to the offspring.

The programme I saw also had a piece about polar bears .... It showed a male on the freezing ice, and showed how it's fur is like crystalline reflectors, but in reverse. The IR the bear loses is reflected back off the white fur and re absorbed through its black skin.

Because I don't think we are in hibernation mode all night, I expect to produce some urine, but if I wake up thirsty in the night, I doubt I've fat burned .....

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-09T12:24:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-209938>

Update on my sleep biohack ....

Early evening – standard blue blocking, amber glasses
Later evening – same but using standard sunglasses underneath to reduce light intensity. Further blacked out one curtain, but there is a “bleed” of moonlight under the bottom (which I can get rid of if necessary, but standard -6 sun angle includes a bit of yellow).
Before going to bed go into darkened bedroom, have 10 minutes with a purple filter in front of a low level halogen light source. The set up includes very saturated colours of purples, blues and into the green. The room stays very dark. Omits all green yellow, yellow and orange, but has deeper levels of red. The halogen gives a source of NIR. Use a cold migraine cap to simulate drop in temperature at night.

Results – Night 1, felt the light frequencies as cooling the whole body, rather than just the cold cap cooling the head. Eyes started to water, but not excessively, as if it was stimulating tear glands. Got straight into bed, my skin surface felt quite cool. Didn’t fall asleep immediately, (I wasn’t experiencing sleep pressure before I started the treatment), but just lay there in a more relaxed meditative state rather than mentally still active. Went to sleep, woke once but went back to sleep quickly, and woke up again at first light. Went outside at first light and grounded until the sun had risen.
Night 2, as night 1, Eyes still watered, still felt cold, but not as intensely as night 1. Same 1 wake up in the night, felt calm and meditative, went back to sleep quickly, up at first light for first light exposure and watching the sun rise.
Night 3 – my brother was round until later, and I got a bit more exposure to evening unnatural light than normal. Same routine, but didn’t feel it had been as good a night’s sleep.
Night 4 – same routine, but this time checked my pulse with a pulse oxymeter at the start of the treatment, and at the end. Pulse drop, but going to try it again after waiting quietly in front of the light for a few minutes before turning it on tonight to get a more accurate result on the light’s effect on pulse rate. Lay down and checked my pulse again, and again before I got out of bed this morning, and when I first stood up and took 3 steps to look out of the window. I woke once, deliberately didn’t open my eyes, and went back to sleep. I was very hungry when I woke up (previous days hunger came earlier in the day than normal) but this morning I felt like a bear straight out of hibernation in search of food. Craved eggs and mushrooms …

### lohd2015 — 2017-03-10T01:56:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-209999>

I really don't know what to think about being thirsty in the middle of the night. I would like to think I am basically fat adapted because frankly, I got not much fat left on me...Lately I've been very thirsty at night. I did go through a period when I was not. So I am just assuming the body is going though cycles, as everything, including healing moves in spirals. Each spiral hopefully takes you to a higher level.

The tearing of the eyes is of special interest to me. I find that if I cannot tear during my nighttime routines (massage, breathing, meditation), I usually would not be able to fall asleep that quickly. It is an excellent indicator for me whether I will sleep well that night or not. So in general, I would try to get myself to relax to the point of tearing before attempting to fall asleep.

### caroline — 2017-03-11T02:24:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210087>

I like the idea of a slim line tank......

when we were away camping the last few weeks - I only pee once during the night - sometimes not at all. Since we have been home the last few days - I am up multiple times during the night....not happy about this!

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-11T14:47:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210108>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=210027)

> Question for you ..... if you are lying down in the dark with your eyes closed .... what do you see on the black screen?

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=210087)
>
> While camping the evenings were quite cool - at home our bedroom is too warm because we don't get any cross ventilation......so all nite long my core temp rises and I need to kick the sheet off. Then I feel a chill and pull the sheet over me again.

These sort of connect with the next stage of my current biohack ...... This is where my brain is taking me ......

I was intuitively reminded of something that happened to me as a child. One of my earliest memories is of waking up in the night and being frightened by the little sparkles of light I could see in the complete darkness of the room. It was getting worse as I worried about it, and I started crying. I remember my Dad coming in, and reassuring me that everything was OK and I went back to sleep. But I think I was seeing photon emissions from my eyes, and the more stressed I got, the more it increased. So if I wake up in the night, I’m trying to keep my eyes closed as much as possible. When I do check it for a few seconds, I’m interested to see if it’s more obvious – I can’t emit photons I don’t have ….? (Life in shaping light – Light emission from fingertips declines with age...... ). Now I’m looking at light emission from my eyes at night versus the emission and absorption of sunlight during the day in relation to the info in Time 22. Is it pushing or pulling differently ....... Need to go back to the October webinar and find any other related blogs.

I was driving myself nuts after reading about darkness and red light increasing IOP, trying to work out why the body would deliberately increase IOP at night .... Then I wondered if it was to deliver more light to the eyeball at night for repair. With the eyes closed at night, light emission to the environment might be related to the pushing/pulling of figure 1 in time 22, with possibly the blood circulation in the closed eyelid “recycling" the emitted bit back into the body. Is the emission outwards "bleaching" any yellowing of the lens for example? (A bear doesn’t come out of hibernation blind or myopic, so what is happening?) How much does lying awake in the dark with eyes open (emitting but not absorbing) affect the process of regeneration? Was it concerned with the brainwaves generated from lying down with eyes closed, or did it rely on a specific stage of sleep? Does IOP drop as melatonin rises, or is there a sharp rise or drop at midnight? Does that make it even more important to have my eyes closed in “lights out” mode by 10 pm, even if I’m not asleep? Or does IOP need to be high for REM sleep? Still more questions than answers but I found and printed out this article
[http://www.chronobiology.ch/wp-content/uploads/publications/2001\_16.pdf](http://www.chronobiology.ch/wp-content/uploads/publications/2001_16.pdf),
then printed out figure 2,
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-3-11_12-25-28-png.4506/)
and a picture from google images of the core and shell. (I need pictures to connect dots so I can “look” into my body more easily).
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-3-11_12-26-48-png.4507/)

So then I was wondering about IOP being raised when the sleep cycle was core dominant, and its relationship to pulse ...It was why I started checking my pulse, and I’m trying not to get up in the night…. My hack is that I am trying to get – and keep -the right conditions for the proximal and distal skin temperatures to fluctuate at the right time to enter the right phases of sleep.

I am also thinking that if the distal/proximal temperatures are out of sync, it will affect my ability to harvest photons collected on the skin (shell) surface in the day, (if I’ve collected any) and/or (in a low quantum yield), the ability of my blood to deliver photons released into the blood from my fat cells, to the “shell” mitochondria. A bear in darkness in hibernation or in extreme cold on the ice has the skin covering and fur to recycle the photons/heat emitted from the shell surface. Does hair on the human head protect core temp and recycle photons at night in the same way? At my latitude can I optimize both systems …?

Last night I did my light therapy, went to sleep quickly, lots of vivid dreams, but woke up early (about 4 am) really hot at the skin surface. Not sure if my system was responding at the right time but just being a bit over zealous, or if my leptin receptors were saying "how much fat is she holding .....turn up the furnace ....) The search continues .....

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-11T15:13:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210110>

My other interest in IOP (and glycation generally) is from this …….
[http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0009711](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0009711)

At first glance it looks like a potential treatment for cataracts and AGEs in the eye, but intuitively I thought it had much more to offer. The study was done on human organ donor lenses, possible negative effects on lenses in situ blah blah. But what interested me was the 405 frequency and, even more, that (800nm) _“The observation of blue auto fluorescence indicates that the photobleaching was mediated by two photon absorption of the infra-red laser light leading to ultraviolet equivalent effects in the focal region. Multiphoton effects have a step-like progression with increasing laser intensities from no effects over photobleaching to material ablation and tissue disruption.”
_
I think Mother Nature already has this covered ….. but perhaps because we don’t live under a red sun (possible material ablation and tissue disruption from too much of a fake red sun?), maybe it’s from the inside out, to protect the cones and RPE ….. So can the eye heal (or glycation reduce in the body generally) if it’s the bloodstream delivering 405 and/or multiphoton 800nm? (Does a higher ratio of that frequency increase the quantum probability of a multiphoton entanglement? Does it need the absence of the observer (unconsciousness of sleep?) Would an excess of unopposed red into the bloodstream be able to cause sufficient bleaching to damage the RBCs?) Do I like my orange filter for glycation because it’s got the red and IR – and the orange it contains avoids an overdose or just lifts my spirits? If 405 and multiphoton IR can heal the eye from the bloodstream, I want to know if there’s a circadian rhythm to it, (the pushing pulling phenomena of Time 22?) and does that rhythm require the higher IOP of darkness?

So many dots ….. not enough connections …..
