Sue, the dots are what will make the connections...in time...when you are ready. Some people have no or very few dots.....
I love reading your dots, and thanks for the links to the research.

As for your question regarding what I see on the 'black screen', I closed my eyes in the dark after I read your question (it was before dawn) and was surprised that I couldn't see anything. Like you, I used to see all sorts of lights darting around, sometimes I would see patterns, weaving in and out. But that was with eyes closed. I believe you saw the lights with your eyes opened?
So I thought I'd give it another go last night before I fall asleep, but my new biohack is so effective that I fell asleep very quickly. I will attempt it again tonight.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-12T09:59:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210179>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=210158)
>
> As for your question regarding what I see on the 'black screen', I closed my eyes in the dark after I read your question (it was before dawn) and was surprised that I couldn't see anything. Like you, I used to see all sorts of lights darting around, sometimes I would see patterns, weaving in and out. But that was with eyes closed. I believe you saw the lights with your eyes opened?

Yes I do, don't remember a time since my dream when I didn't. I thought everyone could .. I can be walking through the house in the dark and I still see it. Just haven't paid much attention to it over the years in terms of how strong the phenomena was, or whether it was changing. I see it in daylight behind closed lids, but the effect is different because of the light coming back in through the lid, or from the bloodstream in the lid. Then its more about the change and any movement of colour on the back screen, which subtly alters depending on time of day, and I love sunning my eyes with closed lids. In the dark I regularly see purple on the screen. I've started paying attention to it now, because there's a definite lowering of the effect on one part of the screen, which I think is a patch of damage on my retina. If I've been using the computer too long, if I close my eyes for a few seconds, I can see the photic irritation on the edges of the patch, so I'm trying to stop using the computer before it starts ...Taking regular long blinks to inspect the back of my eyelids.  My night time treatment is subtly improving the patch, and I can see a change in my vision in that spot in daylight now if I close the other eye and just look through the damaged one.

With eyes open in daylight, there is too much light from outside, and I don't see the sparkle phenomena. I only remember seeing someone's aura once, at a conference of healers/dowsers/meditators, where I think the collective brainwave entrainment and the not too bright light in the room gave my brain the right conditions to be able to see it ... (Amazing experience....). It would be cool to hack my way to being able to see one again .....

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-12T13:29:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210183>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=210089)
>
> Good point Sue. But I think in my case, the rate of fat generation is not keeping up with the rate I burn them. I know I can generate fat. (I have some lose skin to prove it). But how to get the slimline tank to operate optimally....now that is my goal also. I would love some fat (in the right places...!)

I was watching a programme that included a piece on a man who had no subcutaneous fat ..... so any he made went to his visceral fat, increasing his risk of heart disease, diabetes blah blah. He spends several hours a day exercising to keep his visceral fat under control. (Not an easy situation but I did start thinking what was overexercising doing to his heteroplasmy rates .....).

If the SCN - liver signalling is out of whack, I think we lose fat in the wrong order. I've experienced it myself, (but the dots weren't there at the time) and see it sometimes in a woman who has lost weight and it seems great on the scales. Seeing myself naked should have showed me that I wasn't just losing it from where I wanted it to go from first, for a good reason. Instead my focus was on the scales. My belly, hips and thighs would resist it, but I lost breast tissue, and looked haggard if I stuck to a diet for too long. Once stopped, the tanks would refill, with a bit extra just in case.  I eventually got the message, stopped dieting and researched why, before I joined, but it was reversing the damage that led me here. Now if I see a woman who has lost a lot of weight, even clothed I can see how their bodies are responding to an SCN-liver mismatch. I don't see it in a naturally slim woman, but if its been a strict chronic diet whilst leptin resistant, the face has dropped .....

But it got me thinking I need more subcutaneous fat because I'm in a lower quantum yield. (Thinking of the pics in Simon's report of his trip ....). In a high quantum yield perhaps its about having the right amount of visceral fat (and the ability to tap into it at night and refill it during the day - the liver as a solar organ). Working properly, it would leave the "right" amount of fat in the right places, based on the need for less in a higher yield environment. A circadian mismatch in a high quantum yield might prevent the liver from releasing visceral fat, and to protect the fat needed for reproduction etc, the body may take subcutaneous fat and other tissue.

Perhaps after menopause the system changes when the tanks are empty ....Looking at an extreme - in the same circadian mismatch in a low quantum yield in an already thin woman with high heteroplasmy (my mother, who deliberately kept herself thin), the effects I witnessed were devastating. She wasn't getting the right signals, or harvesting photons, and eventually the little she had in her very slimline tank was used up, her muscle became her fuel, and her progression into Alzheimers speeded up. Although it wasn't a dementia unit, when I visited her (and my FIL the year before she went in), I noticed that of those there, the skinnier the resident, (no stored photons?) and the more physically mobile they were under stress, (emitting more?) the more cognitive decline was noticeable, and how it progressed over time.

I haven't CT'd for a couple of weeks, because I'm interested in seeing what focusing on the circadian cycle during sleep will do on its own .... Now I've got a better idea of that, I'm going back to it in a different way. (Refilled the baths yesterday). Since Mum's funeral I haven't gone back to work, just do a couple of hours a week when no-one is there, and a bit of admin at home. I think people think I'm grieving for Mum, but I'm taking time out away from people, and focusing on what I need to do for me. I've stayed connected to people that give me a 1+1 =3 or more, but I'm guarding my energy. Giving myself time to think - and research and study - without distractions. I actually feel now that if I can get enough dots connected, and just as importantly actually do the work I need to do, I can alter my destiny. I am not doomed to follow the path my Mum and grandmother went down, they went down that path as a result of choices they made. I will be 60 this year, and I have time to make different choices, and act on them, and hopefully not put my daughter through watching me going down the same road, or see it as her own inevitable destiny. And that makes me feel sooooo much better.

### caroline — 2017-03-13T05:13:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210226>

I love following your journey Sue .... you are a very special woman.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-13T16:45:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210250>

My brain is now in this direction ....

I’ve been looking at a picture of the magnetosphere in relation to the sleep cycle on and off for months, and how the earth turns and enters and exits the “tail” at night. The liver as a solar organ dot now has me thinking about the daytime crescendo and decrescendo solar signal to the liver (slight pause at midday significant?) versus the night time decrescendo to a midnight maximum subtraction (midnight pause significant?), before the slow crescendo into the next cycle of additive solar influence. (Interesting in relation to TCM organ clock and 5 elements).

I’d buy a magnetico in a heartbeat if I lived in London, or in a high NNEMF environment. But I live in the sticks, ground floor, I don’t pick up wifis from neighbours etc, and I’ve in the past been intuitively drawn to trying to use the constantly changing natural environment if I can, because I think the cells respond to change in frequency, not just frequency i.e. a fake 8 hour noon from using my reptile lamp may be providing some frequencies I need, but could be offset by the circadian mismatch of an 8 hour noon and a lack of crescendo and decrescendo. I’ve wondered about how an extended fake noon and an extended fake sunset affect the assimilation of harvested photons at night. My mitochondria might think I’m on a different planet …..

My ten minute light therapy with the (hopefully) minus solar angle frequencies is designed to give me a different signal to the extended fake sunset of my blue blockers. But I intuitively think that it is a combination of sleep related signals – including the magnetosphere - that make it seem like it is working, the results might not be the same or as obvious in a different sleep environment.

Provided I am relaxed and calm, my pulse when I lay down is around 60 bpm, so in deeper sleep it should drop down and between the pulses, my cells will be quieter, especially as the need for ATP is much lower. If this – and cooling and the magnetosphere - can help my brain tell the time, if my respiratory proteins in my cells aren’t inflamed, I’m hoping that the combination of factors will help leptin to enter the leptin receptors. Interesting that in the 5 elements system the liver is green, connection again to the study showing green frequencies cause leptin to rise (and IL8 and VEGF). [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22380691](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22380691)

This train of thought led to: Do the frequencies of light stimulated by eating after sunset, or the wrong frequencies from artificial light at night “dilute” the frequencies the liver needs at night, stopping a natural cycle of leptin, IL8 and VEGF in regeneration generally? Or the reverse, if all 3 were high permanently, (no proper crescendo to a daytime solar maximum and decrescendo to a midnight minimum), is it holding the system in a state of permanent growth (obesity, cancer), or over stimulating immune function? Then what about the liver itself, does it need to experience a solar minimum signal to regenerate itself?

Maybe I'm just juggling too many dots .....

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-13T19:44:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210269>

Another update on sleep biohack.

Sleep seems to be improving - major sign was the full moon last night (and the lead up before) hasn't affected me. Not seeing it (and the colour it emits even at 1 lux) seems to make a difference. I was reminded of something in Lyall Watson's book Supernature. _"The Hughes Aircraft Laboratory in California has developed a "tilt meter" so sensitive that it has been able to record lunar tides in a cup of tea._" So does a hibernating animal or a dormant plant under snow "count" the number of full moon lunar tides signalled through the water in its cells, to time when it should come out of hibernation or start its spring response?

Woke up early this morning but it was getting light, the birds were tweeting and a couple of pheasants were having a loud conversation outside my bedroom window.  There's been a gradual shift in my waking mood - more contented feeling, looking forward to the day. It seems to be becoming the norm, not just a one off bonus every so often. Feel rested, even on less sleep (expected in longer light cycles). Perhaps I'm tapping into the midnight to 4 am hibernation zone more often .... whatever it is, I'll take it.

Very interesting insight regarding subcutaneous fat and visceral fat. So extrapolating those ideas....I am currently in a high quantum yield environment and am trying my best to be in sync with my circadian signals. All signs are good for now...will need time to see what happens. But one thing I have noticed is I am starting to put back a tiny bit of muscles and maybe (just MAYBE) a hint of subcutaneous fat.

I have been looking at my 'black screen' for the past dawns/nights. I do notice sometimes that the screen has some very faint patterns in either light green or purple. The colors are very faint. I can't really make out the patterns as they are so faint.

Two of my friends in the U.K. also lost their mums in the past two months. One is a really good friend. I was just talking to someone on the beach this morning about how one deals with the severed energetic bonds upon the death or the end of relationships. I think it is really important to focus on what to do with the energy. What you are doing is wonderful, and healing, probably for you, your mum, and for your daughter in the future. Keep up the great work and do know that what you do here affects a lot of people. Me included. Thank you Sue, for being you.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-14T11:21:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210311>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=210281)
>
> I have been looking at my 'black screen' for the past dawns/nights. I do notice sometimes that the screen has some very faint patterns in either light green or purple. The colors are very faint. I can't really make out the patterns as they are so faint.

Mine is like a galaxy of tiny twinkling stars. When I do see colours its like a moving nebula in the galaxy, mostly purple but occasionally something else, but its a fairly subtle phenomena in darkness with eyes closed. In the day the light through the closed lid (as opposed to just from the bloodstream) makes the phenomena different. I know I'm visually sensitive (just hadn't connected it to seeing photons in the dark...). For instance I have never been able to watch scary movies, (I went to a movie over 20 years ago, thinking it wasn't going to be scary, and I spent most of the time with a coat over my head ). I have never liked tv shows where they give - however short - a strong visual of any form of assault, or threatening behaviour. Anything like that at night could keep me awake for hours. I thought that its because although my left brain "knows" its not real, its only a movie or tv show, my visual system acts "as if" it is real, and leaks energy (photons), and I'm especially sensitive. However interesting a plot is, a book that conjures up unpleasant images in my mind has the same effect .... When my redox was better when I was younger that sensitivity was a blessing. I could anticipate visually what was likely to happen if I didn't take evasive action immediately, and in a guided meditation I would "see" in the now what the meditation guide said a few seconds later, a bit like walking round a corner in front of him, seeing what he was about to describe. In a low redox state I lost the gift, (but its coming back) and I realise that watching my mother over the last 2 years of her life was a major source of photon leakage via my visual system. Now its up to me ..... the environment my visual system is faced with from now on, will determine if my extra sensitivity goes back to being a gift, or if it sinks back into becoming a curse.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-14T12:18:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210312>

An example of reclaiming my visual system ....

I was out at first light this morning, sat on my animal skin, and visually taking in the environment. Something was nagging at me .... I know I am missing something ..... So I closed my eyes and asked Mother Nature to show me what I was missing. I had a visual flash of myself laid out on my animal skins, grounding, sleeping/resting in the early morning light of a beautiful morning, nude, with a cotton cellular blanket on, for some warmth in the cold morning air, but which will still let photons through. Good spring weather early morning camping here I come .... skins and a blanket are now ready by the door.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-14T16:53:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210336>

Keep up the great work and do know that what you do here affects a lot of people. Me included. Thank you Sue, for being you.

I love the input of dots and encouragement from you and other forum members (thank you..) ....It helps, and even if I'm talking complete nonsense being able to verbalise through my journal gives me an outlet, instead of thoughts and dots just going round and round my head ....

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=210281)
>
> Very interesting insight regarding subcutaneous fat and visceral fat. So extrapolating those ideas....I am currently in a high quantum yield environment and am trying my best to be in sync with my circadian signals. All signs are good for now...will need time to see what happens. But one thing I have noticed is I am starting to put back a tiny bit of muscles and maybe (just MAYBE) a hint of subcutaneous fat.

Sounds like a good sign, do you have definite seasons, or is the yield high all year?

With very definite seasons, animals at this latitude mate in the crescendoing of spring light, despite being lean and possibly still little food, relying on light harvesting on the skin surfaces, water and magnetism, as if _anticipating_ the abundance to follow. The decrescendo of light after the summer solstice, the anticipation of winter .... (stored abundance, fat, cold, and/or hibernation).

I'm thinking that if my circadian cycle and my leptin receptors are out of whack, its like a year round winter "survival mode." I don't think I've been in survival mode for a long time, but I need the leptin sensitivity to be ongoing, not so intermittent because of not entering the hibernation zone every single night ..... Once I'm doing that regularly (and I go to the natural leanness of Spring), I can hopefully go into "anticipation mode." I want the changing light foreseeing the future, rather than taking a survival default. If I start off lean, putting on a bit of body fat in the right places at the right time of year is a good thing at my latitude. But I definitely don't want the store store store survival mode of an unchanging high summer signal of artificial blue light .... Not with my especially sensitive eyeballs ....

### lohd2015 — 2017-03-15T01:42:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210372>

The decrescendo of light after the summer solstice, the anticipation of winter .... (stored abundance, fat, cold, and/or hibernation).
>

I don't think I've been in survival mode for a long time, but I need the leptin sensitivity to be ongoing, not so intermittent because of not entering the hibernation zone every single night ..... Once I'm doing that regularly (and I go to the natural leanness of Spring), I can hopefully go into "anticipation mode." I want the changing light foreseeing the future, rather than taking a survival default. If I start off lean, putting on a bit of body fat in the right places at the right time of year is a good thing at my latitude. But I definitely don't want the store store store survival mode of an unchanging high summer signal of artificial blue light .... Not with my especially sensitive eyeballs ....

Where I am now, in the northern part of Yucatan Mexico, there is no real distinctive seasons. I am wearing about the same clothes during the 'winter' season here as I did last summer around June. It may be a bit more windy and I see people dressed in long sleeves and jackets. I guess the locals feel the difference, but to me, every day is summer here.

I can't imagine having such sensitive sight as you. If I were you, I'd be really afraid to watch scary movies...or read scary books also. I sometimes sense beings/energies around me and I am extremely grateful I don't see anything. I sometimes hear things, but they are always of a benevolent nature. If anything, the intention is always to help me. But if I don't listen..... like I'd be going out and I hear "Take an umbrella". I look at the sky, all clear and bright blue. So I go nah......and guess what.

I can relate to what you felt regarding your redox and your sight sensitivities. I am trying to hack this aspect myself (with my hearing sensitivities) in the Yucatan. Will see how things go.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-15T12:49:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210392>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=210372)
>
> I sometimes hear things, but they are always of a benevolent nature. If anything, the intention is always to help me. But if I don't listen..... like I'd be going out and I hear "Take an umbrella". I look at the sky, all clear and bright blue. So I go nah......and guess what.

In myself I would see that as coming from my own expanded awareness in that moment, calculating future probabilities. A risk calculator? I have more visual sensitivity than auditory, so it would more likely present itself as an image than an internal whisper. My reaction depends on the consequences of not acting on it. So out in the natural environment if an expanded sense told me to run NOW, the potential consequences of not doing it are different, better be safe and wrong, than sorry. But if I saw a high probability of rain later, it is a probability not a definite, and the worse that can happen from ignoring the precognition is I'd get wet, so I'd probably go nah.... as well.

As you hack your auditory sense, it would be interesting to see what effect it has on your tinnitus.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-15T14:18:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210397>

I woke up this morning at around 5, big difference in the photon screen. The only way I can describe it was that the twinkling was brighter, and there was a vivid moving purple nebula. It was as if instead of looking at stars on a night sky, I was closer to the stars and a nebula. I lay there with closed eyes and just watched for a while. It was lighter when I went out, cloudy and with just a hint of drizzle, more like early morning dew. So I put the skins so that I could lie on my side facing east, and got under the cellular blanket. It wasn't warm enough so I pulled my dressing gown over me too. Just rested for an hour, sometimes eyes open, sometimes not. At the end it had been too cloudy to see the sunrise, but it didn't seem to matter. The outside of my dressing gown was slightly damp, but it hadn't soaked through. It reminded me of an animal getting damp on its water resistant fur. It was starting to drizzle a bit more when I came in, but it was a good non stressful experience, some early morning light, and more surface connection to the earth's magnetic field.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-17T17:41:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210533>

Wednesday night I was awoken from a vivid dream, and it took me a while to get back to sleep, and I slept later. Camped for about 45 minutes when I got up, under a grey sky. The dream was strange, not the dream itself but there was a running commentary, describing what I could see in the dream. Last night I slept until nearly 5 am this morning, and was out just at first light, watching the predawn light start taking out the stars, very slowly at first and then it speeded up. It was interesting to see what time the birds started tweeting, then what time they started moving around the sky. I was thinking of the predawn light crescendo giving one signal to the nocturnal, a different one to me. My meditation had me thinking about light emission from the fingertips, and one hand being dominant. Mine is the right, so that got me thinking about whether my right eye was the same .... and therefore potentially a higher photon leak source ... Crown chakra right eye connection, violet/purple ....

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-18T16:23:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210597>

A weird dream woke me up at 12.30 (I went to bed at 8), and kept me awake for a while, and I was lying there musing and looking at the screen and had the thought what does nicotine do to it. I got my nicotine nasal spray and used it. Massive mistake, but at least from the patterns it produced I've found out not to use it again day or night (not that its often). ...It was a bad visual trip in my right eye particularly, that I wouldn't have noticed in daytime, made worse by stressing out about it in the dark of night. (Horrifying at the time, but interesting looking back. OK I was a complete idiot, but look for the gift, look for the gift). By 2am I was out in the rain grounding, then had to wander round trying to find DS's dog who is here for the weekend and came out with me. She came up behind me, whoofed her presence and I nearly jumped out of my skin. By 4 am the visuals had eased off and I was back in bed in a migraine cap and slipped into sleep until about 7. Lesson learned the hard way.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-20T16:05:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210748>

More weird dreams ... in one I was looking at the cones in my eyes as if through a microscope. In another the dream went forward, and then it was as if it was a video rewinding in slow motion.  I then got thinking of the John Ott's pictures on photosynthetic streaming captured on video under a microscope being rewound. So I had a look on ebay and found a 1000X USB microscope  Not for my eye cones  but I did think it would be interesting to see if I can capture photosynthetic streaming in a living plant leaf under natural sunlight, and then see what happens if I use say my Bioptron full spectrum, or a reptile lamp etc. Or next to a mobile phone or .....or .... What will 1000x show on my skin .... a vein ..... a drop of blood .............. The hack list is growing .....

Ooh I do hope its going to be as much fun as I think it will. At least the anticipation is good for my dopamine levels.

### caroline — 2017-03-21T03:22:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210814>

let us all promise to never ever stand still...... moving forward is our only option!

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-21T09:33:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-210827>

I seem to be needing less sleep. 5 hours with vivid dreams seems to be enough at the moment, perhaps partly because its spring. I think its the 10 minutes of light therapy before bed, with its subtraction of yellow. When I'm out at first light the sky lightens, but the slow crescendo of yellow appears before the orange and red. When it reaches a certain level, the birds start chirping, even before sunrise. I like to think the birds help call the sunrise in.  I was out at 4 a.m. this morning, lying on the ground looking at the moon and the stars. I've started keeping as warm as I need to, not trying to make it an air CT time, I stay out longer. Just make sure I'm grounded underneath, and then cosy up under the blankets. But I was looking first at the moon, (the light reflected being more in the now ....) and then at the stars. I was thinking of my eyes emitting photons which were interacting with photons from stars whose light had left them thousands of years ago. Had I found the photons or did they find me? (I've thought about that in the past, but this morning I was re-mind-ed). I was thinking about a woman's years connected to her moon time, versus a post menopausal woman now has time to look beyond her moon time to the stars. If she is fully connected during the day, is the naturally lowering melatonin levels of aging her chance to experience more of the quantum All That Is during grounded, colder, darkness wakefulness without the penalty of sunlight speeding up time? Came back in to find Reality 9, how women gain time at menopause..... Gotta just love a bit of synchronicity.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-22T13:00:35+0200

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-25T19:07:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-211088>

@[lohd2015](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18338/)
Had to get a magnifying glass but here's what I got .... Can't tell you anything about the ratios, but it will give you a general idea ...

**Triple burner**
Rose Otto (Rosa damascena)
Roman Chamomile (Arthemis nobilis)
Lavender (Lavendula augustifolia)
Virginia Cedarwood (Juniperus virginiana)
Frankincense (Boswellia Frereana)
Lemon (Citrus limonum)
Clary Sage (salvia sclarea)

**Liver**
Pink grapefruit (Citrus paradisil)
Bergamot (Citrus bergamia)
Roman chamomile (Anthemis nobilis)

**Urinary bladder**
Atlas cedarwood (cedrus atlanticus)
Cypress (Cupressus sempevirens)
Tea tree (Melaleuca alternifolia)

**Heart**
Roman chamomile (Anthemis nobilis)
Mandarin (Citrus reticulata)
Lavender (Lavendula augustifolia)
With a grace note of Rose Otto (Rosa damascena)

**Heart protector**
Bergamot (Citrus bergamia)
Palmarosa (Cymbopogon martinii)
Geranium Pelargonium asperum)
Ylang ylang (Cananga odorata)

**Spleen**
Lemon (Citrus limonum)
Pink grapefruit (Citrus paradisii)
Frankincense (Boswellia Frereana)
Rosemary (Rosmarinus officinalis)

**Small intestine**
Sandalwood (santalum spicatum)
Peppermint (Mentha piperita)
Cardomom (Elettaria cardomomom)

**Stomach**
Ginger (zingiber officinale Roscoe)
Lemon (Citrus limonum)
Peppermint (Mentha piperita)

**Large intestine**
Fennel (Foeniculum vulgare Dulcis)
Patchouli (Pogostemom patchouli)
Bergamot (Citrus bergamia)
Sweet Orange (Citrus aurantium Dulcis)

**Gall bladder**
Clary sage (Salvia sclarea)
Lavender (Lavendula augustifolia)
Sweet Orange (Citrus aurantium Dulcis)

**Lung**
Eucalyptus (Eucalyptus radiata)
Lavender (Lavendula augustifolia)
Pine (Pinus sylvestris)

****Kidney****
Atlas cedarwood (cedrus atlanticus)
Virginia Cedarwood (Juniperus virginiana)
Scots pine (Pinus sylvestris)
Ginger (zingiber officinale Roscoe)
Cinnamon leaf (Cinnamomum zeylancum).

Sue, thanks for the list. I find some of the application rather interesting, for example Clary sage for Gall bladder, and Sandalwood for small intestines. I personally find Sandalwood to be MOST effective on Urinary/Bladder/Kidneys.

I do like the Triple Burner mix though...as I have a weakness for Rose otto (damascena or alba). A most interesting combination of top, mid, and base notes. I can just imagine how it COULD smell.....ahhhhhh.

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-27T12:35:48+0300

I suppose I see it more as a combination of vibrations, the organ or system is just a name, or an intention. I tend to pick intuitively (right brain) without looking at the name. If I start analysing (left brain) first, my inner critic would start off .... and my intuition then takes a back seat ... As long as my left brain is confident that none of the possibilities is harmful, I give my right brain free run. As my right brain told my left brain .... quantumly each combination will be signalled to the whole system anyway.

I damaged my right eye trying to convince my eldest brother about my Mum's long term care, and when I found out after she died that he had made an agreement with her that I would be honouring whilst he lived in the sun ..... the expression I used to DH was that I was so angry I am beside myself ..... (pushing to the right?) So it was a bit of a shock to see the picture of being beside yourself ..... I'm working on that .....

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-06T09:35:57+0300

One of the reference papers ... phosphene phenomenon: a new concept.
[http://5mp.eu/fajlok/bokkon-brain-imagery/phosphene\_phenomenon\_a\_new\_concept\_www.5mp.eu\_.pdf](http://5mp.eu/fajlok/bokkon-brain-imagery/phosphene_phenomenon_a_new_concept_www.5mp.eu_.pdf)
So my seeing photons in the dark is a phosphene phenomena....

_"Our visual system not only mediates information about the visual environment but also is capable of generating pictures/lights of nonexistent visual worlds within the brain as afterimages, illusions, phosphenes, dream pictures, visual hallucination, and visual imagery. B´okkon (2005, 2006)"_

(Puffer fish mandala?)

_"Phosphenes are an early symptom in a variety of diseases of the retina or of the visual pathways, but **healthy individuals can perceive them as well**  (Onofrj et al., 1998; Brigatti and Maguluri, 2005)."_

Down the phosphene rabbit hole I go ...

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-07T14:57:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-211954>

Speculation - if I saw pictures as a result of phosphenes generated by ROS, then in one sense it can be a good thing, in another it’s a bad thing if they have been spontaneously emitted. i.e. I’ve compared if I meditate and think about something specific (or leave it open for spontaneous images as part of the meditation). Those exact same images might appear because the phosphenes are created/used in response to my intent (thought) versus a “download” when I don’t expect it. I see spontaneous downloads as OK sometimes if I understand the context, (like the images my Mum downloaded) but how far over the line do I need to be before what I see becomes classed as a hallucination? Is it when I have no control over the emission of the images, or when I reach a point where I can’t stop them? (Like my mother during the latter stages of AD). So is an image in response to my thought (intent) (correct ROS-phosphine interaction?) Or is the thought in response to the image? (Excess ROS-phosphine interaction, brain inflammation?) Same image, different context………….

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-08T10:16:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-211992>

So from the phosphene rabbit hole .... ROS and RNS ... Starting with UBi 10 - biohacking ROS and RNS.

Weather last few days has been lovely, UV 5 ish and clear skies.  I'm wearing a sunhat between 11 and 3 (12-4 British summer time adjustment), sometimes earlier or later, depends on how I feel.

The skull is more easily penetrable, (free radical effects on fatty brain tissues of highly powered sunlight?) Scalp also moves blood around near the surface, high UV and blue levels could bleach the RBCs, (effects on brain circulation?) I think shading the eyes also reduces the risk of cataracts, maybe more important if someone (like me) already has blue light damage to the eyes. Much less glare, which I think is also protective of the DHA in my eyes, more important if my liver long loop isn’t optimal. Wearing a hat still allows eye and skin synchronization, but I'm keeping a cool head ... I haven’t worked it all out yet, but having done it hatless for a long time, and not getting the results I expected, this year I’m trying something different, unless I'm having a timed session sat up to my neck in cold water .... Two short sessions of that yesterday with 4 hours sunbathing as well, looking at the bluebells and primroses growing round the tub .... glorious.

I am starting to wonder if I should be wearing sun glasses 12:00-2:00 ish. I sure don't want my cataracts to get any worse.

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-08T14:26:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-211998>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=211993)
>
> A picture of your bluebells would be lovely Sue.....
>
> I am starting to wonder if I should be wearing sun glasses 12:00-2:00 ish. I sure don't want my cataracts to get any worse.

I thought about it but my concern was the liver (solar organ) circadian cycle ....

Ubi 10 - the eye gut and first cytochrome must all see the same frequency of light to tell time correctly. Blue light bends most under the force of gravity so it can be used as reference for biologic clock to tell circadian time. (More about DHA and melanopsin).

I wonder if it is also about the ratio of blue and yellow frequencies, so crescendo to solar (organ) noon, decrescendoing in the afternoon. Speculation but there is a dip in yellow frequencies when the sun is 6 degrees below the horizon post sunset .... so blue and or yellow in the day..... hence going the hat route first.

### caroline — 2017-04-09T03:32:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-212029>

Tony bought me a really nice hat awhile ago - I don't usually like to wear a hat but I ran across it this morning and am sitting in the house wearing it .....duh!

I keep wondering how much lite is getting in my lazy eye ....I don't even know why it would make a difference that it doesn't focus properly?

I am so far behind in reading ....whereas you are doing a great job of keeping on top of things.....

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-09T09:51:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-212035>

So ROS and RNS
Ubi 10, ROS changes, indicator that body doesn't have the energy needed to cope with signalling particles. So is the ROS recycled to a phosphene to stimulate not (yet) existent visual worlds, visual imagery .... i.e. visualising an innovation or solution ....? Cool that ROS and RNS aren't just wasted...

If environment persists for longer time scales ROS and RNS drop, so does NAD+ at cytochrome 1. Does this mean we lose the phosphene "intuitive" benefits, and rely instead on existing patterns (fixed behaviours)? Less consciousness?

Now trying to picture if the cold and pressure of my migraine cap is stimulating quantum gas release ..

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-09T10:27:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-212040>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=212029)
>
> Tony bought me a really nice hat awhile ago - I don't usually like to wear a hat but I ran across it this morning and am sitting in the house wearing it .....duh!
>
> I keep wondering how much lite is getting in my lazy eye ....I don't even know why it would make a difference that it doesn't focus properly?
>
> I am so far behind in reading ....whereas you are doing a great job of keeping on top of things.....

Yeh I'm keeping on top of the reading ... its everything else that is far behind ... But when Mum died I talked to DH about it, and I'm taking as long as I need to get my energies back , with his blessing. Month or a year is OK with him.  So I spend up to 4 hours a day studying, as much outside as I can. Get the basics done in the house, make sure the washing is done, and do the main meal. Do more if the weather is bad than I do if its good. Getting good at the 5 minute work break (come inside and rush round for 5 minutes then head outside again). I've become pretty reclusive, haven't been in a supermarket for weeks, rarely go out.

I can see more through my bad eye in strong daylight, but virtually nothing in the dark, so I know that light is getting through. I think there's enough to influence circadian rhythm. Its like in that eye I'm not blind, I just can't see ... Because its not in both eyes, I've taken a risk not getting a proper diagnosis, but I'm under no professional pressure to do something that can't be undone, and I am not facing or accepting as my truth, someone else's idea of my prognosis. But the flipside is that if it had a name I would be able to study the subtleties of the condition .... easier to hack cataract than "not blind can't see eye condition." The naturopath Bruce Fife had a couple of interesting thoughts on cataracts in one of his books, I'll look it up ....

### caroline — 2017-04-09T11:18:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-212041>

I can so relate to rushing around inside to do a couple of things and then get back outside. Have you thought of cooking outside? When we are away with our camper trailer we are outside from Dawn to dusk - everything is done outside. The last place we were - in a national park with no light pollution and an outdoor cold shower was the best. We could see the milky way at night time.

Since the camper is sitting in the driveway - I am thinking of just pulling out the kitchen and cooking there!

I find it so frustrating to be inside at all - but we do have to have good nutritious food to eat! It would be so much easier to order a pizza - lol

housework is definitely not a priority ....as long as I make the bed and we keep the bathroom clean.....

What do you do Sue? you seem to have done some very interesting things in your life.......

We are seriously considering moving down the coast to be within walking distance of the ocean - I think we can actually afford to do that down there. There is a lot less population - just country villages. But now comes all the research to make sure we do the right things....

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-09T16:11:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-212051>

Our weather is too changeable, so I take things outside to prepare, come back and throw things in the oven or slow cooker. For the main meal either from scratch or finishing off if its already in the oven has a max of 30 minutes. I can set something going and take a kitchen timer out with me set to when I next need to go and do something. The national park sounds fab - I was watching Stargazing Live Australia which ran for 3 nights recently, seeing the milky way from Australia must be amazing.

What do I do? Not a lot at the moment. I learned to use intuition the hard way - in my first marriage it was a survival skill, I learned to risk calculate and remote view what I was heading into, but got out when I couldn't stop low risk escalation . It was worse between the divorce and my remarriage, I was constantly looking over my shoulder and watching my back. I couldn't sit in a restaurant with my back to the door for years. I thought blind terror slowed time, like a film running in slow motion. On my remarriage I started going to a new meditation group, remote viewing the stars, sending consciousness up into a space ship meeting beings of light, meeting other aspects of myself operating in parallel universes ....nothing seemed crazy ... That led to being invited to join another meditation group, held at a subtle energy training school. Off the wall planetary stuff.... Which led to the course .... 12 weekends over 2 years, with a minimum of 1000 hours additional study, essays, case studies per year between weekends. Becker, William Tiller, Bentov, Saxton Burr .... Then 2 years supervision. By that time I was dealing with DS2 and the fluoride problem .... Gave up a very well paid job, took additional professional development training in understanding sensory issues from a more conventional angle. Worked for a time with all things sensory - I still have my old meter which I would show people what their microwaves etc did to the meter .... Organised workshops for therapists to teach parents how to assess and use complementary therapy for sensory issues on their own children. I went through the how to trust your own knowledge and intuition about your child's sensory problems, and the other therapist would teach head massage, hand massage, foot massage ... aromatherapy ... etc etc. But my not being conventionally trained kept a lot of doors closed. Its not about knowledge, its about how many boxes you tick. Paid by a charity to teach a volunteer running a meditation group in a prison for those with long sentences or lifers. (I got asked box ticking questions, but said - very nicely \- take it or leave it. They took it). Trained in complex homeopathy (which I can now relate to heteroplasmy), but earlier successes didn't hold against the tide of nnemf. Any supplement or homeopathic remedy can be as suppressive as a conventional pill, if all its doing is suppressing symptoms. Not what people want to hear.

Taking on my mother changed things again, I knew I was losing my intuitive and cognitive edge. I attracted less interesting work, but as her needs increased I became less and less in a position to take anything anyway. I've kept up my professional insurances etc, and done ongoing training ... but I'm at a crossroads ...not ready to make a decision, but its OK to stand at the crossroads for a while and see what other paths open.....

anyway - the treatment is to cover the good eye and try to make your lazy eye focus..... would this benefit you?

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-10T09:05:55+0300

>

I have the opposite problem, the eye keeps trying to see, which tires it (and me). I'm in use it or lose it but don't wear it (or me) out mode. The best way to describe it is like looking through layers of coconut oil smeared on glass with a few streaks of mud which in the right light I can watch phosphenes bounce off. With what I'm doing now I think the eye is self cleaning a bit better than it was ... but I've thought that before ... I don't think I have the original injury, its what happened afterwards and some poor treatment decisions I made .... I've just started doing eye breathing exercises .. I go with the dominant nostril, and "breathe" though the open eye on the same side, keeping the opposite eye covered or closed. Swap when the nostril dominance changes. Finish with both eyes open "breathing" together. Having seen the beside myself picture, I'm also doing hand flapping outside in natural light. (I think some people with autism do it to stimulate peripheral vision, and I think it shakes up an over contracted bioenergetic field in front of the eyes. Having shaken it up, I then "push" the field to expand it, and imagine myself centred in my energy field. I'm also mentally directing energy to the eye. ). We shall see

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-10T09:54:00+0300

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=178267)
>
> I went to a seminar on Saturday about wifi, they banned mobile phones, no artificial lights etc. Lot of ill people there. I had a slight problem with my vision in my right eye when I got there, but it got gradually worse as the day went on. I had a three quarters eclipse ..Left eye was fine. It is the same eye I had a retinal tear in a few years ago. But one of the speakers at the seminar was talking about microwaves coking from the inside out, and my feeling was that my optic nerve was inflamed, got DH to search optic nerve neuritis, which gave me a breather from being dragged off by him straight away and having strong lights shone into my eyes. If I could see some improvement by the morning he would give me another 24 hours .....Step 1, CT. Early night. Sunday morning 4 hours mix of CT and AM sunshine, used a cold wet chamois to cool my eyelids, and then faced the sun with my eyes shut. every so often I would hop out of the tub and get the sun on my cold skin. Enough improvement to buy me another 24 hours. good night sleep Sunday, more improvement Monday morning. Bad night sleep Monday (family meeting about my mum's Alzheimer's did not go well) but still some improvement. I can now see the light coming through the windows, I can see the grass, I could see the dog walking towards me across the grass. the eclipse is shrinking and I'm getting more light coming thru what is left. Main treatment CT, AM light, grounding, seafood, water. But it's working, big time
> WIfi router also coming out shortly, no longer just on a timer, going to cabled. DH realises I'm as strong as my weakest link, and this is my second scare ......Anyway, time up on computer ...

September 2015 .... was it that long ago ....

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-13T10:53:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-212260>

Musings this morning

CPC 13 – Hyperferritin cataract syndrome. Picture of man’s face with IR camera.

_“You can see the areas around the eyes are whiter compared to the center of the eye where the lens is. The eyelid/orbicularis iris muscle has massive blood flows from the facial artery as you can see below. In this condition they would be loaded with ferritin and this would alter these tissues spectral emissivity relative to the lens.”

“Ferritin is normally the storage protein that puts iron into the cells in the circulatory system under the direction of sunlight but the powerful sunlight in the cornea and lens is precisely what these patient need to avoid. Ferritin also is a highly charged human protein because of the iron and oxygen it contains. This is why cataract formation occurs in my opinion.”
_
Although hyperferritin cataract syndrome is a 100% genetic disease and
_“This is one condition where specialize glasses blocking the spectral absorption of iron containing proteins may help. The problem with these glasses is they would also affect growth and metabolism pathways with blockade of UV and IR light”.
_
Linking this to Reality 8, how women gain time at menopause .. Depending on context, (not enough constant connection to the earth, dehydration etc) if a woman has high iron and ferritin levels in the blood, does she risk an epigenetic version in relation to risk of developing cataracts?

Is this why I’m intuitively drawn to wearing a wide brimmed hat in strong middle of the day sunlight?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-15T19:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-212362>

Cornea, lens and vitreous are unusual tissues, insofar as all three are transparent and allow, at least in the young, healthy eye, the passage of light to the retina with minimal scatter, distortion or absorption. The vitreous, in the human eye the largest of the three tissues, is a viscoelastic gel with a high (99%) water content and few resident cells. The vitreous is an optically neutral tissue. The task of focusing light falls, therefore, to the cornea and lens. The cornea is the more refractive of the two tissues, a consequence of its location at the ocular surface. The large refractive index (_n_) difference between air and tear film (_n_ = 1.000 versus 1.376, respectively) ensures that, in humans, the cornea contributes two-thirds of the focusing power of the eye. The transparency of the cornea is secured by the regular and rigid organization of collagen fibrils and the uniform distribution of keratocytes in the corneal stroma, which accounts for 90 per cent of the corneal thickness. The cornea and its non-transparent extension, the sclera, are inelastic structures that also function to provide mechanical support to the eye globe. One consequence of its relative rigidity is that the refractive power of the cornea (approx. 43 Diopters (D) in humans) is fixed. Variable focusing (accommodation) is a task that, in humans at least, falls exclusively to the lens and is controolled autonomically by the brainstem.

Unlike the cornea, the lens is located within the eye and its refractive surfaces are bathed by ocular humors. To focus light, the refractive index of the lens substance must significantly exceed that of the surrounding media (_n_ = 1.336). In fact, the refractive index of the human lens fiber cytoplasm varies from 1.380 near the lens surface to 1.409 in the center of the tissue. These high values reflect the extraordinary concentration (sometimes exceeding 450 mg ml−1) of crystalline proteins in the cytoplasm of lens fiber cells. Given that light scatter in protein solutions is usually proportional to protein concentration, the transparency of the lens substance is unexpected. The explanation for this apparent paradox is that at very high protein concentrations, short-range-order interactions between lens proteins virtually eliminate light scatter through the process of destructive interference. This process changes as the redox of the eye changes around the eye and effects the lens.

1\. Michael R., Bron A. J. 2011. The ageing lens and cataract: a model of normal and pathological ageing. Phil. Trans. R. Soc. B 366, 1278–129210.1098/rstb.2010.0300 ([doi:10.1098/rstb.2010.0300](http://dx.doi.org/10.1098/rstb.2010.0300)) \[[PMC free article](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3061107/)\] \[[PubMed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21402586)\] \[[Cross Ref](https://dx.doi.org/10.1098%2Frstb.2010.0300)\]
2\. Muller L. J., Pels E., Schurmans L. R. H. M., Vrensen G. F. J. M. 2004. A new three-dimensional model of the organization of proteoglycans and collagen fibrils in the human corneal stroma. Exp. Eye Res. 78, 493–50110.1016/S0014-4835(03)00206-9 ([doi:10.1016/S0014-4835(03)00206-9](http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/S0014-4835\(03\)00206-9)) \[[PubMed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15106928)\] \[[Cross Ref](https://dx.doi.org/10.1016%2FS0014-4835\(03\)00206-9)\]
3\. Muller L. J., Pels L., Vrensen G. F. 1995. Novel aspects of the ultrastructural organization of human corneal keratocytes. Invest. Ophthalmol. Vis. Sci. 36, 2557–2567 \[[PubMed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7499078)\]
4\. Kasthurirangan S., Markwell E. L., Atchison D. A., Pope J. M. 2008. _In vivo_ study of changes in refractive index distribution in the human crystalline lens with age and accommodation. Invest. Ophthalmol. Vis. Sci. 49, 2531–254010.1167/iovs.07-1443 ([doi:10.1167/iovs.07-1443](http://dx.doi.org/10.1167/iovs.07-1443)) \[[PubMed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18408189)\]\[[Cross Ref](https://dx.doi.org/10.1167%2Fiovs.07-1443)\]
5\. Duncan M. K., Cvekl A., Kantorow M., Piatigorsky J. 2004. Lens crystallins. In Development of the ocular lens (eds Lovicu F. J., Robinson M. L., editors. ), pp. 119–150 Cambridge, UK: Cambridge University Press
6\. Delaye M., Tardieu A. 1983. Short-range order of crystallin proteins accounts for eye lens transparency. Nature 302, 415–41710.1038/302415a0 ([doi:10.1038/302415a0](http://dx.doi.org/10.1038/302415a0)) \[[PubMed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/6835373)\] \[[Cross Ref](https://dx.doi.org/10.1038%2F302415a0)\]

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-24T10:11:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-212819>

Went down to Cornwall on Thursday for my 25th wedding anniversary weekend. Stayed in a hotel, in a room in the separate courtyard building. It didn't pick up wifi but there was a bright courtyard light so I had to use an eye mask the first night, but the following morning we went to the supermarket and brought a roll of thick black plastic wheelie bin liners which we pinned up for nights 2 and 3, and they worked really well (on my holiday packing list now). Bought a bottle of red wine and put strands of saffron in it to steep. ( - medicinal ) Ate lots of seafood. One really good sunny day, spent mostly on the beach. Other days not quite so good, and cold wind, so did barefoot clifftop walks in tan through and a short skirt, and put on a jumper when it clouded over in a strong cold wind on the cliff top, but got at least one third solar panel exposure during daylight hours, and at least 5 miles walk each day. (I need to fit in some more walking at home .....) On the way back we stopped at a national trust old house, big place with a lot of garden and woodland, could do most of it barefoot. Feel recharged.

I have a bottle of saffron infused Malbec brewing .... I find it more uplifting than a glass of red, its like a bonus dopamine hit- if a few bits of saffron came out in the glass I just knock it back. While I was away I just had a small amount at lunchtime, and a glass in the evening, stuck to normal red when we went out to dinner. Now I'm home I've had a cup of saffron tea after having lunch (melted ghee with turmeric, threw in some spinach leaves and broke a couple of eggs on top, cooked just enough to wilt the spinach and keep yolks nice and soft). Think the ghee will help absorption of the fat soluble parts of the saffron. Will have one glass of infused wine with my meal later, for the resveratrol hit. Saw an ad for supplements with a lot of the same nutrients in - but I'm doing it with food, saffron tea - and wine.  Keep finding more about it, including its antidepression links, although they eye stuff is what drew me first. Will be interesting to see what happens when its added to light water magnetism.

However, in the process of vibrational energy dissipation occurs via internal conversion in the excited electronic state of the "donor" pigment (say Chl b), states are reached that are in resonance with certain (strongly vibrating: think 100 Hz in mitochondria) states of the "acceptor" chlorophyll a. This resonance is what makes energy transfer possible. So when the 100 Hz vibration is absent electron transfer suffers. This lowers the amount of ATP that can be made from light and cells seek other sources of electrons from foods. This is the quantum basis of obesity from a quantum perspective. It is 180 degrees in opposition to the energy excess idea of over eating over energized. Obesity is an energy deficit disease of mitochondria and not a food excess. This is why healthcare today struggles to help people who are overweight lose weight. Buying diet foods made by man makes this harder but it does make for a great long term business with predictable cash flow.

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-29T11:29:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-213064>

Wiki on crocetin and hydrogen bonding

_“The sodium salt of crocetin, transcrocetinate sodium…..”._

_“Similar to other oxygen diffusion-enhancing compounds, transcrocetinate sodium appears to improve oxygenation in hypoxic tissues by exerting hydrophobic effects on water molecules in blood plasma and thereby increasing the hydrogen bonding between the water molecules. This in turn causes the overall organization of water molecules in plasma to become more structured, which facilitates the diffusion of oxygen through plasma and promotes the movement of oxygen into tissues.”_

Wiki on oxygen diffusion compounds

_“One of the first substances that was reported to produce an oxygen diffusion-enhancing effect was crocetin.”

“Oxygen diffusion-enhancing compounds are thought to act by exerting hydrophobic forces that interact with water molecules. These interactions result in greater hydrogen bonding among water molecules, which constitute the majority of the blood plasma medium. As hydrogen bonding increases, the overall molecular structure of water in the plasma becomes more lattice-like, a phenomenon known as structure building. Structure building reduces resistance to the movement of oxygen through plasma via diffusion. Since blood plasma offers the major barrier for oxygen to move from the red blood cells and into the tissues, the more structured character of water imparted by the oxygen diffusion-enhancing compound will enhance movement into tissues”._

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-02T05:30:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-213197>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=212362)
>
> Cornea, lens and vitreous are unusual tissues, insofar as all three are transparent and allow, at least in the young, healthy eye, the passage of light to the retina with minimal scatter, distortion or absorption. The vitreous, in the human eye the largest of the three tissues, is a viscoelastic gel with a high (99%) water content and few resident cells. The vitreous is an optically neutral tissue. The task of focusing light falls, therefore, to the cornea and lens. The cornea is the more refractive of the two tissues, a consequence of its location at the ocular surface. The large refractive index (_n_) difference between air and tear film (_n_ = 1.000 versus 1.376, respectively) ensures that, in humans, the cornea contributes two-thirds of the focusing power of the eye. The transparency of the cornea is secured by the regular and rigid organization of collagen fibrils and the uniform distribution of keratocytes in the corneal stroma, which accounts for 90 per cent of the corneal thickness. The cornea and its non-transparent extension, the sclera, are inelastic structures that also function to provide mechanical support to the eye globe. One consequence of its relative rigidity is that the refractive power of the cornea (approx. 43 Diopters (D) in humans) is fixed. Variable focusing (accommodation) is a task that, in humans at least, falls exclusively to the lens and is controolled autonomically by the brainstem.
>
> Unlike the cornea, the lens is located within the eye and its refractive surfaces are bathed by ocular humors. To focus light, the refractive index of the lens substance must significantly exceed that of the surrounding media (_n_ = 1.336). In fact, the refractive index of the human lens fiber cytoplasm varies from 1.380 near the lens surface to 1.409 in the center of the tissue. These high values reflect the extraordinary concentration (sometimes exceeding 450 mg ml−1) of crystalline proteins in the cytoplasm of lens fiber cells. Given that light scatter in protein solutions is usually proportional to protein concentration, the transparency of the lens substance is unexpected. The explanation for this apparent paradox is that at very high protein concentrations, short-range-order interactions between lens proteins virtually eliminate light scatter through the process of destructive interference. This process changes as the redox of the eye changes around the eye and effects the lens.
>
> 1\. Michael R., Bron A. J. 2011. The ageing lens and cataract: a model of normal and pathological ageing. Phil. Trans. R. Soc. B 366, 1278–129210.1098/rstb.2010.0300 ([doi:10.1098/rstb.2010.0300](http://dx.doi.org/10.1098/rstb.2010.0300)) \[[PMC free article](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3061107/)\] \[[PubMed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21402586)\] \[[Cross Ref](https://dx.doi.org/10.1098%2Frstb.2010.0300)\]
> 2\. Muller L. J., Pels E., Schurmans L. R. H. M., Vrensen G. F. J. M. 2004. A new three-dimensional model of the organization of proteoglycans and collagen fibrils in the human corneal stroma. Exp. Eye Res. 78, 493–50110.1016/S0014-4835(03)00206-9 ([doi:10.1016/S0014-4835(03)00206-9](http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/S0014-4835\(03\)00206-9)) \[[PubMed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15106928)\] \[[Cross Ref](https://dx.doi.org/10.1016%2FS0014-4835\(03\)00206-9)\]
> 3\. Muller L. J., Pels L., Vrensen G. F. 1995. Novel aspects of the ultrastructural organization of human corneal keratocytes. Invest. Ophthalmol. Vis. Sci. 36, 2557–2567 \[[PubMed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/7499078)\]
> 4\. Kasthurirangan S., Markwell E. L., Atchison D. A., Pope J. M. 2008. _In vivo_ study of changes in refractive index distribution in the human crystalline lens with age and accommodation. Invest. Ophthalmol. Vis. Sci. 49, 2531–254010.1167/iovs.07-1443 ([doi:10.1167/iovs.07-1443](http://dx.doi.org/10.1167/iovs.07-1443)) \[[PubMed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18408189)\]\[[Cross Ref](https://dx.doi.org/10.1167%2Fiovs.07-1443)\]
> 5\. Duncan M. K., Cvekl A., Kantorow M., Piatigorsky J. 2004. Lens crystallins. In Development of the ocular lens (eds Lovicu F. J., Robinson M. L., editors. ), pp. 119–150 Cambridge, UK: Cambridge University Press
> 6\. Delaye M., Tardieu A. 1983. Short-range order of crystallin proteins accounts for eye lens transparency. The conclusion section sentence _"The results of this study indicated that saffron can be used as a natural UV-absorbing agent."_ caught my attention .... So yesterday I drank saffron tea, and had a quarter teaspoon of turmeric mixed with just a bit of red wine ...(will add black pepper next time) and went outside for a couple of hours. It was intermittent cloud, but nice when the sky cleared before the next cloud came along ... best times between the clouds it was UV4. I got pinker than I would have expected, subtle but noticeable ....

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-07T18:49:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-213604>

So today I moved home office ..... from a small bedroom with a north facing window, out to the conservatory/sun room. If its nice and I'm not working I'm outside anyway, and there's still room for a comfortable sofa and chair. Sold the rest of the room furniture yesterday. Spent the money on a new computer monitor, anti glare, 4 settings for lowering blue light emission, no flicker, and its wired in as before. The room faces south, no need for lights in daylight hours, doors at the east and west end to open and let in more natural light and pull the breeze through, and 8 windows to throw wide open. Why didn't I think of this before .... If its cloudy and there's a break in the clouds when I'm working I'll see it and nip out for a sun break.

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-08T12:09:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-213642>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=213641)
>
> sounds like a wonderful space Sue..... why didn't you think of it before???

DH tried it a few years ago, but got glare and reflection from his then monitor. If we'd got rid of some of the furniture then he could have positioned the desk better, but he doesn't spend much time on the computer during the day anyway so it wasn't worth it. .... but technology has moved on ... it was the flicker effect thread that got me thinking about it again and having a look at what's available now. Then I just got a picture in my mind of a possible new set up. The east door has regularly been blocked off when I've suddenly found myself with other people's stuff (sorting out FIL's paperwork, keeping some of Mum's stuff in case she wanted something, my DD when she moved out of her flat and went abroad to study), there always seemed to be something) ....and I've got a few more boxes of books... but its declutter central here at the moment. I'm creating a space I want to spend time in when the weather isn't good enough to be outside... read, meditate, sit in the open doorway out of the wind and rain, but still be able to gather photons ... Air CT in the winter. The old office is going to have my tanner and bioptron stuff, get it out of the bedroom.

I think my mindset has changed about technology too .... Like my monitor, putting a gel filter on it seemed a good idea (and was better than doing nothing), but waiting until the monitor stopped working before replacing it wasn't an optimal decision, particularly with my eye problem. I can see blue skies and sunshine from here now ..... off for a sun break.

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-23T15:39:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-214495>

Update on my hack.

I get out as soon after dawn as I wake up, when the air is coolest, and I drink a pint of water to signal that water is abundant. (My equivalent of going to the watering hole at dawn. Plants stop photosynthesising if they detect low water, so I’m giving myself the human equivalent signal of good water availability. I do the same at sunset, and drink regularly during the day). Yesterday it was UV4 at 10 a.m. I slept a bit later but was out at 6.30 a.m. so got some early frequencies and then some crescendoing UVA and then some UVB. (This morning it was cloudier but I got to see the sunrise at 5 a.m. and cloudbathed, I’m still getting a percentage of the light). Yesterday although the cool breeze meant it wasn’t an issue, if the wind had dropped I knew I could get some decent sun before it got too hot. I got in the shade for a while at 11.30, after eating my first of 2 meals in the day, when my blood would have been directed to the gut rather than the skin surface. It got to UV7 at 1.p.m. so at around 1.30 I hopped into the CT tub for a few minutes and then dried off in the sun, then got back into the shade. I spent about 8 hours outside, without overheating, but the environment as a whole (breeze, CT) meant I didn’t need to sweat.

I have been using ghee as a sun cream. I tested it first on one half of my body to see the effect, (going pinker earlier and developing more of a tan, particularly on the knee and top of my foot. As I’ve tanned the stretch marks on the ghee side didn’t show a noticeable difference any more. I stopped the test because the difference was obvious, and the inside of my right leg seemed to be showing signs of more fat loss than my left, going from the knee to my right inside leg).

I think the carotenoids that give it the yellow colour helps reduce the time I need to spend outside if for any reason I can’t be out for long enough, and accelerates the tanning process when I can be outside longer, or it’s very sunny. I’m not sure if ghee is more beneficial for me because of my extra padding/remaining glycation/or the condition of my skin. But from having reread in light in shaping life, that casein micelles from mammalian milk emit light, I am wondering if the fat from mammalian milk acts as an amplifier, (the colder the environment, the fattier the mammalian milk, so maybe mother nature has made body fat a carotenoid assisted light storage organ, and a light recycler – in the winter I think high carotenoid levels in the body fat might also serve to reflect light emission/leaking light back in, and help harvest what little light there is. A polar bear recycles light emission by its white fur structure reflecting IR back onto black skin, so maybe human mammals at higher latitudes need to recycle more than nearer the equator. I’ve been thinking of body fat as another solar organ). So I’m hacking ghee as a surface covering during sun exposure to get light into my fat tissue as well as other tissues and the blood. It makes my skin glow, stops it drying out, it seems healthier. I'm also hoping the carotenoids and fats are attaching to my sulfated proteins and producing voltage ....  I’m intuitively drawn to using it, whereas coconut oil has never seemed right (SPF?). Skin tags under my arms are shrinking, but I’ve now got to expose my armpits and under the girls to more light, sitting reading keeps some parts out of direct sunlight, the tan advancement of the exposed areas now _really_ shows where I’m missing. I might be way off on what is happening (wouldn't be the first time .....) but the tan I have now is more like my tan last year at the end of September. Its better than that already, and to be at this level before the end of May is amazing when I think of the amount of cloudy and rainy days we’ve had in the last 2 weeks.

I’m still also hacking dietary carotenoids, so I drink saffron tea, and a hot tea with golden paste (turmeric, black pepper, ghee), particularly if it’s cloudier. The carotenoids in turmeric may not stick around in the blood long, but it can provide a short light boost, particularly when the quantum yield is lower …

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-27T10:46:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-214837>

@[seanb4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1910/) – this update is in response to your other thread, I’m not in a highly populated area, but have tagged you in case there’s something here of use?

We’ve had a hot spell …..

I’m still trying to get my skin to work better as a solar panel, and an organ of absorption and excretion. I will use soap if I need to but any detergent (shower gel, shampoo etc) strips my skin of natural oils. When I go outside soon after dawn, I am using ghee as a sun cream straight away. I was running an experiment yesterday with glasses of either hot or cold tap water, with a small blob of ghee plopped in, left in direct sunlight. I was intrigued enough by the result to do it again more scientifically, taking water temps and having a non ghee control, rather than my I wonder what would happen if ….I also want to try it with coconut oil).

When I first started applying it, I was wondering if I was imagining that I was sweating slightly more, but over time I would say that my sweating response has definitely improved. A teaspoon of ghee does my whole body. I can increase the sweating by drinking a hot tea made with turmeric, black pepper and ghee. I drink a good amount of water first thing in the morning, and early in the evening, with regular smaller amounts during the day. I am treating my gut as the roots of a plant – and plants stop photosynthesizing if they detect low water levels. So I’m trying to keep the signaling my roots that water is available, without overdrinking.

Yesterday the sun was very intense, and later on I felt I’d really had enough, I didn’t feel right. I hadn't drunk any hot tea. So I got a migraine cap out of the freezer, stuck a wide brimmed straw hat on top to stop my nose from burning, and got into the CT tub in direct sunlight. The water was cool not cold. Twenty minutes of that was amazing. When I got out, I applied a tiny bit more ghee, rubbing it on to my wet body. Swapped my now warm migraine cap for another one, and laid out in direct sun with the cap on and sunhat covering my head and face. (My hat is wide weave, it’s like a Kiniki hat in that I can see through the holes, it doesn’t block all the sunlight from my face, the best way I can describe it is dappled shade). The cooling the surfaces and ghee got me an extra 1 ½ hours of sun time, and I felt really relaxed. I spent over 8 hours outside, with no burning. I got in the shade to eat lunch and for a while afterwards, when my blood would have been directed to the gut. My max outside has been 10 hours, but the early morning light and AM light is critical to avoid burning. From Reality 12 – I like the thought of how often my mitochondria are experiencing the sun’s breath.

My water/ghee experiment has offered a couple of clues why, but from first observation, I’m intending to take regular dunks in cool water in this heatwave, (cool rain on my leaves?). Plain water doesn’t take all the fats out, and I need to keep my head cooler in the heat of the day. (I have been sticking my head in the shade or wearing the sun hat on its own so far).

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-27T13:46:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-214841>

I think the leftover rain from the thunderstorm we had last might be heading in your direction, its brightening up here.  The reason I don't use butter is that ghee doesn't go rancid in the same way.. Overcook butter and it burns (not what I want if I'm in a UV7) and although there's not a lot of protein in butter, I want the fat attaching to my own sulfated proteins. Also when I'm only using a teaspoon full, its a more condensed source of the carotenoids, which I think is important. I think that's what makes it better for me than coconut oil in a lower quantum yield. Sweating isn't too much of a problem, much better than it used to be, but its not optimal. The ghee seems to extend the time I can be outside without feeling I've had enough, particularly if I can keep my head cooler. In the mornings I have taken out a teaspoon of ghee in an eggcup and just slapped it on, my hand and body heat melt it and it spreads very thinly, and get DH to do my back .... Yesterday after the CT session, I applied a little more, just slapped a bit more on my skin whilst I was drip drying in the sun after getting out of the tub, just to top up any minor loss to the CT water. I don't mix it with water to apply it. The water comes later from the CT tub, or rain ..... or sweat if its hot enough.  Skin is supposed to have lipids. They have a major role in keeping the skin healthy, and a teaspoon full for the whole body isn't going to be clogging up my pores ...
