### Sue-UK — 2017-05-28T14:47:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-214915>

@[seanb4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1910/)

I’ve found the quote I was looking for in relation to skin lipids ….A book on sunlight therapy I have says (my bolding of text). _“The second level of defense is on the surface of the skin itself, and is due to the same free oxidizing radicals as in ozone therapy, but in a different form. In this case it is the lipids or oils in the skin which provide the oxidizing effect. In 1936 an elegantly simple experiment showed that when the skin was exposed to ultraviolet light for eight hours, the ‘active oxygen content’ went up by 10-15 fold. (Stevens, FA, ‘The Bactericidal Properties of Ultraviolet Irradiated Lipids of the Skin’, J. Exp. Med: 65; 121-126, 1937). **It was then demonstrated that a large part, at least, of this oxygen was in the skin lipids or fats.** These lipids were sufficiently ‘active’ to fog a photographic plate, and they killed haemolytic streptococcus, a relatively nasty micro-organism, in just a few hours.”…_……

Another bit which gets me outside, however low the UV or however cloudy: **_“It seems that even without ultraviolet light, skin lipids will take up some oxygen from the air and use it to kill bacteria_.** _However, this effect is much more intense under UV. Since ultraviolet light or sunlight will have a direct effect itself by killing off the organisms on or near the skin, there is clearly a double impact in this layer of defense…..”_

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-28T17:02:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-214923>

Musings ..... Crazy alert .... Homeopathic UV anyone?

Northern latitudes – mammals need for higher subcutaneous fat levels, plus also for hibernation, physiological insulin resistance, and possibly a light recycling system. Lowering UV levels of fall/autumn triggering accumulation of fat. Rising UV levels triggering leanness. Is losing remainder of fat or having low fat levels waiting for summer protective? Would a gradual seasonal increase in UV trigger faster weight loss in obese humans as a protective measure?

From one of my books on sunlight: “ …._the secondary radiation, (the energy put out by an organism or solution in response to an input of ultraviolet energy) became stronger the more dilute the solution. Thus a 0.02 per cent solution of nucleic acid produced its peak effect in one fifth of the time taken by a 1 per cent solution. Similar results were found with suspensions of bacteria_.” _(Ref Gesenius H, ‘Blutstrahlung and Carcinom-Diagnostik’, Radiobiologia: 1;33, 1932)._
Another bit (but the reference is missing), _“A further remarkable property of bioradiation is that of amplification. When six quartz test tubes containing bacteria were set side by side, and the first was irradiated with UV, there was a secondary radiation emitted from the other end of the row, which was twenty seven times greater than the input UV.”
_
So that had me thinking about fat cells, and whether a stuffed to the gills fat cell would have a much higher solution dilution of nucleic acid than an almost empty one. IIRC a fat cell also has very few mitochondria (the bacteria connection), definitely compared to say a neuron or a muscle cell. So I’m wondering, (apart from fat as a food electron source, cold protection etc), if the lowering UV stimulates rising fat levels in preparation for winter because an amplifier would be useful for scavenging and amplifying any available photons, (or recycling emitted photons?) but if that the same high dilution cell would be detrimental in a high UV environment. So does Mother Nature signal a reducing dilution in the fat cell as UV increases seasonally (fat loss, unconnected to the food eaten, shit off a shingle  ) rather than risk shorting out the mitochondrially dense parts of the system, or damaging the RBC’s as the season progresses?  In my defence I did start with a crazy alert, but until I understand it I'm glad I'm doing the early morning light and building up the sun callus slowly ...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-215173>

Indigo aura anyone  (I was wondering if woad was the ancients version of MB... )
_"When Australian toxicologist Elizabeth Gillam was studying bacteria implanted with human DNA, her cultures unexpectedly turned blue. She suspected a mold contamination. But after conferring with Fred Guengerich, a colleague at Vanderbilt University, Gillam realized she'd stumbled onto something wonderful: The bacteria were producing the indigo molecule as part of their metabolism. "This is a good lesson for student scientists," says Gillam. "If something looks bizarre, don't discount it. It might be much more interesting than the result you expected.""
[http://ngm.nationalgeographic.com/ngm/0306/resources\_who.html](http://ngm.nationalgeographic.com/ngm/0306/resources_who.html)_

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-01T10:00:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-215235>

Back to the fat cell .... I found [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC291976/pdf/jcinvest00192-0227.pdf](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC291976/pdf/jcinvest00192-0227.pdf) interesting.

_"Thus, adipose tissue serves as a depot of large capacity which accumulates vitamin D proportionate to its concentration in plasma and releases it at a much slower rate that is proportionate to its concentration in fat. These features of accumulation and release would protect against toxicity during short periods of vitamin overdosage, would tend to maintain a relatively stable concentration in blood under natural conditions where absorption and production of the vitamin could vary significantly, and would provide continued maintenance of vitamin D in plasma for hepatic conversion to 25-HCC during a relatively prolonged period of low intake. The fact that the biological activity of plasma of humans falls exponentially after a pharmacological dose of vitamin D (8, 33) at a rate similar to that which we have observed in rats (i.e. ti 3-4months cf. ti - 80 days in rats) lends support to the concept that these same features may characterize the storage and release of vitamin D in humans."_

The paper was from 1971, so with a now very changed environment, (if you can't fat burn, leptin resistance, no 100hz resonance etc,) then the D3 could remain locked up ....

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-6-8_10-8-24-png.4992/)
The outside temperature this morning at around 8.30 was 18C and there was also a very strong wind signal. The sky was grey, but there was still light up for grabs.
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-6-8_10-9-48-png.4993/)
So I expected my blood to move inwards to the core, (and my skin got lighter) but my MB ghee coated skin could still collect photons. When I came back into the conservatory (around 22C and out of the wind) I started getting pinker as the blood returned to the surface. I’m beginning to think achieving a summer tan in a low quantum yield isn’t as important as I thought (it fades later in the year anyway – A leaf reabsorbing its chlorophyll and leaving the “tan” part of its leaves to drop in the autumn?) And maybe having less of a tan helps what UVB we do get in a UK low quantum yield to penetrate the subcutaneous fat? My SQ fat levels, even when I was at normal weight, and underweight, are at the high end of normal. (When I observed my Mum losing her SQ fat in the last months of her life, I remember thinking that her body was giving up her SQ fat in a desperate attempt to get light into the now very prominent veins on her arms). Years ago I used to think I was programmed in the womb to store calories as a survival mechanism against famine, but now I am wondering if the fat cells are storage for light and carotenoids as well as fat, to provide a constant supply in a low quantum yield or in the cold. The less light, the more fat. (Plants ….chromoplasts contain lipid droplets in which carotenoids are stored, and elaioplasts are a lipid storing leucoplast).

I'm determined to find a way of getting to optimal in a UK low quantum yield environment ....

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-10T23:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-215786>

Every vitamin in humans works via a coupled feeback control loop. When you take an exogenous vitamin is uncouples the feedback mechanism built by nature using light frequencies. Vitamin D produces toxicity by depleting the body of vitamin K metabolites and that part of the feedback mechanism where vitamin A cycling in the eye and retina can protects against systemic vitamin D toxicity by preventing the excessive production of vitamin K-dependent proteins, especially matrix Gla protein, also known as MGP. Light frequencies from the sun are critical in this little dance because they contain a system of checks and balances. When a system loses negative feedback control you uncouple cycles from one another. Let me give you a clear example of a LOSS of negative feedback control: in this type of coupling mechanism two components of the system have opposite effects on one another. Consider prey and predator as a couple. Consumption of prey by predator has a positive effect on the eater, and a negative on on the eaten animal. The negative effect on predators on prey prevents uncontrolled growth of the predator's population. This stabilizes the population sizes of both groups when they are coupled. This is precisely how light from our surfaces couples cell growth via ubiquitin rates via our surfaces. Blue and red light in the sun array are like predator and prey........they each perfectly balance one another. UV-A and UV-B are the keys to understanding how the system yokes. Look at the picture below to see how nature really works to couple animals, sun, and vitamins.

At the moment sunset isn’t til 915 ish pm with dusk ending at 10.15 ish, so I’m hacking getting the sunset and dusk frequencies naturally, the problem will be the winter when its dark much earlier.

Reality 4 “Melanopsin is the more important opsin because it is the gate keeper of the central retinal pathway that links the retina and the leptin receptor. Melanopsin works with light 430-465nm at dusk when we move from bright daylight to sunset.” So at dusk there is a lowering intensity of light, a drop in 460-480nm, green and yellow drops off, (higher IOP for night vision from drop in green?), - rods, cones and ganglions doing the maths …. …. But in comparison to other frequencies at dusk there is a spike at around the 425-430nm mark.

So is sunset to total darkness use of amber glasses a mismatch? Does chronic use during the day affect the melanopsin repair cycle? The recipe is different, high yellow, low/no 460-480 but also no 425-430. If melanopsin repairs during day but then no signal later to do its job, what are the effects on the leptin receptor and liver DHA long loop? A light amber filter from my Lee filters swatch has Y=75.13%, and a deep amber at Y= 63.90%, so I’m thinking my amber blockers are in that region. ### Sue-UK — 2017-06-12T16:50:26+0300

Copying this here as its relevant to my SQ fat interest ...

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=215930)
>
> ELF-UV in my opinion is the key to understanding the optical redox ratio and the solar deficit in fat tissue below the skin. People forget that leptin is a fat hormone and that human fat is DESIGNED to be right below the skin. Pathologic fat is found in the viscera and abdomen. This is a redox ratio that has been shown to be a sensitive biomarker of changes associated with the relative rates of key metabolic and biosynthetic pathways in cells, including glycolysis, oxidative phosphorylation, glutaminolysis, and lipid synthesis. The frequencies are the key to understanding how mitochondria do what they do in both WAT and BAT. I think light frequencies are the key to understanding all this. Shifts in cellular metabolic pathway utilization also lead to alterations in the rate and distribution of NAD(P)H binding to dfferent oxidoreductase enzymes, leading to distinct NAD(P)H fluorescence lifetimes in fat tissues. NADPH is the key to the PPP and FAD is key to understanding how food electrons from fats enter the inner mitochondrial membrane. It still appears the redox shifted state is the key to the Warburg metabolism. I have another Reality blog on this very topic.
> Optical redox ratio has been calculated by taking the ratio of mean fluorescence intensities within the segmented cytoplasm regions as: redox ratio = FAD/(NAD(P)H+FAD). The key to this ratio is the AMOUNT OF WATER that surrounds a mitochondria and that water comes from mitochondrial functioning or dysfunctioning. This is the big missing link. Water production in a mitochondria is a function of the incident light rays a cell assimilates and slows to create the things it needs to get the job of physiology done properly.
> CITES:
>
> 1.  Quinn, K. P. et al. Quantitative metabolic imaging using endogenous fluorescence to detect stem cell differentiation. Scientific reports 3, 3432, doi: 10.1038/srep03432 (2013).
>
>
> 2.  Varone, A. et al. Endogenous two-photon fluorescence imaging elucidates metabolic changes related to enhanced glycolysis and glutamine consumption in precancerous epithelial tissues. Cancer research 74, 3067–3075, doi: 10.1158/0008-5472.CAN-13-2713 (2014).

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-15T13:44:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-216240>

@[lohd2015](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18338/) in response to your picture of the coloured glasses.
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-6-15_11-30-16-png.5046/)
Getting ready for my post sunset blockers hack.
These photos were taken in strong sunlight, but you can see how much of the yellow and green has been reduced by the filter. According to the swatch the yellow is at 25.68%, which is higher than my current home made blockers at 18.9%, but – for melanopsin -these ones have the “430-465nm at dusk when we move from bright daylight to sunset” but not so dark that I can’t function in dimly lit areas.
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-6-15_11-34-3-png.5047/)
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-6-15_11-34-51-png.5048/)
I was babysitting last night and decided to hack DS’s tv on its normal brightness setting (much brighter than we have the settings). They are staying in our mobile home between house moves, so I walked barefoot across the field, and wore my wine coloured kaftan. Couldn’t see the sunset itself, but saw the sky frequencies from the decking just outside the front door and then went back in and hacked the tv with the blockers. Felt the tv was too bright, but it didn’t stop me falling asleep for a while. I deliberately kept the tv on until they got home, so I could test what it would do to my sleep. It was nearly midnight when I got home, after having walked back barefoot in darkness. Went straight to sleep in my red room and woke up as normal as soon as the birds started chirping, and was out for the sunrise.

Going to make the most of the natural later sunset and dusk frequencies while I can, but I’m hoping that as winter sets in, to enable me to function after 5 pm, these are a better choice than amber.

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-17T15:16:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-216423>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=216420)
>
> Lovely. Which evoked a thought:
> The church...ultimate biohacking sanctuary in the past.

Years ago on holiday in the sun I used to go into churches in the extreme heat of the day when no services were on, to look at the stained glass set up. Spirituality without the religion. One in particular stayed with me, the area for the priest and choir was irradiated with yellow stained glass (keep them awake and in the now ....) but the congregation area was lit in a soft purple light, like the dusk frequencies. Closing the eyes helps the brain tune to the 7.83 frequencies, (praying) whilst bathed in the dusk frequencies at an evening service before heading home under darkening skies?

I was tired on Thursday evening, and wanted to go to bed when the sun set, but was going to miss the deeper dusk frequencies. So I decided to lay in bed with my eyes closed for a few minutes in my new glasses, and then get up and close the west facing window blackout curtains. Instead I fell asleep with the glasses on, and woke up a bit later because I'd rolled over and squashed the glasses into my face. I've already got a sheet of a deep purple filter, its too dark to function as glasses, so my next hack if I want to go to bed earlier in the longer light cycle is to stand at the open side of the window with the glasses for a few minutes before blacking out that side, leave the other side getting the natural evening light, but with the deeper purple filter over it to make a stained glass window. If I then get into bed and go to sleep, DH will close it when he comes to bed at a more normal time.

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-19T13:40:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-216531>

I don’t usually record what my current sunbathing routine is, but I did yesterday so that I could re-examine it in relation to hormones going on and off etc, and see if I could think of any realistic marginal gains.

I was out at 5 a.m. and the air temp was 16C so I used warmed ghee. From 5-8 is the visible and IR light. I’m only guessing the UVA was in by 8 a.m. because my damaged eye functions better as a camera in strong UV and it was at 8 that I “saw” the shift. But because I’m not sure of the exact time UVA comes in, for me at this stage of the light cycle it makes getting the dawn to7 ish light critical. The UV was forecast as 4 at 10 a.m. So I stayed out until 11 a.m. Had the meal to do, so from 11 til 1 I was in and out as much as I could, but got at least an hour. It was 31C at 1 p.m. From 1 (UV8) to 3 I was under dappled shade. I got sweaty, so at 3 p.m. I did a 20 minute session in the CT bath (cool, not cold water, I took it as it was). By that time it had got a bit cloudy (guessing 50% blue skies, with some white clouds). I dried off outside. The clouds disappeared after about an hour. From about 4 until sunset I was in and out regularly. It was the heat of the evening, (and I think my blood coming to the surface to collect light as the UV disappeared?) that caused me to sweat, so I l drank more water and let my body get on with it, and then took a cool shower before bed.

I was outside for more than 12 hours, only used the ghee at 5 a.m., drank plenty of water. I’ve found that by starting my skin adaptation from dawn, I don’t burn, or have any skin discomfort at all. I have been wondering if my shade from 1-3 and going in and out between 11 and 1 had benefits. Blood coming to the surface in a strong UV (particularly on the head), is likely to get bleached. So the break from the UV might stimulate the blood to come to the surface and harvest, and stimulate sweat to cool the surfaces down without almost immediate evaporation? My equivalent of going in and out of the canopy?

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-19T14:02:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-216532>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=216427)
>
> Thinking more about churches and stained glass...when there is service, the music, prayers all work in synergy with the color frequencies to tune the cells.
> looking forward to hearing more about your color hacks. Most fascinating and informative. thank you for sharing, Sue.

The stained glass window didn't work. I think it might do better at a lower latitude, but the lack of intensity of the evening light at my latitude, together with shielding from trees ruled it out, and if its not right this close to summer solstice, it definitely won't work in December, and in any case it will be dark by 5 p.m.  Last night's hack was to use a salt lamp. I made it a temporary cone shaped hat, with a peg on the top to hold it together, and used a sheet of diffuser film on top of that. It was placed over 8 ft away from where I sleep. I went to bed early, and DH switched it off when he came to bed.

When we went to Cornwall we stopped on the way for something to eat, and the café were using a clear vase with fairy lights in a dark corner, and I said to DH it would be better in a red vase. As we walked back to the car we passed a charity shop ..... which had just such a red vase, so I bought it. I've ignored it up to now because I was thinking of a winter use, but it matches my red room colour .... I'd tried it yesterday with fairy lights in and the hat on and didn't like it. But this morning I had the thought that the base and light of the salt lamp might fit it nicely ... and it does. The temporary hat is in for new hack tonight.

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-20T17:41:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-216624>

So I've been trying to make some links between my eye problem, and the dusk frequencies.  First I found [https://www.karger.com/Article/Full...etinal Interface in Ocular Health and Disease](<https://www.karger.com/Article/FullText/353447The Vitreous, the Retinal Interface in Ocular Health and Disease>). The section on aging vitreous and its consequences interested me, and the mention of vitreochisis. The section on floaters and photopsias caused by vitreoretinal traction also. Since improving my screen in terms of blue, flicker and anti glare, and adding in my new post sunset different blue blockers, the slight floatering in my left eye have improved. For me, not squirting nicotine up my nostrils is the best way to avoid photopsias, so I've got no problem with them, and I don't get dry eyes any more, no need for CO at all.

On another tangent, I was looking for a simplified version of a Jablonski diagram to do individual molecules, and found one on this unrelated topic ... or so I thought. [http://kortum.rice.edu/uploadedFiles/Kortum/Publications/1998/Fluorescence Spectroscopy\_A Technique with Potential to Improve the Early Detection of Aerodigestive Tract Neoplasia.pdf](http://kortum.rice.edu/uploadedFiles/Kortum/Publications/1998/Fluorescence%20Spectroscopy_A%20Technique%20with%20Potential%20to%20Improve%20the%20Early%20Detection%20of%20Aerodigestive%20Tract%20Neoplasia.pdf)
FLUORESCENCE SPECTROSCOPY: A TECHNIQUE WITH POTENTIAL TO IMPROVE THE EARLY DETECTION OF AERODIGESTIVE TRACT NEOPLASIA. On the page with the Jablonski diagram, I found a table of endogenous tissue fluorophores including collagen crosslinks and collagen powder. So I'm wondering if the dusk frequencies (without the UV, a drop in the daytime blues, and a drop in yellow) has any repair bearing on (first paper) collagen crosslinking/collagen fibres/HA-collagen interactions (HA-collagen interactions "_insure proper hydration and spacing, thus decreasing light scattering and enhancing transparency. It also provides the vitreous with its visceoelastic properties. "_

Back to more reading, but worth continuing the hack anyway

Report on my new evening blockers. They are definitely better than amber for my n=1. But I wouldn't drive in them, because I would risk falling asleep at the wheel. If I use them when going to bed at night, they help me get to sleep faster, but stretched out in front of a tv it knocks me out for about 40 mins. I hacked them again at DS's last night. But I chose a programme from iplayer, a science and nature programme about an 80 year old man still diving for fish ..... Ten minutes in and I fell asleep, so lost 40 minutes in the middle. Chose another programme (astronomy), same thing happened. Chose a third, a programme about fungi, start and end really interesting but again lost the middle completely. Perhaps I'll have to stand up to watch it next time.  But I deliberately chose programmes that had no bad vibes, or adverts. If I take a long blink or inspect the back of my eyelids in them, I'm vulnerable to things getting by my BS radar. It would be interesting to see a sunrise +6 % versus dusk -6% EEG.

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-23T14:32:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-216882>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=216821)
>
> That's amazing. Which color are you using for your evening blocker Sue-UK?

 [They](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?attachments/upload_2017-6-15_11-30-16-png.5046/) are a lavender shade. Used them with my night light afterwards for a bit last night, but took some MB. Not a good nights sleep, lets just say my stars in a jar must have had a CME, woke at 12.30 ish and it kept me awake, I had restless leg in my right leg (but didn't think that was a bad thing at the time ... it felt like the lack of sensation to a tuning fork on my foot, that I'd paid a chiropractor to get to the better level it is now, was having another go at getting better IYKWIM. The nerves were attempting to "react" if that makes sense ... Really weird sensation). The phosphene show on the back of my eyelids was something else ...It was like a reverse fraunhofer lines picture - black with shiny streaming dots where the fraunhofer lines on the normal spectrum would be. Moving in an ordered rather than random way. Then pulses of gold or purple, like a nebula, occasionally a bit of green or red. I finished up at 2 a.m. outside grounding looking up at the real stars for about 20 minutes, then went back to bed and slept til 4.30. Then outside until 5.30 to get the sunrise, and then crawled back into bed until 8, which is not like me. Still thinking about it ....

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-24T10:31:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-216984>

_"I finished up at 2 a.m. outside grounding looking up at the real stars for about 20 minutes, then went back to bed and slept til 4.30."
@[seanb4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1910/)_
This jumped out at me after what you said. And I also remembered that my hacks at DS's were followed by a barefoot walk home in the dark. So yesterday I did a repeat with the MB... except I just used the glasses for 10 minutes before getting into bed. As soon as I felt the first restless leg signal, I put on 2 grounding straps, one on my wrist, the other on the opposite foot. The wires go out the window and straight into the earth. (When I first tried it, my decision was based on my n=1 variables, location, not via any house wiring etc etc.) I don't usually sense they are doing anything, they seem neutral, so this was a test for that too. The restless leg symptoms stopped, I positioned myself comfortably for me, which was rolled over on my right with the strapped hand cupping my right ear, and in the recovery position with my left leg touching my right making a circuit. Like a modified Eeman Relaxation Circuit. All unpleasant symptoms stopped, the light show on my eyelids was interesting but in a soothing kind of way, and I went to sleep. Must have pushed off the straps when I'd had enough, they weren't on this morning. Really good dream filled sleep.

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-27T19:07:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-217267>

I found this too ...[http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article/file?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0026388&type=printable](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article/file?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0026388&type=printable)
UV-Sensitive Photoreceptor Protein OPN5 in Humans and Mice
What interested me was figure 1 C and the mention of yellow light - still trying to work out signalling differences at sunrise and sunset ... I've been looking at it on and off for several days, but I just don't get what it means. Pictures C and D have something to tell me, but I don't know what. I can't get the words to connect to the picture.

C) Bistable photoreaction of human OPN5. The reconstituted sample for human OPN5 was obtained by solubilizing the membrane fraction of a stable cell line expressing human OPN5 (HEK293S-hOPN5#48). This partially purified human OPN5 was, as in panel B, subjected to illumination with 357-nm UV light (curve 1; 80 µW/cm2) **and subsequently with >480-nm yellow light** (curve 2; 3.6 mW/cm2, given that it was 550-nm monochromatic light) to obtain difference spectra in a similar manner to panel B. The difference spectra (curves 1 and 2) were similar in shape to those for mouse OPN5, indicating bistable photoreactions between a UV-absorbing pigment and a blue-absorbing species. Note that enhanced decrease of absorbance in 380-nm region during UV illumination (curve 1) can be partly due to photoreaction by non-OPN5 molecule(s) as this photoreaction also occurred in the control preparation using HEK293S cells (curve 3). (D) Estimation of the absorption spectrum for human OPN5. The spectra for human OPN5 and its photoproduct were estimated from curve 2 in panel C by using spectral templates for opsin-type photopigments

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-28T15:22:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-217334>

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-6-28_11-12-59-png.5192/)

Time 9. "Melatonin has many different functions in light and dark environments. "
"Neuropsin light sensors send light info from our skin and eye to our hypothalamus. Fibers from the hopothalamus descend to the spinal cord and ultimately project to the superior cervical ganglia, from which post ganglionic neurons ascend back to the pineal gland where the mother load of melatonin awaits to clean up the mess a day creates in our brain. Thus, the pineal gland is similar to the adrenal medulla, in the sense that it transduces signals from the sympathetic nervous system into a hormonal signal."
So does yellow after dusk keep the mother load of melatonin waiting?
Then there's serotonin
Found this [http://www.jbc.org/content/215/1/337.full.pdf](http://www.jbc.org/content/215/1/337.full.pdf) from 1954 showing a second peak at around 540. So does yellow-green after sunset delay the gut melatonin factory?
Back to time 9....

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-01T10:02:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-217694>

[http://jcs.biologists.org/content/127/2/388.long](http://jcs.biologists.org/content/127/2/388.long) Light-harvesting chlorophyll pigments enable mammalian mitochondria to capture photonic energy and produce ATP
Sorta makes sense that an animal living in the cold who can get through the snow to get a bit of chlorophyll could use it to generate ATP. Even without the sun, fat burning might generate enough photons .... A fat sparing back door in cold and dark? Eke out the fat stores a bit longer? Or - the only time they go for it - give a starving polar bear a bit more time?

I've been looking for how animals photosynthesise differently, but in terms of survival it also makes perfect sense for animals to innovate a novel photosynthetic use for something that had already proved itself.

### lohd2015 — 2017-07-01T15:48:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-217711>

It is thought
that low hypothalamic dopamine levels and excessive
sympathetic tone in the CNS alter the metabolic
state resembling changes in mammalian species
that undergo hibernation. Such animals switch to a
state of insulin resistance in muscle and liver along
with accelerated hepatic glucose production and
gluconeogenesis, hyperglycaemia, adipocyte insulin
resistance, increased lipolysis, enhanced fat oxidation
with increased plasma free fatty acids and
triglycerides as well as obesity.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-02T12:07:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-217759>

My new screen. Just pegged up the reflective paper to see if it worked as I want before fixing it. Tried it after a CT session early this morning. Stood in the east facing doorway with it like a box behind me. Used a couple of infra red lamps to bounce light off it, so a mix of natural and reflected light. Out of the wind, with a little bit of warmth. Don't need it yet, but when the winter starts heading in, with the cold, low light levels and more CT ...... bring it on.

Where I'm using it should be fine, but it is very reflective, so I don't think I would use it like I am if I was in a high RF area. That part of the garden is protected by a hedge, then the driveway and an 8 ft evergreen hedge, plus a tree line the other side of the hedge, plus other trees and hedges in line with the nearest cell tower, so I'm reading 0.0019 ish outside the east door. Indoors I checked the readings each side of the divider while DS had the wifi on, (higher but still on bottom green button on the cornet) and I've just been playing with a piece of the paper and an ipad. Used in reverse I'm thinking it could have deflect away uses, but I wouldn't want to use it the right way round in the wrong environment. If the ipad sat on the reflective surface result was anything to go by  it could become a DIY microwave oven. More testing on the list ....

### Lahelada — 2017-07-02T18:07:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-217780>

Hi Sue , love the goldfoil set up. I must admit I am confused by your choice of evening blockers . Violet = 659 hz= approx 400nm . If you are hacking within the sound healing realm that would be approaching earth night time harmonics ,but not quite getting there. I ask as I get the same effect from walking barefoot at night on a conductive surface. From a visible light perspective it seems to be a mismatch though . At this time of night you do not perceive UVA even via your peropsin. ("Expression of peropsin in human skin is related to phototransduction of violet light in keratinocytes.")

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-07T16:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-218171>

**Reality 7:_“A photocatalyst operates much as chlorophyll does in a plant, absorbing energy from light and causing a chemical reaction in water to charge separate it and liberate energy in the form of hydrogen and free electrons. In my opinion, the process of photocatalysis is simply a form of_** _“artificial animal photosynthesis.”Calling it artificial is a misnomer, only because photosynethesis has been ascribed to plant porphyrins at the present moment. Science is now realizing that animals may have this ability buried in the connection of hemoglobin and their mitochondia. The phosphate proteins are the key linkage. When some one looks at hemoglobin and chlorophyll side by side, and realize how much phosphate is present in RBC’s one can see that mammals have always used this process as plants have. We just have missed the process because we are ignorant about quantum mechanisms in cells. Cells have harnessed the power of phosphorus in all our proteins to make our peripheral clock genes operate properly. When they don’t harness this power you get diseases in that organ because the mitochondria become faulty and heteroplasmy rises. This is why phosphorylation of proteins is found everywhere one looks in high energy metabolic pathways in a cell.”
_
Back to [http://jcs.biologists.org/content/127/2/388.long](http://jcs.biologists.org/content/127/2/388.long) Light-harvesting chlorophyll pigments enable mammalian mitochondria to capture photonic energy and produce ATP………….Chlorophyll dropping its magnesium in the acid environment of the stomach, finishes up in various tissues in the body, ability for mammal to use with sunlight to create ATP.

(I am particularly interested in the paper’s reference to the lens and _“we previously observed that chlorophyll metabolites enabled photonic energy capture to enhance vision using a mouse model.”)
_
I want to link this to Reality 14 next, but so far it sounds like a hack to me.

Don’t want to use a chlorophyll supplement – usually one source of chlorophyll, and plant leaves themselves contain other pigments that work synergistically (for example sun protection). Also if it’s taken within the whole leaf I think it could have a better chance of having the magnesium stripped off in the stomach acid than chlorophyll drops in water. Currently have a powder mix of wheat and barley grasses, spirulina, spinach and kale. Plus I add in some seaweed sprinkles and a little MB, mixed with minimal water. Tried it one day with 4 small doses at regular intervals, (energy levels good all day and only had one meal), and yesterday only took one small dose. Noticed a sustained increase in energy levels with the 4 small regular doses with a sunlight chaser. So worth hacking it properly. But I’m going to buy ingredients separately and ring the changes as the season progresses, choosing things that are capable of growing or seaweed being harvested on the coastline in the UK. I don’t want to signal “same” by using the same mix constantly. (Reality 7: _Your environment is never static so neither should your mito-hacks or your beliefs be. We are built to be non linear beings being able to harvest Earth’s linear seasonal environments with their ever changing solar light frequencies._ “ ). So change it up, use sunlight when I can, but Bioptron red light when I can’t. (Could be a good Quantlet hack?)

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-10T14:27:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-218352>

So a twist on my ghee sun cream hack. Yesterday’s hack was to cut a DHA/EPA capsule open (also contains lemon and rosemary and tocopherols, and fortunately I don't smell like a sardine) and mixed it with my ghee, and slapped it on my skin before sunbathing. I loved the smell, wanted to get a spoon and eat it.  I first just put it on my face and neck before I got in the CT tub, then did the rest after I came out. Interesting thing happened whilst in the tub, I turned round and knelt on the top step, to give my back some early morning sun, and was looking at a flowering plant growing through the hedge right behind me. It is pretty well past flowering, most of the petals had faded or dropped, but there was one in front of me that was still waiting. In my contemplative state I could see waves of energy (a bit like a white mist) pulsing towards the flower along the stem. A few leaves higher up also had it to a lesser extent, but although the whole hedge was bathed in the same sunlight, and the air conditions were still, the flower was gently being moved. It was fascinating to watch, and became a “wow” moment of grace.

After putting on the rest of the DHA mix I sunbathed for a while in the morning sunlight, would definitely have got a fair bit of UVA but with things I had to do I didn’t get as much UVB as I would have liked. But with what I did get my skin seems to have developed more of a tan anyway. And my skin liked it. A lot. And my energy levels were really good. So that got me searching for topical DHA and I found this, [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26747719](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26747719) Topical Docosahexaenoic Acid (DHA) Accelerates Skin Wound Healing in Rats and Activates GPR120. The mention of GPR120 had me looking that up, and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GPR120](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GPR120). Interesting, particularly the mention of rhodopsin. So thinking quantum links to the eye I did a search of rhodopsin in the blogs – several to reread, but have started with Time 11, can you supplement sunlight. Big clues in there already. Lots on topical EPA but one is Topical EPA and protection from sun damage- [http://www.jlr.org/content/47/5/921.full](http://www.jlr.org/content/47/5/921.full)
I’m juggling rabbit holes …

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-11T13:25:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-218479>

I kept getting pictures in my head of basking seals and sea lions.  Some risk great danger to bask in the sun on floating ice. So that got me thinking about their DHA laden skin in the sun. I was looking for another blog but intuitively picked up Ubi 5 The Sea Within. Lots of sea related stuff, and " DHA is the ultimate evolutionary creation for "harnessing" solar power; it allowed eukaryotes to become fully capable of providing a larger margin of safety, because they could capture more energy from the environment." ... "DHA turns sunlight into a DC electric current that a cell can use in thousands of different ways, by virtue of the laws of physics." "When we eat an Epi-Paleo template, we are able to harness the full solar power of the sun, to live a somewhat disconnected life. We become able to harness the proper frequencies of light that cells need to interact with water, to create a human life". (and more). But my feeling is that, if having been doing the epi-paleo, my skin for whatever reason hasn't been accumulating DHA, in an environment with less sun availability, the topical application of DHA is helping. Ubi 5 is today's study blog.

For the last 2 nights, since starting this hack, I've slept through until 6.30 a.m. instead of being awake before the sunrise. Will be interesting to see if this is just coincidence, or if its a hack induced trend.

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=218481)
>
> Sue-UK, how do you find the DHA topical application to be different from the MB topical applications? Thank you for sharing.

I went back to plain ghee because although getting as much light as possible was the priority, rather than aiming for a tan, I thought I would get what tan came naturally, and not risk any bleaching effects of MB (which I can't hack properly anyway, the weather is too inconsistent.), and I'm taking MB orally anyway in my green sludge mix. The ghee is nice, but I almost feel that my skin is sucking in the ghee/DHA/EPA mix. It wants it. Really wants it. That could be an n=1 based on the remaining glycation, I'm not sure, but my skin feels more fluid under the surface IYKWIM. I've now found and am interested in [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15979259](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15979259) In vitro percutaneous absorption studies and in vivo evaluation of anti-inflammatory activity of essential fatty acids (EFA) from fish oil extracts.

I'm drawn to putting a drop in the green sludge. Not every time, but I just go with my intuition. ### Sue-UK — 2017-07-12T12:15:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-218572>

From wiki on GPR120
_"GPR120 has also been shown to mediate the anti-inflammatory and insulin-sensitizing effects of omega 3 fatty acids. Lack of GPR120 is responsible for reduced fat metabolism, thereby leading to obesity.
Additionally, GPR120 has been implicated to be involved in the ability to taste fats. It is expressed in taste bud cells (specifically cell type II, which contain other G-protein coupled taste receptors), and its absence leads to reduced preference to two types of fatty acid (linoleic acid and oleic acid), as well as decreased neuronal response to oral fatty acids."_
This drew me because of the sense of taste, I've just started massaging my tongue with my DHA/EPA ghee mix. Reading the new blog CPC14, Tinnitus the quantum view point [https://www.jackkruse.com/cpc-14-tinnitus-quantum-view-point/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cpc-14-tinnitus-quantum-view-point/), I'm going to add in my ears, and tie back my hair when I sunbathe. Love this blog.

> From wiki on GPR120
> _"GPR120 has also been shown to mediate the anti-inflammatory and insulin-sensitizing effects of omega 3 fatty acids. Lack of GPR120 is responsible for reduced fat metabolism, thereby leading to obesity.
> Additionally, GPR120 has been implicated to be involved in the ability to taste fats. It is expressed in taste bud cells (specifically cell type II, which contain other G-protein coupled taste receptors), and its absence leads to reduced preference to two types of fatty acid (linoleic acid and oleic acid), as well as decreased neuronal response to oral fatty acids."_
> This drew me because of the sense of taste, I've just started massaging my tongue with my DHA/EPA ghee mix. Reading the new blog CPC14, Tinnitus the quantum view point [https://www.jackkruse.com/cpc-14-tinnitus-quantum-view-point/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cpc-14-tinnitus-quantum-view-point/), I'm going to add in my ears, and tie back my hair when I sunbathe. Love this blog.

I printed out the paper Free Fatty Acid Receptor GPR120 and Pathogenesis of Obesity and Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus. _"GPR120 is highly expressed in mouse pituitary and human breast cancer cells"_ Also (in mice), _"In addition, intracerbroventricular injection of Omega 3 and 9 fatty acids results in significantly decreased food intake and increased leptin-induced anorexigenic effect, and thus results in significant reduction of adipocyte size and loss of body weight. Most importantly, they identified that GPR120 is co-expressed with neuropeptide Y in the arcuate nucleus in hypothalamus."_ So its been back searching the blogs for neuropeptide Y. (Wonder how does GP120 quantumly tie in with leptin ....seasonal light changes/ UV specifically/both?) The human breast cancer cell mention is interesting .....made me pause for a moment and think about my hack, but the skin tags under my arms are going .... which I think is a very good sign. Reading that the highest GPR 120 expression in a cynomolgus monkey is in the lung and colon, had me using a cotton bud with the mix on to apply inside my nose  breathe that signal in ... I've also done a scalp massage, just a little bit, and left it for 24 hours before washing my hair. Switched to east facing CT when I get up, then apply mix and get in the sun/cloud bathe ... Loving this hack.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-19T15:32:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-219380>

GP120 is loaded with aromatic amino acids and also uses a tyrosine receptor to work. Samething is true with IGF-1 and many of the IGFBP.

Now you can listen to this to make some new connections:
[https://elitehrv.com/hrv-ketones-glucose-food-sensitivities-alessandro-ferretti](https://elitehrv.com/hrv-ketones-glucose-food-sensitivities-alessandro-ferretti)

I met Alessandro in London during Jamie Ward's bio-hacking event in London and he lives in the UK. I dropped this note on his wall about the podcast: I think your next step in this story needs to be tied to IGF-1 and all the IGFBP with respect to BG/HRV ratio I've got a sense that the double edged sword of IGF-1 is tied to the electron excitement that is present or absent and it will correlate with HRV waveforms. I told you when I spoke in London that the leptin receptor was an ‘account’ for protons and electrons. The mechanism of counting is done several ways. One is in the retina and the information from leptin in the SQ fat comes via the water networks in CSF. As you know CSF is an ultra filtrate of blood plasma. This is where the idea of BG/HRV enters as the light finger print. Insulin is a solar hormone and IGF-1 is the key to sulfation of many tissues and cellular componets by sunlight. Key elements in the blood and arterial walls need sulfation too and this imparts charges to both tissues. All bits of matter that make things up roughly have equal numbers of electrons and protons. The basics of what most people forget is that if this were not so, there would be an excess of positive or negative charge, and this would create a massive force pushing all the excess charge out, leaving behind a core group of neutral charges behind. When sunlight moves electrons in our bio-molecules with the energy in light waves is alters the arrangement of our bio-molecules. This is why the ratio of HRV links to glucose, IGF-1, and all the binding proteins. I have a sense the link will be to the golden ratio. Why do I say this? Different molecular arrangements have different energies associated with them. This difference in energy is how bio-molecules are quantized to light frequencies that will be contained with the HRV data. It also explains how metabolic networks became coupled to sunlight frequencies when cells were an evolutionary idea 3.8 billion years ago. Some of my thoughts you might ponder............

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-24T16:22:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-219932>

Time to update my progress….

Mum’s death was a turning point, I knew I was barely treading water for the previous 2 years, and I’ve given myself time to recover from it. I did very little CT during May and until summer solstice, concentrated on getting the light. Now if it’s a clear sky first thing I get in the sun first, and then CT, then sun again. If its cloudy or raining its straight in the tub, and then get the sun when I can once the skies have cleared. I can cope with days where the weather is so bad I can’t get out much at all, which I hope means I’m either holding charge better or not leaking energy because of the less stressful situation, or a bit of both. Interesting was that from day 1 after solstice I could feel the light was now different, I’m more aware of the frequencies moving towards the next season. More aware of what my body senses, (a better mitochondrial swarm?) rather than constantly living in my head IYKWIM. Not spending stressful hours in the nursing home, where the population of staff and residents is more than I have within a mile radius of where I live has made a difference too. I’m not constantly in mitigation mode.

My home made evening glasses have made a big difference to my sleep. I thought my sleep was good, then my “red room” set up improved it again, but these have taken it to the next level. I’m seeing my friend this week who is hacking them too, to see if its N=2 result. Because I’m finding them making me sleepy too early, which will be a problem when its dark by 5 p.m., I’ve just started hacking another filter earlier in the evening. They are pink, but its not the visual colour, its about the frequencies they let through. I already had a “pink” gel that seemed to have a good range of early evening frequencies, slightly more yellow than the later ones, so I’m starting with that one, but there’s a couple more I am interested in. But so far so good. Natural light, the new ones, and then the more purple ones seem to give better shift towards sleep than the “same” of amber, which don’t have the decresendoing of yellow, or the saturated colours of evening. If they continue to be a better choice than amber, my friend is up for another hack. I think there’s mileage in using different ones throughout the day say in a constant blue lit work place, rather than the “same” of amber, but I’m not in that position, so that’s further down the list of potential hacks, but in the meantime I’m interested in learning more about it. Moving my office to the conservatory where I have 2 doors and 8 windows I can open, and changing my monitor has made a big difference, and I can see the breaks in the clouds to nip out for a sun break. I’m already preparing for winter with my reflective screen, and have bought 2 cellular 100% cotton cot blankets that I’m going to stitch up down the sides and top leaving gaps for my arms and head, to make myself a kiniki-style kaftan. The holes in the fabric let light through, but will give me some warmth too. With my new planned hacks, CT and a couple of trips for a week in the sun, I’m feeling really positive that I can still make progress through the winter.

The ghee and DHA on the skin has also made a big difference. I do it for a few days and then take a break for a couple of days to see how my skin responds, it’s a treatment for as long as I need it, perhaps then do it occasionally, but I don’t want it to become a lifestyle choice. I want my skin to get to a point where it no longer needs to do it. But at the moment, that and sunshine is giving me a big improvement in skin tags under my arms. Head massage with it and leaving it for 24 hours gave my hair great condition after washing it afterwards. Massaging it on my tongue and ears, and using a cotton bud to wipe it round the inside of my nose seems to be improving my sense of taste and smell, and there’s no sign of the occasional tinnitus. I don’t seem to need to eat so much fat, which I think is right for this time of year. Much as I’d like my right eye to improve first, I’m leaving it to my inner doctor to do what its doing, and prompt me in the right direction. But Jacks post on the zebra fish eye, has me looking at DHA and eye microglia, so it will be interesting where that takes me. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19627444](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19627444) . I suspect the blog on the long loop of DHA and the liver will be up next for a review. But with my better sleep (and I’m losing belly fat,) I think my liver is getting more in tune …. Onwards and upwards.

### Queen of Elves — 2017-07-24T16:57:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-219937>

Your ghee and DHA hack seems pretty interesting! I will try that for my tinnitus.

Where do you spend your winter trips? I am looking for a location too, but I don't want to fly a lot. Is one week really enough for you or do you do several trips instead of one longer trip for a reason?

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-24T17:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-219944>

> [Queen of Elves said:](/goto/post?id=219937)
>
> Your ghee and DHA hack seems pretty interesting! I will try that for my tinnitus.
>
> Where do you spend your winter trips? I am looking for a location too, but I don't want to fly a lot. Is one week really enough for you or do you do several trips instead of one longer trip for a reason?

When my Mum was alive, with other family members talking about me being away, and pointing me out in a photo, she coped with me being gone for a week without losing her memory of me, (AD). In the last year I would wear the same outfit every time to help recognition, as facial recognition was being lost. If she didn't see a family member for much longer, she didn't recognise them again. Once it was gone it was gone, and as her main visitor and advocate I didn't want to risk her losing her memory of me. I would have been anxious taking a second week. Also, I take care of my granddaughters one day a week in term time, so if I go for one week I can do a swap with the other grandmother without any hassle, I do the same in return, and my granddaughters don't notice. Going in term time means the flights and accommodation are cheaper, and it means I can go 2 or 3 times, which also means when I come back from one, I can look forward to the next a few weeks later. Its better than going for longer but then looking at a long wait for the spring ... Anticipation of another winter break helps my dopamine.  Also if we make a mistake and where we go turns out to be a NNemf hellhole, its only a week. We've stuck with Fuerteventura for the last couple of years. First trip we had a pretty good place, but had a look round to see where else to try. Last 2 times have been at the same resort, no wifi in the apartments, cross the road down the hill and onto a beach. Away from the main tourist spots, resort next door was naturist, so no problems being nude on the beach. The kids thought we were boring going back to the same island all the time, but we knew we liked it, knew where everything was, so getting into chill mode was faster ... Its been what I needed ... sun, sun and more sun! Its also only a 4 hour flight from the UK.

### Sue-UK — 2017-08-30T10:56:43+0300

My -6 solar evening blockers definitely help my sleep, still working on the earlier evening ones ... may need to use a 2 filters combo, but I have too many hacks going at the moment, and preparing to give a 2 hour talk in late September to 6 people with or connected to people with EHS... Got to do it at home because I get the RF down to 0.001 mW/M2. No tech so paper based handouts ... I've also started an eye drop hack - testing a base formula at the moment before adding the SkQ1. I've been obsessed with the microbiome, not just the gut but the skin and eyes, so I'm also doing some probiotic preparation work, particularly after reading [http://www.nature.com/articles/srep40826?WT.feed\_name=subjects\_biological-sciences](http://www.nature.com/articles/srep40826?WT.feed_name=subjects_biological-sciences) _Association of the Intestinal Microbiome with the Development of Neovascular Age-Related Macular Degeneration._ With the light levels starting to drop, my microbiome could become an important source of light, (and dopamine?) so I need the good guys to step up to the plate. After some heavy supplementation for a month, I suddenly didn't want to take them any more (apart from the occasional chewing and putting under my tongue of one capsule (dose is 10), and I suddenly got a real craving to eat raw salmon. I'm gonna roll with that one for a bit, my inner doctor is whispering, so just stocked up . And I'm hacking a homeopathic throat spray that I've used in the past, but added liquid nicotine, not to homeopathic proportions but a little goes a long way when used sublingually .... > I've just started hacking a grounding yoga and fitness mat  Using it as a runner along the bottom of the bed, so that my, and DH's, feet and calves are on the mat during sleep. Its connected to a rod in the earth outside the window. Much better than a grounding strap. Much easier to keep clean, more comfortable than a strap round my foot, and it can be used elsewhere, even outside as a camping mat, or in a tent, just stick the grounding rod into the soil .. Who knows I might even use it for yoga ...

Can I clarify the grounding yoga mat... Is it a conventional yoga mat? Does that carry a charge up the mat??

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-02T18:54:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-222759>

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-03T10:18:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-222782>

My grounding yoga mat decision was based on a lowering light frequency, and thinking about it being critical for mammals in northern latitudes to be grounded most of the time, particularly during sleep/hibernation. When I thought about it some more I calculated that during the winter I'm not going to be grounding outside as much, and calculating how many hours a day I'm ungrounded even in summer is a concern. Throwing open what windows and doors I can if the weather is bad or if I have to work indoors gives me some light, but I'm not grounding at the same time, and any extra could give me an edge. I'd also like to try grounding if I'm using my Bioptron, or an IR light, or my vertical tanner in the winter, or for meditation. The conservatory isn't heated, so I can be in the cold lane and add some IR if I want. I've got my cot blanket kaftan ready, but DS2 is off to uni in a couple of weeks, so I'll also be able to do more nude indoors. Cold lane here I come! The weather was lovely yesterday but today its pouring with rain, so it'll be CT in the rain, but the forecast for the next 2 weeks is shite, and I don't do outside in storms ... If the weather grounds even the large birds its telling me something, and I take my cue from them.  I read an article [http://file.scirp.org/pdf/JCDSA\_2014111211461709.pdf](http://file.scirp.org/pdf/JCDSA_2014111211461709.pdf) **Grounding the Human Body Improves Facial Blood Flow Regulation: Results of a Randomized, Placebo Controlled Pilot Study.** Even though the study was using the property wiring system, and included TENS patches, I was interested in the pics of increased blood flow to the eyes.  I'd already got 3 grounding rods outside, bedroom, conservatory and sitting room, so I bought an extra connection lead so that I can just take the mat off the bottom of the bed and leave the lead, and easily reattach the mat in the dark ... and the second lead is so that during daylight when I can see what I'm doing I can use it in the other 2 rooms. UK winter ...? bring it on...!!!!

So inspired, Sue!

Out of curiosity, why don't you go outside in storms?

That whole adage about cows sitting down because it's going to rain... sometimes I see them down when it's not going to rain or doesn't rain - do you think they're tuned into something we aren't? Don't they follow ley lines and that's why they are all facing the same way ususally... Sorry, not sure why I'm going on about cows.

I think our house is grounded and so the move to Spain is going to see me in a first floor apartment for the first four weeks = not great! May purchase your recommended kit to take with us for the villa but not sure about the electrics outside so it may be a waste of time.

Off to go read that facial blood flow article...

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-04T11:36:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-222841>

 Just to clarify, I'm not recommending the kit to you - I'm hacking it.  No I don't think cows sit down because its going to rain. They may sometimes be facing the same way because of the wind direction or wind speed? (With what they produce they may want their heads upwind....)

Intuitively and from my inner critic  I get that unless there was a critical reason for taking a risk (such as a lost cub), most animals would go to ground/seek shelter in bad storms, and flying in high winds uses up energy .... It would leave me anxious - so there's no benefit in being outside naked or in the CT tub. For me it becomes a different sort of environmental mismatch.

Ah yes, wrong choice of word.

I tried to find something to post about the cows. I believe it was when Google Earth started they noticed the pics of cows in fields showed that they mostly point the same way. Ever since I read it every field I go past it's true - apart from maybe 2 or 3 rebel cows - they are all facing the same way. In my effort to find something I've got sidetracked with information on ley lines that I'd like to look into. presumably it would be interesting to stick a grounding rod in one!

Yes, I see what you mean about bad storms. I guess they are only compelling to me because I know I can go back inside whenever I please...

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-04T16:51:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-222855>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=222832)
>
> not sure about the electrics outside so it may be a waste of time.

You'd need to measure. I was adamant about having an outside grounding rod because I know there's no interference, but DH decided to measure if I had chosen to use the electric socket. He removed the live and neutral from a plug, and just from the earth pin to a separate grounding wire his voltmeter picked up .8 volts, so I'm glad I went the route I did. Its purely intuitive, but the image I got in my head is of electrons coming up from the grounding rod outside being imprinted by the magnetic field as the earth revolves. I saw it that at night particularly, when the magnetic field is the dominant signal, as the earth enters the magnetotail, the electrons would be imprinted (for want of a better word), differently as the night wore on, like an electron clock mechanism. I doubt the electrons would be "same" all night.

### Sue-UK — 2017-09-08T14:21:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-223157>

Update on my grounding yoga mat. I'm sleeping longer ... dreams are more detailed. A couple seem to have woken me up, but I think about them for a minute or so and go straight back to sleep. I added in a visualisation when I first get into bed, I put a picture in my mind of the grounding rod and imagine electrons coming up the wire to the mat and into my feet and legs. I've done that meditation before, both with a grounding strap and also without a grounding device, a kind of "virtual electron" thing. With the mat I can "sense" it on the skin in contact with the mat, but that might be my imagination ... I used it yesterday in the conservatory, stretched out on the floor listening to the September webinar through my wired in computer, with the doors and windows open. I felt very relaxed but alert, and quite rested afterwards. This morning it was pouring with rain so I stretched out on it to read, so far it hasn't helped my understanding of equations but I live in hope ....

You could apply it to any hack or even any particular event such as a night's sleep ... The positron interests me, particularly after watching

### Sue-UK — 2017-10-13T13:05:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-225735>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=223533)
>
> Rereading QED, the strange theory of light and matter by Richard Feynman. The phrase: _"..._ in order to correctly calculate the probability of an event , one must be very careful to _define the complete event clearly -_ in particular, what the initial conditions and the final conditions of the experiment are." has kept jumping out at me.

I had a chance to hack this when I went to Spain last week to visit my brother. It was the first time we would be spending time together since my Mum's funeral, so it was an opportunity for a bit of fence mending, which could have gone either way. On the flight out there was an operational error, the plane had been fueled for Cyprus instead of Malaga, which meant that the plane would be too heavy to land at Malaga. So we spent ages on an outside runway going up and down to burn off excess fuel. I started off thinking crap extra zapping, and just thinking about it I could feel my energy leaking. So I stopped and got into a meditative state and just thinking I would be fine, I'd done longer flights, I could ground and get some sun when I got there ..... Same on the holiday, in terms of cell towers and population density it was the worst I've been exposed to on holiday since my journey began. But although I was constantly aware of it, I just kept reminding myself that I also had good sunshine, opportunities to ground, the sea, seafood .... How I thought about it (the observer) could influence the "initial conditions." And the "final conditions" changed accordingly .... There was no rehashing of what had happened over my Mum, just lots of laughter, and the power of the sun kept me in the zone .... No fences needed mending, and I chose not to let fear make what I couldn't avoid worse. Really good recharging week.  And I'm booked for Fuerteventura in mid November for another recharging week. I don't want my kids to have travel a long way or to fly in specially, when they are coming home at Christmas anyway, so I'll be spending my 60th birthday in the sun. For my family I'm picking another date near Christmas as my birthday this year, so I'll get to celebrate it twice.  Dopamine for the win.....

### Sue-UK — 2017-10-13T13:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-225737>

I'm transferring this to my journal, as its changing a hack.

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=225473)
>
> I've been looking for a chart to show the 0 degree frequencies and came across this. a) is for rural b) for city. (source [https://www.nature.com/articles/srep26756#f1](https://www.nature.com/articles/srep26756#f1) )
>
>
> On these and the picture of the +6 and - 6 degrees elevation, the blues have what appears to be changes in saturation/intensity/ratios compared to other frequencies, rather than complete blocking. Although using amber glasses may be better than doing nothing post sunset, is there a downside in terms of the SCNs circadian "accounting", by not sensing the blues it would get from the natural environment?

My -6 degree blockers aren't so sedating now, I can wear them without nodding off to sleep. I think it may be due to the light levels dropping anyway. I'm not going from it being very light late into the evening to -6, so the change in stimulus isn't so strong. I've now got two pairs -one is the -6 filter on its own, and a second pair where I've added a grey which takes it down a notch. I printed out the picture of the +6 and -6 frequencies, and have drawn in the 0 degrees one as best I can , and am going to make up a pair of 0 degree glasses. So my hack now that winter is coming and it will be dark very early is to go from +6 to 0, and then -6 and a slightly darker -6.

I didn't do this hack for long, it didn't take too long to intuitively wonder about it, and when I realised that I wasn't comfortable with my friend hacking it too, I stopped. ### Sue-UK — 2017-10-25T10:29:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-226529>

With no moonlight I'm working blind, and that affects my sense of where I am in time and space. Worked round it yesterday by practising a route to the bathroom along the other wall, using the sense of touch. Worked out how many steps to the next marker, light switch, picture ... then turn left, two steps forward and stick my hand through the doorway. If I can feel tiles on the wall on the left hand side I'm in the right place .... Do the reverse to get safely back to bed.

### Sue-UK — 2017-10-27T10:26:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-226694>

Continuing with about 11 hours in darkness, after the simulated -6 solar elevation. Sleep not great, but last night the need to get up and pee was gone, so a step forward. Biggest problem is waking up because I'm hot. Not a sweating type of hot, but a pilot light on type of hot. It seems to be in a cycle. I cool off, go back to sleep and then later it happens again. Until sunrise creeps under one of the blackout curtains, I've got no idea what time it is, or how long I've slept between pilot light ups, or how long I'm awake when I wake up. Its probably habit just wanting to know, but I tell myself that an animal in a den wouldn't be clock watching, it is what it is ...

### Sue-UK — 2017-10-28T10:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-226791>

Yesterday I did a CT session at noon, clear blue skies so best chance of getting some UV and cold at the same time. Bobbed up and down in the water for a while, and then stood up in the bath and drip dried in the sun. The other thing I did was to open up the bedroom windows so that I deliberately created a cold draft around my face, so that I was sensing the outside environment better, but with the covers on my body I was warm enough to sleep. Used the glasses as usual, and the migraine cap when I first got into bed. With that, the better timing of CT to take advantage of the UV, and the summer bedding, I was comfortable, no pilot light flares, and I slept until first light. There was then enough light to move through the house without bumping into things, and I was out to see the sunrise. Great night's sleep.

I've got a chiropractic appointment this morning, and my chiropractor's advice last time was not to do extremes of temperature for a few days whilst my body is processing any adjustment, so lunchtime CT will have to wait. ### Sue-UK — 2017-11-03T12:54:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-227261>

The clocks went back last weekend, so I'm now working with solar time .... and its dark by around 5pm,. Temps have dropped a lot, but so far living in the cold lane, no heating yet. Trying to get into complete darkness by soon after 8 for 5 nights a week, and around 9 for 2 nights. Bedroom is cold, summer covers and letting the cold air in. Using the migraine cap from the freezer when I go to bed, (pulling the eye flaps over my eyes) means I start off thinking I'm too cold to sleep, but after a while the pilot light kicks in..... Deep, dream filled sleep, wake up warm just before sunrise.

I've got a 4 hour flight to Fuerteventura at the end of next week for a solar recharge week so I'm in deuterium depleting mode to better handle the flight ....

### Lahelada — 2017-11-03T16:49:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-227271>

You made me look .... I was not conversant with the abbreviation IOP, looked and found another tunnel in the warren. For what its worth

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3170121](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3170121) "Intraocular pressure (IOP) can be altered by changing body position. This report describes two experiments evaluating variations in IOP, as well as neural functioning of the retina and visual cortex (as measured by pattern-reversal electroretinogram and visual evoked potential), associated with whole-body, head-down tilt. The subjects, ten per experiment, were visually normal with IOP less than 19. In the first experiment, IOP elevations were induced by varying the angle of tilt in discrete steps between +90 degrees (upright) and -90 degrees (inverted). In each position IOP was measured and significant elevations (up to 3x baseline) were noted. These elevations were maintained for 1 min during which simultaneous retinal and cortical biopotentials were measured. In the second experiment, 6 degrees head-down tilt was maintained for 2 hr during which time the IOP and both biopotentials were measured repeatedly. Our findings confirm the effect of body position of IOP, while also revealing that head-down tilt produces significant reductions in neurophysiological function at both the retinal and cortical levels. The neural effect is maximized when 6 degrees head-down tilt is maintained for 20 min./"

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4274296/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4274296/)
"

### Sue-UK — 2017-11-10T11:43:55+0200

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4033305/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4033305/) Dim Light at Night Disrupts Molecular Circadian Rhythms and Affects Metabolism
I was interested in this because of the 14:10 light/dark cycle compared to a 14:10 light/dim light cycle, and my current hack of trying to get at least 10 hours complete darkness at night. I found this pic online,
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-11-10_9-19-12-png.5875/)
so was interested in where 5 lux comes in, and its effects on WAT. (I thought full moon was 1 lux, but perhaps that's a max). So perhaps 5 lux later into the evening says "late summer put on fat". Even a one lux full moon isn't every night ...

### Sue-UK — 2017-11-20T11:20:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-228473>

Did a freezing exercise with my well water, some CT and 10-11 hours darkness at night for a week before I went, and it worked well. Carried it on when I was out there, found some gerolstein (sp?) water, most of the day outside, got into the ocean at least 5 times a day (and if needing to pee was an Olympic sport I'd be on the national team ). Last ate just before sunset, then got into the dark for 12 hours ... might seem mad on holiday, but walking about outside under resort lighting wasn't very appealing, and it was less about holiday and more about a chance to heal. I also wanted my charged with sunlight blood doing a proper discharge in darkness (deuterium blog) - and I think that could take longer than 8 hours (Alexander Wunsch comment that regeneration needs more than 8 hours of darkness). Think I had some major deuterium depletion going on.  Had my 60th birthday when I was out there, deliberately kept it low key, as I'm officially celebrating it at New Year with DH, 3 children and DIL with a 4 day visit to Amsterdam and Utrecht. If I can get myself able to handle that with ease, I can think about long haul flight destinations .....

### Sue-UK — 2018-01-15T12:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-232591>

So new year new opportunities  Instead of putting money into a pension fund, for the last 18 years I've put it into buying a 2 bed first floor flat in a 4 flat complex in our town, and renting it out. The current tenant is moving out mid Feb, so the plan is to sell our current home, after moving back into the 2 mobile homes we lived in a couple of years ago. We were living there so that we could get our current house ready for sale, but my FIL and then my mum's health made it difficult, and in the longer term I needed more space for 2 adult children still in full time education. It wasn't possible to get the house ready, and there wasn't much point in living in the mobile homes when we were still paying the overheads of the house ..... so it all went on the back burner. But a year after Mum died, and then the tenant moving out means that we can rethink it .... Last time we were going to do some remodelling of the bathroom and kitchen before selling, but now we're going to sell it as is .... When DS1 was moving he had a brand new kitchen installed to help the sale ..... and the buyers ripped it straight out, wanted something different .... so we've rethought our earlier decision.

Keeping a 5 bedroom bungalow with a large garden is too much, and I liked the mobile homes (in summer at least - haven't tried a winter ....) But with the overheads of the flat going to be much less than my current house ... I'll be able to go to the sun for longer in the winter anyway ... ...Using the flat means that our 2 adult children in education have somewhere to go over the summer and any holidays, space to meet friends etc and when they are not home we have somewhere to go if we want a night out in town without having to drive home etc ....

The change from living in the mobile homes last time is that we are going to turn one into a day area, using the double bedroom as an office, and the second twin bedroom as an extension of the kitchen for food storage, perhaps a small freezer etc. The second home sitting room will be a bedroom, one bedroom can take my light therapy equipment, 2 for storage, and things like the washing machine and dishwasher can go in this mobile home to be used in the daytime, but it can have the breaker switched off overnight. And with the kids in the flat, we don't need wifi at all ....and they can do their own washing and cooking,  and won't be moaning about my not wanting to have meals after sunset .... Its near my well water, and my CT tubs can go back out the same as before. Since I last lived there the decking has been done, which has linked the 2 homes better, and I shouldn't fall down the steps again .....  As the homes are nearer the neighbours, although its still a reasonable distance, the next job is to go over with my meters to recheck the environment ....but at least we've got the makings of a plan .......
