Carried it on when I was out there, found some gerolstein (sp?) water, most of the day outside, got into the ocean at least 5 times a day (and if needing to pee was an Olympic sport I'd be on the national team ). Last ate just before sunset, then got into the dark for 12 hours ... might seem mad on holiday, but walking about outside under resort lighting wasn't very appealing, and it was less about holiday and more about a chance to heal. I also wanted my charged with sunlight blood doing a proper discharge in darkness (deuterium blog) - and I think that could take longer than 8 hours (Alexander Wunsch comment that regeneration needs more than 8 hours of darkness). Had my 60th birthday when I was out there, deliberately kept it low key, as I'm officially celebrating it at New Year with DH, 3 children and DIL with a 4 day visit to Amsterdam and Utrecht. ### Sue-UK — 2018-01-27T11:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-233612>

The salt thread is interesting. [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ht-world-deuterium-fractions-will-rise.20745/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/why-will-seaweed-be-more-important-in-a-5g-blue-light-world-deuterium-fractions-will-rise.20745/)

After watching the video I bought the book, and a few dots started to connect, which is why I weighed myself before starting a salt hack. This quote on the thread from Jack connected some more dots :

_"The antagonistic pleiotropy (AP) theory posits that aging occurs because alleles that are detrimental in older organisms are beneficial to growth early in life and thus are maintained in populations. Although genes of the insulin signaling pathway likely participate in AP, the insulin‐regulated cellular correlates of AP have not been identified." Answer: young humans before full myelination in brain need deuterium to complete growth but once myelination is complete then mitochondria need to deuterium deplete to control growth and metabolism pathways into adult life. This is the control for the insulin signaling systems._ **_The proxy for the system is salinity measures._** _As deuterium fractions in tissues rise as we age salinity measures lower. This is why those with insulin resistance have high BUN/creat ratios and why they are more apt to have SIADH, HTN. Na brings Vitamin C into the brain for TCA cycle protons recycling to lower the KIE of deuterium in TCA intermediates as we age to control volume changes in the matrix.. This is why SIADH is associated with cognitive decline in TBI and aging. Na also brings calcium into bone in cases of osteopenia. **Low Na = low Ca and Mg = mitochondrial dysfunction.** Low salinity in CSF and the body is a feature of all nnEMF exposure in environments. The higher nnEMF in your environment the lower salinity becomes in blod and CSF and lower salinity = sleep disorders. Most people with high insulin levels have sleep apnea due to deuterium rise. BUN rises as salinity decreases because deuterium fractions are increasing. Salt helps cool our body because it augments skin vasodilation ( like UVA light does). As man made blue light exposure of skin rises, dopamine creation in the eye and brain drop and our salinity in CSF drops and obesity manifests easier due to deuterium content in the choroid of the eye and the skin and subcutaneous fat mass due to melanopsin. The TRP receptors of melanopsin are affected negatively by deuteration by altered salinity. All makes sense Raymund when you understand bio-physics of the KIE of deuterium. Most don't. Most have no clue why PUFA's in fake fats are bad........they increase proton leakage in mito and allows more deuterium in matrix. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5418193/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5418193/)_
I’ve made some ghee (500g unsalted butter) and when I strained it I put in 2 tablespoons of Cornish Sea Salt and Hebridean seaweed sprinkle mix (it’s a 50/50 mix), stirred it through and then restirred again just as it was setting. (Once its run out I’ll use 1 tablespoon each of Redmond sea salt and some seaweed sprinkles). A dollop stirred into cooked green veg is yum ….. I’ve also made up a batch just with 1 tbsp sea salt (salted caramel taste – yum) and I’m going to make a pudding with raw egg yolks, maybe a bit of thick clotted cream, cinnamon … just play with the recipe. I’m also putting some Redmond sea salt on my tongue before drinking my once freeze well water with a slice of lemon in it. (Sodium, vit C, brain hopefully!)

Deuterium notes: I don’t think the breathy test would tell me the heteroplasmy rate of (for example) my liver …. So I’m not testing, or buying commercial DDW. I would be too tempted to use the expensive deuterium depleting option of commercial DDW as an excuse not to maximise what costs me little or nothing ….. I might be tempted to have my deuterium loaded cake and eat it too,  thinking I could offset it with low ppm DDW.
But I think theoretically, and in a low nnemf environment, it could be done without commercial DDW, provided no Warburg shift has already occurred (that would be a whole different ballgame for me). My aim is to work on reducing intake, and work on redox so that I improve the deuterium depleting detox pathways (sweat, bile, urine, breath and sexual fluids etc) naturally. On my list so far is:
Avoiding nnemfs, artificial light, blue blocking etc
Seasonal Epi Paleo diet (reducing/excluding intake of high deuterium foods)
Aim for water lower than 150 ppm
CT (depletes deuterium)
Sunshine
Sleep, particularly REM. Cold, dark and boring, 10 hours min complete darkness

Now added in the salt hack for getting ready for a 4 hour flight and the 10 days I’m in Fuerteventura. Dipping in the sea and then sunbathing always makes me feel great, but I’ve been wondering if one reason it feels so good because of the salt. Does the salt on my skin from the water help me absorb the light, and would having low skin salt storage affect how much light I can absorb in the UK? (Sea salt in the CT tub?)
I thought I was eating enough salt, but after a couple of days of upping it, I bought some Roquefort cheese which is 3.2g of salt per 100g. Normally I would have to hold myself back from eating the 150g pack, but this time a 30g ish piece was plenty. Hmmmm…

### Sue-UK — 2018-02-15T17:46:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-234820>

Fuerteventura was a good break, but unfortunately a lot of rain and strong winds.  Had a good view of the sea, but the sunrise was only visible once because of clouds on the horizon each day. My black bin liner wasn't needed as a blackout curtain, but it did come in useful on the beach. If the heavens opened I just stuffed all our belongings into it to keep dry, and got into the sea, and got out when the clouds passed over and we got a bit of warmth. I became a cloud watcher to see there was black clouds coming, with some blue sky behind it so I could plan my next move, or whether the bad weather was going to go on for long time and we were better off going for shelter. The sea was very rough and I'm a poor swimmer so I did walks along the beach with the sea up to my thighs to exercise, only got caught a few times by a wave that went over my head, or knocked me clean off my feet .... I had to switch between being a cloud watcher and a wave watcher ..... but my awareness increased as the days went past. Never enough heat to sweat, but the temperature contrast between the cold of the sea and getting out when the sun did appear was enough to bring my blood to the surface for a bit of light harvesting ..... and I still got more UV in a couple of days than I've had since I last went in November. I'd printed out [https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/jpa/19/5/19\_5\_207/\_pdf](https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/jpa/19/5/19_5_207/_pdf) _The Turnover of Body Water as an Indicator of Health_ to read on the plane (Along with Elizabeth Blackburn's book The Telomere Effect). I was interested in the concept of the total body water turnover being higher in the young and in the healthy versus unhealthy, couldn't improve mine through sweating, but if CT offers a similar effect via the cold and increased urine, I just decided to go for it as much as possible, whilst getting as much UV as I could. (UV 6 for the clear sky hours).

I took a list of waters and their deuterium content, and Gerolsteiner at -57 was the best I found so I drank that. (I also compared the mineral content of that, Buxton (UK) water bought at the airport on the way out, and a local bottled water DH was drinking while we were there. The sodium content of Gerolsteiner was 118 mg per litre, Buxton 24, and DH's bottled water was 11.) Ate fish every day, took seaweed sprinkles and some Redmond sea salt with me, and tried to keep deuterium loaded foods to a minimum.

Not quite the holiday I expected, but a good recharge all the same.

In the shorter term, rural UK suddenly isn't looking safer after all.......

### Sue-UK — 2018-03-22T13:40:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-237729>

We were a few days later moving because of the weather front dubbed the beast from the east 1.0. The pipes froze, which at least tested our insulation ... Made improvements, we were in there for the beast from the east 2.0, and nothing froze, but it took living in the cold lane to the next level, particularly moving from one mobile home to the other ....The day home is now called the East Wing, the night one is the West Wing ..... The flat was still completely empty and it wasn't safe to try and move stuff in, so didn't have that escape option, but we could have moved back into our old house. Because that won't be an option next winter we decided to just wait it out, as if we were snowed in, and see how we coped. We didn't lose power so we were fine, and after moving and clearing out furniture and washing walls and doing spring cleaning for hours every day I was quite glad of the break.

### taiyang — 2018-03-22T16:43:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-237744>

my father grew up in a house with a spring in the basement supplying drinking water. the house was built brick by brick by my great grandfather. the electric wiring was redone by my grandfather at some point, but now my brother is moving in. both grandparents died at 94 and were never cared for by others (except that they cared for each other) and were never in a nursing home. i'm going to ask my brother what kind of wiring was in there.

anyway, as a child my father was fat. how could that be when he was living in such an ideal environment- one that led to his parents living to 94? their activity levels and sun exposure was the same. he was also barefoot a lot. they were a farm family.

so i think food and maybe toxic exposure through pesticides/herbicides are what threw my father's metabolism out of whack. those factors may have interfered with the great light/water/magnetism environment he was in. i don't think food quality should be prioritized under cold and sun. it's got to be of equal importance in my opinion.

### taiyang — 2018-03-22T18:51:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-blogs-and-a-changing-journey.13824/post-237754>

"I could use tips when facing (sudden) swollen feet and ankles.
Specially in otherwise healthy youngsters in their 90's."

I believe there is more than one reason for swollen feet and ankles.
"As part of the immune system, inflammation has a useful role helping the body to heal after an injury, for example, but it can damage organs and blood vessels if it persists when it is not needed. One of the vagus nerve's jobs is to reset the immune system and switch off production of proteins that fuel inflammation. Low vagal tone means this regulation is less effective and inflammation can become excessive, such as in Maria Vrind's rheumatoid arthritis..."

In the example in the article the vagal nerve stimultion was helpful because the woman's inflammation "persist(ed) when it (was) not needed." 1) so i would think when inflammation/swelling is needed, you don't want to interfere with that process. 2) you have to determine what is causing the inflammation/swelling. maybe it has nothing to do with the immune system, but instead has to do with the circulatory system and the blood not being pumped around the body effectively. i remember once i noticed my fingers got a little swollen after a long day's hike. this was after i had dropped considerably weight over 3 years of living in taiwan. i believe i lost not only fat, but muscle, some of which was probably heart muscle. i don't think i would need a vagal nerve stimulator for that.

**Raising the tone**
Having an electrical device surgically implanted into your neck for the rest of your life is a serious procedure. But the technique has proved so successful - and so appealing to patients - that other researchers are now looking into using vagal nerve stimulation for a range of other chronic debilitating conditions,
including inflammatory bowel disease,

obesity.

that was not helpful in water management

"Hospitals already track
heart-rate variability -
vagal tone

To avoid delays in turnaround time when requesting multiple tests on frozen samples, please submit separate frozen specimens for each test requested.

Freeze.

Temperature

Frozen

9 days

Stable x2

Nonfrozen specimen; nonserum specimen; gross lipemia; gross hemolysis

I think food isn't as important unless it's as important ....

I'm in mid move so don't have the book to hand, but I've read of studies done on animals having pesticide levels so high they were unfit for human consumption, losing body fat when they were detoxed. I think that exposure in the womb can turn on a particular gene or oversensitize its volume control, or interfere with the way the fat burning enzymes work.

I have some biometric markers that point to something turning on a "thrifty" gene in utero, and the environment over the years meant the volume switch seemed to be turned up to max. Circadian rhythm, Redox before detox, 3 legged stool, for a loooooooooooooooooong time food seemed to be of equal importance until suddenly it wasn't ......

### Melanie Procter — 2015-04-14T03:59:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ultrasound.13827/post-161128>

I copied and pasted out of that article a list to add to our growing list. There are probably lots of other nuggets around the blog to add to our list. I believe we would find ideas in the redox or mitochondria blogs. I know Jack talks about the TENS unit in both of these blogs. I wonder if TENS could be added to the list.

Here is what I snipped from the Gerald Pollack link I placed above.

Spring water -- under pressure (deep in the ground) becomes structured.

Glacial melt -- ice turns into Structured Water (EZ water) when melting . . . The phase between liquid and solid is structured water.

Vortexing -- A vortex occurs naturally in nature, as in streams, rivers, waterfalls, etc. The vortex is a kind of mechanical perturbation or agitation. Vortexing is a very powerful way of increasing structure. There are devices on the market which vortex water. One such device is the [Natural Action Structured Water Unit](http://shop.structuredwaterunit.com/).

Juicing -- is water that comes from the plant cells. Structured juice water!

Antioxidents -- Most of the tissues in our body are negative. Our cells are a negative charge; oxidants are a positive charge. Antioxidents maintain the negative charge in our body.

Sunlight -- critical to our health. Light builds Structured Water (EZ water.)
Circulation -- Red blood cells work their way through capillaries; light is the driver of flow. Add light and flow increases. Something other than the heart (pressure) is driving the blood.
\- See more at: [http://www.structuredwaterunit.com/Dr\_Gerald\_Pollack.html#sthash.9cJG6KHR.dpuf](http://www.structuredwaterunit.com/Dr_Gerald_Pollack.html#sthash.9cJG6KHR.dpuf)

### mark444200 — 2015-04-14T08:16:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-western-australia.13836/post-161140>

I've been a lurker for a little while now, chatted to Yew-Wei a bit (even visited him during a road trip around aus!) and watched the facebook group a bit. (Mark Janssen on there).

My health info:
Gut problems (bad stools, acne) as well as dry eyes (almost entirely from computer / screen use! .. perhaps i'm sensitive to EMF's?) and sore joints. In high school I went on roaccutaine for my acne twice for long durations. I also suspect some autoimmunity and or leaky gut problems. I wasn't breast fed and I was a C-section birth.

My implementation of Krusian health has been the following; upping seafood consumption, wearing blue-blockers at night, CTing (bought a cool fat burner but it didn't seem to get me that cold, instead I now run a cold bath and chuck the ice packs in that).

In life right now I've recently graduate from my 5 year architecture degree and have a job interview on next Friday. I'm really hoping to get the job for some $$$. However, I suspect my generally feeling crap and dry eyes will return with the extended computer use.

In terms of mitigating extended computer use and EMF exposure, what could I do besides eat tones of seafood? Are there any rules of thumb; 8 hours of computer use can be mitigated by 2 hours of hardcore CT'ing at a certain temperature before bed? Haha maybe i'm asking too much.

I'm also planning on buying some supplements from the Epi-Paleo book when I get some cash; the ones recommended under leaky gut. These ones caught my eye;
N-acetyl-cysteine (NAC), undenatured whey protein (glutathione), colostrum

### yewwei.tan — 2015-04-15T01:24:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-western-australia.13836/post-161209>

Heh, Epic road trip . Nice of you to stop over in Cairns for a night.

I need to check out if there are any blue blockers out there that only block blue light, and are otherwise clear (let the rest of the visible spectrum in). The ones that I've got are basically red-only goggles, so they block out a fair chunk of the visible spectrum, and I personally found them to be disruptive to sleep if I wore them a lot during the day. Then again, you saw where I live, whereby the sunlight shines pretty brightly, and at quite a high angle (more UV). As winter comes to Perth, blue blockers during any screen time may be helpful in your case. (And also, stop watching crazy-as Japanese horror films , don't need any more of that blue light).

Needless to say, try and cut the blue light exposure at night time. I don't know how the hell it can have such a significant effect, but any time I didn't get off the computer by 8pm during my current weight loss experiment, I didn't lose weight  -- [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/take-it-slow.9428/page-42#post-160762](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/take-it-slow.9428/page-42#post-160762)

Instead I got the red LED headlamp you see in my profile pic, and resorted to either reading paper books / kindle, or listening to audio on my iPod. (nice time to catch up on JK webinars )

Good luck on the job interview, and keep posting on the forums if you have any questions.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-04-15T01:35:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-western-australia.13836/post-161211>

> [Josh (Paleo Osteo) said:](/goto/post?id=161210)
>
> link to LED lamp?

Bought 2 of this cheap china-made headlamp for AU$8 each  -- [http://www.ebay.com.au/itm/281563834786](http://www.ebay.com.au/itm/281563834786)

Has white light, but I can easily cover the light and click 3 times to get to a red-only setting. 3x AAA batteries, so it's easy enough to power.

I did measure the EMF off it, and it showed nothing in the ELF and Radio Frequency range. Still, I don't trust the meter, since anything with an electric current through it is bound to produce some level of EMF. So I generally strap it to one of my tall 1.5L water bottles and use it like a desk lamp.

I didn't bother looking for other battery-powered lamps since this little thing was cheap and good.

### mark444200 — 2015-04-30T04:07:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-western-australia.13836/post-162809>

So i'm on day 5 or so of hitting these leaky gut supplements (recommended in the epi-paleo book); N-acetyl-cysteine (NAC), undenatured whey protein (glutathione), L-glutamine, colostrum, liquorice root, zinc, magnesium, alpha liopic acid. Combined with CT at least once daily (makes my body bounce back mega fast from sport; tennis), and tones of mackerel fishcakes. Also noteworthy; often times my girlfriend makes broth, I have a lot of coconut oil with my fishcakes, and I've got a probiotic farm growing in the house (milk kefir every morning with the above supplements, small amounts of kombucha daily, and sauerkraut daily).

My bowel movements have been real good. I think I shat 3 times yesterday, all with a good Bristol stool.

My L-glutamine will run out soon, I didn't get enough. But i'll continue them all till they run out, and I might continue afterwards with the use of the undenatured whey protein and colostrum long-term.

I also tried bulletproof coffee a week or so ago. I enjoyed the feeling it gave me; a very clear head. I could also easily fast until lunchtime without hunger pangs, but it made my craps too runny. Not a good Bristol stool. Mr. Bristol wouldn't of been happy. So I've ditched that. But I've been skipping breakfast the past few days anyway; and eating an early lunch around 11am which has been working very well. I'm not sure if its just a mental thing or a physiological thing. Perhaps having tones of fish and DHA makes me less hungry, because i'm getting good nutrients. I'm too dumb to understand that at the moment, but the experiential is cool. Onwards!

# Thread: How to definitely prove that nnEMF are bad for your health?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-definitely-prove-that-nnemf-are-bad-for-your-health.13883/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 16

### thomas — 2015-04-15T15:32:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-definitely-prove-that-nnemf-are-bad-for-your-health.13883/post-161276>

To me nnEMF is a big issue. I mean if nnEMF are bad for your health, then it changes everything about how and where you should live. So far I have acted on doubt and precaution principle trying to minimise exposure. But I am not in 100%,.
Is there a way to definitely convince yourself (and others) that EMF basically kills you like cigarettes do. Pieces of information? Books/Publications? Doing some experiments/THings to do? Or maybe going to some places and meeting people?

### Lahelada — 2015-04-15T15:56:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-definitely-prove-that-nnemf-are-bad-for-your-health.13883/post-161280>

Maybe this helps . However we live in a study and counter study culture. Maybe the old adage "if in doubt go without" is applicable.

[http://mobile.nytimes.com/blogs/well/2011/02/22/cellphone-use-tied-to-changes-in-brain-activity/](http://mobile.nytimes.com/blogs/well/2011/02/22/cellphone-use-tied-to-changes-in-brain-activity/)

[http://www.menshealth.com/health/cell-phone-brain-damage](http://www.menshealth.com/health/cell-phone-brain-damage)

Or maybe just think about why the manufacturers tell you not to put the phone to your ear,when carrying itnon a belt clip to keep it from touching the body and that said clip may not contain metal.

### Lahelada — 2015-04-15T15:59:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-definitely-prove-that-nnemf-are-bad-for-your-health.13883/post-161283>

Forgot this one.

[http://www.who.int/mediacentre/factsheets/fs193/en/](http://www.who.int/mediacentre/factsheets/fs193/en/)

### JoeBranca — 2015-04-16T06:48:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-definitely-prove-that-nnemf-are-bad-for-your-health.13883/post-161367>

The message from the blogs are to get the fundamental physics of why EMFs are a big deal, and then look at the solid research out there on documented effects of EMFs, and understand deeply the mechanisms from the physics /quanutm biology perspective (like interactions of waveforms with transition metals, alterations of water chemistry etc). Off the top of my head three biggies of studied effects are calcium efflux. intracellular dehydration, and increased permeability of the BBB. Probably more, but these are clinchers for me.

### yewwei.tan — 2015-04-16T08:04:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-definitely-prove-that-nnemf-are-bad-for-your-health.13883/post-161372>

Nora Volkow has done direct research on Cell Phones effect on Brain Metabolism, where nn-EMF increases glucose consumption drastically (to slow circadian clocks) and is a hallmark of a Warburg Metabolism -- [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3184892/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3184892/)

Good books are:

\- 'Going Somewhere' by Andrew Marino -- [http://www.goingsomewherebook.com/health.html](http://www.goingsomewherebook.com/health.html)
\- 'The Body Electric' by Robert O. Becker and Gary Seldon -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The\_Body\_Electric\_(book)](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Body_Electric_\(book\))
\- 'Cross Currents' by Robert O. Becker -- [http://www.amazon.com/Cross-Currents-Robert-O-Becker/dp/0874776090](http://www.amazon.com/Cross-Currents-Robert-O-Becker/dp/0874776090)

Also, smoking cigarettes ain't gonna kill you if you live in a nn-EMF-free environment . Unfortunately, that sort of environment is impossible in today's world, unless maybe you're living in the middle of the Congo Rainforest far from nn-EMF emitters, constantly grounded, and with a thick canopy of leaves (and water) blocking out any nn-EMF (and even then, I doubt the levels will ever be back to the natural baseline before we started shooting nn-EMF into the atmosphere).

### Hope2Learn — 2015-04-16T16:48:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-definitely-prove-that-nnemf-are-bad-for-your-health.13883/post-161431>

Reading the pamphlet from my daughter's cellphone it doesn't mention emf, only RF. it states "WHO findings released May 17, 2010 shows no increased health risk due to radio frequency (RF) energy, a form of electromagnetic radiation that is emitted by cell phones." then goes on to say that "there is no scientific evidence that proves that wireless phone usage can lead to cancer or other problems, including headaches, dizziness, or memory loss" and then it gives a link to the FCC article that quote is from. Furthermore, they state that the NCI has concluded that potential health concerns related to using cell phones "and specifically the suggestion that using a cell phone may increase a person's risk of developing brain cancer - are not supported y a growing body of research on the subject."

I have to give KUDOS to apple corporation for being far more honest than LG/Verizon.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-16T17:45:18+0300

### cmegan — 2015-04-15T18:08:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-6-kelp-tablets-per-day-ok.13888/post-161296>

Hi! I would like to increase my kelp tablets supplementation from 3 to 6 tablets per day and I wanted to double check that this is safe. They are Nature's Way brand with each tablet having 360 mcg. of Iodine, so 6 tablets would give me 2160 mcg. (2.16 mg.) of iodine per day in a natural form. I'll be traveling to a place for the next few weeks where I will not have that much access to seafood.

Sorry to post in the beginner's thread but I couldn't find the iodine thread, I'm assuming it was discontinued.
Thank you!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-15T19:27:58+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-15T23:13:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-6-kelp-tablets-per-day-ok.13888/post-161335>

# Thread: I'm starting to get it but my brain still hurts sometimes.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeanne — 2015-04-17T15:52:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/post-161515>

I have been a silent observer for a few months on here. I heard Jack on a podcast and knew I had to find out more! I read his book and was intrigued! The diet part is not an issue for me--I have been low carb for years and LOVE seafood--especially oysters! Honestly, when I read the part about CT I just thought to myself "there's no damn way I'm doing that. I HATE cold." Well, guess what? I've been doing CT. I started with just walking my dogs in the extreme cold we experienced this winter. Then I graduated to the face baths...now I'm sitting in the tub in cold water. I have only been able to be in for 20 mins max so far. My biggest problem is recent menopause. It just sucks, actually. I am on bio-identical hormone replacement and feel good but the weight gain has not be conquered ... yet. I'm hoping CT will help shed the extra menopause weight. One of my biggest challenges with CT has been just sitting in the tub! I get SO bored. Does anyone else have this issue? Thanks to Dr. Kruse and all the folks who post on here! I feel like a whole new realm has opened in my brain with regard to health, longevity and quality of life.

### nicld — 2015-04-17T16:23:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/post-161520>

Hi Jeanne and welcome.

A great way to pass the time while doing CT is to put podcasts on an MP3 player and listen away.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-17T17:05:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/post-161524>

New podcast out today at selfhacked with Joe Cohen [http://selfhacked.com/2015/04/12/in...use-emfs-biohacking-water-dha-and-blue-light/](http://selfhacked.com/2015/04/12/interview-with-dr-jack-kruse-emfs-biohacking-water-dha-and-blue-light/)

### fitness@home — 2015-04-17T17:37:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/post-161526>

Jeanne,

Firstly, yes menopause sucks . HAve been fighting to get back into a size 6-8 for the past few years.

I turn on some music, light a candle and do some deep breathing for relaxation. If I am really antsy and can't focus on the relaxation aspect I will read or surf on tablet (with my blue blockers on).

My cat has got into the habit of following me. He stretches out on the rug and waits for attention...

BTW, welcome!

### Jeanne — 2015-04-17T18:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/post-161532>

Thanks fitness@home and nicid. And, of course, Dr. Kruse! I appreciate the suggestions and will try that! That's funny about your cat--my dog does the same thing. I just read a great book: "Sex, lies & Menopause." It fits very well with all the information on here. I am recommending it to my daughters and girlfriends. Has anyone else read it?

### shah78 — 2015-04-17T23:14:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/post-161567>

When I was a 20-30 minute CTer I sat in the tub and used it as a "meditative tool". Oh the good old days..... Now I bring the kitchen sink into the hour-ninety minute "production". Sort of like the story of mankind.  Now I have the computer, the uvex glasses, the big ass breakfast and the land line phone nearby. Hilarious. But ,boy can I stay in that tub now. .....Try it all kinds of ways. What a trip it is!

### nicld — 2015-04-18T00:11:16+0300

Once you do that, you contain the light that illuminates your uniqueness to share with the world. CT becomes easy then too because you build up you DC current to repel the cold.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/post-161657>

Welcome, Jeanne! Sounds like you are doing great. Boredom can be an issue, with indoor CT. I used to read. Later on I would kinda zone-out, to the point of falling asleep! Having the company of a pet sounds like a good solution.

### shah78 — 2015-04-18T19:58:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/post-161660>

>
> shah78, Thanks for the suggestions! I can't even imagine staying in the cold tub for an hour or more! But then, two weeks ago I couldn't imagine sitting in a cold tub of water at all!! Nicld, that's a GREAT suggestion! I love Vikings.

Please start to imagine doing it. Eat 100% of your protein as seafood for a few weeks/months. I rarely if ever went over forty minutes over the first two and a half years. Then last October I ramped up seafood from twenty five percent to over ninety percent and I effortlessly started sitting for over an hour sometimes ninety minutes and even two hours. The transition took all of four days.! YMMV.

### Jeanne — 2015-04-18T20:11:41+0300

### JMO — 2015-04-18T20:50:42+0300

Thanks Jeanne! I'm always looking for ways to help DD with confidence in her sport and that looks very interesting. Look at you already stepping in to share your knowledge...glad you joined the forum.

### prAna303 — 2015-04-18T20:53:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/post-161666>

> [Jeanne said:](/goto/post?id=161515)
>
> One of my biggest challenges with CT has been just sitting in the tub! I get SO bored. Does anyone else have this issue?

This is how i would do it...

If you do it at home, it is a simulation of the real deal, this makes it boring for sure. I would listen to music but not on headphones, you should use a good hifi system. Always get sunlight, clouds should not make you skip it. I never do the concept of CT but i live it.

I do it in nature with natures own light, this makes it so vivid, you get to listen to birds, the wind, snow falling and you get into a timeless state quick. I do freedive, this makes it like the CT idea is but you get water, seawater filled with all the good stuff. You get a good squeeze on your whole body, i do it without any wetsuits.

When you do things that should be natural in a manmade world they become boring, that world demands action 24/7 so you do not decay, nature is your origin your memories are built upon these factors.

### kovita — 2015-04-20T21:30:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-starting-to-get-it-but-my-brain-still-hurts-sometimes.13942/post-161830>

> [Jeanne said:](/goto/post?id=161515)
>
> I have been a silent observer for a few months on here. I heard Jack on a podcast and knew I had to find out more! I read his book and was intrigued! The diet part is not an issue for me--I have been low carb for years and LOVE seafood--especially oysters! Honestly, when I read the part about CT I just thought to myself "there's no damn way I'm doing that. I HATE cold." Well, guess what? I've been doing CT. I started with just walking my dogs in the extreme cold we experienced this winter. Then I graduated to the face baths...now I'm sitting in the tub in cold water. I have only been able to be in for 20 mins max so far. My biggest problem is recent menopause. It just sucks, actually. I am on bio-identical hormone replacement and feel good but the weight gain has not be conquered ... yet. I'm hoping CT will help shed the extra menopause weight. One of my biggest challenges with CT has been just sitting in the tub! I get SO bored. Does anyone else have this issue? Thanks to Dr. Kruse and all the folks who post on here! I feel like a whole new realm has opened in my brain with regard to health, longevity and quality of life.

try to do CT outdoors if the place you live allows. I do the CT using natural water temperature in my area, most of the days trying to do it in the lake/river/pond...whatever is around. It is much more enjoyable and find it as well much more refreshing and energizing. I rarely measure time or temperature anymore. I hardly do any CT in the summer, as you can guess, the water temperature in central Europe is almost 20 degrees celsius by that time. For me this approach worked just right since i started 1,5 years ago. I sort of feel like I should take the nature's lead in this sense and my kids (aged 5 and 3) started to follow me voluntarily the last autumn, so it proves it is "easy to do" this way. I am no CT hero ;-) I managed 20 minutes in the ice hole this winter (4 degres celsius) without much pain, but that is just about all ;-) I think the life as you change it to get healthier and feel better should be enjoyable and for me following the nature's rhytm has been so far enjoyable beyond expectations ;-)

# Thread: Expect great things to happen for you today here
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/expect-great-things-to-happen-for-you-today-here.13943/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-17T16:08:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/expect-great-things-to-happen-for-you-today-here.13943/post-161517>

Make sure you pay attention to all signals you get today........because some of the signals you think are not from him just might be. Be open and discerning using your intuition and realize that your intuition grows as you strengthen your mind. The way to strengthen your mind is giving it things it is made up of. DHA and seafood. You have gotten that message a long time. Maybe this is the day that message hits home. Every time you look at your phone with its blue light it destroys your DHA in your brain. This limits your ability to receive messages from anyone and not just your intuition. Think about that reality every time you use technology from here on out. And you know, the collective intelligence of nature is working through each and everyone of us everyday whether we perceive it or not...........the question is.......messages can be given but are the being received? A receiver needs a good detector in the head............without out it what good is a message? Ponder that reality. Somebody is directing me to direct your attention and focus to this message. We will see if you listen. As a newbie you need to think this way when you begin.

People, in this community are transmitters of a bigger message........we are supposed to broadcast things to other detectors........I call them starfish.......I have done my job for today right here with this one post, have you done yours? Do you hear the message this site wants to propagate to the world?

It is hard to see what you don't know is even there-- The eye cannot see what the mind does not know. You would think a person of deep intuition would implicitly understand this, and apply it to "other aspects" of their being. Choices leading to wellness that allow you to continue to be a broadcaster of a good signal is radically important to this life and to the many people who will come and go from here. What's interesting to me today in this thread today, is that our connectivity to the virtual world now outweighs the connection with our natural world. I want you to think about these thoughts today. My intuition is telling me to broadcast it here on this thread for other detectors to receive. I am compelled to say it today. I am not sure why, but I sense it is important. I fell if I don't say it and remain quiet, because it might disturb someone conformity, it might force you realize that hope without direction is just a half truth. Hope is not a plan. A half truth always becomes a total lie. Hope requires actions. These ideas should make one realize that a disconnection with nature its going to force other broadcasters of any healthy message into a no win situation if we don't reconnect with the natural world.

-   People don't care how much you know until they know how much you care...........somebody must care to tell you this...........intuition told me to share this idea with you.

-   Question of the day now is..............who was the message for and will they listen? Is your detector plugged into what it should be plugged into?

-   Inquiring minds will question and ponder this...........if they are to become a starfish.

### fitness@home — 2015-04-17T17:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/expect-great-things-to-happen-for-you-today-here.13943/post-161527>

> What's interesting to me today in this thread today, is that our connectivity to the virtual world now outweighs the connection with our natural world.

Sad but true that much of our contact with others is in the virtual world vs natural world. Will make it a point today to reach out and touch someone's life in person.

On the other hand, I wouldn't have met all these fine people and learned so much here without that same virtual world.

Great post...off to ponder it now

You would think a person of deep intuition would implicitly understand this, and apply it to "other aspects" of their being. Choices leading to wellness that allow you to continue to be a broadcaster of a good signal is radically important to this life and to the many people who will come and go from here. What's interesting to me today in this thread today, is that our connectivity to the virtual world now outweighs the connection with our natural world. I want you to think about these thoughts today. My intuition is telling me to broadcast it here on this thread for other detectors to receive. I am compelled to say it today. I am not sure why, but I sense it is important. I fell if I don't say it and remain quiet, because it might disturb someone conformity, it might force you realize that hope without direction is just a half truth. Hope is not a plan. A half truth always becomes a total lie. Hope requires actions. These ideas should make one realize that a disconnection with nature its going to force other broadcasters of any healthy message into a no win situation if we don't reconnect with the natural world.
>
>
> -   People don't care how much you know until they know how much you care...........somebody must care to tell you this...........intuition told me to share this idea with you.
>
>
> -   Question of the day now is..............who was the message for and will they listen? Is your detector plugged into what it should be plugged into?
>
>
> -   Inquiring minds will question and ponder this...........if they are to become a starfish.

Always apprecite these thoughts. Thank you Jack

### caroline — 2015-04-18T01:51:42+0300

This is so unreal ..... I got your message this morning ....before I got your message! Before I read this!

Nicole asked me a simple question about how I was this am. I decided it was time to talk about the conference we are organizing to bring you down under.

We have spent months doing due diligence and trying to wrestle all the details into submission. We are far from home free ..... But at least we have our FB page up and running - many thanks to Roy Palmer for mentoring us and spending so much time giving us direction .....we are, indeed, a bunch of newbies .....but we are willing and able and driven to do this!

All the detail is mind bending to say the least .... But we are driven to bring you here and pay forward what you have given us the last four years.......

Our FB page .....GOHWell foundation
Global Optimal Health and wellness

This train is leaving the station ..... Slow but steady and for absolute sure!

We need tons of help and support and ideas and imput..... But we will get this done!

This is all quite remarkable for a bunch of newbies to achieve ..... We are alive and well - and broadcasting ....hear us roar!

# Thread: Just wanted to say hi from Holland
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wanted-to-say-hi-from-holland.13966/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Peertje80 — 2015-04-19T14:33:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wanted-to-say-hi-from-holland.13966/post-161700>

If been lurking for quit sometime here. Just wanted to say hi! Searching for better (optimal always seemed so far away) for a long time.

I suffer from hidradenitis suppurativa (HS) since puberty. Lucky for me I'm a mild case. But I always figured this couldn't be right.

When I entered my twenties I began to suffer from muscle cramps... sometimes during the day, but usually at night. Especially at that moment when you fall a sleep. I had cramps in my calf's, hands, respiratory muscles and jaw. But mostly calf's and hands.

Later on they figured out I even had spasm during my deep sleep cycles... but the doctors didn't know what it was. I was exhausted, so they tried something. I was on an anti epileptic drug for one week and I was done trying. It totally drove me insane. This was not what I wanted for my future. So my journey started in 2006. Though I didn't have any direction to go.

The best thing I accidental did for my health was moving out of the city to a coastal village (I lived some 100 metres from the sea). Being at the beach, walking barefoot in sand and water and learn how to kite surf. Because when I did, my muscle cramps disappeared (like snow for the sun as we say in Dutch). I never had it since and that puzzles me a bit. Wondering when hell will return.

When I hit my thirties I began to suffer from some kind of itchy eczema on my hands. Saw my MP about it, but all he got to show for was steroid cream. And an answer to my 'stupid' question what in the hell could cause this, he replied annoyed: "We don't really know what causes this". This answer stroke me with fear. How could doctors not know what causes something so common and simple like eczema? What if I have a real serious medical issue? Are they just going to try something again? This was an issue for me since ever since I was a kid I feared to die before the age of forty.

I started an elimination protocol for food and environment by myself. First night shades (yes I quieted smoking over night after 15 years, no sweat). Nope wasn't that. Then grains, yes I got something there. I even had a very strong reaction to my hair conditioner, which had wheat glutamine in it. I tested positive for anti-iga. So I went paleo with a good amount of fermented vegetables and some dairy. I'm Dutch, so I eat dutch cheese. There is no way I'm not. After 4 years of scratching my skin to death my eczema disappeared in about 1 year. My skin is still recovering however.

Really funny thing is (well actually scary), when I convinced my mother to get tested, she got diagnosed with Coeliac disease. Finally after 57 years getting in and out of hospitals and doctors blaming her to make up her symptoms, she's got her diagnoses. And just in time. 1 month after her coeliac diagnose, her specialist diagnosed her with hepatocellulair carcinoom due to non alcoholic fatty liver. He finds 2 stage 1 tumors. They have been removed, my mother is now 6 months cancer free. I'm hoping she sticks to her DHA loaded gluten free diet and tumeric supplements, so she might heal her liver some more.

Well I didn't intend to write such a long story. Sorry. I will just stick around to optimize my life further and who knows, maybe one day, I will even be HS free

Thank you all. I really like reading here.

### nonchalant — 2015-04-19T22:34:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wanted-to-say-hi-from-holland.13966/post-161740>

Welcome Peertje! Sounds like you have a good attitude about health. Nice that you have found some of your own answers already. The coastal village environment sounds ideal. Your muscle cramps probably won't return, if you keep visiting the ocean.

One of the first places that circadian rhythm problems show is in the skin (and gut lining). So altered light (wrong color, wrong time of day, wrong intensity) often manifests in skin issues of some sort. We have been recently learning that nitrogen cycles depend on sunlight -- I think that is what Dr. Kruse was referring to.

I used to sail -- kite surfing sounds awesome!

### Peertje80 — 2015-04-19T23:19:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-wanted-to-say-hi-from-holland.13966/post-161742>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=161740)
>
> Welcome Peertje! Sounds like you have a good attitude about health. Nice that you have found some of your own answers already. The coastal village environment sounds ideal. Your muscle cramps probably won't return, if you keep visiting the ocean.
>
> One of the first places that circadian rhythm problems show is in the skin (and gut lining). So altered light (wrong color, wrong time of day, wrong intensity) often manifests in skin issues of some sort. We have been recently learning that nitrogen cycles depend on sunlight -- I think that is what Dr. Kruse was referring to.
>
> I used to sail -- kite surfing sounds awesome!

Thank you nonchalant. Being on the water sailing is awesome too. Didn't know that about skin problems. Yeah I think I get the light thing. I'm a natural red head. So I was raised to avoid sun for ever and always. My mother was always running after me with sun screen. Swimming during summer was done fully clothed. I spend most of my time indoors with books or behind the tv, pc/console. I'm a child of the nintendo blister generation. I lived most of my live like a vampire avoiding sun as if my live depended up on it. Wearing sunglasses everywhere. So I know I have to work on the light. On work days I sometimes get as much as 12 hours of screen time a day too. I don't get the nitrogen cycle yet. English is a second language for me so I have re read a lot.

The sea is always calling. This winter I couldn't surf because of personal circumstances. But it left me cranky and sick. Its call is too strong to ignore.

# Thread: PaulG Weightloss Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paulg-weightloss-journal.13983/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2015-04-20T10:08:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paulg-weightloss-journal.13983/post-161779>

Hi

Thought I would start a Journal...

**Quick background**

-   Long time (6+ years) Jack Kruse advocate/starfish
-   46 years old, Male
-   Big guy - 6 foot 4 inches (BMI 25-29%)

**2010**

-   **Bad =** Overweight, Adrenal Fatigue, High Estrogens, Homocystein, Low Vit-D, Melatonin, DHEA, Adrenal Fatigue, Testosterone, low tpo antibodies, fatigue etc. Probably Leptin Resistant, High BUN/Creatine ratio, High LDL
-   **Good =** Low HS CRP, Blood sugars, High HDL, got down to 95kg/209IBs following Paleo/Low carb/Keto + regular exercise/gym/muscle building

**2014/2015**

-   **Bad =** Poor body composition, overweight, high blood pressure
-   **Good =** Normal Estrogen (Blood at least), Normal Homocystein, Predicted High VitD (Let you know in a week!)
-   **Some Setbacks** \= Hernia, Calf muscle tear (taken 12 months to repair)

**April this year**

-   I got on the scales I was 114kg/251IBs the heaviest weight I have ever been with the devil on my shoulder whispering "Jack Kruse is full of shit"

The more rational side of me has analysed how I have failed Jack Kruse principles rather than the other way round....

-   Too many Calories - eating bigger portions, my own and left overs from kids plates, snacking, eating cereals, toast
-   Little to no exercise - have had a horizontal existence for way too long, sitting, not moving apart from occasional walk/swim
-   Too much EMF - Lived with a smart meter this past 18 months plus close by neighbors one's,
-   Too much time spent on computer, tablet etc (e.g > 3hrs per day).
-   Indulging in regular bad habits such as hoping onto to internet first thing in the morning, checking email 20+ times a day, spending way too much time on JK forum, superficially reading the blogs and feelings of inadequacy in not getting/remembering the big picture and connecting the dots.
-   Blue light at night - I do wear blue blockers but watch occasional films and web surfing at night
-   Good old fashioned laziness
-   Eating too late (6-7pm)
-   Too many carbs - having been low carb for 3 yrs have increased bad carbs - gluten-free bread, pasta etc
-   Coffee and sugar - Coffee makes me hyper and affects my sleep if I drink it after 11pm
-   Poor sleep
-   Stress/Worry - Been long-term unemployed and job application rejection, feeling guilt about not providing for my family etc.
-   No CT - Living in Australia JK recommends CT on a regular basis but I have dabbled rather than made it a regular habit (lack of commitment)
-   Low-Medium level DHA - I try to eat seafood regularly but because kids are not into it probably have it only once or twice a week.
-   Too much fat not enough vegetables - Overindulgence of Coconut Oil/Butter/Bacon not enough raw vegetables
-   Procrastination, disappointments, obsessions and other male maladies
-   No Intermittent Fasting
-   Alcohol - I have poor tolerance and know I should quit
-   Not enough grounding - Need to ground my computer and bed.

**Action Plan**
Well, that's the bad news, the good news is that since adopting some changes my current weight is 108kg/238Ib which is a loss of 13Ibs in 2 weeks (Not bad..!). So what have I done?

-   Most morning got up at 7am and walked/hiked for 1-3hrs.
-   CT 3 times per week at 10 degrees Centrigrade/ 50 degrees Fahrenheit
-   Cut back on calories by adopting 1-2 eating pattern. DHA Laden lunch only Sun/Mon/Wed/Fri and breakfast plus Dinner before 6pm (Tues,Thur,Sat)
-   Kept carbs to less than 50g most days
-   Significantly reduced computer time
-   More positive mindset
-   Set goals e.g. get back to 95kg/209Ibs by Aug 2015
-   Tracked results (see attached xls)

The idea behind the tracker is to use it as a daily tool in order to help establish long term and effective habits. Please feel free to take a copy and use. The idea is that to map out a 'Perfect Day' and to honestly assess compliance in order to build up regular and repeatably habits. I know there are apps to do this but I don't want to be using a phone first thing in the morning or last thing at night so old school print out works for me. I think quite often we focus too much on the minutia and lose focus on the fundamentals. When Sarah @[SeaHorse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/562/) a few months ago said she was taking a break from the forums to focus on getting optimal I really resonated with that I thought I should do the same.. I have been taking 3hr barefoot hikes through ancient forest here and it really has been amazing. Thoroughly recommend all you closet technology addicts open your ears

Would appreciate any advice/thoughts people have but feeling good at the moment and love this community.

Quick update...

I have been supplementing with Nicotine Gum past two weeks and avoiding some FODMAPs such as onion and garlic (Wife is on Fodmap diet). Side effect of the gum has been hiccups and general spaced out feeling.

Pains in my wrist have increased during this period

Nicotine is a [vasoconstricter](http://www.upcscavenger.com/wiki/vasoconstrictor/#page=wiki) as is caffeine and I have been drinking a lot of coffee and eating chocolate and not eating many nitrate-rich vegetables that help Nitric Oxide production such as Spinach, beets, celery, aragula lettuce, iceberg lettuce, carrots, parsley, cabbage, radishes, collard greens. It is also winter here and not getting so much Sun.

See [20 ways to improve Nitric Oxide naturally](http://anabolicmen.com/boost-nitric-oxide-naturally/)

As to the weightloss...I got down to 107KG two weeks ago but it has jumped back up to 110KG - poor sleep, alcohol, poor motivation and inability to stick to my goals the culprits.

### PaulG — 2016-12-18T08:58:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paulg-weightloss-journal.13983/post-205430>

I just completed a 14 day water-fast, was getting some bad restless leg symptoms till I drank some seawater and supplemented with magnesium, potassium, celtic sea-salt and K+. Lost 10kg/22IBS though and feeling good now. Ketones went up to 6mmol/L. Re-feeding with blended orange and carrot plus watermelon for next two days. Think it has helped the tinnitus too. In terms of hunger after day 4 I had no hunger signals but obviously missed food but it was very manageable.

**Lessons Learnt / Recommendations**

-   Research thoroughly how to do fasts properly and safely. eg [here](https://thequantifiedbody.net/10-day-water-fast-results/) and [here](http://www.fitnessthroughfasting.com/breaking-a-fast.html)
-   Before starting your fast make sure you have been eating a 'clean' diet for two weeks before in order to reduce detox reactions.
-   Do smaller fasts (3 days) before attempting longer-periods

-   Prepare yourself mentally for the first 3-4 days which are the worst
-   During the fast ensure you supplement with minerals and balance your electrolytes
-   Get sun and grounding daily. Ideally be outside as much as possible
-   Consider doing cold shower before bed as this will offset the higher body temperature that is typical at night
-   Track progress - weight, blood pressure, ketones
-   Make sure you go for walks especially late pm/evening to help sleep
-   Avoid technology/blue light at night
-   Drink water when thirsty - but don't over do it. I was getting up 2 times per night because of drinking too much, too late in the day.
-   Ideally don't drive and minimise anything that requires focus unless you are over detox and running on ketones. For me I was in and out of detox reactions for at least 8 days.
-   Use the fast as a platform for changes, if you are fasting for weightloss then going straight back to old habits of eating doesn't make sense. Personally, I am going to give alternate day fasting a go.

restarting food slowly

Body can burn sugar or fat but not both at the same time.
sequential use, sugar first, insulin controls, only low insulin allows fat use
there is always enough sugar to last for at least 2 days

Quick background

Long time (6+ years) Jack Kruse advocate/starfish
46 years old, Male
Big guy - 6 foot 4 inches (BMI 25-29%)

2010

Bad = Overweight, Adrenal Fatigue, High Estrogens, Homocystein, Low Vit-D, Melatonin, DHEA, Adrenal Fatigue, Testosterone, low tpo antibodies, fatigue etc. Probably Leptin Resistant, High BUN/Creatine ratio, High LDL
Good = Low HS CRP, Blood sugars, High HDL, got down to 95kg/209IBs following Paleo/Low carb/Keto + regular exercise/gym/muscle building

2014/2015

Bad = Poor body composition, overweight, high blood pressure
Good = Normal Estrogen (Blood at least), Normal Homocystein, Predicted High VitD (Let you know in a week!)
Some Setbacks = Hernia, Calf muscle tear (taken 12 months to repair)

April this year

I got on the scales I was 114kg/251IBs the heaviest weight I have ever been with the devil on my shoulder whispering "Jack Kruse is full of shit"

\[/quote\]
\=================================================================
\[qu ote="PaulG, post: 205430, member: 1838"\]I just completed a 14 day water-fast, was getting some bad restless leg symptoms till I drank some seawater and supplemented with magnesium, potassium, celtic sea-salt and K+.
Lost 10kg/22IBS though and feeling good now. Ketones went up to 6mmol/L. Re-feeding with blended orange and carrot plus watermelon for next two days. Think it has helped the tinnitus too. In terms of hunger after day 4 I had no hunger signals but obviously missed food but it was very manageable.
\[/quote\]

April this year 114kg/251IBs
now Dec 20/2016 Lost 10kg/22IBS
current weight 114-10=104kg or 251-22=229lbs
height=6 foot 4 inches
per table above, acceptable weight is (160-200) lbs
average 180#
229-180=49lbs to go

during your 14 day fasting you lost 22 lbs
estimating, you really have been loosing weight for about 10 days, the first days you were getting rid of sugar stored in liver.
so you can loose about 2.2lbs/day
to loose the remaining 49lbs you need another (about) 23 days of fasting.
Or as long as it takes,
look at the weight scale.

49/2.2=23 days
redo the calculations if you are overweight by different amounts
and/or if you want another goal for the final weight.

Do not exercise during fasting.
Watch your blood pressure, specially if you are taking any bp medications.
Eventually you should wean out of any bp meds.
.

///

**EDIT:**
**To the 23 days of fasting add the 4 days up front for getting rid of sugar in the liver.**
**so (4+23)=27**

/////////

Overall, after next fatty acids analysis, look at your LA & ALA results, when adjusting your dose.

So, how to figure out how effective your glasses/blue blockers are for cutting out blue light..use this chart

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

From [Paleohacks](https://www.paleohacks.com/sleep/will-plain-red-or-orange-lenses-block-blue-light-for-sleep-17104)

If you are not colour blind and your monitor has a normal colour range, the two bars should appear identical for any effective blue light filter. In theory you also could print out the image to test ambient filtered light sources (like light fittings or sodium lamps).

Quality of filters will vary from something no better than cellophane, up to EN207 laser protection visors (i.e. Argon/Krypton lasers in the case of "blue blocking" glasses). Considering the disability-adjusted life years of inadequate sleep; don't buy glasses lacking a spectrum graph/histogram data sheet. Unless you prefer wearing placebo glasses.

Most blue-light filters will halve the amount of light reaching the eye; for this reason strong care should be taken if driving at night with tinted glasses in a jurisdiction where this is otherwise legal. The same [photosensitive ganglion cells](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photosensitive_ganglion_cell), whose blue-light origin in sea dwelling animals controls our circadian rhythm, also controls our pupil dilation - hence the impression of brightness while wearing filtered glasses. Pupil dilation might not offset the overall light reduction in low light conditions.

Filtered glasses and light sources have proven to be far superior to any software solution I've experienced (f.lux for example); possibly due to the inflexibility of the operating system colour-space or the [impure light spectrum](http://powerelectronics.com/lighting/led-backlighting-lcds-requires-unique-drivers) of liquid crystal display.

Incidentally, if your PaleoHacking also includes an experiment with [biphasic sleep](http://www.livescience.com/12891-natural-sleep.html), filtered glasses can be useful to avoid melatonin disruption during the sleep interregnum.

### Da-mo — 2017-07-22T22:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/post-219772>

For the TV - I run mine through a computer/HTPC with f.lux on it. Then I throw something over the HTPC itself since it has blue leds.

I have some original Blue Blockers and they work good but I need safety glasses and bifocals as well at work where the lighting is blue hell.

So I went on ebay and ordered a few things . . .

Bifocal blue blocking z87 safety rated glasses [http://www.ebay.com/itm/Bifocal-Sun...hash=item27fe880f58:m:m7Vhnn6cl0EqMoFpnBh0UsA](http://www.ebay.com/itm/Bifocal-Sunglasses-z87-Safety-Bi-focal-Reader-HD-Sports-Golf-Blue-Blocker-/171774054232?var=470722864848&hash=item27fe880f58:m:m7Vhnn6cl0EqMoFpnBh0UsA)

(3-Pack) Uvex Skyper Safety Glasses, Black Frame - Orange AntiFog Lens #S1933X\_ (3-Pack) [http://www.ebay.com/itm/3-Pack-Uvex...499852?hash=item3600ca6bcc:g:dcwAAOSwWTRW1MQE](http://www.ebay.com/itm/3-Pack-Uvex-Skyper-Safety-Glasses-Black-Frame-Orange-AntiFog-Lens-S1933X-3-/231941499852?hash=item3600ca6bcc:g:dcwAAOSwWTRW1MQE)

I dont like wearing glasses where I can see the frames - they make my eyes feel like Im peering down a tunnel and cause a bit of eye muscle strain. These should be pretty good in that regard as well as having a bit of wrap=-around for peripheral protection

Looking forward to getting them and testing them. Apparently the Uvex block 98%. I'm hoping the bifocals will perform ok and if so I am going to try to get my employer to start supplying them.

### PaulG — 2017-07-23T05:05:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/post-219795>

Read this excellent summary by Jason Lauritzen [https://medium.com/@jasonlauritzen/...e-your-own-blue-blocking-glasses-1a3d2694ad60](https://medium.com/@jasonlauritzen/blue-light-and-health-plus-how-to-make-your-own-blue-blocking-glasses-1a3d2694ad60)

Other options include
1\. Contact BPI directly. They'll tint lenses for you for $45-$60 per pair excl postage. You need CR-39 lenses - so ensure you ask for those w/out any coatings. You must send in the lenses out of the frames.
2\. Try buying from [https://www.readingglassesetc.com/](https://www.readingglassesetc.com/)
3\. DIY BPI Tinting (See Jason's article for instructions)

### Da-mo — 2017-08-04T11:20:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/post-220888>

So the bifocal safety glasses weren't as good as I was hoping. They block a little but not as much as f.lux at 1700K color temperature. Still waiting for the Uvex to arrive, hopefully I can fit some prescrition glasses underneath them when needed.

Interesting comparing lights around the place here at work. The control room is a blue hell - all flourescents. But out on plant the whole turbine hall is lit predominantly by sodium vapor lights which are actually surprisingly good.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Here's a good page on these lights - old tech but still very efficient - specified for use in places where light pollution needs to be kept to a minimum like around observatories. Interesting reading on them here [http://www.lamptech.co.uk/Documents/SO Introduction.htm](http://www.lamptech.co.uk/Documents/SO%20Introduction.htm)

> **_Introduction_**
> Since its commercial introduction in 1932, the Low Pressure Sodium lamp has consistently maintained its enviable position as the most efficient light source available. In recent years, increasing competition from other discharge lamps is resulting in a slow decline in its market, but it continues to be specified for new installations, particularly in Belgium, the Netherlands, and other global regions in the vicinity of important astronomical observation sites in view of the greatly reduced light pollution it causes. The construction of a typical LPS lamp is illustrated in Figure S1.
>
> _Figure S1 - A Typical SOX Lamp_
> _**Lamp Construction**_
> For reasons of optimal efficacy the sodium vapour is kept at low pressure, which calls for a discharge tube of large dimensions having a relatively low operating temperature. This permits the use of ordinary glass types such as soda-lime silicate or borosilicate, however a protective layer of special borate glass is blown onto the inner surface of the glass tube so as to reduce the rate of attack by the chemically corrosive sodium vapour. To reduce the length of the long discharge tube it is customary to fold it into a U-shape, although linear designs also exist. The discharge tube is dosed with metallic sodium and also contains a rare gas filling, usually neon-based, which facilitates starting. The electric current is supplied via thermionic electrodes at either end, which are similar in construction to those of the low pressure mercury fluorescent lamp, but of somewhat heavier construction on account of the higher lamp currents. The discharge tube requires thermal insulation to ensure a high lamp efficacy, and this is provided by mounting it inside a secondary outer bulb. It is evacuated to minimise thermal conduction and convection losses, and in the more modern lamps is coated on its inner surface with an infra-red reflective film to minimise radiated heat loss. The outer bulb is equipped with either a bayonet or pin-type cap to ensure the correct alignment of the discharge tube in the optical system of the luminaire.
> _**Basic Characteristics**_
> The reason for the remarkably high efficacy of the low pressure sodium discharge is not so much because its discharge converts electrical energy into visible light particularly efficiently, moreover it is due to the fact that the wavelength of light it radiates happens to be very close to the peak sensitivity of the human eye under normal viewing conditions. Figure S2 shows the energy balance of a typical low pressure sodium lamp, which reveals that in fact only about 30% of the input power is converted into visible light. This percentage is comparable with other modern discharge lamps. Figure S3 meanwhile illustrates its spectrum superimposed on the sensitivity curve of the human eye, which shows the proximity of its radiation to the most effective wavelengths.
>
> _Fig. S2 - Energy Balance of a SOX LampFig. S3 - Eye Sensitivity to Sodium Light_
>
> The lamp has been subjected to continual improvements in materials and manufacturing technology over the years, which has allowed it to unfailingly maintain its position as the most efficient light source available. Figure S4 illustrates the elevating efficacy of each of the principal light source technologies over time, and there is no technical reason why low pressure sodium should stop here. Whether or not manufacturers find it commercially interesting to make the necessary investments in further improvements is another matter however! That fact alone is responsible for the flattening of the LPS line since the 1990s, when all further research on this technology effectively ceased.
>
> It is interesting to note from this chart that since the inception of each light source technology, it generally maintains its relative position in the league of efficacies at all times - rarely overtaking or falling behind a competitive light source. The sole exception to this is the semiconductor light emitting diode, which has only witnessed serious development in very recent times. There is no doubt that this unique light source will overtake many of its competitors in its efficacy, however it remains to be seen whether or not LED's can be developed in other areas, particularly luminous flux and cost, which would see them begin to threaten other light sources. The prime position which Low Pressure Sodium holds at the top of this chart is expected to be maintained for many years into the future.
>
>
> _Figure S4 - Effects of Technological Progressions on the Efficacy of Light Sources_
>
> _**Advantages**_
> Low Pressure Sodium light has a number of very unique properties arising from its spectral output, many of which make it technically the most suitable light source for road lighting and these are covered in detail on the [next page](http://www.lamptech.co.uk/Documents/SO2%20Spectral.htm). In addition, the large physical size of the lamp means that it has a low surface luminance so it is less likely to give rise to glare, and the low operating temperature permits the use of compact optical systems and lightweight plastic lanterns. They are the favoured light sources for tunnel illumination, particularly in Japan and Korea where underground roads extending 10 miles or more are not unusual. The long lamps may be aligned end-to-end to produce a continuous line of light and this almost totally eliminates the stroboscopic effect of driving past high brightness lights at speed. Driver fatigue is drastically reduced there is a well proven link between low pressure sodium lighting and reduced accident rates in tunnels. Fluorescent lamps also lend themselves well to this application, but SOX offers a more energy efficient solution.
>
> Furthermore, the lamp itself is relatively inexpensive and can be operated on low cost electrical control gear. Of increasing significance is the fact that it contains zero mercury, and can be easily disposed of as non-toxic waste without incurring extra expense at its end of life. Most high pressure sodium and all other light sources employed in street lighting contain mercury and special restrictions apply to the disposal of used lamps. A final advantage is that in the case of a momentary power supply interruption, the lamp will restrike as soon as the power is restored and no cooling down period is required.
>
> The burning position is generally confined to the horizontal position ±20°. Greater inclinations can result in the liquid sodium running down to the lower end of the lamp with the result that the upper part of the lamp becomes depleted of sodium vapour and efficacy is lost. Vertical burning is permitted only for the low wattage lamps, but only with the cap uppermost. Illumination with the cap down would cause an accumulation of sodium behind the electrodes, and the glass-to-metal seals in this region are a weak point which can fail in the presence of excess sodium.
> _**Disadvantages**_
> No light source offers a full set of advantages, and the SOX lamp is certainly no different. One of its principal weaknesses is that no colour rendering is possible under its monochromatic light. Althogh that is not necessarily a limitation in outdoor lighting, and can even be beneficial in street lighting, its principal weakness is that its rated life is shorter than other types of discharge lamps. Typical installations have to be re-lamped every two years whereas the expensive maintenance schedule can be extended to three or four years with high pressure sodium, and the reduced maintenance cost of the latter can totally offset the cost of energy savings with low pressure sodium.
>
> The long life SOX-Plus lamp was introduced in 1994 to counteract this change, but has only been partially successful because the lamp was essentially unchanged, it simply had a longer guarantee period. The SOX-PSG lamp introduced in 2003 is a much more satisfactory solution in which premature failures before 6000 hours, i.e. the first 18 months of normal streetlighting service, have been completely eliminated. A three year maintenance cycle is perfectly feasible with this lamp which attains a survival rate of 92% after 12,000 burning hours. Even though there may be room in low pressure sodium technology to extend its lifetime further, manufacturers can earn greater revenues with more modern light source technologies and for commercial reasons it is no longer interesting to invest effort in the further improvement of the low pressure sodium lamp.
>
> _**Applications**_
> The monochromatic output of the low pressure sodium lamp precludes its use from all indoor general lighting applications, and all outdoor applications where colour rendering is required. This confines its use almost exclusively to the lighting of major trunk roads, where the high luminous efficacy often makes it the most economical light source - so long as maintenance costs are not so high that the longer-lived high pressure sodium becomes a more viable alternative. Security perimeter lighting around buildings is another important application, in which the monochromatic radiation is outstanding for allowing enhanced visiual detection of movement, changes in contrast etc. Owing to the fact that its radiation is monochromatic, it can easily be filtered out with a single wavelength filter plate, and this makes LPS the favoured source to reduce light pollution in the vicinity of astronomical observation sites. By placing a narrow bandwidth filter over the analysis equipment, the sodium radiation can be removed from the sky-glow while allowing all other wavelengths to pass. Perhaps their only important indoor application is in the general illumination of photographic laboratories producting black-and-white prints. The monochromatic yellow radiation is at a wavelength which will not cause the photographic papers to fog, and permits high illumination levels in the working environment
>
> _SOX High Mast Installation at the Van Brinenoord Motorway Interchange, Netherlands_

> 3\. DIY BPI Tinting (See Jason's article for instructions)

I'm planing on buying from Bluetech the lenses that block range from 400-500 .

I should wear them only when I'm in a blue artificial lighting .
If I'm outside in the sun I should not put anything on my eyes...

### Brent Patrick — 2017-08-20T07:36:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/post-221951>

> [shiran said:](/goto/post?id=221944)
>

> I should wear them only when I'm in a blue artificial lighting .

**I should wear them only when I'm in a blue artificial lighting** ?. CORRECT

**If I'm outside in the sun I should not put anything on my eyes.**? CORRECT [https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-17-gears-of-the-eye-clock/](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-17-gears-of-the-eye-clock/)

**Can I wear the blue tec at night when the sun sets as well**?. WHEN SURROUNDED BY ARTIFICIAL BLUE LIGHT YOU ARE PROTECTING YOUR EYE CLOCK

Uvex are a good cheap option for night time ,I use every night and sets me up for a good sleep.

Cover your skin at night and during the Day when inside under blue light or using devices etc TV ,, Uncover outside so your skin and eyes make sense of its environment and can receive the Suns light frequencies for circadian rhythm. [https://www.jackkruse.com/time-7-photoelectric-effect/](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-7-photoelectric-effect/)
