Hi there
I wondered if you could confirm something for me as well? I'm -13 in both eyes (plus a cataract) so need glasses but thinking of replacing my current day ones (that currently will have standard UV protection on) with ones that don't block the UV. Is this safe all the time? Especially as I have a cataract?
I also have a pair of blue-tec ones I try and use in the day for blue light protection and some UVEX for night which help me sleep.
I'm thinking of getting a pair of prescription ones made up liek the Uvex nighttime ones as they are pretty cumbersome and hard to wear on top off of my glasses. Can you recommend where I can get those done at all?
Thanks in advance.
Emma

### Brent Patrick — 2017-08-23T10:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/post-222080>

Hi Emma

""UV. Is this safe all the time? Especially as I have a cataract?""

From Dr kruse FB NOV 21ST 2015 which may be of value and answers your question!

**''The eye doc beliefs: UV light causes cataracts. We all know that D3 can only be made via UVB exposure.''**

**''Eye doc explain this recent data please: "In five early-stage PSC \[posterior subcapsular cataract\] patients taking 5,000 IU of 25-OH D daily for vitamin D deficiency, there was resolution of their cataracts during the 2-year follow-up period."**

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26132781](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26132781)

**''Association between serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D levels and age-related cataracts**

**Here is another paper showing the same protective effect of higher vitamin D reducing age related cateract.
I've just skimmed this paper but it struck me the reason why the women were less protected may be because their highest quintile had significantly low 25(OH)D that did the mens highest quintile group.
"The mean vitamin D concentrations in men (20.0 ng/mL) and women (17.5 ng/mL) were low and in the range indicated by clinical guidelines as mild to moderate vitamin D deficiency."**

**Because vitamin D is more effective at resolving inflammation when 25(OH)D is at/above 50ng/ml 125nmol those with very low levels would not be likely to be getting much protection at all.**

**So if UV light exposure is bad for the eyes why does UV light make cataracts go away?**

**Eye docs have a lot of explaining to do..................**

**You still think UV light causes cataracts or might it be unopposed unbalanced blue light from man made sources?
**
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26132781](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/26132781)

Emma the prescription Glasses are a dilemma for me as I live in Australia and I am able to purchase Prescription but the blue blocking is not sufficient Dr kruse has mentioned this as well with testing he has done ...BUYER BEWARE
But I believe there is a member on this forum who makes these prescriptions

Emma read Ubiquitination 24 and the follow up questions and answers from Dr Kruse many good nuggets to Help you!

[https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-24-are-myopia-light-and-aging-linked/](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-24-are-myopia-light-and-aging-linked/)

### Charyam — 2018-02-28T12:06:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/post-235927>

UK tinted prescription and plano lenses in the UK :-
I've finally managed to get prescription glasses tinted in the UK with BPI Diamond Dye BPI#37604. [http://www.callbpi.com/pdf\_misc/thera.pdf](http://www.callbpi.com/pdf_misc/thera.pdf)
Lenses need to be uncoated , (no antiscratch and no UV protection)
ASDA did this using a lens supplier called TANT (you'll need to tell them this)
I sent my glasses to SPECTRUM in Manchester 0161 203 9000, [info@sdlenses.co.uk](mailto:info@sdlenses.co.uk), ask for Billy.
They were great, really helpful.
It'll cost about £7.50 recorded delivery to send a pair to them.
They'll tint and return them for £30.00. You can send an old pair with a written lens prescription and they will supply prescription lenses at an extra cost, all done in house.
You can do the same with PLAIN non prescription (plano lens) or visit them as they do sell frames as well. I think their price to supply frame and plain tinted glass is about £50.

### jodellefit — 2018-03-19T01:58:17+0200

### Charyam — 2024-11-22T22:08:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/post-342811>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=342030)
>
> I'm about to get my 2 yearly eye test. If I need new glasses can you explain what I need to tell my optician? I have astigmatism and myopia.

Sorry for the delay
Spectacles these days are acrylic. Acrylic aborbs the tints. Glass lenses do not absorb
I suggest you contact Spectrum (they do lenses and frames for opticians) they will be able to advise.
They won't be the only supplier in the UK, you may be able to find someone local to you.
Spectrum will use your written prescription with your own frames and supply lenses and tint them as required.
I'm going to go see them soon with my prescription and choose some frames. Also taking my son's frames and prescription.
Will be getting 70% tint of the BPI DIAMOND DYE 550nm BPI# 37604.
Other tints are available

### [BPI Therapeutic Tints](https://www.callbpi.com/Therapeutic_Tints.html)

 www.callbpi.com

I think I found it in Ask Jack:
Josh (osteo):
According to multiple sources (NASA, FDA, and others) here are the wavelengths of the UVA, UVB and UVC regions:

UVA 400 nm - 320 nm
UVB 320 nm - 290 nm
UVC 290 nm - 100 nm

The big dog for cell regeneration appears to be the combination of IR (810 i guess) with 380nm (Popp) which is in the UVA range...
Skin filtration/sulphation i assume is the big player in cutting the freqs down to size to fit our quantum computing system

Jack:
Reason is simple.........read CT 7 carefully then consider the Ubi data: Sulfation blocks and reflects bad sun frequencies.....what frequencies do we need? AM.......light.

We need IR light to make EZ water.......initially but we need the UV light to make the EZ larger.........bigger EZ the more light can be turned into DC electric current in the CNS and PNS and distributed over collagen..........

So start with IR to make EZ water, soak in Epsom Salts to block any bad light then go into the bad light to get the EZ you generated with IR bigger?

And this is cool too:
Why must your dietary platform have DHA in it? Realize that in eukaryotes, of which you are all a member DHA has not been replace in any mammal in 600 million years. Why is DHA in your tissues big? DHA is how we capture and move electrons around our cells and tissues. Cholesterol needs DHA to work well. Why? Electrons.cholesterol is a fluorophore or chromophore depending on what planar position it is in atomically based upon electrons addition or subtraction. Electron addition is reduced cholesterol. Oxidized cholesterol is missing electrons. This changes its optical physical properties. You won't get that level "clarity on cholesterol" from dwelling on the wrong dietary issues about particle size with regards to how it works. Cholesterol with electrons reduces light emission. Oxidized cholesterol increase its emission in the UV range. That is why it is bad..........no other reason.
Different light emission leads to different signaling. This is why physicists study the composition of light with spectroscopy........it matters in us too.
The atomic arrangement of cholesterol atoms in its ring structure determine the light it emits. You wont hear that in Mr. Moore's book........so I put some index on the glass eye to clear things up how physics dictates how cholesterol really works. Read Roeland Van Wijk book on biophotons for more detail on the data done on this for 90 years.. The science is deep and there is no way to make it easier. You need to come down to my level and see the light. If you dont you'll continue to be fed a lot of crap buy a lot of people, some who you think are your friends.

### lookingforanswers — 2015-04-20T16:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/south-gulf-coast-of-florida.13996/post-161807>

What does everyone think of the south gulf coast of Florida for living? We're currently searching for an area with low EMF. I am SUPER sensitive to any and all forms of EMF at the moment, so we're looking for a house with land away from any cell towers. There seems to be a lot of nice-sized lots in that area, but I'm not very familiar with it other than to know I love the gulf coast beaches like Siesta Key!

Thoughts?

### bionaut — 2015-04-20T19:29:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/south-gulf-coast-of-florida.13996/post-161818>

I live in near orlando. It is really good compared to Northern Va. The gulf panhandle is where we are aiming to end up eventually. It is not perfect you have got towers and many long wave antennas but there is a lot of sun and a higher O2 tension, which I think is totally making the difference. It is pretty awesome but over the next 20 yrs the maunder minimum should be causing an increase in storm potential. So I am opting for a little north and inland to avoid flooding of a property that I would own.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-21T00:07:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/south-gulf-coast-of-florida.13996/post-161848>

Truth Bomb: How can the environment changes your reality and your healthy fast?
Let me give you and example of how a field can determine your reality. Assume your weight is 180 lbs. on Earth. Now you step into a time warp, and I am going to weigh you simultaneously in our own solar system:
On Mercury, you weigh 68 lbs.
Venus: 163.2 lbs.
Mars: 67.8 lbs.
The Moon: 29.8 lbs.
Jupiter: 425.5 lbs.
Saturn: 191.5 lbs.
Uranus: 160 lbs.
Neptune: 202.5 lbs.
Pluto: 12 lbs.
On a star like the sun, you would weigh 4872.9 lbs.
On a white dwarf, you would weigh 234, 000,000 lbs.
On a neutron star, you would weigh 25,200,000,000,000 lbs.
Weight is determined by the gravitational and electromagnetic field that matter exists in. That is reality. Light affects you more than food does. It is time for you to realize it.

CranioSacral Therapy (stopped the migraine instantly during the session on the right parietal release). I know the skull is supposedly 'fused' but I am merely relaying my personal experience here. ### Cpt.Tired — 2015-04-23T23:11:40+0300

>

I agree 110% on the cold for headaches.
Back in my early partying days, when I would wake up with a migraine, I would go to 7-11 and grab a Slurpie. I would sip it fast to induce a "brain freeze headache". Obviously that was painful, but when it dissipated, the headache would be gone! I would be all ready to go drinking again Saturday! (That was a looong time ago..)

But that doesn't work for me anymore. It just lingers.

But my headaches don't feel near as intense as they used to be. I don't wait for them to 'go away' like I used to. I deal with them as soon as I feel symptoms -which usually requires me to leave work, drink a bunch of water, take the Mito. RX (if I have everything) a triptan, a naproxen and then lay down.
I know my headaches would be much better if I didn't take stimulants, but then I would feel just lifeless and bored. No zest at all.
For some reason my headaches get much better if I roll my head back, shrug my shoulders up and flex these muscles really hard. If I hold this position, my headache will go away. But it just comes back again when I release. I really don't know why this works. I have wondered if I have a disk pushing against my spinal cord but there are so many other factors as well.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-need-a-migraine-rx.14000/post-162259>

You know, you might try the following: take a magnesium malate every couple hours - getting in 6-8 per day - and drink a ton of cold water with it - keep eating seafood so eventually you can hold the charge... I'm assuming your calcium is effluxing like crazy from your environment so since you don't seem to be improving in that realm, crowbar it with the mag... the malic acid will help get rid of the aluminum you undoubtedly have - which IMHO screws up your enzymes worse than any SNPs you might have...

If you're eating carbs, that will increase your need for the mag - oh God I'm so stupid - your environment acts like one big carb - which means you need more mag - I would also add chromium -

By stimulants, I'm assuming you mean caffeine - I will just tell you my husband no longer has migraines and all he did was stop drinking pepsi (or 7 a day)... he still has his crappy American diet with other sodas - but not pepsi -

Mag malate is quite cheap and may help you "find your focus" by also removing aluminum - it certainly will help you be less of an antenna...

### Robert_S — 2015-05-01T14:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-need-a-migraine-rx.14000/post-162965>

Dopamine? how about specific amino acids? like Tyrosine? or DLPA? They are the precursors to dopamine.

Also might want to check out the DHA/Uridine/Choline thread at longecity. It's over 4 years running. It's interesting.

I'm leery of taking uridine everyday, but when I did take it, I felt pretty damn good. Supposedly up-regulates dopamine and is synergistic with DHA.

Rhodiola is also another herb that would be worth exploring. My understanding is that it's both an MAOI-A and MAOI-B inhibitor. Both of which involve dopamine. I have read this a couple times, but didn't see the study to validate.

Penny, I just wanted to thank you sincerely for this post. My wife has been struggling with debilitating migraines the last 10 years and I have been trying my best to figure out what was happening to her, to no avail, until I tried your suggestions!

Our life went from borderline hell (8month breasfeeding child, no sleep, migraines... ugh), to a happy place in a few days. THANK YOU.

The migraines are now way less frequent/powerful and we seem to be able to control them with heavy doses of magnesium malata and magnesium bicarbonate + some of the supplements you mentioned -

p5p (1 a day)
25mg zinc gluconate (1 a day)
2-4pills 1500mg mag malate per day
magnesuim bicarboate mixed in throughout the day,.
cod liver oil, 2Ts day
home made sourkraut all week
lowering extra glutamate foods (soy sauce, dried shitake, fish sauce,etc)
CT soon.

I've also started implementing Kruse's ideas as heavily as is doable. We currently have a 8month son, my wife is breastfeeding, and we live in central Los Angeles. 5G/wifi signals are everywhere.

**I'm wondering if you had a minute to recommend any other steps she could take?**

I am ordering hydroxy b12, pantethine and molybdenum now.

Little background:
My wife has struggled with poor health the last 10 years, been stuck in a blue light room designing in front of a computer. She developed psoriasis,
debilitating migraines (she would take exedrin for years). She HATES cold water and has never been able to participate in sports due to scoliosis. She was also stuffed with grains and dairy as a kid and developed ear infections and was pumped full of antibiotics throughout her childhood. Joy.

Now she eats a home made paleo diet (4+) years - evolving to epi-paleo the last 2months, and her health is improving heavily. But the migraines have persisted, so here I am, looking for the next step beyond the food... I've switched all the lightbulbs to red incadencent, installed Iris on the computers and we got our Uvex glasses. Oysters were found fresh at Lax-C downtown LA... And we're considering moving closer to the ocean if we can afford it.

My wife is off Irish decent, red hair, fair skin with frekles. Her redox is without a doubt, probably quite poor... but improving.

Thanks in advance for any help,

Gabriel + Amanda
LA,

You might want to add salt - eat a little salt before using water to hydrate better - google Jack Kruse Potassium for why - magnesium clings to water and when your mitochondria don't make enough water, there is nothing for magnesium to cling to - and so magnesium atp-ase slows down (the enzyme that makes ATP) so you get dehydrated - Dr. Kruse has spoken in many places about magnesium - there is a Knoll's post in the blogs - you can also add vitamin B1 in whatever form you like - 200 mg once a day and she can chew nicotine gum - if she gets nauseous on the gum, just stick her face in a salad bowl of ice water - even just doing that is enough to increase your redox - anyone can stick their face in ice water... after the shower, just rub epsom salts followed by baking soda all over the body and then chase it with a 30 second cold shower - that one move will halt neuropathy... And don't discount the flicker effect from your immediate lighting - google jack kruse flicker effect - actually now even the lighting manufacturers are talking about the flicker effect - go to incandescant bulbs - yellow, red or even regular incandescent bulbs are better than those energy savers - get iris for the computer and stick it on health mode so the screen is red and there is no flicker - you can also look into Ben Q monitors which have the flicker free effect built right into them - at the very least, get flux for the computer - camping would be a great move and walking on the beach is fantastic - good luck finding parking these days And watch the sunrise and sunset and put on some UVEX blue light blockers on Amazon - $8.00 and it'll save your eyes - If she is a stress box, you can try some adaptogens like ashwaghanda - l-serine would be great before bed along with taurine - honestly, it is getting more and more difficult to mitigate the harm that is coming to us - red light therapy is also massive - you can check out rubylux for their light bulbs which work great - try to get an 850 nm infrared light - works great - even sticking ice between the bottom of your head and clavicles would cool down the cerebral spinal fluid which is a fantastic health move - there is a redox rx, and a mitochondria rx - in fact, you can read all the rx's - they are marvelous take care

BTW, all B vitamins, turmeric (would be a great move for anyone), aspirin emit light - B5 and B12 and turmeric emit it massively...

ok, so she's breastfeeding which requires huge amounts of energy - which she doesn't have because her mitochondria is being clobbered by her environment - so looking at this from a purely energy perspective - start thiamine - vitamin B1 - it helps the mitochondria work - we were all recently told to take it - you can take 200 mg 4 times per day - it only lasts 15 minutes so I don't think you can OD on it - you can also take benfotiamine which is bound to fat so it stays longer in the body and there is another darling form of it out there that I haven't tried - JK's latest patreon post chats a lot about how this all works but the long and short of it is she needs more energy - and that is a fabulous way of getting it - epsom salt foot soaks which she breastfeeds would be great - and I would guess her occurence of migraines would go down once she stops - which I wouldn't recommend because then the baby is even more immune compromised... While you are working on your nnEMF mitigation, waiting for test results, etc.
May I suggest - **Magnesium,** it is a natural **calcium channel blocker**, blocks sodium attachment to vascular smooth muscle cells, increases vasodilating PGE, binds potassium in a cooperative manner, increases nitric oxide, improves endothelial dysfunction, causes vasodilation.
Vascular constriction not just hampers brain function, but can contribute to pain, like migraines.
What kind and how much? Magnesium L-Threonate 500 mg/day to 3000 mg/day; however, it is a stole softener; so titrate the dosage starting at 500 mg, increasing as she seems to "handle" it.
Also consider incorporating potassium supplementation, which is used as a chemical bond with magnesium. I don't have a dosage recommendation, potassium can change the heart rate, so titration is important, monitor baseline heart rate and see if it changes as the dosage increases.

From a nutraceutical point of view, look into GABA and glutamate and migraines - [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3451832/pdf/10194\_2001\_Article\_11.pdf](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3451832/pdf/10194_2001_Article_11.pdf)
To increase GABA, the nutrients to take are: Pyridoxy-5-Phosphate (p5p) and zinc (avoid - Aspartic acid)
GABA is both an amino acid and a neurotransmitter. Glutamine is an amino that is a substarte for glutamic acid and the neurotransmitter GABA.

### DrEttinger — 2020-06-22T16:10:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/we-need-a-migraine-rx.14000/post-290430>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=290413)
>
> Nnemf is what's wrong. You need to know the field levels of _all_ your living spaces. 'Faraday' for wifi won't solve anything because any wireless device being used will assault you with 15k to untold millions of µw, not including "background" sources outside your home. Until you learn nnemf is everything then you'll continue to waste your time and health, sad to say but I see it here all the time.

I had my first migraine headache at the age of 9 (1973). It was a 10 out of 10 and woke me up out of a sound sleep. There was no Wifi, computers, cell phones 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5G. 13 channels on the TV and limited AM and FM stations. Background nnEMF was EXTREMELY, EXTREMELY LOW. I would have many more migraines until I found the solution one year later. After seeing many different specialists, my dad took me to one of the only chiropractors in town. Literally after one chiropractic adjustment, I wouldn't have another one until I was twenty. At that time they were histamine-induced from lots of alcohol - we liked to party. I would get facial, chest, and back flushing as well as the headaches. This was not for lack of sun exposure or nnEMF as I surfed almost daily and computers and cell phones were still non-existent. I drove a VW bug, all analog. They eventually went away on their own and I would only have one more bout of them around age 30. Alcohol-induced, but this time it was from an electrolyte imbalance (dehydration and/or acidosis). As soon as I repleted potassium and magnesium they were gone. It's been 25 years and they have never come back.

My point is, there are humans on this planet, well at least one, that experienced migraine headaches that were not Wifi, Bluetooth, 4G, 5G, cell tower, blue light, nnEMF induced. Thank god I didn't waste my time moving to a cave to get away from EMF and used my human biology degree and clinical experience to handle my last two attacks of migraines.

Moral of the story:
1\. Humans can experience health problems that are NOT nnEMF or blue-light induced.
2\. It is possible that a human can get better from a health problem w/o moving to a cave in order to eliminate nnEMF or without the need to layout on a beach 8 hours a day.
3\. Believe it or not, there have actually been people who have been diseased who have gotten better with diet, medications, supplements, prayer, meditation, exercise, rest, surgery, cannabis oil, and taking a placebo.
4\. Yes, in 31+ years of practice I have witnessed that the more one is connected to nature the lower the chance of disease, and the faster one will heal if diseased. And, the more one removes himself from nature the risk of disease increases and the harder or longer it takes to heal.

Just for fun, here is another point of view. Let's take all the billions that believe in a "creation theory" by a supreme being or God.

**The Beginning**
1In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth. 2 Now the earth was formless and empty, darkness was over the surface of the deep, and the Spirit of God was hovering over the waters.

3 And God said, “Let there be light,” and there was light. 4 God saw that the light was good, and he separated the light from the darkness. 5 God called the light “day,” and the darkness he called “night.” And there was evening, and there was morning—the first day.

And so on...

Would a God intelligent enough and skillful enough to create a circadian environment as his first step and have the insight to create perfection in all design be so stupid as to not have the ability of foresight? I had a migraine this morning and think what you posted here would help reduce or stop them hopefully. [e](https://www.amazon.co.uk/MAG365-1132-Magnesium-Regular-300/dp/B0147C517I/ref=pd_day0fbt_1/257-3013508-6366058?pd_rd_w=SCpUO&pf_rd_p=93e1d4b1-bb8c-45f0-baaf-a13934852b31&pf_rd_r=3M4BG1W10GPBH5DS27ZA&pd_rd_r=35779930-4944-45a5-b9f5-b64fe0c73b4d&pd_rd_wg=BtFKA&pd_rd_i=B0147C517I&psc=1)

Lower dopamine levels may increase painful sensitivities to external stimuli (such as light) and influence moods and behaviors during attacks
[https://www.theraspecs.com/blog/we-...e-light-sensitivity-during-a-migraine-attack/](https://www.theraspecs.com/blog/we-may-now-know-why-we-experience-light-sensitivity-during-a-migraine-attack/) "Dopamine is one of the main neurotransmitters controlling sensory sensitivity," said Dr. Kenneth Casey

You may want to investigate why your dopamine may be low.

Dopamine levels in the brain directly correlate with voltage intensity - [https://jackkruse.com/your-gutneurotransmitters-and-hormones/](https://jackkruse.com/your-gutneurotransmitters-and-hormones/)

Migraine is fundamentally a disorder of the brain, not a disorder of structures outside the brain - [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...rent-of-dha-csf-semiconductors-in-brain.7564/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/migraines-a-deficiency-in-current-of-dha-csf-semiconductors-in-brain.7564/)

Dopamine controls hormone release from the pituitary so you lose control of your hormones. - [https://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/](https://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/)

Circadian rhythms in headaches and neuropathic pain - [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/migraines-headaches-and-the-circadian-mechanism.21576/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/migraines-headaches-and-the-circadian-mechanism.21576/)

from

### Fractality — 2015-04-21T07:46:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-friendly-hello.14006/post-161885>

I came across Dr. Kruse's work and this excellent forum in my continued quest for self-engineering towards a greater ideal. I purchased the Epi-Paleo Rx book and hungrily read it. Now I am here to absorb more information.

My ideal is a blend of optimal brain and body health with a moderate inclination towards building a proportional physique. I'm searching for a balance between living in accordance with my genome and developing a physique that adheres to the golden ratio. Vanity is part of that, but it is also driven by the feeling of pushing my genetics to its limit.

I have a background in experimenting with nootropics and will continue to do so, with added excitement as I focus on optimizing my environment.

Fortunately, I have not had to suffer from any serious biological malfunction. I am not afflicted with any Neolithic disease. However, I realize that I can still optimize a great deal of my life. As I get older, I recognize the importance of living according to the genome in an effort to inhibit the aging process. I won't always have youth to fall back on. It is good to build habits young.

A big thank you to Dr. Kruse and the community as a whole.

# Thread: Asian Chicken cakes - from Eat drink Paleo  / Irena Macri
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-chicken-cakes-from-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14025/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 35
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2015-04-21T13:50:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-chicken-cakes-from-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14025/post-161908>

I most often use Salmon trimmings for this recipe

800 g chicken mince \[1.7 lb\]
2 eggs
1 long red chilli, finely diced
2 garlic cloves, minced
2 TBSP fresh coriander, chopped
1 TBSP chopped thai basil
3 TBSP chopped green onion
thumb sized piece of grated fresh ginger

2 TBSP fish sauce
1tsp sesame oil
Zest of 1 lime
2 TBSP fresh lime juice

2 TBSP coconut oil for frying

mix all together and make flat cakes and fry

we serve it on fresh baby greens and more herbs and cucumbers ...and dressing of choice. They keep and reheat really well. Also really good cold as part of a nosh plate.

We also serve them on sauteed kale \[in lots of butter\] with poached eggs and hollandaise sauce

enjoy!

### fitness@home — 2015-04-21T18:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-chicken-cakes-from-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14025/post-161930>

Sounds wonderful! Going to try recipe twice... once with salmon and then again with mackeral.

Pretty sure I will fry it ouside on the grills burner . Thanks for sharing!

(This one will be all mine...Chuck won't eat spicy)

### fitness@home — 2015-05-05T03:02:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-chicken-cakes-from-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14025/post-163307>

OMG! This was good.

I picked up a couple locally raised chicken breasts at the Farmer's market this weekend. Coarsley chopped the meat and combined it with other ingredients. I had forgotten to pick up ginger root, so this mixture sat in the fridge all day absorbing flavors. I added ginger root when I got home and them pan fried them in ghee.

Heaven. Mixed some homemade mayo with a few drops of siracha sauce and served it on the side.

Can't wait to try this with salmon...

P.s. didn't have a chili pepper, so I substituted a good sized teaspoon of chili pepper paste.

### caroline — 2017-07-14T09:12:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-chicken-cakes-from-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14025/post-218795>

### james coldsnow — 2017-07-14T09:39:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-chicken-cakes-from-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14025/post-218796>

### caroline — 2017-07-14T10:01:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/asian-chicken-cakes-from-eat-drink-paleo-irena-macri.14025/post-218797>

okay then! ..... A stick blender usually has a tall beaker

put two eggs in bottom of beaker
add 2 TBSP of lemon juice
and 2 TBSP apple cider vinegar ..... let come to room temperature - don't beat!

now I add all kinds if things that we love ..... garlic cloves, bit of good mustard, lots of fresh basil, fresh parsley, chopped green onions.
good salt
pepper
a little bit of raw honey
2 cups of EVOO

put stick blender right at the bottom of beaker and start blitzing - after awhile slowly bring stick up to the top and I pulse up and down until I am happy with it.

you can use just 1 egg and half everything and forget a lot of the other stuff for just plain and boring mayo!
you can use MCT oil for some of the oil.

let me know how it goes!

### rlee314 — 2015-04-21T23:22:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-natal.14028/post-161957>

really, \*wish\* jack would do a prenatal blog series including lactation. and/or an optimal reset w.a focus on prenatal, post natal and lactation and the first 7 years postpartum for children.
epi paleo.
listen to the optimal reset. there's a Q&A there w/a focus on prenatal and pregnancy. Yew also has a nice summary of this on his qwiki or whatever.

'zip code is more important than genetic code'
environment trumps genetics.
a persons's environment determines how genes express or don't.
MTHFR is a small part of the methylation cycle.
you can get tested via 23andme-takes a few weeks
some insurance companies cover the basic MTHFR test.
knowing you have-or don't have the 2 most common MTHFR SNPs-is one part...knowing if they are expressing or not is another...treat the PERSON not the SNP. just b/c you have the SNP doesn't necessarily mean it's causing you issue--IME and IMO.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-22T00:38:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-natal.14028/post-161974>

If you have the SNP's here is the key point: Your steepness of decline will be tied to ubiquitin rates. "Well-controlled studies" will not mesh with your N-1 because without control of the environment, especially in nutrition studies, ubiquitin rates are never controlled and SNP's expression is linked to this exponentially. When someone with snp's is really sick it tells me ALWAYS LOOK OUTSIDE OF YOU AND NOT INSIDE

This is why I get so pissed with people on SNP's.......they think they can change the inside of their genome with supplements.....and they can't. This is akin to the story of the KT event and why geologist did not believe the asteroid story for so long. When Alvarez postulated the sequence of events that occurred 65 million years ago do you know why geologist thought he was crazy? Because prior to 1990, no one had ever thought that rocks could be changed from outside forces from space........thy just thought climate weathering was the key. His ideas made them realize they had to look further outside. I am telling people that SNP's are not tied to endogenous breakdown in your nucleic acids or the way you work. It is tied to the environment you allow and via ubiquitination rates they become expressed.........that is when they are a problem. SNP's treatment requires an environmental clean up. The Rx should not be prescribing to do things the other way around trying to solve the SNP presence. It is similar to the KT event. It is an environmental epigenetic issue tied to an altered environment increasing ubiquitin rates. SNP just slow down epigenetic changes and this is why in environments with higher exogenous energy flows they get sicker faster than other people. I said this in the Optimal reset Q & A and I am saying it here.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-22T00:39:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-natal.14028/post-161975>

I just saw and 19 yr old with a major pregnancy low battery issue in clinic.........

### Josh — 2015-04-22T01:01:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-natal.14028/post-161981>

SNP's seem to be a red herring for many as the real issue is redox regardless of SNP's. A good redox with SNP's is likely to not show any expression and a poor redox without SNP's may have horrendous methylation function issues. Kendall Stewart developed a system to essentially see whether people could sense the middle of their head to gauge the severity of the cognitive effects and Dr. Kruse has a comprehensive redox tracking system. In addition there are s/sx's that can be tracked and ameliorated with bypass nutrients until redox is restored in appropriate cases. This is what I have done with success on myself and others. If people do not fix their lifestyle/environment issues, then evidence of methylation function issues persists and can be transgenerational.

### Josh — 2015-04-22T02:04:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-natal.14028/post-161986>

Based on what I have understood/misunderstood, methylation is about the ability to expose portions of our DNA to innovate in response to our environment. This includes everything from the development of a fetus to immune function and neurogeneration/degeneration. It is dependent upon redox potential to maintain mass equivalence so that the distances between things are optimal for communication and innovation. SNP's have become a shorthand for assessing the likelihood of limitations on the predominant pathways for these functions based upon ease of testing and the dissemination of likely correlations from multiple sources. Unfortunately, this has obscured the larger relevance of the redox and mass equivalence/signalling issues that so-called "methylation defects" are a subset of. The information available is useful and relevant, but it is neither the cutting edge nor is it comprehensive with regard to all affected processes.

### kovita — 2015-04-22T17:29:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pre-natal.14028/post-162091>

Melanie, from my experience all the lifestyle choices as revommended by JK are appropriate in prenatal period. Prefferentially before already. That is unfortunately often going against the common beliefs and official obgyn recommendation. I have not experienced ANY worsening of my situation during thisregnancy, obviously the putcome is still unknown. By all measn my body seems to be dealing with the complex and demanding task of "fetus hosting" better than most of my obgyn officially healthy pregnant patients. I would suggest a good listening to your own body as important approach as well. Being hardcore keto during the whole cpurse of pregnancy may not appropriate for all and during all phases of pregnancy and specially not if you did not do ketodiet before falling pregnant. There high energy demand periods and calmer times and your body will always direct you to what you need. I personally needed a lot of raw fish and seafood, sea begetables, egg yolks, nature, outdoor CT, cold, stretching, dry brushing, good books and feelings of happiness and internal peace.

# Thread: Can photosynthesis also change when colors change?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-photosynthesis-also-change-when-colors-change.14030/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-21T21:11:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-photosynthesis-also-change-when-colors-change.14030/post-161949>

This video should start at 1:36. What are you looking at? Dr. John Ott found that under natural sunlight, chloroplasts (responsible for photosynthesis) move in an orderly fashion (they're the dots you see moving in a circle). However, if light was filtered through glass or chloroplasts were only exposed to red or blue light, the orderly stream got disrupted and clumps formed, signaling an alteration in cell function.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-21T23:27:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-photosynthesis-also-change-when-colors-change.14030/post-161958>

"Well-controlled studies" don't fare without control of the environment, especially in nutrition where ubiquitin rates are never controlled

### Penny — 2015-04-22T05:50:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-photosynthesis-also-change-when-colors-change.14030/post-162027>

I love the sun - I can actually feel it charging me - I don't care how hot it is - except maybe Arizona summers - I can still function pretty well - but without it, I'm totally parked - like I just want to hibernate - which means my batter isn't charged...

That was interesting about UV light healing...

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-22T06:29:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-photosynthesis-also-change-when-colors-change.14030/post-162032>

Why does life change above the IR band? The spectral array changes until that band in biomolecules and then after it they become disordered.

### raysblue — 2015-04-22T10:54:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-photosynthesis-also-change-when-colors-change.14030/post-162053>

[http://www.motherearthnews.com/nature-and-environment/john-ott-zm0z86zhun.aspx](http://www.motherearthnews.com/nature-and-environment/john-ott-zm0z86zhun.aspx)

### MrPinkies — 2015-04-22T00:12:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bracelets-and-the-kruse-cruise.14031/post-161969>

Hi friends...

As some of you know, and for those who don't... I make bracelets.. This kinda took storm when I sent my great friend, Ruben, one with a lock and starfish on it. Not sure if Ruben is on the forum or just on Facebook, but, he absolutely loved his and posted a picture of it. I have been making bracelets non-stop! This is something that I greatly enjoy to do, it's very relaxing, but, it's starting to have a greater meaning for me.. Jack and I have been talking and was thinking that I could pre-make some bracelets and send them to Jack for them to be available on the Kruse Cruise.

**What I want to find out is if anyone is interested in this??**

I will post some pictures below... just know that colors, charms, etc, are changeable... I can make whatever I get my hands on.. I tend to stick to metal charm and buttons, wooden and glass beads, and leather... For the ladies, they usually stick to the single row of beads and the gentlemen like the double rows better.. but.. I can make either one for the same price.. As far as charms goes... I have not had any problems getting starfishes, but, the locks, they discontinued the one that I loved... I think I have found another one that I like, but haven't ordered them yet..

# Thread: Robert S. Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 41
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Robert_S — 2015-04-22T05:02:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-162021>

Stardate: 4/21/2015

Location: planet Earth, a decent sized rock hurling through space at 69,000 mph, going around and around and around in circles totally reliant on a giant ball of fiery gas/plasma for heat, light and life.

Background: Other than not being able to really walk or stand for any length of time (more than 20 - 30 minutes) and being in my mid 40's, I always thought I would break down in my 60's and 70's. Thought I could procrastinate the inevitable. I guess not.

My first back surgery was in 1988. I was 17. L4/L5 Laminectomy / Discectomy. Didn't help the pain. But destabilized my spine over the next 27 years.

My next and LAST ever back surgery was in 2013. By a neurosurgeon, no less. It was a 'simple' micro-discectomy. 4 mm incision, one probe, bada bing bada boom. In/out. Remove 20% of the L2/L3 disc impinging on the nerve, no problem. Or so they told me. Less than hour. Don't worry about reading the fine print. It's a piece of cake.

Well...not really. Instead I was left with nerve pain down to my knee that I didn't have BEFORE the surgery. I have never had so much pain as I did that night after that surgery. No worries, this happens sometimes, just come back in for a 'nerve block' shot. That helped for about a week. I spent over a year in PT, with 3 different PT's before they gave up on me.

What some doctors don't tell you about micro-discectomies is that while it is an 'outpatient' procedure these days, it still takes up to a YEAR (one revolution around the sun) to heal and in my case quite a bit more. Or potentially never. It's a coin-flip. Or worse, more pain than when you started or in completely different area.

So perhaps it is emotional healing and forgiveness that I need to practice. Perhaps that should be my health goal. Perhaps my total disillusionment of the medical model is really my core issue affecting my adrenals.

Or maybe it's being forced to participate in an affordable 'health' care model that seems fundamentally broken. And focuses on quick fixes instead of creating core health. And ironically in Virginia, we chose not to participate in expanded medicare, so many of us still don't get health insurance. But that might be the bright side of the story.

For example, the recommended food pyramid suggests that eating a ketogenic diet will pretty much kill you. From clogged arteries and/or acidosis. That's what one doctor told me. Literally.

But...but...I feel so much better eating a ketogenic diet?

Here are 2 studies. I find them a bit confusing. Cause they seem to be measuring the exact same thing and have 2 completely opposite conclusions:

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/24380692](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/24380692)
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25855687](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25855687)

And this is medical SCIENCE? Did I miss something? Did I mis-interpret the results?

Quackery is beginning to look better and better to me, all the damn time! With the exception of emergency medicine. And even then, I wonder. Maybe I can avoid that path altogether.

Anyway, so this is my journal and my journey. Day 1 or 16,425 depending on your point of view.

I do suffer from bouts of keto-mania, particularly in the evening when the cortisol winds down. Or up. I can't tell these days. So if this journal is ever less than coherent, then it could either be the distortions in the electromagnetic field of my brain or perhaps some unpaired electrons floating around in the ionosphere.

Be well.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-22T05:40:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-162025>

Why do two studies have different results? Should be obvious if your reading this series on ubiquitination closely: "Well-controlled studies" will not mesh and give similar results because without control of the environment, especially in nutrition studies, ubiquitin rates are never controlled for. Two environments will yield two opposite results quite easily. This is why I dont believe any nutritional studies. I made the point about CRON data and why I knew modern CRON data will never match the one's done in the 1930's. 2015 does not equal 1930. And it gets even worse. Harvard labs dont equal Oxford's labs, and mice dont equal men..........the way science is done is a joke when you understand what an uncoupled system really means.

### Robert_S — 2015-04-22T05:55:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-162028>

That's why I am here. Cause I have come to similar conclusions over the last couple decades. You are preaching to the choir. Most of the time. But I like it. Haha.

I honestly meant no disrespect in any of my posts to you personally. It's hard to convey tone of voice in an email. Let alone lack of visual. 55/38/7 (Visual/tone/words). They left out touch in that British nonverbal study. I guess that's forgivable.

And out of my many faults, blatant honesty is one of them. Almost to a fault. Sound familiar? Haha.

Thanks for the chat, but my circadian clock on the wall says it time to power down for the evening and recharge my batteries.

I hope that it turns out Dr. Jack, that I will owe you a debt of gratitude for helping me/us to remember the part of us that is innately earth-bound.

### Robert_S — 2015-04-22T18:15:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-162097>

Thanks. The funny part of that sci-fi novel is that it's a true story. Haha.

The thing I like about this site is that...it's a different way of looking at things. Both macro and micro.

I totally agree, it's insanity to keep doing the same thing over and over and expecting a different result. At some point, gaining perspective on the situation seems worthwhile. Let's look at the bigger picture, sort of thing.

I'm dumb enough, or naïve enough, or smart enough to actually try this stuff out in my own life. And then judge by the results I'm getting based my own experience. So I'm glad to see others here doing the same thing.

I'm also making an effort to trust myself more and more. To trust intuition which has been almost beat out of us in the name of science and deferring to experts.

Lately, I've begun to meditate more. And getting into the stillness, the quietness of ourselves, inside, also seems to be good for the adrenals. Turning off the blue light / computer / TV helps encourage that process  Somehow I have more time and energy on my hands? Huh? How'd that happen? Might as well meditate and just let go and be. That seems natural.

So I've been in/out of ketosis for the last 5 years. On a totally ketogenic diet, I had lost 80 lbs (about 5 years ago). I kept most of it off during that time. I used to be a body worker. Then I got totally burned out and started doing computer work to earn a living. Decreased mobility, lots of blue light and lots of stress.

After a few years of that, I could barely walk. I could no longer mange the post-laminectomy instability which I had been managing for 25 years, somewhat successfully. But that eventually gave way. And so I opted for a 'minimally invasive' surgery to help get me back on my feet. I thought enough time had gone by and the technology was sufficiently adequate to be helpful this time. It was a gamble on my part. Given an estimated 85%-95% chance of making a full recovery by the expert. I had another back surgery.

Unfortunately I couldn't stand long enough to actually cook meals (for a long time after that last surgery), so I went off the reservation for a while. And ate whatever was easiest. It didn't matter to me. Avoid the pain and buy time. That was my goal. So I gained back some of the weight, which is now rapidly coming off.

So I knew the value of ketosis. For me that was a major piece. But I knew I was still missing a few more pieces.

In January of this year, I had a nasty sinus infection. I was eating a total SAD diet. And went and got some antibiotics. Which I would typically avoid like the plague.

But I took levaquin for 10 days. And the very first dose of levaquin, it felt like a long-time depression had lifted. And I started feeling better than I had felt in a LONG time. Like a decade long. It was kinda like coming out of a deep, dark depressing haze. The fog started to clear. And it helped with the inflammation.

So in February I began reading about DHA, Uridine and Choline on the Longecity website. I was back in ketosis by then. And starting to feel better. Uridine is 'interesting'. Kinda like playing with a box of matches. For me, it seemed incredibly healing. I still wonder about it. Is this stuff too good to be true?

About a week later, I took the fish oil & the uridine and it was like a switch went off in my brain. Primed to burn fat for fuel (ketosis), had an ignition switch (Uridine) and the fish oil totally rocked! More inflammation went down.

The combination of DHA and ketosis is powerful. Tremendously healing. And for me, the uridine seemed to enhance the effects of the fish oil. In a really good way. I still can't recommend it, because I don't know what the long term effects are, but I was willing to throw caution to the wind.

Then a month later, I ran into Jack Kruses' site and he was saying that a ketogenic diet, DHA & circadian is the way to go.

And that matched my own experience to the tee.

For as long as I can remember, my thing has been going out to the state park or to a local wildlife refuge frequently to walk. And an old girlfriend, who just happened to be a clinical psychologist, and I used to always take walks out there (wide open, lots of sunlight, no artificial EM fields). And I called it Vitamin N - she suggested that people could be suffering from Nature Deficit Disorder (NDD) OR Nature Deficiency Syndrome (NDS). It's genetic, I hear.

So putting the three together has been tremendously helpful. And it's only been a couple months, now. I highly doubt it's just a coincidence that all this has been timed with the arrival of Spring in the northern hemisphere.

I might not understand all the terminology, the vocabulary, the words used on this site, but I understand the basic concept(s). And more than that, I'm willing to try it and see how well it works for me.

So that's my story, in a nutshell and I'm sticking to it.

Going forward, it ought to an interesting ride at the very least....

### Robert_S — 2015-05-01T15:21:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-162967>

Journal note: I ate a high carb meal again (about 9 days after the last high carb meal). Consciously. Not habitual or craving induced. Am doing a little experiment. Ate the exact same meal this time. Much earlier in the evening though. Noticed an increased heart rate and that was about it.

The last time I did this (9 days ago), I lost 6 pounds. I'm not sure how that works metabolically, but am wondering if it has to do with scarcity. Starvation and fat storage. Not sure.

But feel pretty good this morning. A little trace of ketones left, nothing significant. Yet. Probably around lunch time I'll be back in ketosis again, I'm guessing (18 hours from dinner).

Might supplement breakfast with coconut oil or MCT oil. Breakfast this morning? Tuna with bacon and avocado. The breakfast of champions...haha.

### Robert_S — 2015-05-07T19:09:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-163537>

Update: On Tues and Wed I had the best 2 days I've had in 2 1/2 years, pain / fatigue /and inflammation wise.

I got lots of dha, sun and outdoor nature exposure. Going to bed earlier, etc. Was feeling significantly better.

I did have some guayusa tea yesterday (has caffeine). Considerably less than coffee. Only 2 teaspoons in the AM.

Haven't had caffeine in a couple weeks. And feel noticeably MUCH better not drinking caffeine.

But today, I am totally wiped out. Viscerally exhausted.

Not sure exactly why, but am gonna ride the lull. I'm guessing the caffeine messed up my sleep cycles.

The good news is I got a shiny new hepa air purifier today. It's pretty loud. Even on low. Might have to cycle on/off it maybe with a timer.

I can already tell in the last couple hours that the purifier was much needed.

Win some, lose some.

Overall, I feel like I am headed a more constructive direction.

Song of the week? Huey Lewis & the News : I want a new drug...

### Robert_S — 2015-05-09T17:38:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-163709>

I noticed that whenever my weight loss stalled, or plateaued or held steady for a week or two (even though in ketosis), that if I drank extra water, the weight loss would quickly resume. Coincidence?

I noticed this phenomena years ago. But just read [http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-4-coherent-water/](http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-4-coherent-water/) and finally put 2 + 2 together.

I knew this instinctively, but water has zero calories. It shouldn't have anything to do with weight loss?? Haha.

The perceptual filters we use (our rose colored glasses) is the major obstacle, I feel, in my own 'get optimal' program.

I am beginning to get glimpses of what cleansing the doors of perception really means.

### Robert_S — 2015-05-12T22:34:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-164003>

I got my omega 6/3 test back after 2 weeks of waiting.

it said I had a 2.8 omega 6 to omega 3 ratio. This is VERY puzzling.

Especially considering all the inflammation I still have.

So what I need to eat more omega 6 fatty acids?

Weird results : z

DHA to EPA ratio = 7.6 to 1
AA to EPA ratio = .8
AA = .7%
EPA = .9%
DHA = 6.8%

It looks like I need to increase my EPA levels more than anything. Maybe increase AA?

### Robert_S — 2015-05-12T22:46:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-164004>

To be honest I was expecting much better data results. Instead I got a few numbers and not a lot of context for it.

### Robert_S — 2015-05-12T23:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-164013>

The info on arachidonic acid (AA) is interesting.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arachidonic\_acid](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arachidonic_acid) (yes, I am sourcing Wikipedia...got a problem with it? haha)

Now if ANY of this is true, then I am guessing I need to increase AA. I could double it if need be. Sources? Tilapia.

Supplementing AA increased lean body mass? increased strength (when used in conjunction with resistance training)?

Another study suggested that AA either increases Alzheimer's odds or slows down and reduces Alzheimer's progression.

A couple other things to note, supplementing with AA in one study lowered systemic inflammation. And high AA in muscle increases insulin sensitivity?

I had high insulin levels in my last blood test. Glucose was about 95.

I know one day of results doesn't mean I should rush to conclusions, but I can take AA and see if it helps or not. It should either increase inflammation or decrease it. Might even help my hippocampus?

OR I could be totally misinterpreting the results and be completely wrong.

I do know I feel better with a higher EPA to DHA ratio. Always have. Especially taken with PS (phosphatidyl serine). Experientially.

Ironically, and quite humorously, based on that AA info, I am either headed for early Alzheimer's (totally not funny) or have practically zero risk for Alzheimer's. Either/or.

None of my family has it. But I do have minor memory issues, especially short term. Which I attribute to chronic pain/inflammation.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-05-13T02:57:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-164032>

### Robert_S — 2015-05-13T17:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-s-journal.14033/post-164133>

Thanks. I was hoping for a 40:1 n-6 to n-3 ratio. I could just happily keep taking my fish oil. But this is what I got to work with  Things could be worse.

I haven't used commercially processed cooking oil in a long time (other than coconut, lard or butter)

I read a lot about this in the last day or so (not to mention over the last 20 years).

There are basically three camps out there:

1) Ignore it, just eat fast food.
2) Omega 3's are the best based on our overuse of omega 6 and seafood is the top choice (and/or fish oil supplementation)
3) Plant based EFA's (flax, sunflower, EPO, etc) - 18 carbon fatty acids are the best, including lots of omega 6 (provided they are handled with extreme care).

And this may seem counter intuitive to the omega 3 push that we all hear about in the media, etc. But omega 6's are still essential.

So I think based on my results, I am going to use Udo's oil (1 tsp to 1 Tblsp) 4 to 5x per week.

I just took a teaspoon at breakfast and I can literally feel the anti-inflammatory effect. It's like taking two aspirins, only better.

Then I'll continue to eat fish several times per week.

And finally re-test. In a month or so. This could be totally wrong. So we'll see.

I suspect it will take several tests over the summer to hopefully figure it out. But time and money well spent in my opinion.

### Jeanne — 2015-04-22T20:03:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-professional-athletes.14058/post-162117>

My son is a pro. baseball pitcher and is in his season now. He is experiencing some "tiredness" in his shoulder making it feel "heavy." He isn't hurting but it's early in the season and he feels like it is inflamed. He eats extremely healthy; ie no grains, no sugar, no sports drinks, etc. He takes curcumin, krill oil, coq10 to reduce inflammation. My question for anyone out there is this: would CT work for him? I recommended it to him and he's agreeable to trying it, although he's pretty cold sensitive (like his mom). Would icing the shoulder be good as much as he can? Any suggestions greatly appreciated!

### Lahelada — 2015-04-22T21:23:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-professional-athletes.14058/post-162121>

[http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-therm....com/cold-thermogenesis-5-biologic-magnetism/](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-5-biologic-magnetism/http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-5-biologic-magnetism/)

### DavidL — 2015-04-23T06:11:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-professional-athletes.14058/post-162166>

Why are the players in the NHL getting younger? Rinks are full of blue light and these guys fly a ton....I think it's becoming a young man's game due to default.
I will say though my old teammate Chris Kunitz is doing pretty good at 36 but he is an exception.

### Jeanne — 2015-04-23T16:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-professional-athletes.14058/post-162203>

> [David Limacher said:](/goto/post?id=162166)
>
> Why are the players in the NHL getting younger? Rinks are full of blue light and these guys fly a ton....I think it's becoming a young man's game due to default.
> I will say though my old teammate Chris Kunitz is doing pretty good at 36 but he is an exception.

Very true, David. Baseball is the same. Players drafted out of high school AND SO many players now in the MLB are from Cuba and The Domenican Republic. I wonder if the reason the foreign players seem to do so much better is their environment for the first 20 or so years of their life wasn't full of blue light and technology. My son has done well to this point, I think, because his diet growing up was much better than the ordinary. However, the constant blue light; hotel rooms, roommates keeping the TV on at night, awful food choices on the road, etc are taking it's toll. He's willing to do whatever he can but I just need to figure out what to recommend with these circumstances. If he doesn't have access to seafood, would fish oil or krill help?

### Nittygrittydanny — 2015-04-23T17:10:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-for-professional-athletes.14058/post-162207>

[http://espn.go.com/espn/feature/sto...ue-jays-prospect-daniel-norris-lives-own-code](http://espn.go.com/espn/feature/story/_/id/12420393/top-blue-jays-prospect-daniel-norris-lives-own-code)

I hope he chooses to follow a path that gets him to a MLB mound.

I have a cousin who was being drooded over by pro baseball in highschool. 6'4" consistant 90+mph fastball that moved, while in JUCO was taught a slider that was a natural pitch for him. By the time he got to the summer wood bat leauge his sophmore season, his arm got tired, he rehabed, but like most kids he was up late, ate poor and his arm/shoulder never returned to what it was at 19 and he walked away from baseball.

Today he's an Army Ranger. In conversations I've had with him he says he doesn't regret walking away.

I hope your son goes as far in baseball as his heart desires and if the day comes where he knows it's time to pursue his next dream, he'll do so without hesitation.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-30T18:26:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/factors-in-finding-a-place-to-live.14060/post-249765>

Well, I'd say up front that even if you are **mobile** (not geographically tied down by family or occupation obligations) and **independently wealthy**, any habitable place on earth has and always will be a compromise. So trick for finding a good domicile is to select a geographic location with maximum positive attributes with fewest major downsides.

If I understand Dr Kruse's teachings for _Black Swans_, then all indicators point to moving your keister out of mid>high latitudes and into in low latitudes, that is, **close to equator** (sunlight, at sea level, strongest magnetic field strength is there, etc).

Among downsides to equatorial regions (I'm sure you could enumerate more) are susceptibility to destructive hurricanes in some regions, and propensity for many such regions to be mired in **3rd world living standards and political/social instability**. Those are reasons I wouldn't go south to Central America or wherever.

A big factor to consider is that from scientific climatology cycle studies and historical evidence it is clear our planet is just entering a new _Grand Solar Minimum_ event. What this means for us is challenging earth changes like:

\- Weakening of earth's magnetic field (which can also reduce protection from solar flares and CMEs)
\- Cosmic ray flux increasing with resulting bad consequences (more cloudiness and coolness, uptick in volcanoes and earthquakes). Volcanic eruptions lead to yet more filtering and blocking of sun's rays
\- Jet streams become more erratic leading to more violent and extreme weather events, including atmospheric compression events (catastrophic flooding) and drought
\- More cooling, precipitation, and snow-- leads to more cooling from albedo effect (reflection of sunlight from snow)
\- Food supplies become precarious due to much shorter growing seasons and weather disruptions

Takeaways from above factors suggests:

\- Steer clear of high latitudes and elevations (e.g. much of Canadian shield will possibly eventually revert to glacier formation)
\- Live at least below 35 degrees N latitude or so (roughly) to avoid worst of cold climate, ferocious winter storms, and also be able to grow much of your own food (that will be key in coming decades)
\- Check online disaster and prepping sites for location of earthquake fault lines and volcanoes, hurricane hot spots, and tornado highways-- avoid them like plague
\- Check FEMA online flood maps to see if your preferred area is susceptible to flooding (even '500 year' floods are now possible)
\- Ideally, build a home underground or out of sturdy material as used in monolithic dome houses (unprecedented hailstorms coming)
\- If you can afford it, over-insulate your home to prepare for long periods of bone-chilling cold weather
\- Try to become as self-sufficient as you can in case grids go down for long periods or food distribution system is disrupted

If this all does come to pass, think what chance your typical urbanite has while commuting to work from suburbia (and being debilitated by typical lifestyle including RF and new 5G network exposure) to be viable in a changing climate. He or she is dependent totally on a fragile electric grid and fuel supply system, plus a just-in-time food delivery system from a compromised agricultural system, and facing a thin veneer of civility in a challenged society.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-31T18:15:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/factors-in-finding-a-place-to-live.14060/post-249819>

As someone else mentioned, it is very advantageous to have a potable water source on your property, such as a _fresh running stream or lake/pond_. No one knows if water supplies might be severely compromised in a future emergency situation. With a good non-powered water filter (e.g. _Berkey_ filter) you are assured of safe, clean water even if grid and other utilities are down.

Other factors include (a bit on woo woo side but valid nonetheless) avoiding living in areas with **geopathic stress zones** (they are common)-- also watch out for radon-contaminated areas. Also, **Feng Shui** principles are a time-honored guide to choosing and siting your living quarters in ways harmonious to life.

Your home search criteria punch list should of course include bullet points like avoiding:

-   population centers
-   nearby military and missile bases
-   nuclear plants and power plants
-   high tension electric power lines (pylons)
-   cell towers
-   major industry and mines
-   crop fields (pesticide/glyphosate contamination)
-   areas susceptible to wild fires (especially in western USA)
-   be sure to investigate current and projected zoning/development plans (don't be blindsided by that new factory near your spot that the county is trying to attract in future).

### Sea Girl — 2018-09-16T16:34:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/factors-in-finding-a-place-to-live.14060/post-251125>

It seems like we might be looking for a better place to live. I am trying to compile my checklist (in order of importance) to help us find the best possible option. I took some ideas from this and other posts and tweaked them to suit our search (East coast of Australia). Please correct me or add any additional points that you would consider important.

Place :

1\. As far away from nnEMF sources as possible (>3km from cell towers, none in direct view; >10km (or more?) from airports, military facilities, police and fire stations; >1km from high voltage power lines)

2\. Low population density (not in the vicinity of big cities, a small number of neighbours, not many smart meters around)

3\. Warm climate with lots of sun (>35 latitude, a good number of sunny days per year, especially in winter when the sun is the lowest). At the same time, not too hot and humid in summer so that we could comfortably spend most of the time outside and not use aircon all the time.

4\. Within 15-30min drive to the ocean – quiet sandy beaches with good waves

5\. Access to fresh seafood including good fishing spots

6\. Within 15-30min drive to shops, schools, kid’s activities

7\. Vast forests / national parks nearby to go camping on week-ends.

I don’t include in my list “good water supply”, though it is important. It is not likely that we will move to the place with desalinated water, so we’ll filter the municipal crap water anyway along with buying imported.

Property (block of land / house):

1\. Decent block of land (the more land the better, however we won’t be able to afford more than 2-3 acres) surrounded by mature trees.

2\. Land not subject to flooding, with no extreme risk of bush fires

3\. No high hills visible from the top of the house (not to see a cell tower popped up there one day).

4\. Room for veggies to grow, animals to keep, kids to run. The possibility to be self-sustainable in worst case scenario.

5\. > 5km away from farms / golf courses with heavy use of pesticides

6\. > 2km away from busy roads

7\. Single storey house, not located high on the hill

8\. Brick (preferably double brick), steel roof, concrete slab

9\. No termite infestation / no mold contamination

10\. Smart meter located away from bedrooms and living areas (I assume, you cannot opt out in Australia?)

11\. Possibility to build a small house on the same block (off the grid), maybe with underground space

### Phosphene — 2018-09-22T00:45:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/factors-in-finding-a-place-to-live.14060/post-251494>

[@Butters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9350/), This is said with all respect (and I’m partly talking to myself). You are young, smart, relatively healthy, and easy on the eyes. I presume your wife is as well.

The world is your oyster! Opening it just requires vision, planning, confidence, execution, and faith. I know it all seems overwhelming with low dopamine. I know it all too well.

We all want to be in the Yucatan, sun baking on the sandy patios of our safe, private (or shared) beachfront villas (or cottages), glass of lovely Malbec in one hand and deuterium-depleted water in the other, seafood grilling on an open fire. Oh Yes we do.

I think most of us also want to figure out a way to be Starfish, and pass this hopeful, gorgeous, healing light on to others. To be of benefit to our confused, hurting world.

If we focus on the idea that many others will benefit from our good fortune, I believe the universe is much more likely to play along.

All that said, you are unfortunately correct that this idealism requires some money.

So how are you (we) going to get it?

I’m a bit ahead of the game since I've been living below my means and saving money for many long and tedious years. Even when the (insanely expensive) kids came along, we found ways to keep saving. I got a little off track the past few years with puzzling health crap and a rather inconvenient midlife crisis, but thankfully not too much financial damage done. Could have been much worse. I’m still getting back on track but the future looks bright, and of course I’m not wearing shades.

I’m somewhat jealous of those starting out now. Yes the world is more daunting, confusing, and downright frightening than ever, but there is still much beauty and love, and many more avenues for creative opportunity.

I think one of best places to start could be the new-ish FIRE communities:

Financial Independence, Retire Early

Even if we don’t plan to retire, this is the best resource I’ve found for preparing to exit the Matrix. I’ve been following many of these principles all my life, but creative new suggestions abound. Just starting to research where to dig in deep.

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) is all over this and perhaps has other suggestions? I know he mentioned some resources elsewhere.

[https://www.google.com/amp/s/qz.com...inancial-independence-and-retiring-early/amp/](https://www.google.com/amp/s/qz.com/work/1274905/understanding-fire-financial-independence-and-retiring-early/amp/)

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-22T18:19:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/factors-in-finding-a-place-to-live.14060/post-251538>

I retired early to be in more control of my health and life. It was not about becoming a 'financially independent' person of leisure, traveling & living in exotic places, lying about on the beach while sipping rum all day, and other idleness.

Tipping point for me was when my tech department moved to new office quarters that were exposed to multiple nearby cell towers (or masts as the Brits call them) as well as a wi-fi network. I took some very high microwave RF readings (as well as ELF exposure from building wiring).

I first tried some mitigation steps (with my understanding manager's approval), including installing silver reflective fabric (made my cubicle looked like it was covered with mosquito netting-- very, very strange indeed) plus carbon sheathing to absorb more microwaves.

This helped, but only to a point. You can't work 100% in a cubicle- there are meetings etc. So I bailed.

FIRE, as you called it, to me means self-sufficiency and frugality-- e.g having a modest but energy-miser abode, ability to grow most of your own food, and happy and secure environs. I'm not there yet but making progress.

I'm not counting on our financial system/economy remaining stable in coming years so all this self-sufficiency effort makes sense to me.

Good luck to all in this endeavor.

### Inna — 2018-09-22T20:03:17+0300

### Jill1 — 2015-04-23T01:11:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healthy-food-may-not-be-leptin-rx.14062/post-162132>

.
I was reading an article in the Journal of Pharmacology - talking about how eating what you think is healthy food may not be. Spinach can cause weight gain. How? Histamine. Whats been dubbed the "Bee Sting Reaction". Leads to swelling, water retention, and more fat. Not sure if there's something to it or not?

Foods High In Histamine:

-   Alcohol
-   Pickled or canned foods – sauerkrauts
-   Matured cheeses
-   Smoked meat products – salami, ham, sausages….
-   **Shellfish**
-   Beans and pulses – chickpeas, soy beans, peanuts
-   Nuts – walnuts, cashew nuts
-   Chocolates and other cocoa based products
-   Vinegar
-   Ready meals
-   Salty snacks

Dr. Loren Cordain, Ph.D. says that this “bee sting reaction” can result in histamine intolerance is, “…a vastly overlooked condition that can derail many.”

Dr. Holly Lucille, N.D., echoes this fact. “Yes, a cup of green beans eaten at the wrong time can actually cause weight gain,” she says. “Many otherwise healthy foods can cause severe bloating and weight gain if The Bee Sting Reaction occurs.”
..
.

# Thread: Should I be drinking plain RO water or alkalised RO water??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-be-drinking-plain-ro-water-or-alkalised-ro-water.14086/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sammy — 2015-04-24T02:50:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-be-drinking-plain-ro-water-or-alkalised-ro-water.14086/post-162245>

I am finally going to buy a reverse osmosis system for my family. But I don't know what type of RO system I should be getting.

Plain ol' RO water actually has an acidic pH (~6.2)

If I opt for an alkaliser (I believe they add marble, CaCO3 and sand, SiO2), it increases the pH to ~9-10

According to pg 97 of Pollack's 4th Phase of Water,

"In **acidic water** conditions, the ample hydronium ions in bulk water should continually chip away at EZ mass, tilting the balance towards a **smaller EZ**. We have confirmed this experimentally: sufficiently acidic pH does diminish EZ size. **Salts erode the EZ similarly**. Consider NaCl: While the Cl- component can combine with H3O+ in the bulk to yield HCl + H2O, the positive Na+ can invade the negative lattice, and go on to create NaOH by extracting a lattice OH- unit. The EZ erodes and adds a water molecule to bulk water. Wherever the lattice is open, **positive ions of any sort can enter and cause EZ erosion**."

But what happens if I am adding a salt(is CaCO3 even a salt lol) to make the water more alkaline????
Am I still getting the EZ benefit of the alkalised water??
Does the Ca2+ cation behave the same way as the Na+ cation to destroy the EZ?
Is it offset by CO32-?
Should I even be adding Ca2+ to water in the first place in this microwaved world???
Why is RO water even acidic???

In need for some advice! Thanks guys

### Penny — 2015-04-24T06:44:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-be-drinking-plain-ro-water-or-alkalised-ro-water.14086/post-162260>

One thing to watch for in an RO system is most of them use 17 gallons of water to make one... I think the WATT one just uses up one -
